events.id: Spiritual Retreat (3 Day Event)-Silverstream Retreat, 3 Reynolds Bach Drive, Silverstream, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Spiritual Retreat (3 Day Event)
events.venue: Silverstream Retreat, 3 Reynolds Bach Drive, Silverstream, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Welcome and Thank you for your interest in the Energy & Beyond: Spiritual Retreat.

This weekend with accommodation is created as a fun-filled weekend of learning with like-minded people.

Ari and Tiffany will be guiding you through a full weekend of enhancing your spiritual awareness, learning new skills, and honing the ones you already have. All this in a SAFE, RESPECTFUL AND INCLUSIVE ENVIRONMENT.

This is a weekend of full immersive interaction with spirit, your soul and heart. Bring comfortable clothes, rain jacket, yoga mat, walking shoes for wet conditions and an open mind for learning.

All bedding and towels are provided. Each room has a bathroom across the corridor, shared between two. Breakfast, morning tea and dinners are all provided. On the menu we have vegetarian curry and soups for dinners, porridge for breakfast. If you would prefer something different you are welcome to use the kitchen facilities. We also ask you to bring your favourite snacks and preferred lunches, which can be stored in fridges if need be. If you have any special dietary requirements please let us know. We will endeavour to do our best however there may well be cross contamination that we are unaware of.

We would appreciate you taking this opportunity to connect without technology by leaving your phone in your room.

Arrival time is Friday 5th June 12:30pm onwards. Departure time is Monday 8th June 2pm. A deposit will be required for confirmation of your booking. All payment to be made before the date.

We have an exciting programme covering a variety of spiritual learnings.

Yoga and meditation for you to relax
Ortho-Bionomy Breathing
Energetic Fields and Protection
Energy Work
Exploring and enhancing your Spiritual abilities
and more

Upon arrival you will be gifted a workbook to fill in your learnings and reflection, this is a great way when you get home to tap back refreshing and reflecting how far you have come in the following days, weeks and months. It’s how we grow…


Ari: ari.true.essence@gmail.com

Tiffany: Beingwithspirit@gmail.com

Ari: 022 369 8462
Tiffany: 021 738 437

events.id: Come Together 2026--Wellington NZ
events.name: Come Together 2026
events.long_description:
New Zealand’s most successful rock series ever returns in 2026, focusing on an electrifying trio of landmark albums. Come Together is bringing three iconic albums to Wellington. This year, they take on Bruce Springsteen’s Born to Run, Radiohead’s OK Computer and Talking Heads’ Stop Making Sense. Each concert will faithfully recreate an album and follow up with a set of classics and deep cuts that span each artist’s catalogue. 
The group is made up of a rotating roster of Aotearoa’s most acclaimed rock musicians. What began as a bold celebration of legendary rock albums has since exploded into one of New Zealand’s most electrifying live concert experiences. The collective includes Jon Toogood, Tami Neilson, Liam Finn, Delaney Davidson, Reb Fountain, Julia Deans, Dianne Swann, Mel Parsons, Ed Knowles, Arahi, Jazmine Mary, Deva Mahal, and many more. Their endeavours have drawn thousands of fans, cementing Come Together as a must-see event on the music calendar.

The group is made up of a rotating roster of Aotearoa’s most acclaimed rock musicians.

events.id: Heathers the Musical-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Heathers the Musical
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s Westerberg High, where popularity is a matter of life and death. Welcome to “Heathers the Musical”. Based on a movie with the same name, the show is a coming-of-age story dealing with love, heartache, defying expectations, and being true to yourself. It originally premiered in Los Angeles in 2013. Since then, the musical has had record-breaking seasons across the globe. It now makes its professional New Zealand debut in 2026, and Wellington is in line to join the school assembly. Described as a high-octane, black comedy rock musical, Heathers is packed with a mix of ballads and showstoppers. The catchy tunes are written by Kevin Murphy (Desperate Housewives) and Laurence O’Keefe (Legally Blonde: The Musical). 
The 1989 film starred Winona Ryder and Christian Slater. It quickly achieved cult status among audiences. Both the movie and musical follow high school student Veronica Sawyer. Veronica is just another nobody until she’s unexpectedly taken under the wings of the beautiful and cruel Heathers. When mysterious teen rebel J.D. arrives, Veronica learns that it might kill to be a nobody, but it’s murder being a somebody. 
This special production will feature a stellar Australasian cast. So grab your scrunchies, break out the Corn Nuts and wait for the bell to ring.

events.id: Orchestra Wellington-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Orchestra Wellington
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With the overarching theme of ‘collaborations’, Orchestra Wellington is looking forward to bringing people together with their 2026 concerts. A dazzling array of musicians, all at the top of their game, will collaborate with the local orchestra. These include the New Zealand String Quartet, NZTrio, Ballet Collective Aotearoa, four brass bands, and many choirs and soloists. 
The orchestra is not just going big with the number of performers, but with the sheer scale of the works presented. Wellington audiences will get to hear Leonard Bernstein’s Chichester Psalms, Hector Berlioz’s Requiem Grand Messe des Morts, and Carl Nielsen’s Symphony 4.

events.id: Professor Brian Cox: Emergence-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Professor Brian Cox: Emergence
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s a stage show that covers vast rivers of galaxies in the cosmos, the ecosystems of Earth, and the structure of the human brain. Emergence is the latest show by English physicist, presenter, and personality Professor Brian Cox. He takes to the stage at TSB Arena to contemplate the dazzling complexity of the universe and how it’s built on profoundly simple principles. 
Emergence is also a scientific history of 400 years of human discovery. How did we come to understand so much about the cosmos? Guided by mathematics, curiosity, and an aesthetic sense of symmetry, our species has begun to read the story of the Universe. We’ve started to carry our spacecraft to the edge of the solar system and outwards to the stars. Brian’s engaging style makes these questions come to life. The audience will be left awestruck and thrilled as the show covers vast topics in an accessible way. 
Brian has appeared in many landmark science programs for BBC radio and television over the last 15 years, from the Peabody Award-winning Wonders of the Solar System to the worldwide hit series The Planets. His previous touring show, Horizons, was performed to nearly half a million people across the world.

events.long_description:
The NZSO’s reputation for artistic excellence attracts many of the world’s leading musicians, conductors, and soloists. In 2026, some of the music-world luminaries include violinists María Dueñas and James Ehnes, trombonist Jörgen van Rijen, pianist Isata Kanneh-Mason, and New Zealand piano prodigy Shan Liu.

events.id: Circa Theatre Season 2026-Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Circa Theatre Season 2026
events.venue: Circa Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Inside Circa Theatre’s waterfront venue, you’ll find two performance spaces. Both offer a smorgasbord of professional theatre throughout the year. Playwrights from international heavy hitters to brilliant Kiwi voices are represented. It also includes programming from comedies and cabarets to contemporary dance and pantomime. 
Before or after your show, visit the on-site café, brasserie and restaurant, Chouchou. It opens out onto a deck, where you can take in views across the harbour. Look up to admire the art installation above. Called ‘Velarium’, it undulates in the wind, mirroring the water in the harbour. 
Circa Theatre began its life in 1976. A breakaway group of actors and theatre practitioners decided that Aotearoa needed a new and innovative approach to theatre. Five decades later, it is still pushing boundaries, making people laugh and cry, and creating theatrical memories. 
If you don’t have your own Circa memory yet, there’s an opportunity to create one nearly every night of the week.

events.id: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season-TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mills Albert Wellington Saints 2026 Season
events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Mills Albert Wellington Saints take to the court for what is sure to be a massive 2026 season. After taking out the 2025 season with a nail-biting win over the Southland Sharks, can they defend their championship title? Kiwi coach Aaron Young takes to the helm again after winning coach of the year. Hutt Valley local Jordan Hunt returns to the team after representing New Zealand in the Tall Blacks, and Melbourne’s Akech Aliir joins the Saints, bringing 6’9” of power and athleticism. 
The 2026 regular season features nine home games at the TSB Arena. Saints’ fans can expect high-energy basketball and a family-friendly atmosphere. Tip-off times have been optimised to make it easier for young fans to witness their heroes in action. 
Join the game court-side and put your lungs to the test as you cheer on your favourite Wellington side.

events.id: River City Express-Paekakariki-Facebook
events.name: River City Express
events.venue: Paekakariki
events.long_description:
Travel to the beautiful riverside city of Whanganui behind one of our magnificent coal-fired steam locomotives. Once there, you can explore Whanganui at your leisure, or choose to enjoy a relaxing cruise along the Whanganui River aboard the historic coal-fired paddle steamer PS Waimarie.
Departing the Kāpiti Coast while much of the country is still asleep, our journey quickly leaves the coast behind and winds through the rural landscapes of the Horowhenua. After a stop in Palmerston North, we head north along the Main Trunk through Feilding and Marton, before turning onto the Marton–New Plymouth Line for the final stretch into Whanganui. Crossing the mighty Whanganui River, we then roll slowly down the short 5km branch line to Taupō Quay, right in the heart of the city’s riverfront.
Option 1 - Sail On The Queen Of The River - Waimarie
Departing from the Riverboat Centre Wharf, your cruise journeys upstream along Te Awa Tupua – the Whanganui River, revealing spectacular views of Whanganui best experienced from the water. Informative onboard commentary highlights the rich riverboat heritage and key landmarks along the way. Guests can also view the working boiler that powers the paddle steamer, and may even have the opportunity to meet the homing pigeons and send a message back to the Riverboat Centre in true riverboat tradition.
Fares
Departing Paekakariki/Paraparaumu/Waikanae/Otaki/Levin
Adult 13+ yrs - $209
Child 2-12yrs - $179
Departing Palmerston North/Feilding
Adult 13+ yrs - $189
Child 2-12yrs - $169
Option 2 - Explore at Your Own Pace
Join us on the banks of the Whanganui River to celebrate the iconic PS Waimarie as she prepares to leave the water for her scheduled dry docking. This relaxed riverside gathering is a chance to celebrate one of Whanganui’s most treasured heritage vessels, with live music, great local food, heritage tram rides, tastings from regional producers, and a vibrant atmosphere at the Riverboat Centre.
If this isn't your cup of tea, browse local shops, enjoy a coffee by the water, or simply relax and take in the scenic river views. You may wish to visit the historic Durie Hill Elevator—New Zealand’s only public transport elevator—or take a leisurely stroll into the nearby town centre to discover more of Whanganui’s charm.
Fares
Departing Paekakariki/Paraparaumu/Waikanae/Otaki/Levin
Adult 13+ yrs - $159
Child 2-12yrs - $139
Departing Palmerston North/Feilding
Adult 13+ yrs - $139
Child 2-12yrs - $119
Optional Add On - Guided Sarjeant Gallery Tour
General Admission - $35 including Bus Transfer
Group bookings of 10+ please contact us via email admin@steaminc.org.nz or call us on 0800 783 264 for a personalized package.

events.id: Porirua Parkrun-Bothamley Park, Champion Street, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: Porirua Parkrun
events.venue: Bothamley Park, Champion Street, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
What is Porirua parkrun? It is a free 5km walk or run event - It’s not about coming first or winning. You can go at your own pace and just enjoy being with other people. It is inclusive and the perfect way to start your weekend in the outdoors chatting to people.

The start occurs just upstream of the Bothamley Park carpark. This car park is off Champion street, opposite the intersection with Windley Street. Head to end of carpark where the concrete ends and there is a gate. For location see https://goo.gl/maps/wEohfp9bRu82.

The course is following the old coach road (circa 1870's) established for the early farms in the area. Today this historic track is located within a park, away from cars and now used by walkers, runners and cyclists.

The track is generally 3 metres wide with a gentle gradient and a compacted gravel surface beside the Kenepuru Stream through a picturesque wooded wild valley. (Note there is a pinch point for a short section after the 2km marker where participants need to go single file each direction for approx 30 metres)

The valley runs east-west with mature trees which means this has to be one of the most sheltered places, out of the wind to exercise in Wellington.

What does it cost to join in? Nothing - it's free! but please register before your first run or walk, so you can get your timing results. You only need to register with parkrun once, to participate at any of the parkrun events around the world. Don't forget to bring a copy of your barcode. If you forget it, you won't get a time.

How fast do I have to be? Walking is fine as long as you can finish the 5km. Please come along and join in whatever your pace!

One dog per adult is welcome, as long as it is on a short hand held lead.

Children welcome but those 10 years and under need to be kept within arms reach of a supervising adult.

Note there is no toilets in the park, so go before you go.

We're friendly! Every week we grab a post park run/walk coffee/tea at Get Fixed within Kai Tahi at 20 Parumoana Street, to mix exercise with socialising.

There are volunteering opportunities even week. So those doing Duke of Edinburgh or similar challenges can get volunteer credits. Ensure you contact us well in advance at "porirua@parkrun.co.nz"

events.venue: 3 railway ave & Maoribank Park, Upper hutt, New Zealand 5018,3 Railway Ave, Maidstone, Upper Hutt 5018, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Upper Hutt Rams Old Timers Day 2026
Upper Hutt Rams RFC would like to invite all past and present players, teams, members, supporters, sponsors, volunteers, and families to our 2026 Old Timers Day and Club Day.

Reconnect with old teammates and club members from the Rams, Upper Hutt, and Rimutaka Rugby clubs, bring the family, and celebrate with us at Maidstone Park.

The day kicks off with junior rugby in the morning and wraps up with our Premier side taking on HOBM at 2.45pm for the Gillies Melville Cup.

This is a celebration of our entire club and community, including junior rugby, senior rugby, netball, life members, volunteers, supporters, and sponsors who have helped make the Rams the club it is today, and all those who continue to build its future.

Let’s pack Maidstone and celebrate the proud history and spirit of the Rams community. More information to come.

events.id: Financial Planning for Retirement-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Financial Planning for Retirement
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Financial Planning for Retirement
Massey University's annual Retirement Expenditure Guidelines study (2019) found:
Just 40% of pre-retirees believed they were well-prepared for retirement, and 57% considered retirement to be a concern.  They also confirmed, New Zealand Superannuation is not sufficient to fund the retirement most people want.
Ministry of Social Development (2020):
22% of all people receiving New Zealand Superannuation also receive additional support payments, with over 44,000 superannuants receiving additional accommodation support,
And if you needed to obtain Government assisted accommodation, at the end of March 2020, more than 16,300 households were waiting for state or social housing.
Living in a motel or boarding house with strangers (who may have other vulnerable backgrounds) is not an ideal or safe location to spend your retirement years.   But for many, it is the only option they can afford.
If you have not planned for your retirement, then this course is you! 
This course has been developed for people in their 50’s or 60’s that are looking at retirement and have no idea if they can financially survive it: what to do, what $’s they need, how or where they can generate additional income, or even where to go to get help. 
It is targeted towards those that still have some time (for some - not much) to make adjustments, learn some basic financial skills, make some big decisions, and positively influence the quality of their pending retirement. 
You will be introduced to a simple step by step guide to developing a financial plan for retirement. 
You will work though a case study in class, and can then go away and repeat the process with your own data/details.  You will be provided with comprehensive course notes to enable you to update the case study information with your own information. 
At each step, key considerations and financial concepts will be defined, explained and discussed to help you understand the financial requirements of retirement.  
Are you ready for the retirement lifestyle you hoped or dreamed of?  If not, then this course is for you.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Disclaimer:  The tutors are not financial advisors.  No investments, products nor specific recommendations will be provided.  A step by step planning process will be taught by working through a case study.  Key considerations that should be used by the participants when looking at their own options to bridge the financial gap between what is available through superannuation and what is required to meet lifestyle goals will be provided and discussed.  Product names may be used in examples and explanations to help participants understand the financial concepts and the planning process.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Financial Planning for Retirement
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Financially successful people don’t achieve their wealth by accident – they make and execute clear plans. Your retirement is on the horizon, and research shows you’re most likely nowhere near financially ready for the retirement you desire and deserve! Get a complete and utterly practical financial plan, so you can have the retirement you want.

Massey University's annual Retirement Expenditure Guidelines study (2019) found:

Just 40% of pre-retirees believed they were well-prepared for retirement, and 57% considered retirement to be a concern. They also confirmed that New Zealand Superannuation is not sufficient to fund the retirement most people want.
Ministry of Social Development (2020):

22% of all people receiving New Zealand Superannuation also receive additional support payments, with over 44,000 superannuants receiving additional accommodation support, and if you needed to obtain Government-assisted accommodation, at the end of March 2020, more than 16,300 households were waiting for state or social housing.

Living in a motel or boarding house with strangers (who may have other vulnerable backgrounds) is not an ideal or safe location to spend your retirement years. But for many, it is the only option they can afford.

You will work through a case study in class, and can then go away and repeat the process with your own data/details. You will be provided with comprehensive course notes to enable you to update the case study information with your own information.

At each step, key considerations and financial concepts will be defined, explained and discussed to help you understand the financial requirements of retirement.

Are you ready for the retirement lifestyle you hoped or dreamed of? If not, then this course is for you.

events.id: T-Rescue Training-Intergen House, 50 The Terrace, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: T-Rescue Training
events.venue: Intergen House, 50 The Terrace, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Key objective of the session is: to train other people in carrying out T rescues in mildly rough water
The expectation is that the trainer is the person in the water and the trainee is in the kayak cockpit so the trainee is warm for most of the session. Only at the end will we ask the trainee to fall out and be rescued, and that's voluntary so that they can have that experience to inform their own ability to rescue others.
Key areas of focus:
Managing the swimmer around the kayak
Lifting the kayak onto the spraydeck � ideally we would have some weight in the bow so that this is hard
Holding the kayak on edge to support the other boat � this is the single item that people have most trouble with
Safety
No need to run safety in protected water but in the rough water we will reserve one trainer for safety

Towing
Ideally we would also practice putting a tow line on to take the kayaks out to protected water and also taking the kayak to swimmers who are separated from the kayak back to their kayak
On-Shore
On-shore training to run through the lifting and kayak on edge

events.id: Cheesemaking - Halloumi & Ricotta-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cheesemaking - Halloumi & Ricotta
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Discover how easy it is to make halloumi and ricotta cheeses from scratch. You'll make the cheese and get to take it home too! All ingredients provided.

Description
Cheesemaking - Halloumi and Ricotta

By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• Make halloumi and ricotta at home
• Use equipment from your own kitchen and/or know where to source it from
• Practise good hygiene and sanitation when making cheese at home
• Describe different types of milk (store-bought versus raw)

All ingredients are provided (vegetarian rennet is used) as are recipes and detailed handouts to take away with you. Bring an apron, dishcloth, 3 or 4 teatowels, a pen to take notes, a 2 litre container and plastic sealable food bags to take your cheese home.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Conflict Management and Resolution Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Conflict Management and Resolution Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Conflict Management and Resolution Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
While many strive to avoid it, suppressing thoughts, feelings, and values to sidestep disagreements can lead to unresolved issues. Learning to manage and resolve conflicts equips you with the tools to navigate these challenges effectively, fostering understanding and collaboration.
 Aim of this workshop:  To empower you with the skills and confidence needed to effectively manage and resolve conflicts.
 Learning Outcomes:
By the end of the workshop, you will:
Recognise how your actions and behaviors influence conflict.
Develop your ability to clarify and manage expectations.
Acquire practical tools for effective conflict management.
Create a sustainable action plan to continuously apply conflict resolution strategies.
[You must be a NZ citizen or resident to register in this course for free and you must provide photo ID when you attend.)
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Come along for a relaxed and creative morning of sewing, crafting, and inspiration!
Bring your own sewing, quilting, knitting, crochet, or crafty project to work on, or simply come and have a play with the fabric and supplies we already have available. Whether you’re experienced or just keen to try something new, everyone is welcome.
We’ll have a sewing machine, overlocker, and plenty of fabric to experiment with. Maybe you’d like to add an embellishment to a top, trim some cuffs or a hem, or start creating something completely new — this is the perfect space to have a go and get creative!
Feel free to bring along any spare fabric, craft supplies, or treasures to swap and share too.
Come enjoy a fun morning of creativity, chatting, and connecting with others in our community.

We’d love to see you there. We will be in the main entrance/drop in space.

events.id: From Rags to Reuse – Upcycling and Sustainability Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: From Rags to Reuse – Upcycling and Sustainability Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Stella Carruthers, textile artist and eco lifestylist, will guide you on this one-day workshop to learn about sustainable living from a reduce and reuse perspective. Learn about textile waste as a global issue and explore rag rug making techniques - you will leave the workshop with the skills to finish a project at home. Investigate the 8 pillars of the zero-waste movement and how they can inform low waste living. Identify the key components of greenwashing so you can make informed purchases as a conscious consumer. Finally, leave the workshop feeling empowered to explore wider social and political actions as they apply to waste issues in contemporary society.
Most materials provided but please bring a pair of small sewing scissors and sewing shears if you have them, pen and paper, and your lunch.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- talk confidently about textile waste as a global issue and be empowered with the skills and ideas to reduce your personal textile waste footprint through recycling and upcycling practices
- have the beginnings of a rag rug made using the plait and stitch technique and gain the skills and knowledge to finish the project at home.
-Identify and describe the 8 pillars of the zero-waste movement and how they apply to recycling practices in contemporary Aotearoa New Zealand society.
- Understand what greenwashing is and have debunked some common greenwashing myths prevalent in society.
- Have learned some practical tips for living low waste in the city.
- Feel empowered to explore wider social and political action as it applies to waste issues in the modern world.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get The Job - CV And Cover Letters-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Get The Job - CV And Cover Letters
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Are you looking to polish your CV and cover letter writing skills to nail your next job application? This course provides you with the tools to create a strong and updated CV and cover letter writing, using skills that are personalised to you.

Description
Explore how to appropriately structure and polish your CV and cover letter in order to achieve optimum results. This course prioritises you. Identify your key skills and weave these into your CV and cover letter to land you your next job interview.

Whatever job you are going for, there are key characteristics that should not be missed in your CV and cover letter. The focus of this course is to provide you with the same lens as a recruiter. By learning how your job application is reviewed by a recruiter, you will gain skills to streamline your CV, and improve your cover letter writing. This will increase your chances of being a successful candidate and sets you up to land an interview for your ideal job.

It doesn’t matter what previous experience you have - everyone has a range of skills that they can offer to their future employer. Fortunately, a strong and realistic CV and cover letter will showcase these skills and increase your chances for job success.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Recognise the features of an effective, well-crafted CV and cover letter and apply these to your own job applications;
- Apply different approaches, styles and techniques while writing your CV and cover letter depending on the context and job;
- Help others with their CV and cover letter writing (friends and whānau).

[Note: You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the free course fee option. A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Prerequisites
There is no prior knowledge or experience required. This course is applicable to all levels of candidate experience - it doesn’t matter if the participant is applying for their first ever job, or they are well experienced in the job market. I tailor my content to empower everybody regardless of their previous experience or job experience.

events.id: Jewellery Making-Soldered Fidget Rings-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery Making-Soldered Fidget Rings
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Classic textured rings, with a unique and exciting twist! Learn to make your own fine jewellery fidget rings from copper, brass and silver - beginners welcome! All materials included in course fee.

Description
In this one-day workshop you are offered a hands-on opportunity to create custom rings with fun mobile components and unique textures. We'll also practice and build experience in the essential jeweller’s fabrication skills of sawing, filing, soldering, sanding and polishing.

What are fidget rings? Also known as spinner rings or stim rings, fidget rings have loose bands attached that can move and swivel around - it's interactive sculpture you can wear!

In this course you’ll be lead through the design and creation of custom copper and silver rings, with copper, brass and silver wire spinner bands adding novelty and movement to a classic ring design.

This course offers beginners a fun introduction to jewellery making, and for those with some jewellery experience, it offers a great way to advance and expand your making techniques to new levels.

All materials included in course fee.

Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby. Remember to bring your reading glasses if a have them.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: 7 Hospital Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Have you been looking for a chance to visit Kaicycle Urban Farm? Come along our Community Open Day in Hune / June. This year we’ve decided to keep our community open days going throughout the winter as we’ve had so much interest which is awesome! Come along for as much or as little of the day as you would like.
9:30am - 12.30pm COMMUNITY WORKING BEE - Registrations required.
Growing local kai in ways that are good for the whenua is climate action. Come and get your hands in the māra, learn practical skills, and meet some cool people. Please register at www.kaicycle.org.nz/volunteer
12:30PM - 1:30pm, KAI BREAK
Join us for a shared lunch and a chilled hang out in the māra. Please bring some kai to share if you can.
1:00PM - FARM TOUR
If you haven't been to our urban farm before, this is a great opportunity to find out what we're all about and how our farm and community composting works.
1:30pm - 3.00pm WORKSHOP: GROW YOUR OWN HERBS
All the deets here: https://kaicycle.org.nz/new-events-and.../growing-herbs

events.id: Massage (By appointment only)-12 King Street, Upper Hutt-Facebook
events.name: Massage (By appointment only)
events.venue: 12 King Street, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Oasis Centre offers Massage by appointment. During your spiritual Massage session you will feel your body, mind and soul nurtured in the most spiritual, unwinding and relaxing way. Mindful healing touch and care is given so that you can receive the full benefit of this experience.
The benefits of Massage Therapy are many and varied, and include:
•Body realignment
•Clarity for the mind
•Motivation
•Pain relief
•Rejuvenation
•Relaxation
•Relief of stress and tension
•Sense of calmness
•Therapeutic tension release
Massage is an excellent therapy for relieving pain and stiffness from the muscles of the face, scalp, neck, body, legs, upper back to the shoulders. We often use hot stones to promote deeper relaxation and muscle release.
Massage/Spiritual Healing is also an effective method to bring about a deep relaxing, enjoyable and harmonious healing. In some cases it will deal with the emotional release that is required at the time.
Come and enjoy the healing atmosphere. Have a Massage session and experience the benefits it gives to your mind, body, soul and spirit.
Each Massage session usually takes about an hour. Bookings are required as spaces are limited.
*Bookings Only*
Cost: Unfortunately we have had to set a $25 minimum on the Koha/Donation.
Phone Anne on 027 6404968

This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Learn to retain names and lists, learn vocabulary, deliver presentations without notes, and boost your professional efficiency. events.id: Manahau Embodied Movement With Kaia Hawkins-Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Manahau Embodied Movement With Kaia Hawkins
events.venue: Nōku te Ao Capital E, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central, Wellington., Wellington
events.long_description:
At Korikori Nohinohi with Nōku te Ao Capital E, a different artist or performer joins us on the first Saturday of every month.

This June, Join Kaia Hawkins for a joyful session of waiata and guided movement. Dance together, feel the music in your body, laugh, connect and share joy. No dance experience needed, just a willingness to let the rhythm carry you!

Kaia Hawkins is an Open Floor movement facilitator creating playful, inclusive spaces where whānau connect through music and movement. Her work centres embodiment, joy, and intergenerational connection.

There’s no need to book, just come along at 10am and join in the fun.

Find us at Ngā Paiaka Bleachers, on the ground level of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui.

We appreciate donations to support our free tamariki programmes.

Ages: best suist ages 0-5
Address: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria Street, Wellington Central

events.name: Massive Theatre Company Essentials Workshop
events.venue: Te Whaea: National Dance and Drama Centre, 11 Hutchison Road, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
This popular two-day workshop is for people aged 20 years and over who are keen to learn how Massive Theatre Company creates theatre. You may be a beginner, have some previous training, or be an experienced practitioner - all levels and abilities are welcome.

Taught by Massive's Artistic Director, Sam Scott, the workshop content changes each time, so it is suitable for those who have attended a previous Massive workshop as well as those who have not.

The workshop runs from 10am to 4pm each day. Please arrive no later than 9.50am, so you are ready begin the workshop at 10am.

Massive Essentials do tend to fill up fast so book your spot now! To reserve your place click the link below or copy it into your browser, then click on the 'BOOK MY PLACE' button to go through to the enrolment form:

https://www.massivecompany.co.nz/workshops/massive-essentials-ages-25-up/events/wellington-june
Important: You must be available for both days of this workshop, and attend each day in full, to participate in this workshop.

We welcome a koha for attendance.

events.id: Self-Guided Tour of Stonehenge Aotearoa-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Self-Guided Tour of Stonehenge Aotearoa
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Explore Stonehenge Aotearoa at Your Own Pace

Our self-guided tour includes a map and a 12-minute audio-visual presentation to help you uncover the secrets of the henge. Afterward, head down to the stone circle and try out its unique acoustics — clap your hands and hear the echo respond, or whisper and see how far your voice carries.

A self-guided tour typically takes around 30 minutes, but many visitors stay longer simply because it’s so much fun. The bigger your group, the better the experience!

Please arrive at least 30 minutes before closing time.
Available during our opening hours: 10 AM – 4 PM.

events.id: Shared 90-Minute Guided Tour-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Shared 90-Minute Guided Tour
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Join a Shared Guided Tour and experience Stonehenge Aotearoa through the eyes of an expert. In this 90-minute journey, you’ll uncover how ancient cultures measured time, tracked the Sun and Moon, and connected the heavens with life on Earth.

Your experience begins with a captivating introduction and a 15-minute audio-visual presentation explaining how the Henge works. Then, step outside with your guide for a detailed tour of the stone circle, where astronomy, archaeology, and mythology come together in one unforgettable setting.

Perfect for visitors seeking a deeper understanding of the stars and stories that shaped human history.

events.id: Moving Meditation: Attention, Space, Endurance, and Hip Articulation with Djibril Sall-80 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Moving Meditation: Attention, Space, Endurance, and Hip Articulation with Djibril Sall
events.venue: 80 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Moving Meditation: Attention, Space, Endurance, and Hip Articulation with Djibril Sall
This series of workshops explores movement as a form of meditation by training attention through the body, space, relational awareness, and endurance. Participants are guided through slow floor-to-standing warm-ups, spatial orientation exercises, group navigation tasks, and sustained hip articulation practices. The workshop emphasizes clarity, choice, and duration, making it accessible to participants with varied movement backgrounds. Previous movement experience is helpful but not required. Participants are encouraged to work at a self-determined pace and level of intensity.
Djibril Sall is a queer Senegalese performer, choreographer, and writer whose work sits at the intersection of racialized migration, belonging, and in-betweenness. Born in Dakar and raised in a working-class family in the Deep South of the United States, his practice is shaped by movement across classed, racialized, and geopolitical borders. Drawing from his own migration history, he asks how trauma might become a departure point for pleasure, disclosure, letting go, and open-hearted connection.
Workshop Info
Tuesday 2nd June, 10-11.30am
Thursday, 4th June, 6-7.30pm
Saturday, 6th June, 11am-12.30pm
Tuesday, 9th June, 10-11.30am
Thursday, 11th June, 6-7.30pm
Saturday, 13th June, 11am-12.30pm
Footnote Dance Studios
Level 1, 60 Dixon Street Te Aro
Studio only accessible via stairs
$10 per workshop
We encourage you to participate in at least 2 workshops to get the most out of the experience.
Djibril’s visit to New Zealand has been made possible by the Goethe-Institute New Zealand, in partnership with Footnote New Zealand Dance and Lōemis.
For more information and to register visit www.footnote.org.nz/events/workshop-djibril-sall

events.id: WIDance Open and Youth Classes Term 2 2026-Te Whaea, 11 Hutchison Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: WIDance Open and Youth Classes Term 2 2026
events.venue: Te Whaea, 11 Hutchison Rd, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come dance with us!
Join the WIDance team as we slide, bounce, wiggle, roll, and dance our way into Term 2, 2026!
Whether you’re a seasoned dancer or just starting out, our classes are designed to be accessible, engaging, and fun for everyone.
Our classes offer a safe and inclusive space to find joy, laughter, and creative expression through contemporary dance, improvisation, dance-based games, and composition. Through movement, we build body and space awareness, confidence, and connection.
Wellington Youth Class:
Looking for a fun, creative, and confidence-boosting activity for your young person? Our six-week Youth Dance program is designed for young people aged 7–16 (flexible) to learn, play, and grow through the art of movement.
Over the term, dancers will:
Build foundations in contemporary dance
Develop body awareness and coordination
Engage in social, team-building games and improvisation
Explore creativity with props and playful movement tasks
We celebrate individuality — each student is encouraged to move in their own way, discover new skills, and enjoy the magic of expressing themselves through dance. Whether a beginner or experienced, every dancer is welcome!
Wellington Open Class:
Our open class has been running for 15 years and is a vibrant, welcoming space where dancers of all experience levels come together to move, create, and express themselves. Each session blends technique, improvisation, and choreography, giving you room to explore your own rhythm and creativity.
This year, our technique work now includes exercises from UK based Stopgap Dance Company’s IRIS syllabus — an inclusive, dynamic approach that supports strength, awareness, and artistry for everybody.
"IRIS is an inclusive dance syllabus that gives teachers a framework to develop the disabled dancers of tomorrow. It is a consolidation of Stopgap’s experience of training professional disabled dancers, and it contains tried and tested methods that the company has been developing since 1995.
IRIS is divided into four levels: Include, Respond, Integrate and Specialise, each having its own set exercises, guidelines and assessments. IRIS will give disabled youth and adults the same level of structure, balance and rigour of dance education as their non-disabled peers and provide the opportunity to accumulate skills and experiences necessary for the professional industry".
Term 1 runs for 6 weeks, on the following dates:
Saturdays: 2nd May - 13th June (no class May 30th)
Youth Class: 10 - 11am,
Cost: $55 for the term or casual $10 per class
Open Class: 11 - 1pm
Cost: $120 waged. $85 unwaged. casual $20 per class
New option: Technique class only, 11am to 12pm, $60.
To book:
Email: sumara@widance.nz
Phone: 021 0501565
This project is proudly supported by Wellington City Council.
Who are we?
WIDance is one of Wellington's leading dance providers for disabled and non disabled dancers, striving to provide equal opportunities for all to dance. Established in 2010, we run open classes, workshops, and performance events throughout the Wellington region.
Our mission is to provide equal opportunities to dance experiences for diverse communities, advocating for access and empowerment.
#danceforall #accessforall #communitydance #disabilitydance

events.long_description:
Stephanie Murray Mortgages Winter Ladies Day Fashion in the Field
Ladies Day at the Races returns to Trentham Racecourse on Saturday 6 June 2026, bringing with it a fantastic afternoon of winter racing, socialising and style. Alongside a great card of racing, we’re excited to introduce the Stephanie Murray Mortgages Winter Ladies Day Fashion in the Field.

Fashion in the Field is all about embracing winter racewear and showcasing your raceday style trackside. Whether your look is classic and elegant, modern and tailored, or bold with statement winter pieces and accessories, this is your chance to dress up and be part of the fun.

This year’s categories are:
Best Dressed Lady, Best Dressed Gent and Best Winter Ensemble

So gather your friends, plan your raceday outfits and enjoy a stylish winter day out at Trentham Racecourse. Whether you’re entering Fashion in the Field or simply coming along to enjoy the atmosphere, Ladies Day at the Races is the perfect excuse for a fun afternoon out this winter.

We can’t wait to see everyone’s winter raceday style this June.

events.id: The Art of Chocolate High Tea | 6 JUNE 2026-Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand,Level 2, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: The Art of Chocolate High Tea | 6 JUNE 2026
events.venue: Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand,Level 2, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Art of Chocolate High Tea | 6 JUNE 2026
Experience an exquisite selection of the finest French Pâtisserie prepared by our highly creative & talented Diplôme de Pâtisserie students.

About this Event

Experience an exquisite selection of the finest French Pâtisserie prepared by our highly creative and talented Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand Diplôme de Pâtisserie students. Tickets are allocated for specific days and seating times, please ensure you arrive promptly at the time-slot you have booked for.
The Art of Chocolate: make sure to bring your sweet tooth as this terms High Tea service will be a love letter to all things chocolate.

Ticket Information:
IMPORTANT NOTE: Tables are arranged per order. If you purchase tickets separately you need to e-mail to advise which other ticket holders you wish to be seated with.
Date: Saturday 6th June. (other sessions are also available, to purchase tickets for these events please click here. Tickets are non-transferable, please ensure you purchase tickets for the correct date.)
Price: $45 per person includes High Tea and complimentary Tea and Coffee.
Booking Times: 11.30am | 12:00pm | 2:30pm | 3:00pm
Tickets are allocated for specific days and seating times, please ensure you arrive promptly at the time you have booked for.
The estimated time to enjoy the full Le Cordon Bleu High Tea experience is one hour. We invite you to sit back, relax and enjoy our students' collection of French Pâtisserie.
Dietary Requirements: Our menu is set and we are unable to make any menu alterations, or cater to any specific dietary requirements (including religious diets) or food allergies. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.



What is Brasserie Le Cordon Bleu?
Brasserie Le Cordon Bleu is our training restaurant where our students get practical experience in a real restaurant environment - all under the expert guidance of their chef lecturers. In the kitchen, we have students from our Diplôme de Pâtisserie programme. In the dining room we have our Bachelor of Culinary Arts and Business students serving you and putting their theory skills into practice.
Brasserie & High Tea Refund Policy
• No refunds are provided unless the event is cancelled by Le Cordon Bleu NZ.
• Customers can arrange for someone else to take their place.

events.id: Dark Beer Week 2026-Garage Project Taproom, 91 Aro St, Aro Valley, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Dark Beer Week 2026
events.venue: Garage Project Taproom, 91 Aro St, Aro Valley, Wellington
events.long_description:
Dark Beer Week 2026

Got milk? We have! The cows have finally come home, they’re all here, and it’s time for Dark Beer Week 2026.

Head to GP Aro and GP Kingsland between June 2-8 for a week stacked with velvety stouts, porters and their kin.

Headlining Dark Beer Week this year is a brand new release and your new favourite stout: Black Milk. A good, honest imperial oat milk stout, sweet and creamy and full-bodied and loaded to the teats with rich, malty character. It’s your first chance to try this addition to our core lineup. Get to know each other before it hits the supermarket aisles.

But that’s not all:
Black Denim will be appearing as a limited-release smoked maple pecan porter
Sova is making its first foray, a Czech dark lager, cold-conditioned at length and perfectly suited to the side-pour taps

And, to top it all off, a bevvy of beloved rebrews: Cereal Milk Stout, Aro Noir, Iron Pig, Dr Pete’s and Snug all on tap, Great Deluge and 12 Anniversary Quad as bottle pours, and even Tiny Stout for a non-alcoholic treat.

A good, honest imperial oat milk stout, sweet and creamy and full-bodied and loaded to the teats with rich, malty character.
Get to know each other before it hits the supermarket aisles.

events.id: Mad Max Marathon-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Mad Max Marathon
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We are bringing the desert back to the big screen with our MAD MAX MARATHON (1979-2024) – all five films, back to back, in one continuous cinematic ride.
From the origins of Max Rockatansky to the intense mayhem of Fury Road and Furiosa, this is your chance to witness one of cinema’s most iconic franchises the way it was meant to be seen – loud, immersive, and on the big screen.
Themed food and drink specials will be available through out the day!
Schedule:
12:30pm - MAD MAX (1979) 88mins
2:15pm - MAD MAX 2: THE ROAD WARRIOR (1981) 96mins
4:00pm - MAD MAX BEYOND THUNDERDOME (1985) 107mins
5:45pm - DINNER BREAK
6:30pm - MAD MAX: FURY ROAD - BLACK & CHROME EDITION (2015) 120mins
8:45pm - FURIOSA: A MAD MAX STORY (2024) 148mins
Pricing for the entire marathon is $55, if you’re planning on only watching one the films, single film tickets are $15 and can be purchased from their individual film entires.
General admission seating

events.id: Community Working Bee | Paekawakawa Reserve-46B Derwent St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Community Working Bee | Paekawakawa Reserve
events.venue: 46B Derwent St, Island Bay, Wellington 6023, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Paekawakawa Reserve is community-owned and driven, and we we love helping hands!
Our spring & summer working bees are about weeding, tree releasing, and chatting with lovely people. No experience needed, cup of tea provided.
Volunteers meet just before 1pm at the Derwent St entrance, or if coming later just follow the voices into the valley.
More than 20 years ago, Island Bay locals fundraised to buy the the Reserve, and it's been run on volunteer love ever since. Come and be a part of it!
Bring:
Water
Good shoes
Gardening gloves
Any questions, feel free to message us on Facebook.

events.id: June Just Sew-2 Forresters Ln, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: June Just Sew
events.venue: 2 Forresters Ln, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for our Just Sew - June edition!
Got a tear, a missing button, or a hem that’s come undone? Don’t toss it - fix it! Our monthly Just Sew sessions are all about giving your clothing and textile items a second life. This is a great way to take climate action in your own life – and learn new skills along the way.
Book below and bring along 1-2 clothing or lightweight household items (cushion covers etc) for repair. We will have sewing machines, needles, thread, and a selection of patches and buttons ready for you. If you have something special you would like to use, feel free to bring it along.
Our skilled volunteers will be here to help with repairs such as holes, tears, hems and missing buttons. Whether you are completely new to sewing or just need a bit of guidance, we will support you through it.

events.id: Miriama Kamo: Less Waste, Greater Place-Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Miriama Kamo: Less Waste, Greater Place
events.venue: Wairarapa Events Centre, Holloway St, Carterton
events.long_description: Come and join Miriama Kamo live for a special one-off afternoon at the Wairarapa Events Centre. Known for her work on Marae and Sunday, Miriama is an award winning reporter and one of Aotearoa’s most trusted storytellers. In this engaging kōrero, she shares her personal journey, the experiences that have shaped her, what matters most to her, and how she’s embraced a low-waste lifestyle in a way that’s practical, realistic, and grounded in everyday life. It’s a chance to hear her speak in person, ask questions, and walk away with ideas.

events.id: What the H***rmones? Pilates & Hormone Health Workshop-14 Johnston St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: What the H***rmones? Pilates & Hormone Health Workshop
events.venue: 14 Johnston St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
When you understand your body, everything changes.
Join Luisa & Eli for a Pilates technique breakdown + hormonal health masterclass, a two-part workshop that will teach you to:
- Move with better technique and less injury risk
- Train in a way that actually fits your hormonal cycle
- Build a more intuitive, sustainable relationship with exercise
- Feel empowered in your body — at every stage of your cycle
*Part 1: Pilates Technique Breakdown with Luisa*
Move beyond simply doing Pilates and learn how to do it well. Luisa will guide you through a detailed breakdown of key Pilates movements. Expect a hands-on, educational experience that sharpens your practice and helps you feel more confident in your body. You'll learn:
- Proper technique and alignment
- Breathwork and pelvic floor activation
- How to build strength safely and effectively
- Progressions that help you get more out of every session
Part 2: Hormonal Health & Training with Eli Hoyos
Eli breaks down how your body's natural rhythms influence your energy, strength and recovery, and what to actually do with that information.
We'll map each hormonal stage to the natural flow of a Pilates class — from breathwork and pelvic floor connection, to warm-up, to higher-intensity phases, and back down to cool-down.
Come away with a framework you can actually use!
WHO THIS IS FOR:
*Anyone who wants to learn more about proper technique & alignment in Pilates movements
*Anyone curious about how Pilates can support hormone health (yours OR the people you know & love!)
*Anyone looking to maximise performance to help you get more out of your Pilates/movement routines.
Haere mai!
About the Facilitators:
*Luisa Gordillo*
Luisa is a Certified Pilates instructor with a passion for holistic wellbeing, specialising in body awareness, injury prevention, and mindful movement. Trained in physiology and anatomy, she creates safe and inclusive classes for all levels. Her classes offer a full-body workout that builds strength, balance, and body awareness. Luisa empowers individuals through movement to improve everyday life.
*Eli Hoyos*
Eli is a Holistic Hormonal Health Coach and the founder of Elivate, a fitness and wellness system built specifically for women. After years of watching women train hard, eat well, and still feel stuck, she made it her mission to give them what the fitness industry never did: a strategy that actually works with their biology. Her approach combines cycle-syncing methodology, movement, and nutrition to help women understand their bodies and finally see lasting results. Learn more at elivatebyeli.com.

Luisa will guide you through a detailed breakdown of key Pilates movements.
You'll learn: - Proper technique and alignment - Breathwork and pelvic floor activation - How to build strength safely and effectively - Progressions that help you get more out of every session Part 2: Hormonal Health & Training with Eli Hoyos Eli breaks down how your body's natural rhythms influence your energy, strength and recovery, and what to actually do with that information.

events.id: Climbers, creepers and perchers - Guided Walk-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Climbers, creepers and perchers - Guided Walk
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Starting at the City Lookout beside the Cable Car terminus, Upland Road, Kelburn, this 60-minute walk will finish at the Founders’ Entrance. Many plants on the route down unobtrusively fill the gaps between the ground and the canopy, and are easily overlooked. We will consider the important role they have in maintaining Wellington Botanic Garden's ecosystems.
Meet at the Cable Car Entrance Lookout, Upland Road, Kelburn.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)
This is part of the regular walks run by the Friends of the Wellington Botanic Garden. They run throughout the year with a different topic every time.
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: French Film Festival - A Dash of Love-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - A Dash of Love
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
This exciting debut feature from Maël Piriou follows a delightful cast of characters on a road trip that will change their lives.

Julia Piaton (also starring in Colours of Time) plays Mélanie, a gifted lawyer suffering from a terminal illness. Deciding it’s time to live life on her own terms, she convinces her old school friend Benjamin (Quentin Dolmaire, also starring in The Money Maker) to accompany her on a road trip to Spain.

Joining them as their burly chauffer is Lucas (Grégory Gadebois, also starring in Jean Valjean), one of Mélanie’s clients who has recently been released from prison. In exchange for her legal services, Mélanie ropes him into taking the wheel.

The unlikely trio embark on an unforgettable journey across stunning vistas that will lead them to understand more about themselves, and each other.

Mélanie is driven by urgency, while Benjamin lingers over every detour, stretching out the miles in an attempt to somehow slow down the clock. What began as an impulsive getaway becomes a poignant exploration of friendship, intimacy, and the true meaning of independence.

A Dash of Love is a tender and deeply human reminder for us to embrace joy while we can. This is a trip you will most certainly want to take!

Herbs can be grown anywhere. They are often full of beauty and scent as well as being useful for home remedies, teas and flavourings. Learn about some of the basic 'simples' and how to grow and use them (including taste and smell tests), then help replant our herb border.
This is a hands-on workshop, learning through doing, so please wear garden-suitable footwear and clothing. Herbal teas will be provided. (There may also be chances to take home some baby plants to start off your own patch.)
This workshop will be facilitated by all around lovely human and gardening expert Hannah Zwartz.

events.id: Musical Adventure: Celebrate 160 Years | Presented by The Friends of Old St Paul's-Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Musical Adventure: Celebrate 160 Years | Presented by The Friends of Old St Paul's
events.venue: Old St Paul's Cathedral, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Enjoy an afternoon of music, performance and celebration within one of Te Whanganui-a-Tara's most treasured historic buildings. Built in 1866, Old St Paul’s has stood at the heart of the city for 160 years as a place of music, memory, ceremony and community.
The Friends of Old St Paul’s warmly invite the public to celebrate the 160th anniversary of Old St Paul’s, alongside 60 years since the founding of the Friends organisation – the long-standing independent support group dedicated to championing and preserving this much-loved heritage place.
Musical Adventure Programme
Please note there will be no intermission and timings are approximate.
From 1.45pm
Visitors are invited to watch Bell Master Steve Dean and his team ringing peals on the bells. Please note that while the bells are raised, entry to the church will be via the south porch (with a step up into the building).
2.15pm - 4.00pm
Master of Ceremonies Dave Armstrong – playwright, screenwriter, trumpet player and columnist for The Post – will guide audiences through an exciting afternoon of performances.
2.20pm – Max Toth
Sub Organist at Wellington Cathedral and former Richard Prothero Organ Scholar, Max Toth presents a rare solo organ recital before departing for a prestigious Organ Scholarship at Exeter Cathedral in the United Kingdom. Audiences can expect playing that ranges from lyrical delicacy to thrilling, cathedral-filling grandeur.
2.45pm – Hutt City Brass Sextet
Led by horn player James MacKay, a sextet from Hutt City Brass will perform a magnificent medley sure to shake the timbers of Old St Paul’s.
3.10pm – Nota Bene Chamber Choir
Acclaimed chamber choir Nota Bene, directed by Maaike Christie-Beekman, will present a beautiful selection of classical and contemporary works. Many Friends members will remember the choir from their performances at the Friends’ 2025 Carol Service.
3.30pm – Kathryn Mosley & Eleanor Carter
Senior Lecturer in Collaborative Piano at Te Herenga Waka Victoria University of Wellington Kathryn Mosley joins NZSO cellist Eleanor Carter for a programme of solo and duet performances for piano and cello.
All welcome! Koha appreciated.
For more information on Nota Bene’s upcoming concert Four Hands, Two Grands, and a Choir, visit their website.

events.id: Vintage cake masterclass-Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Vintage cake masterclass
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Step into the charm of classic pâtisserie and learn the art of timeless cake design in our Vintage Cake Masterclass.

In this guided, hands-on session, each guest will receive a 10cm mini chocolate cake — beautifully prepared and ready for decorating. You’ll begin with a live demonstration by Chef Shuchi Bhardwaj, who will walk you through the techniques and piping styles that define elegant vintage cakes.

Then, with expert guidance, you’ll recreate the design on your own cake, icing and piping step by step to achieve a stunning, nostalgic finish.

All materials are thoughtfully provided, including:

• Freshly baked mini chocolate cake
• Buttercream icing
• Decorations and piping tools
• Cake box to take your creation home

Whether you’re a beginner or a baking enthusiast, this interactive class is designed to be relaxed, creative, and enjoyable.

Gluten-free options are available.
$70 per person, including a hot beverage of your choice.

Come for the experience, leave with new skills — and a cake that looks straight out of a vintage bakery window.

events.id: Winter Textured Pendants or Earrings - Taster Class-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Winter Textured Pendants or Earrings - Taster Class
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
This quick, creative taster class is perfect for a fun outing with friends, a thoughtful gift-making session, or a treat just for you.

In just three hours, you’ll learn to craft a stunning pair of textured metal earrings from scratch. You’ll be guided step-by-step through heating and texturing the metal, then cutting and shaping it with traditional jewellery tools. By the end, you’ll have a beautiful pair of earrings to wear proudly or gift to someone you care about!

You will practice on smaller texture samples to work out what texture and what metal you want to go with, and then move on to creating your earrings in warm copper or yellow brass.

Your class materials pack includes everything you need for the class to make a super cool pair of shiny gorgeous earrings.
Using copper and brass as our main material allows us to have more creative fun and create larger earrings with less expense.

We do have a lovely range of sterling silver sheets that you can purchase as extra if you are committed to working in silver!

We use quality pre-made sterling silver and stainless steel earring hooks and hoops for these taster classes so that you can focus on making the main part of the earrings and know that your findings will be just perfect.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany anyone under the age of 16.

events.id: Heathers The Musical-The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Heathers The Musical
events.venue: The Opera House, 111 - 113 Manners Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Following smash hit runs in London’s West End, the WhatsOnStage BEST NEW MUSICAL winner is now back in NYC.

Welcome to Westerberg High, where popularity is a matter of life and death, and Veronica Sawyer is just another of the nobodies dreaming of a better day. But when she’s unexpectedly taken under the wings of the three beautiful and impossibly cruel Heathers, her dreams of popularity finally start to come true. That is until J.D., the mysterious teen rebel, teaches her that it might kill to be a nobody, but it is murder being a somebody…

With a mercilessly catchy mix of ballads and bangers by award-winning writers Kevin Murphy (Desperate Housewives) and Laurence O’Keefe (Legally Blonde The Musical), Heathers the Musical is directed by acclaimed American screen and stage director Andy Fickman.

Since its sold-out Los Angeles premiere in 2013 and New York debut in 2014, Heathers has gone on to three smash-hit West End seasons, multiple sold-out UK and Ireland tours, and a 2019 WhatsOnStage Award win for Best New Musical. In 2022, it was filmed and streamed globally via The Roku Channel. Most recently, the show smashed records at New York’s New World Stages, opening in June 2025 to the biggest advance sale in the venue’s history and immediately extending due to demand.

Heathers the Musical is produced by Bill Kenwright Ltd, Paul Taylor-Mills in partnership with GMG Productions and Stoddart Entertainment. GMG Productions is renowned for bringing high-calibre international productions to the Asia Pacific, including recent New Zealand smash-hits The Play That Goes Wrong, Madagascar the Musical, Beauty and the Beast, and the upcoming production of The Rocky Horror Show.

So sharpen those pencils, grab your croquet mallet, and get in quick — because big fun starts here.

events.id: The Other Boleyn Girl-Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Other Boleyn Girl
events.venue: Gryphon Theatre, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ambition, desire and betrayal in the court of King Henry VIII.

Sisters Anne and Mary Boleyn are rivals for the King’s affection, each drawn into a dangerous game where love is power and power can be deadly. As courtly intrigue deepens, loyalties are tested and reputations hang by a thread.

Wellington Repertory Theatre is proud to present this compelling production of The Other Boleyn Girl, adapted by Mike Poulton from Philippa Gregory’s best-selling novel.

events.id: French Film Festival - An Evening with Victor Hugo-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - An Evening with Victor Hugo
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Director Pascal Bonitzer (Maigret and the Dead Lover) brings this charming tale from the late Sophie Fillières brilliantly to life.

French acting legend Fabrice Luchini plays popular performer Robert Zucchini, who has built his career on a stage act that lauds the works of the famous writer Victor Hugo.

Robert’s bold reverence of Hugo’s ‘male genius’ doesn’t always play well with more enlightened modern audiences, and he finds himself not only at odds with a feminist theatre troupe, but with his adult daughter Lisbeth (Marie Narbonne, The Count of Monte Cristo), who has recently reappeared in his life.

Robert wrestles with how art meets with politics - but more importantly, with his real-life relationships. Where he once took refuge in texts of the past, Lisbeth demands his presence in the moment.

Little by little, with his daughter’s help, Robert begins to reconnect with his past, open up to others and relearn the practice of his craft.

Pascal Bonitzer has crafted a slick, emotional, funny account of modern problems playing out on and off stage. Centred by a brilliant performance from the always-exceptional Fabrice Luchini, An Evening with Victor Hugo is a must-see at the 2026 Festival.

events.id: Sunset Guided Tour-Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Sunset Guided Tour
events.venue: Stonehenge Aotearoa, 51 Ahiaruhe Rd, Carterton
events.long_description:
Experience Stonehenge Aotearoa at its most breathtaking — as the sun dips below the Wairarapa horizon.
Our Sunset Guided Tour blends astronomy, storytelling, and the natural beauty of twilight for an evening you will never forget.

Led by one of our knowledgeable guides, this 90-minute experience explores how ancient cultures used the Sun to track time and mark the turning of the seasons. You’ll discover the scientific and cultural thinking behind the Henge’s unique Southern Hemisphere design before watching the sunset alignment from inside the stone circle.

Weather permitting, you’ll witness how the setting Sun interacts with the structure — a striking moment that links ancient knowledge with the rhythms of the natural world.

Duration and Schedule
• Allow: Approximately 90 minutes
• Start Time: Begins one hour before sunset
• Availability: Offered any day with advance booking

events.long_description:
RUMBLE

events.id: French Film Festival - Colours of Time-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Colours of Time
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Director Cedric Klapisch has crafted a genuine crowd pleasing story that blends the excitement, wonder and art of Belle Epoque France with present day themes around identity, legacy
and connection.

In 1895, young Adele (Suzanne Lindon) leaves Normandy for Paris, a city alive and buzzing with invention, photography and the rise of Impressionism.

In the present day, her descendants inherit an unexpected legacy : a country side house in Normandy, abandoned for decades and suspended in time. Four of Adele’s descendants arrive to survey the Estate, and together end up piecing together the story of Adele’s unexpected life.
Colours of Time is a charming and fascinating family saga, and is sure to be a hit at this year’s Festival.

events.id: Just A Drink Speed Dating (Age Range Mid-20s - Mid-30s)-Waitoa Victoria St,175 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Just A Drink Speed Dating (Age Range Mid-20s - Mid-30s)
events.venue: Waitoa Victoria St,175 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Just A Drink Speed Dating (Age Range Mid-20s - Mid-30s)
Apps are shit. This is better. Speed dating but the women bring 1 single male friend worth meeting. 6-10 dates. 5 mins each. Drink included.

About this Event

Just A Drink creates a way for single people worth meeting to meet each other – without the apps.

How This Event Works
This event works by asking each single woman to bring a single male friend. We do this for a few reasons:
Moral support is built in. You’ve both got someone to go with.
We want to create a way of meeting someone that doesn’t involve the apps.
If a man has been brought along by a female friend, he’s probably all good.

How Tickets Work
If you're a single woman, buy one female ticket for you and one male ticket for your friend – or send them the link so they can grab their own.
If you're a single man, you’re welcome to share this with a female friend you think would be keen and make a plan together.
To get in, you’ll need to arrive as a pair. We recommend not buying tickets until you and your friend have confirmed your plans.

What We’re Trying to Help With
It’s hard for single people worth meeting to find each other.
Out and about, you can’t always tell who’s single.
On the apps, you can’t always tell who you’d actually want to take the time to meet.
The whole thing ends up more tiring than fun.

The apps have helped – you’ve probably met people through them. But a surprising number of men are undone by a format that asks for a few good photos, some self-awareness, and one complete sentence.

So here’s a different way to try meeting people. Come along – it’s just a drink.


Agenda


🕑: 05:45 PM - 06:00 PM
You Arrive At Waitoa (Victoria Street)

🕑: 06:00 PM - 06:15 PM
We Explain What To Do

🕑: 06:15 PM - 07:25 PM
You Go On Up To 10 Dates

🕑: 07:25 PM - 08:00 PM
You Are Free Range

🕑: 08:00 PM
You Leave
