events.id: Ecstatic Dance | Yoga | Cacao - Wellington 💃-St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki,19 Beach Rd, Paekakariki 5034, New Zealand, Paekakariki-All Events In
events.name: Ecstatic Dance | Yoga | Cacao - Wellington 💃
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki,19 Beach Rd, Paekakariki 5034, New Zealand, Paekakariki
events.long_description:
Ecstatic Dance | Yoga | Cacao - Wellington 💃
💫 Move. Release. Connect. 💫

🌟✨ Ecstatic Dance Wellington ✨🌟 presents...

A special event: Yoga | Cacao | Ecstatic Dance 💃 with international DJ Jayla!

We are super pleased to be vibing in Paekakariki for our next Ecstatic Dance with the beautiful Jayla from Spain, blessing our shores with an Ecstatic Dance Bass. To make it an immersive event, we are adding a 45 minute Yoga class to open up the space, followed by a Cacao meditation, before moving into Ecstatic Dance.

Come to experience the fullness of the event or come just for the Cacao & Ecstatic Dance. Ticketing options below ⬇️

💫 What is Ecstatic Dance?
A freeform, drug- and alcohol-free dance experience where you move however your body wants. The music flows like a wave — starting slow, rising to ecstatic peaks, then softening into stillness. It’s therapeutic, uplifting, and a powerful way to connect with yourself and community.

Please note, that this Ecstatic Dance is not verbally guided, we let the music guide you 😊. Dance freely how your body moves you.

🎶 This Dance Floor
Jayla is a DJ from Barcelona (Spain) creating Ecstatic Dance journeys that invite liberation, emotional release and radical
authenticity through movement.

With international experience performing across Ecstatic Dance communities, conscious raves, festivals, and alternative
gatherings, her work is rooted in the body as a place of truth, freedom, and power.

Rather than simply playing music, she creates emotional landscapes: moments of expansion, catharsis, sensuality, and
release. Her sets move between softness and intensity, grounding and explosion, always with the intention of creating
genuine connection: with oneself, with others, and with the dancefloor as a shared experience.

🎶 The Sound System
Here at Ecstatic Dance Wellington, we believe in using a phat sound system with lots of bass for our dances. We will be bringing an ultra vibey and super bassy experience for you!

🎶 Yoga with Paris
Paris is a yoga teacher & somatic facilitator who creates welcoming, inclusive, grounded spaces. Inspired by over 600 hours of training in hatha yoga, yin, and integrative somatics, her offerings are a gentle space for you to connect with yourself, nourish your nervous system, and honour the wisdom of your body. For more info visit www.luminouswellbeing.org/about

🗓 Event Details
📍 St Peter's Hall, Beach Road, Paekākāriki
🕖 6:00pm – Yoga with Paris
🕢 7:00pm – Cacao Meditation with Kimberley
🕘 7:15pm – Ecstatic Dance with Jayla
🕘 8:45pm – Closing Circle

🎟 Ticket options
$45 Full Evening
$35 Cacao & Ecstatic Dance
https://events.humanitix.com/ecstatic-dance-or-yoga-or-cacao-wellington

ONLINE TICKET SALES ONLY due to numbers needed for Cacao.

**If you would like to come but are unwaged or struggling, please send us a message directly, we'd love to help.

☀️ Extra Info
For those coming to yoga, doors are open from 5.45pm.
If you're doing the cacao and ecstatic dance only, you are welcome from 6.45pm. We will start at 7pm sharp with the Cacao.

We are very much looking forward to co-creating with the conscious dance community in Wellington and sharing our magic.

❤️ What to Bring

Yoga Matt (if you don't have one, there will be some spares)

Water bottle

Loose, comfy clothes

Warm layers for afterwards

A pillow and Blanket if you wish

A friend!

🌟 Guidelines

Drug & alcohol free event

Silence on the dance floor

Without phones/cameras on the dance floor

Respect the space & each other 😊

🎧 Tune into Jayla's ED sets:
https://soundcloud.com/jaylarituals

✨ Come dance with us at Ecstatic Dance Wellington — a night of music, movement & magic. ✨

events.id: GLORY – Kāpiti-Te Raukura ki Kāpiti Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati Beach,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: GLORY – Kāpiti
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kāpiti Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati Beach,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
GLORY – Kāpiti
Orpheus Choir Wellington hits the road with Vivaldi’s Gloria, bringing this landmark choral work to Kāpiti.

One of the most recognisable works in choral music, Vivaldi’s Gloria unfolds in a rapid series of short movements, moving between full choir and solo voices with an energy and brightness that has made it a favourite for generations.

Glory brings together Vivaldi’s Gloria and Mozart Requiem in a full concert programme.

These performances also feature Orpheus Scholars, emerging singers studying classical voice at Te Kōkī, the New Zealand School of Music, as soloists.

With thanks to Pub Charity and Creative NZ's Creative Communities Scheme, administered by Kāpiti Coast District Council, for their support.

events.id: Julia-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Julia
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In 2012, Julia Gillard delivered a speech that ricocheted across Australia, New Zealand, and around the world. Now, the woman and the moment is brought to the stage in Julia. This gripping one-woman show puts us inside a very public storm, and reveals the life and career that led to it.

From idealistic young advocate to leader under relentless scrutiny, Julia takes us behind the microphone of the landmark “misogyny speech.” It steps into the personal, political and socio-cultural forces that forged it. Blending real excerpts with scenes inspired by true events, this is more than one leader’s story. It’s a sharp, human look at how we judge power, how we listen to women, and what we demand of those who lead us.

Against a backdrop of progress and pushback on gender equality, Julia takes on fresh resonance here in Aotearoa. It draws the line between conviction and compromise, private life and public duty. And it asks what it costs to stand your ground and say “not now, not ever” in a world that still questions women who lead.

From idealistic young advocate to leader under relentless scrutiny, Julia takes us behind the microphone of the landmark “misogyny speech.” It steps into the personal, political and socio-cultural forces that forged it.
Blending real excerpts with scenes inspired by true events, this is more than one leader’s story.

events.id: Melothesia-6 Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Melothesia
events.venue: 6 Taranaki St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Queen’s Closet returns to our roots, exploring the unique sound world of Restoration London. Composers there worked in a musical landscape coloured by the long shadow of Puritan rule of England, followed by the explosion of creativity and influx of overseas influences with the Restoration. From the 1660s London was a place of seemingly limitless possibilities, as culture repressed during the interregnum burst into life. We will be performing music by composers such as John Blow, Pelham Humfrey, Matthew Locke, John Lenton and of course Henry Purcell, who were creating music in the English tradition, but with a uniquely Restoration flavour of innovation.
This performance will again be in the Cable Room at Harbourside at 4 Taranaki Street, Wellington. Restoration music was lively, sometimes brash, emotional, sometimes dissonant, and always overflowing with creativity. We feature a wide range of the sounds and colours of Restoration music, including hoboys, trumpets, recorders, strings and voices.

From the 1660s London was a place of seemingly limitless possibilities, as culture repressed during the interregnum burst into life.
We will be performing music by composers such as John Blow, Pelham Humfrey, Matthew Locke, John Lenton and of course Henry Purcell, who were creating music in the English tradition, but with a uniquely Restoration flavour of innovation.
This performance will again be in the Cable Room at Harbourside at 4 Taranaki Street, Wellington.

events.id: Rocking Robins Birthday Hop - 6th june-Lower Hutt-Facebook
events.name: Rocking Robins Birthday Hop - 6th june
events.venue: Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Rocking Robins’ first hop of 2026 is coming up on 6 June and its to celebrate our Birthday!
We’re lucky enough to be hosting this at the Belmont Memorial Hall in Lower Hutt, which has a fabulous dance floor. To make sure there’s plenty of space for everyone to enjoy their dancing, ticket numbers will be limited. If you’d like to reserve a ticket or even a whole table, simply email rockingrobinsdance@gmail.com and we’ll send through payment details. Door sales may also be available, if not sold out prior.
There’ll be some food provided by the committee, and we’d absolutely love it if others could also bring a plate to share. Due to the February weather event, the hall kitchen is currently closed, so any shared kai will need to be ready to serve — unfortunately there are no heating facilities available.
In true Robins style, there’ll be amazing music and entertainment, raffles, and more… come along to find out! You’re also welcome to bring along some drinks if you’d like.
We really appreciate your support — it’s never taken for granted to see people turning up to these events. We try to keep prices as low as possible, with any profit made in June going directly back to supporting our nationals dancers and supporters. So grab some friends and come along for what’s sure to be a great night out.

events.id: Sola Rosa - In The Mids Tour-San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sola Rosa - In The Mids Tour
events.venue: San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Celebrating 25 years of making music as Sola Rosa, Andrew Spraggon, the force behind one of Aotearoa’s most enduring musical projects, returns with his eighth studio album In The Mids, due for release on Friday 10 April. To mark the upcoming release, Sola Rosa returns to the touring circuit with the In The Mids Album Release Tour, taking place throughout New Zealand in May and June.

Presenting both new material and longstanding favourites, the live show has evolved alongside the music itself, incorporating a newly developed visual and lighting design that reshapes how the songs are experienced. It’s a presentation that marks a clear shift for Sola Rosa, offering a live context unlike anything the project has explored previously.

In The Mids is a ten-track body of work featuring seven collaborations with vocalists from Australia and Aotearoa. The album marks a creative shift for Sola Rosa, reflecting a renewed joy in studio-based production and an evolving set of influences.

Sola Rosa
In The Mids Album Release Tour
New Zealand May-Jun 26’
Funded through NZ On Air and supported by the NZ Music Commission

events.id: Cate Le Bon-Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cate Le Bon
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington
events.long_description:
Welsh musician, composer and producer Cate Le Bon brings her surrealist art-pop vision to Wellington, showcasing her acclaimed seventh album Michelangelo Dying.

“She’s my favorite modern songwriter, period,”—Annie Clark, aka St Vincent.

Cate’s songwriting breaks away from genre norms, guided by instinct and a restless sense of curiosity. The result is work so distinctive that artists as varied as Wilco, Dry Cleaning and St Vincent have sought her out to shape their sound.

"It’s hard not to compare her to iconoclasts who seem like aliens, such as David Bowie or Björk."—The New Yorker

events.id: Not In Our Neighbourhood-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Not In Our Neighbourhood
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Told through the lens of a filmmaker, Not in Our Neighbourhood follows a group of women whose lives intersect at a moment of change. As their stories unfold, what sits beneath the surface begins to show – in quiet ways, in unexpected moments, and in the spaces between what is said and left unsaid.

With warmth, tension, and flashes of humour, the play draws you into a world that feels familiar, yet just out of reach.

An intimate and affecting work that invites you to look closer – and consider what might be happening just beyond view.

Written by Jamie McCaskill
Directors Maaka Pohatu & Paul McLaughlin
Featuring Hariata Moriarty

Sat 23 May – Sat 13 June
Tues – Sat 7.30pm, Sunday 4.30pm
Preview Night Friday 22 May
Sunday Special: Sunday 24 May
Choose Your Price Night: Tuesday 26 May

Duration: 80 minutes
Audience Care: This production contains references to domestic violence, sexual violence, and strong language.
CIRCA TWO
$25 – $60

events.id: Professor Brian Cox - Emergence-TSB Arena, Queens Wharf, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Professor Brian Cox - Emergence
events.venue: TSB Arena, Queens Wharf, Wellington
events.long_description:
After performing his smash-hit show ‘Horizons’ to nearly half a million people across the world, Professor Brian Cox is back with his new world tour Emergence.

Brian has appeared in many landmark science programmes for BBC radio and television over the last 15 years, from the Peabody Award-winning Wonders of the Solar System to the world-wide hit series The Planets.

In the winter of 1610 Johannes Kepler was crossing Prague’s Charles Bridge when he noticed a snowflake land on his arm. Why, he asked, are all snowflakes six-cornered? “I do not believe,” he wrote, “that even in a snowflake, this ordered pattern exists at random.”

400 years later, we have part of the answer. Snowflakes are made of water molecules, which are made of atoms, which are made of quarks and electrons – which might be made of superstrings – all held together by forces of nature described by quantum theory. But how does such delicate beauty emerge from such abstract simplicity?

Emergence is a celebration of the intricacy of the Universe and an exploration of the laws of nature that sculpted it. From the largest structures in the known Universe – the rivers and flows of galaxies that trace the cosmic web – to Earth’s interlinked ecosystems and the structure of the human brain – from black holes to snowflakes – we observe a world of dazzling complexity underpinned by magnificent simplicity.

How did a quarter of a million-year-old species of great apes on one small planet amongst trillions orbiting around a middle-aged star in an average galaxy figure all this out, guided by curiosity, mathematics and an aesthetic sense of symmetry and beauty? And what might we become if we can hold onto the ideas of the enlightenment so successfully developed and deployed by Kepler and his contemporaries and successors – ideas that have allowed us to begin to read the story of the Universe and carried our spacecraft to the edge of the solar system and outwards to the stars.

Professor Brian Cox said: “I’ve loved creating Emergence – it’s the most ambitious live show I’ve ever written. I’ve been very lucky to collaborate with a wonderful group of scientists, musicians, filmmakers and graphic artists to bring cosmology, biology, philosophy and history to the largest and most advanced LED screens available, with the best sound and lights I could find. I hope the show is an all-encompassing experience, and I hope it leaves everyone, whether they love science or music or history, or simply contemplating the beauty of Nature, with something new to think about.”

Brian has appeared in many landmark science programmes for BBC radio and television over the last 15 years, from the Peabody Award-winning Wonders of the Solar System to the world-wide hit series The Planets.
In the winter of 1610 Johannes Kepler was crossing Prague’s Charles Bridge when he noticed a snowflake land on his arm.
Professor Brian Cox said: “I’ve loved creating Emergence – it’s the most ambitious live show I’ve ever written.
I’ve been very lucky to collaborate with a wonderful group of scientists, musicians, filmmakers and graphic artists to bring cosmology, biology, philosophy and history to the largest and most advanced LED screens available, with the best sound and lights I could find.
I hope the show is an all-encompassing experience, and I hope it leaves everyone, whether they love science or music or history, or simply contemplating the beauty of Nature, with something new to think about.”

events.id: Intro to Improv with PopRox-Aro Valley Community Centre, 48 Aro St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Intro to Improv with PopRox
events.venue: Aro Valley Community Centre, 48 Aro St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Build confidence, embrace failure and unleash your inner silly billy with this 6 week introduction to the art of Improvisation, with Wellington's premier improv professionals.

Over 6 weeks you'll cover the core principles of improv. You will learn to:
- Happily accept offers to make each other look and feel good.
- Find the joy in failing together, so we can be bolder and surprise one another.
- Re-connect with your sense of "play" in a safe and welcoming space.
- Learn positive communications skills which foster collaboration.

This is a 6 week course for folks new to improv, or returning to it after a longer break. Classes are every Tuesday 7:15pm-9:15pm at the Aro Valley Community Centre, from May 12 to June 16. Registration covers the entire course.

ABOUT THE TEACHER
Austin Harrison is one of Aotearoa's most experienced improvisers and teachers with over 15 years experience. He holds a Master of Fine Arts in Theatre from Victoria University of Wellington and is the current Artistic Director of the New Zealand Improv Festival.

Austin believes improv is a powerful connector which builds communities, boosts confidence and reminds us all how to have fun. He's taught improv in a variety of environments, from intermediate schools, right through to over-65s classes. He's an award-winning performer and producer, a founding member of PopRox Improv and has taken shows to the Edinburgh Fringe and Melbourne International Comedy Festival.

ABOUT POPROX
PopRox are Wellington's best-dressed improv company. Known for their slick production values, and bold story-telling they were the resident improv company at Circa Theatre performing monthly throughout 2024 and 2025. Their Production of improv Murder Mystery "Farce Onion" won a Director's Choice award at the NZ Comedy Festival in 2024 and they were shortlisted for the "Best Improv Company Award" at the NZ Comedy Guild Awards 2025.

THE STAND-BY LIST
PopRox firmly believes that improv is for everyone, especially when it comes to sharing skills and knowledge. So we're offering "the stand-by list" for all our workshops in 2026. We'll giveaway any spots remaining one week before this course, on a first-come-first-serve basis. No need to provide a reason, no questions asked. If the entry fee is a barrier for you to come along, then simply email poproximprov@gmail.com and we'll pop you on the SBL. Everyone on the list will be notified whether there was a place available for them at least 5 days before the course begins.

ACCESSIBILITY
Aro Valley Community Centre is wheelchair accessible.
There is cycle parking right outside, and the nearest bus stop is 200 metres away on Aro Street (STOP 6784), or 300 metres to Willis Street (stop 7711) via Palmer Street.
These classes are suitable for blind/low-vision learners and for those living with diasability, who have good verbal communication skills (this is key to the type of work we'll be doing).

QUESTIONS? GET IN TOUCH
If you have questions about the course, please don't hesitate to get in touch with us by emailing poproximprov@gmail.com

events.venue: 92 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011
events.long_description:
Cate Le Bon - Lōemis Festival, Wellington
“She’s my favorite modern songwriter, period.”
- Annie Clark, aka St Vincent

Wowee zowee are we psyched to announce Cate Le Bon at Meow Nui on Saturday 6th June as the kick-off show for Lōemis Festival!

Cate Le Bon is a sonic innovator. Her genre-defying songwriting takes the listener along a breathtaking ride so unique that artists as varied as Wilco, Dry Cleaning, Devendra Banhart and St Vincent have called on her producing skills for the je ne sais quoi that only Cate can deliver.

Her seventh album, Michelangelo Dying, has been described as "opaquely alluring and genuinely moving," by Rolling Stone; The New Yorker says, "It’s hard not to compare her to iconoclasts who seem like aliens, such as David Bowie or Björk." Batting the kudos home, it's not a shock that a multitude of international heavyweight publications included the album in their "Best of 2025".

Listen to Michelangelo Dying and get a ticket to the most stunning show you'll see all year!

Cate Le Bon is a sonic innovator.
Her genre-defying songwriting takes the listener along a breathtaking ride so unique that artists as varied as Wilco, Dry Cleaning, Devendra Banhart and St Vincent have called on her producing skills for the je ne sais quoi that only Cate can deliver.
Her seventh album, Michelangelo Dying, has been described as "opaquely alluring and genuinely moving," by Rolling Stone; The New Yorker says, "It’s hard not to compare her to iconoclasts who seem like aliens, such as David Bowie or Björk."
Batting the kudos home, it's not a shock that a multitude of international heavyweight publications included the album in their "Best of 2025".

events.id: The Commas at DVB Miramar-128A Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Commas at DVB Miramar
events.venue: 128A Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us June 6th at 8 pm for a FREE show from The Commas - no tickets needed!
Welsh vocals that get mistaken for Cartman on a night out, backed by a horn section that actually swings (trombone and sax doing things they shouldn’t), rock-solid bass, keys that sparkle, and Kevin slinging Van Halen-level face melting riffs on his whammy bar laden guitar like it’s nothing.
The Commas are a high-dexterity cover band with zero filler. Expect dancing, loud singalongs, the occasional TV theme, and hits spanning centuries - including the occasional Ska song. From funk to rock to whatever makes the room move, they cherry-pick the bangers and play them with teeth.
No cheese, just proper grooves and “how the hell did they pull that off?” energy.
Come prepared to expect the unexpected.

A Grass Roots NZ Tour

events.id: Tahini Bikini at Moon w/ Nameless and Brainless-Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Tahini Bikini at Moon w/ Nameless and Brainless
events.venue: Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pōneke based funk/soul band Tahini Bikini are performing live at Moon on Saturday, 6th June! Supported by Wellington's freshest soul/hip hop band Nameless & Brainless. Bring your dancing shoes!

Tickets on sale Wednesday, 27th May via UTR - $15 for students/unwaged, $20 GA.

TAHINI BIKINI:

Pōneke-based Tahini Bikini are known for their distinctive sound, a combination of extremely danceable funk, soul and pop influences elevated by frontwoman Mads Taylor’s frank, feisty lyrics and smoky voice.

The seven-piece group released their debut album Fever Dream in 2022, and have since become a fixture at festivals across Aotearoa, performing at Rhythm & Vines, Great Sounds Great, Welcome to Nowhere, Cuba Dupa, Outfield, Twisted Frequency and more. As well as Mads on lead vocals, the band features a talented collective of musicians: Mathew Campbell (Backing Vocals, percussion) Darcy Monteath (Bass), Eric Sebastian (drums), Alex Scott-Billing (Guitar) Cameron Robertson (Trumpet) and Aidan Mcculloch (Saxophone/keys).

In 2025 Tahini Bikini released a run of confident, polished singles, with lines like "tell me does it taste good when you lick those boots?" from 'Mother' emphasising a turn towards a more pointed, critical tone in the Mads’ songwriting.


NAMELESS & BRAINLESS:

Nameless and Brainless are a 6 piece band from Wellington.

What started as some jams for frontman Riva Cowans Masters project between a couple of the bros has evolved into a soulful, hard hitting band.

While this project is fresh, each of the musicians either have degrees in music or have vast experience in the industry.
High energy Kiwi raps in English and Te Reo accompanied by a powerful band and driven by a locked in rhythm section, come party with Nameless and Brainless.

events.id: Breaking Beats X Frederick Crew-38 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Breaking Beats X Frederick Crew
events.venue: 38 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Breaking Beats X Frederick Crew
To celebrate Legoman and his family making the move over to Perth, the gang is getting back together for one proper send-off at Wellington’s newest bass music home — Afters.
This one’s for the heads.
----------------------------
THE HISTORY
Both crews came from humble beginnings.
The Frederick Crew built their name through legendary house parties before stepping up to throw iconic nights at Bodega (RIP).
At the same time, the Breaking Beats lads had just moved up from Wanaka, linking in with the FC crew and causing chaos at Fast Eddies (RIP) back in 2013.
What started as local madness quickly grew into something much bigger.
Breaking Beats would go on to become one of New Zealand’s leading electronic music promoters, while the Frederick Crew remained a constant part of the journey, behind the decks, in the crowd, and deep in the culture from day one.
----------------------------
THE SEND-OFF
Now the crews reunite for a special night celebrating one of the originals.
A proper local Drum & Bass throwdown featuring friends, family, residents, and long-time crew all under one roof.
Expect heavyweight selections, nostalgic energy, and pure Wellington rave culture all night long.
LINEUP
Frederick Crew
Breaking Beats Crew
Zillah
Barbwyre
Hosted by Timmy P MC

Tickets here.
https://www.ticketfairy.com/.../breakingbeatsvsfrederickc...

events.id: Colourful Enamel Jewellery Day-65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Colourful Enamel Jewellery Day
events.venue: 65 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Learn to create beautiful enamelled jewellery with Lisa Sinclair.
Join us for a full-day enamelling workshop where you’ll explore colour, texture, and fire to create vibrant enamelled jewellery in copper or silver.
This hands-on class focuses on the fundamentals of enamelling, showing you how to apply, fire, and layer enamel to achieve rich, expressive surfaces. You’ll make a simple yet beautifully textured pendant or pair of earrings, then learn how to add a pop (or flood!) of colour to bring your piece to life.
Beginners to metalwork are very welcome - pre-formed shapes are available so you can focus on experimenting with colour, pattern, and mark-making. If you have some metalworking experience, you’re encouraged to take things further by creating and texturing your own metal forms before enamelling.
This is a playful, creative workshop and an ideal class for anyone who loves working with colour, surface, and experimentation - whether you’re new to enamels or keen to deepen your skills.
What you’ll learn:
The basics of enamelling on copper and silver
How to prepare metal for enamelling
Applying enamel using different techniques
Firing enamel and building up layers of colour
Creating texture and pattern in metal
Tips for achieving clean, vibrant enamel finishes
The class fee covers enamel materials but NOT metals. Students can choose from a range of pre-cut copper blanks or use their own copper sheet. Findings and chains are available to purchase at a range of good prices to suit all budgets.
Kids aged approximately 12+ are welcome. Any participant under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.
For health and safety reasons, students must wear sturdy closed-toe shoes.
Workshop fee $248, early bird $225 till 10 May.

events.id: Jewellery Making - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery Making - Creative Arts Holiday Programme for Teens
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Craft your own rings or pendant in this introduction to metal-smith jewellery making! In this quick paced one session workshop you’ll learn some metalworking fundamentals to turn raw silver, copper and or brass into shining wearable rings or pendant.

Chain for pendant is not included but can be purchased for $30.

Techniques:
- Use sizers and jeweller’s callipers to measure ring size
- Use files and sanding to refine and smooth metal edges and surfaces
- Use hammers to form metal
- Use jeweller’s flame torches to solder join metal
- Use Dremel & bench polishers to refine & finish metal casts into wearable jewellery

Minimum age is 13 years

events.id: KOHA SUNDAYS at AWHI - Heated Flow-14 Johnston St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: KOHA SUNDAYS at AWHI - Heated Flow
events.venue: 14 Johnston St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us EVERY Sunday for a 90 minute Heated Flow for koha entry (ie, give what you can)! Proceeds are donated to a different charitable organisation each month that's committed to supporting the wellbeing of Aotearoa.
This weekly class is open to everyone, regardless of yoga experience - haere mai! BYO mat or borrow one from us. Towel hire $3 if needed, and make sure to bring a water bottle!
To book: Download the AWHI app to view the timetable, or simply show up in good time on Sunday!
WHAT IS 'KOHA'?
Koha loosely translates to 'gift' or exchange. In other words, pay what you can for the class!
For more information about our studio, visit our website at awhiyoga.co.nz

events.id: Otaki Repair Café-Otaki-Facebook
events.name: Otaki Repair Café
events.venue: Otaki
events.long_description:
Got something broken at home? Give your goodies a second life at the Ōtaki Repair Café hosted by Energise Ōtaki.
With help from the awesome local volunteers, they’ll stitch, glue, sharpen, and repair all sorts of treasures while sharing skills and a good yarn along the way. From wobbly to working again, bring it in and see what can be saved Register your item first (especially bikes and appliances): otaki.repaircafe@gmail.com
Nothing to repair, come anyway and enjoy a cuppa & cake and watch what's happening in the hall or give a hand if you have some skills you want to apply to broken items.

events.name: WELLINGTON | Meditation, Breath & Sound Healing Workshops - Foundations & Advanced
events.venue: 110 Wakefield St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sessions:
ReConnected Foundations Workshop (10:00am - 1:45pm): Meditation, breathwork, and sound healing.
Awaken Your Chakras Advanced Workshop (2:30pm - 6:00pm): Chakra clearing, group energy work & deeper healing.
.
.
𝗛𝗮𝘃𝗲 𝗹𝗶𝗳𝗲'𝘀 𝘂𝗻𝗲𝘅𝗽𝗲𝗰𝘁𝗲𝗱 𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗻𝘀 𝘁𝗮𝗸𝗲𝗻 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗼𝗳𝗳 𝘆𝗼𝘂𝗿 𝗶𝗻𝘁𝗲𝗻𝗱𝗲𝗱 𝗽𝗮𝘁𝗵?
𝗔𝗿𝗲 𝘆𝗼𝘂 𝗳𝗲𝗲𝗹𝗶𝗻𝗴 𝗹𝗼𝘀𝘁 𝗮𝗻𝗱 𝘂𝗻𝘀𝘂𝗿𝗲 𝗼𝗳 𝘄𝗵𝗼 𝘁𝗼 𝘁𝘂𝗿𝗻 𝘁𝗼 𝗳𝗼𝗿 𝗵𝗲𝗹𝗽?
Life throws us surprises - big or small. You're not alone. We've been there too.
That's why we created this one-day experience - a chance for us all to reconnect with the paths that matter most to us!
Get ready for an inspiring experience with like-minded friends as we lead you toward discovering new clarity about yourself and your direction moving forward.
Don't wait any longer; start taking control now by saying yes to this extraordinary opportunity!
We’re bringing a full-day immersion of profound inner work to WELLINGTON.
On Sunday, June 7th, at The West Plaza - Meeting Room 1, 110 Wakefield Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6142, New Zealand, we invite you to experience 2 powerful sessions designed to awaken, clear, and elevate your energy. Choose one or embrace both for a deeper, more transformative journey.
The morning begins with Foundations (10:00am-1:45pm), a session designed to clear mental fog, restore balance, and ignite your inner spark through breathwork, meditation, and sound healing.
As the day progresses, the Advanced session (2:30-6:00pm) will take you even deeper, offering chakra clearing, advanced sound healing, and group energy work, guiding you toward profound energetic shifts.
If you’ve attended a past retreat or workshop, or if you choose to experience both sessions, this journey is yours to take.
All our previous events have sold out, so be sure to secure your spot early.
Love and gratitude,
Matt & Guy

events.id: Welly On Wheels - City to Karori return-12 Queens Whrf, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Welly On Wheels - City to Karori return
events.venue: 12 Queens Whrf, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Try your local cycle route!
Build confidence riding in the city on a guided bike tour designed to help you get comfortable with local routes and street changes. You will practise key cycling skills, learn how to use new cycling infrastructure, and discover interesting features along the way.
These relaxed, supportive rides are organised by Wellington City Council and led by the experienced instructors from Greater Wellington Regional Council's Pedal Ready.
Event details

events.id: Hope is a Shovel-50 Holloway Street, Carterton, 5713-Facebook
events.name: Hope is a Shovel
events.venue: 50 Holloway Street, Carterton, 5713
events.long_description:
HOPE IS A SHOVEL
Climate action strategies for the overwhelmed
with Nadine Hura,
Cameron McCausland-Taylor
& Ricky Utting
In the midst of converging crises, it can be easy to lose hope. But there are things we can do. Join author of Slowing the Sun, Nadine Hura (Ngāti Hine, Ngāpuhi, Pākehā), and Carterton District Council Climate Change Coordinator, Ricky Utting, for a special 2-part workshop examining the whakapapa of climate change and the responses we can activate personally and collectively.
Part 1: 1-2.30pm
Part 2: 3-4.30pm
Come to one or both sessions.
Part 1 will focus on the intersections of Te Tiriti justice and anti-colonialism, with Q&A and readings from Nadine's book.
Part 2 will be practical, interactive and creative, with zine-making inspiration featuring blogger and whale advocate Cameron McCausland-Taylor (Ngāti Awa, Ngāpuhi, Te Arawa, Waitaha).
Bring your questions, enthusiasm, even despair - no-one leaves depressed!
A free event for all ages brought to you by Wairarapa Word and Carterton Library, with support from Carterton District Council, Bridget Williams Books and Almo’s Books.
Copies of 'Slowing the Sun' will be available for purchase and signing through Almo’s Books. The book is also available through your library, in print, as a free e-book.
To receive updates about Wairarapa Word's monthly programme ~ since 2012 ~ send an email with "sign me up for the e-news' to <events.waiword@gmail.com>.

events.id: The Not-So-Straight Artists' Walk Tour-The Cenotaph, Corner of Bowen Street and Lambton Quay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Not-So-Straight Artists' Walk Tour
events.venue: The Cenotaph, Corner of Bowen Street and Lambton Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore some of the locations and stories associated with artists, writers and musicians who are in some way related to Rainbow LGBTI+ communities. We’ll feature writers Katherine Mansfield and Frank Sargeson, artists Dorothy Richmond and Francis Hodgkins, musicians Douglas Lilburn and the Glamaphones choir, international visitors Rupert Brooke and Somerset Maugham, and visit what is widely considered to be the most homoerotic sculpture in Wellington.

Accessibility information: We walk on public footpaths. This tour includes walking from Lambton Quay up to the top of the Bolton Street cemetery and down again (moderate/steep incline).

Cost: FREE

Starts: by the Cenotaph, corner Bowen Street and Lambton Quay

PLEASE REGISTER AT https://walktours.nz/book/
(if there are no registrations the tour won't go ahead)

Ends: The Terrace (by St Andrew's)
Duration: 90-minutes

events.id: Gong Bath at Vogelmorn Hall-Vogelmorn Hall,13 Vennell St, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Gong Bath at Vogelmorn Hall
events.venue: Vogelmorn Hall,13 Vennell St, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Gong Bath at Vogelmorn Hall
$20 -tickets via link: https://events.humanitix.com/gong-bath-at-vogelmorn-hall-rqdcwp3k/tickets
*Limited door sales for $25*

*Please bring a yoga mat, pillow, eye mask and blanket - or whatever it is you need to feel most warm and comfortable *

Discover the transformative effects of sound therapy with this 1-hour gong bath experience.

During this session, participants will be surrounded by the resonant vibrations of gongs, carefully designed to induce deep states of relaxation and calm. As you settle into the experience, allow the soothing tones to wash over you, releasing tension and promoting a sense of inner peace.

Scientific studies have highlighted the benefits of sound therapy, particularly gong baths, in reducing stress and promoting overall well-being. The rhythmic patterns and overtones of the gongs can help entrain brainwaves, leading to a meditative state and improved mental clarity.

Beyond the immediate effects, many participants report experiencing lasting benefits such as improved sleep, reduced anxiety, and enhanced mood following a gong bath session. By immersing yourself in the healing vibrations of the gongs, you can tap into a deeper sense of relaxation and rejuvenation.

Whether you're looking to unwind after a long day or explore new avenues of self-care, this 1-hour gong bath offers a unique opportunity to reconnect with yourself and find balance amidst the hustle and bustle of daily life. There will be cups of tea available afterwards to help us integrate and ground.

**THIS EVENT IS RESTRICTED TO 40 PEOPLE ONLY**

Disclaimer
Gong baths are safe and supportive for most people. However, please consult your doctor or mental health professional before attending if you:

-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)

If you have recent injuries, surgeries, or other health concerns, let me know so we can make sure you’re comfortable.

Everyone responds differently to sound. If at any point during the session you feel overwhelmed or uncomfortable, you are welcome to sit up, move gently, or step outside. Your wellbeing always comes first.

🔔About Me🔔
Erika is a musician and astrologer based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. She has 35+ years musical experience. Erika has an interest in the healing properties of sound for mental and physical wellbeing, which she currently explores through her gong bathing practice

events.id: Regenerating Te Whanganui-a-Tara Wellington — a panel talk presented by Kowtow-2-18 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Regenerating Te Whanganui-a-Tara Wellington — a panel talk presented by Kowtow
events.venue: 2-18 Mercer St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
From te taiao to circular fashion, from community to culture – across Wellington, a different kind of future is already under construction.
This panel brings together makers, growers, and thinkers shaping the city in real time. Together they look ahead: what does Wellington become in 20 years — and what do we need to start doing differently now?
Hosted by Sunaina Born, the panel features Emma Wallace (Kowtow) on designing for circularity and 20 years of purpose-led business; Dr Huhana Smith (Te Whiri Whītau, Massey University) on regeneration through mātauranga and natural fibres; Dr Danielle Shanahan (Zealandia) on a 500-year vision for living with nature; and Tom Cappleman (Coffee Outdoors) on growing community through everyday connection.

events.id: French Film Festival - The Money Maker-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - The Money Maker
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Based on an incredible true story, The Money Maker follows Jan Bojarski (Reda Kateb, The Extraordinary), a young Polish engineer taking refuge in France during World War II. Desperate to survive, he turns his talents to forging identity documents during the German occupation.

Once the war ends, Jan still finds himself struggling to make ends meet. Stuck in underpaid jobs and denied citizenship, he is unable to patent his many inventions. Jan’s engineering expertise seems to be wasted, until a gangster recognises his skills and presents a risky opportunity - to produce counterfeit banknotes...

So begins a thrilling and nail-biting series of events, as Jan begins to lead a double life. For over 15 years he runs a counterfeiting operation from his garden shed - unbeknownst to his family.

Jan’s fakes are so precise he earns the moniker “the Cézanne of counterfeit money.” - but this also draws attention. Jan soon finds himself in the crosshairs of Inspector Mattéi (Bastien Bouillon, also starring in Leave One Day), one of France’s most determined detectives.

Director Jean-Paul Salomé (The Godmother, The Sitting Duck) brings this fascinating and gripping tale to life, with an unforgettable performance from Reda Kateb as the man who shook the Bank of France. The film was a certified hit in France, with over 1.1 million admissions Make time for The Money Maker at this year’s Festival!
events.description:
Based on an incredible true story, The Money Maker follows Jan Bojarski (Reda Kateb, The Extraordinary), a young Polish engineer taking refuge in France during World War II.
Desperate to survive, he turns his talents to forging identity documents during the German occupation.
Once the war ends, Jan still finds himself struggling to make ends meet.
Stuck in underpaid jobs and denied citizenship, he is unable to patent his many inventions.
The film was a certified hit in France, with over 1.1 million admissions Make time for The Money Maker at this year’s Festival!

events.id: Aesthetic Science and Mysticism Workshop-72 Main St, Otaki 5512, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Aesthetic Science and Mysticism Workshop
events.venue: 72 Main St, Otaki 5512, New Zealand
events.long_description:
This workshop explores the relationship between beauty, aesthetics, and spirituality through the lens of yoga philosophy. Together we will reflect on questions such as whether the pursuit of beauty supports or hinders spiritual growth, and how art, music, joy, and sensory experience can become pathways to deeper awareness and mysticism. Through discussion, reflection, and immersive musical experiences, participants will discover how cultivating subtle beauty in everyday life can uplift ourselves and others while supporting spiritual progress.
Cost: Koha to cover room hire.
All welcome.

events.id: Clay and Sip - Emerald Green Wall Art Workshop-Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Clay and Sip - Emerald Green Wall Art Workshop
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
A fabulous and fun workshop that combines creativity whilst sipping a beverage of your choice for a relaxing and unique experience.
Create a stunning piece of wall art using my hand drawn templates and decorate with impressed flower, heart and leaf sprigs, fabrics and more. Around 4 weeks after the class these will be fired and glazed using a brilliant deep emerald green glaze, creating a gorgeous art piece to hang in your home or gift to another.

Alongside that sip a delicious drink from the Southern Cross Bar where we will be working.

Working with clay is good for the soul! Immersing in a gentle creative activity helps to switch off a busy mind, and making these is fun, relaxing and achievable even if this is your first time using clay.

For this class you are invited to make either one large wall piece or two smaller wall pieces, with the option of creating extra pieces for an additional fee.
All materials are provided so all you need to do is turn up ready to have some fun and creativity.
Booking is essential so please book through here (fees apply), use this link https://forms.gle/yeasWbKDvuWQcg8r5 or contact clpates@hotmail.com
Price includes all materials, glaze, double kiln firing and $10 beverage from The Southern Cross Bar.

events.id: James Jin (Violin) and Xing Wang (Piano)-Waikanae Memorial Hall, 7 Pehi Kupa St, Waikanae-Event Finder
events.name: James Jin (Violin) and Xing Wang (Piano)
events.venue: Waikanae Memorial Hall, 7 Pehi Kupa St, Waikanae
events.long_description:
The Waikanae Music Society is looking forward to welcoming back the talented Auckland duo James Jin and Xing Wang whose 2025 concert was so popular. With a similar programme format to last year, they will play violin sonatas by Beethoven and Mozart, followed by some charming arrangements of Chinese folk-songs. The most substantial work on the programme will be Grieg’s third Violin Sonata, the best known and most dramatic of the Norwegian composer’s three violin sonatas.

James and Xing are both in high demand as teachers and performers. James is a teaching fellow at the University of Auckland as well as holding leading roles in orchestras throughout the country, both as performer and conductor. Xing, a graduate of the San Francisco Conservatory of Music, is a sought-after collaborative pianist and chamber musician. As a soloist she has appeared with many New Zealand orchestras, and has given numerous solo recitals in New Zealand and overseas.
The concert, in the Waikanae Memorial Hall, will be at 2.30pm on Sunday 7 June. Door sales will be available, or for more information visit www.waikanaemusic.org.nz

Programme:
Beethoven: Sonata for Violin and Piano in D, Opus 12, No 1
Mozart: Sonata for violin and piano in E minor, K304
Guoquan Li: Fisherman's Song
Yuan Mao: Joy of Spring
Grieg: Violin Sonata No 3 in C minor, K304.

events.id: RSC Sunday Session: Caydn Macintyre-The Raumati Social Club,34 Poplar Ave, Raumati South, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: RSC Sunday Session: Caydn Macintyre
events.venue: The Raumati Social Club,34 Poplar Ave, Raumati South, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
RSC Sunday Session: Caydn Macintyre
Cayden Macintyre is a self-taught pianist whose journey began with a chance moment walking past a café, spotting a piano, and feeling something shift. What started as three songs quickly grew into open mic nights and full sessions, shaped by late nights, instinct, and YouTube practice. Inspired by jazz, life, and after-hours reflection, his original compositions aim to live in the moment and play what’s real.

Growing up in a world where emotion stayed beneath the surface, Caydn now pours everything into the keys each note carrying honesty, tension, and release. This debut performance is more than a show; it’s the first full expression of that journey, shared live for the first time.

events.id: Solo travellers: casual hangout in Wellington (4pm)-57 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Solo travellers: casual hangout in Wellington (4pm)
events.venue: 57 Jervois Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
No pressure, no plan—just new friends

Whether you’re visiting Wellington for the first time, recently relocated, or simply love connecting with curious people, this weekly meetup is the perfect way to share stories, make friends, and go explore, as a group, the magic that Wellington has to offer

What to expect?
• Casual & hostless: no formal host, no schedule: just show up, be yourself, meet fellow solo travellers & expats and decide as a group where you feel like going
• Central meeting point: it is surrounded by cultural landmarks, cafes, and restaurants, making it a convenient and safe starting point.
• Open to everyone: whether you are a local, an expat or just passing through for a few days, you are welcome to join. (English is the primary language, but feel free to switch of course)

How to talk with strangers?
(and create meaningful connections)
To help you get started, I curated a little game of ridiculously good questions you can ask to break the ice, shift the conversation from dull to intriguing and fun, and eventually create meaningful connection quickly. I hope this helps!
www.pierrebonvalot.com/good-questions

The only things you need to do are…
• Leave a message in the comment section to let the others know that you will actually be there (I don’t want you to end up alone there

• If you can’t make it anymore, no problem! But please update your RSVP to let the others know
• Please be gentle and patient with each other. We all want to make good friends here.

Why join?
• Meet open-minded travellers and locals who are keen to connect, learn and have fun together.
• Experience Wellington... as a group.
• Be part of a global community that celebrates solo travelling and meaningful connections between foreign and locals.

How does it works?
1. Arrive at the meeting spot at the scheduled time.
2. Look for other joiners—friendly faces and curious explorers.
3. Introduce yourself, connect with others, and decide as a group where to go.

When & where?
• Day: every Sunday
• Time: 4:00 PM (meetup sometimes displays a different time zone: but it’s always 4pm local time)
• Location: in front of the Albatross Fountain
Hostless: I am setting up the event for you all to meet, but I won't be there myself this time

Hi my name is Pierre
I created this Meetup group because I want to see more human connection happen in the real world. I feel many of us want this, but don't exactly know how to go about it. I hope this helps a little bit.
I also work as a coach: I help people who feel stuck in their life to reconnect with themselves, create real change and ultimately live more fulfilling lives.

events.id: French Film Festival - The Stranger-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - The Stranger
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
- OFFICIAL SELECTION: Venice International Film Festival -
- CÉSAR AWARD WINNER: Best Supporting Actor – Pierre Lottin -

The superb new drama from multi award-winning writer/director François Ozon (Swimming Pool, 8 Women, Under the Sand), The Stranger is a gripping adaptation of Albert Camus’ landmark novella about a detached young expatriate on trial for murder in French-colonised Algeria.

Summer, 1938. Meursault (Benjamin Voisin, Lost Illusions), a quiet and unassuming clerk in his early thirties, attends his mother’s funeral. The next day he begins a casual affair with Marie (Rebecca Marder), a colleague randomly encountered at the local baths, and quickly slips back into routine. However, daily life is soon disrupted by his volatile neighbour (an excellent Pierre Lottin, who won a César award for his performance), who draws Meursault into a dispute involving an ex-lover. And then, one blisteringly hot afternoon, an inexplicable event occurs on a beach, one that will see Meursault’s very moral standing brought to question…

Visually resplendent with sensuous black-and-white imagery, Ozon’s elegant and masterfully realized film shines a contemporary lens on Camus’ classic tale of dissociation
and morality, capturing the beauty and heat of a charged society on the boil. Both impactful and mysterious, The Stranger is one of the major highlights of this year’s Festival
and should not be missed.

events.long_description:
Have you always wanted to try stand-up comedy but don’t know how to start?
Comedian and writer Brendon Green brings you a fun low-pressure comedy workshop.
Sign up here: https://www.nzcomedyschool.com/enroll
Suggested koha: $20
Everything you need to know to get a mic in your face:
-generating ideas
-joke writing
-effective delivery
-where and how to get practice
-how to avoid many common mistakes new performers make.
Brendon Green is a professional comedian and writer with over 15 years of experience. He has written and performed 10 NZ Comedy Festival shows, receiving the Best Newcomer Award in 2012, a Billy T Nomination in 2014, and a FRED Award Nomination for Best Show in 2023.

events.id: Out in the Pool-15 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Out in the Pool
events.venue: 15 Frankmoore Ave, Johnsonville, Wellington 6037, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Our Out in the Pool sessions are regular monthly sessions for all queer, transgender, gender diverse and non-binary folks
We invite our rainbow communities and allies to dive in and swim in a safe and welcoming environment at Keith Spry Pool. These sessions are held on the first Sunday of every month and run 5pm-7pm, starting slightly later in February with our first event on Sunday 8 February 2026.
Out in the Pool evenings at Keith Spry have:
Gender neutral changing rooms and bathrooms, as well as our Men's and Women's changing facilities.
Recreation lanes with inflatables, as well as the Dive board open (as staffing allows).
Lane space will be available to swim some lengths or aqua jog, and the teaching pool will be open as staffing allows.
Spa and Sauna available
Music, swimming and good vibes only.
There is no additional cost for these sessions, simply turn up and pay our regular pool entry fees. There is no need to register, just come on down!

events.long_description:
The Rogue's regular Sunday afternoon jazz. Funky, jazzy, soulful jazz. Beer, hangs, and jazz, on a Sunday, perfect!

This week featuring local guitarist Tyson Smith and his quartet

Tyson Smith likes hats (and is rarely seen without one). He frequently switches between his singer-songwriter hat, rock n' roll hat and DJ hat among various others, but on June 7th he's dusting off his Jazz hat for Sunday arvo Jazz at the Rogue! He has also enlisted some of Welly's finest to join him, though they're responsible for their own headpieces.

With Blair Latham on Sax, Hikurangi Shaverien-Kaa on drums and Elijah Mulheron on bass. Expect a range of exciting twangy, jazzy sounds!

Music from 5pm FREE ENTRY - koha for the band encouraged!

events.id: The Bible Course-St John's in the City Presbyterian Church, 170 Willis Street., Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Bible Course
events.venue: St John's in the City Presbyterian Church, 170 Willis Street., Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Bible can feel big, complex, or hard to navigate, but it doesn’t have to. This winter, St John’s in the City is hosting The Bible Course, an eight‑week journey that helps you see how the whole story of Scripture fits together and why it matters for life today.

This is the first time The Bible Course has been run in New Zealand, and we’re delighted to partner with Bible Society NZ as their pilot group ahead of a national launch during Bible Month in July. Over 140,000 people in the UK have already taken part, finding it accessible, engaging, and confidence‑building.

Whether you’ve read the Bible for years or are just beginning to explore Christian faith, you’re warmly invited.

Each Sunday evening includes:
- A shared meal to begin the night together
- Childcare for preschool and primary-aged children
- A 20‑minute video teaching session
- Time in small groups to talk, ask questions, and explore the themes
- A physical guidebook to use throughout the course (optional purchase: $10)

Spaces are limited, registration is essential
events.description:
The Bible can feel big, complex, or hard to navigate, but it doesn’t have to.
This winter, St John’s in the City is hosting The Bible Course, an eight‑week journey that helps you see how the whole story of Scripture fits together and why it matters for life today.
This is the first time The Bible Course has been run in New Zealand, and we’re delighted to partner with Bible Society NZ as their pilot group ahead of a national launch during Bible Month in July.
Over 140,000 people in the UK have already taken part, finding it accessible, engaging, and confidence‑building.
Whether you’ve read the Bible for years or are just beginning to explore Christian faith, you’re warmly invited.

events.id: French Film Festival - De Gaulle: Tilting Iron-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - De Gaulle: Tilting Iron
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Direct from the world premiere in France comes De Gaulle: Tilting Iron.

June 1940. France collapses and signs the Armistice. In the midst of the chaos, one man, Charles de Gaulle, refuses to give up.

Taking the ultimate gamble he flees to London and seeks to convince the world that the battle for France is neither over or lost. What follows next is a triumphant story of faith, audacity and an unshakeable quest for freedom.

Antonin Baudry has crafted a powerful and dramatic view of a man at the very centre of a turning point in history, on his path to becoming a 20th century leader and cultural icon. The reality of intense politics, family struggles and the impact of war are deftly drawn in this geopolitical thriller, which has been more than five years in the making.

Simon Abkarian commands the screen as the titular General, leading a stand-out ensemble cast including Benoît Magimel, Mathieu Kassovitz, Francois Darlan, Niels Schneider and the César-winner Anamaria Vartolomei (The Count of Monte Cristo). The talented Simon Russell Beale (The Choral) rounds out the cast as Winston Churchill, with the film exploring the complex relationship between De Gaulle and the British Bulldog.

Sure to be a talking point of the Festival, don’t miss out on seeing this keenly anticipated film.

events.id: Sionna-St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki-Event Finder
events.name: Sionna
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Cnr Beach Rd and Ames St, Paekākāriki
events.long_description:
Fresh from supporting Hothouse Flowers on their NZ tour, Sionna bring their first headline show to Paekākāriki– don’t miss this.

Sionna are a dynamic folk duo formed in Christchurch in 2023. Helen (Galway) and Lorcan (Limerick – just a stone’s throw away) combine haunting vocal harmonies, top-class fiddle, and rich, driving accompaniment on bouzouki and guitar. Alongside their musicianship, their warmth and wit make for a night as entertaining as it is musical.

The duo have built a strong following across the South Island, selling out their annual “big gig” at The Piano in Christchurch for the past three years. In 2026, they toured nationwide as support for Hothouse Flowers, earning a huge response from audiences in Auckland, Wellington, and Christchurch. Now, they’re back to show the good folk of Paekākāriki what a full Sionna show really feels like.

One night only:
???? St Peter's Village Hall
???? Sunday June 7th, 2026
???? 7:30pm

Get in early – this one won’t stay secret for long.

Their debut album Mischievous Eyes is available now on Spotify and all major streaming platforms.

events.name: Tawa Residents’ Association bi-monthly meeting
events.venue: 67 Main Rd, Tawa, Wellington 5028, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Tawa Residents’ Association formal bi-monthly meetings are open to the public.
We love to welcome Tawa Residents to join the committee, help out, share ideas or even just come along to hear what's going on in Tawa.
The Association is made up of residents of Tawa, Tawa community board members, Tawa business group members, Northern Ward Councillors and even our local Member of Parliament, its a great chance to find out whats going on and ask those burning questions you have.
We meet upstairs at the Tawa Squash Club on the second Monday of the month, every other month at 7.30pm.

events.id: Saul Williams meets Carlos Niño-171 Cuba Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Saul Williams meets Carlos Niño
events.venue: 171 Cuba Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Visionary poet, musician and cultural icon Saul Williams joins forces with acclaimed percussionist, producer and sonic curator Carlos Niño for their first-ever Aotearoa performances this June.
Together, the duo have earned a GRAMMY Award nomination for Best Spoken Word Poetry Album for their collaborative project Saul Williams meets Carlos Niño & Friends - a fully improvised live recording that captures the depth of their creative connection.
Renowned for his powerful fusion of spoken word, hip hop and alternative music, Saul Williams has built a multidisciplinary career spanning music, film and literature. From his breakout role in Slam to critically acclaimed albums like Amethyst Rock Star and The Inevitable Rise and Liberation of NiggyTardust!, he remains one of the most vital and genre-defying voices in contemporary culture.
Carlos Niño is celebrated for his expansive, collaborative approach to sound, connecting generations of forward-thinking artists through his work as a producer, composer and bandleader, including his ongoing project Carlos Niño & Friends.
For audiences in Aotearoa, this is a rare opportunity to experience two singular artists sharing the stage delivering a live performance shaped by improvisation, connection and decades of creative evolution.

events.id: Cooking Classes-Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cooking Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Authentic Indian cooking classes in Wellington with panaromic views over Wellington Harbour.

In each class, you will learn 4-5 recipes like: potato stuff roti, roasted eggplant curry, wholemeal, roti, naan; shahi paneer; dhal, samosa; palak paneer; gourmet chickpeas; vegetable korma; gulab jamun; kheer; mango lassi; masala chai, and many more...

The classes start at 6 pm or 10:30 am on weekends and weekdays with a 2-hour session followed by a dinner or lunch afterwards. The ingredients, copies of recipes, organic karma drink will be provided with a full course meal in a banquet setting. Custom made classes available for gluten free, dairy free and any other food intolerance and dietary requirements.The ingredients, recipes, complimentary organic karma drinks will be provided with a full course meal.

There are 2 to 8 people in each class in purpose built cooking studio. Also a private cooking classes available.

The menu for the class can be:

1) Spicy potato stuffed paratha and roti, rajasthani tomato relish, aalu matter or spicy peas- potato curry, roasted cumin yogurt raita and fresh mint- coriander chutney.

2) Shahi Paneer (cottage cheese cubes with spices and rich tomato-cashew gravy), garlic coriander naan, cumin jeera basmati rice and sweet fragrant rice pudding with cardamom. Also I will teach how to make fresh paneer.

3) Roasted eggplant curry, plain whole wheat chapatti or roti, jeera aaloo or potato curry, Tuar dahl (split yellow lentil) and Basmati rice with homemade pickle.

4) Palak paneer (creamy spinach curry with cubes of paneer), jeera rice (basmati rice with fragrant spices, panjabi chhole or spicy chickpeas curry and mango lassi. Also fresh paneer making.

5) Khadi (chickpeas fritters in yogurt and chickpeas flour soupy gravy), Aloo-Gobhi (cauliflower and potato curry),  savoury wraps with herbs and spices, fresh pickle and basmati rice.

The total cost is $130 per person, this includes ingredient cost. (As an alternative we also sell cooking class gift vouchers.The food options are unlimited and the class provides fresh and best ingredients)
Please contact for booking: indiancooking@xtra.co.nz

So just bring your taste buds! and Enjoy!!

events.id: French Film Festival - The Musicians-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - The Musicians
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Astrid Thompson (Donzelli) is determined to honour her late industrialist father’s final wish: unite four priceless Stradivarius-stringed instruments for the premiere recital of a new composition by his favourite artist, to be live-streamed for music lovers globally. With just six days to rehearse, a quartet is assembled, but the dream quickly unravels: the musicians are at odds, blinded by their differences and prejudices, and seemingly incapable of working together. With the deadline fast approaching, Astrid tries to coax the reclusive composer of the score, Charlie Beaumont (Frédéric Pierrot), out from creative seclusion in the desperate hope of salvaging the event…

As the film crescendos to a tender, exhilarating finale where harmony is found not only in music but human connection, The Musicians celebrates both the chaos and beauty of collaboration. Set amidst the villas, vineyards and chapels of the stunning Champagne region, with an exquisite original score by Grégoire Hetzel, this is sparkling entertainment for the heart (and ears).

events.id: French Film Festival - The Son Of-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - The Son Of
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
One week after a chaotic presidential election, France urgently needs a new Prime Minister. A hapless but ambitious young parliamentary assistant (Jean Chevalier) has a plan: convince his retired and highly erratic politician father (François Cluzet, The Intouchables) to return from exile and seize the top job. What follows is a breakneck political comedy that transforms into a razor-sharp portrait of power, ambition, and the flexibility of principle.

With The Son Of, former political journalist Carlos Abascal Peiró makes an audacious debut, dynamically navigating the treacherous corridors of the French Republic, his camera stalking the backrooms where ideology evaporates and egos dominate, capturing politicians, fixers and media operators engaged in verbal warfare with astonishing ferocity. Filmed before France’s current political turmoil, the narrative hums with uncanny and prophetic resonance. Think Veep with a distinctly Gallic accent; anyone appreciative of Armando Iannucci’s savage political dissections and Succession will find much to savour here.

Newcomer Chevalier is perfectly cast as the slippery modern opportunist, supported by an hilarious, never-better Cluzet’s reluctant patriarch, Karin Viard’s irresistible party operative and Alex Lutz (The Stolen Painting) rounding out a stellar ensemble. Sharp, unsparing, and mischievously satisfying, The Son Of is a winning satire.

events.id: FFFA - Case 137-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: FFFA - Case 137
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Léa Drucker took home the Best Actress César award for her performance in this gripping thriller from Dominik Moll (Only the Animals), selected for official competition at Cannes International Film Festival 2025.

Drucker plays Stéphanie, an Internal Affairs police officer who is assigned the case of a young man severely wounded during a tense and chaotic demonstration in Paris. When she discovers that the victim hails from her hometown of Saint-Dizier, the case suddenly becomes far more personal.

While nothing initially points to police misconduct, Stéphanie soon finds herself isolated in a system hellbent on protecting its own. With officers refusing to talk, Sophie turns to housekeeper and reluctant witness Alicia Mady
(Guslagie Malanda, Saint Omer), whose testimony holds the key to the truth - but puts them both in danger.

This compelling crime drama highlights themes of loyalty and justice that resonate long after the final scene, anchored strongly by Lea Drucker’s commanding role.

When she discovers that the victim hails from her hometown of Saint-Dizier, the case suddenly becomes far more personal.
While nothing initially points to police misconduct, Stéphanie soon finds herself isolated in a system hellbent on protecting its own.
With officers refusing to talk, Sophie turns to housekeeper and reluctant witness Alicia Mady (Guslagie Malanda, Saint Omer), whose testimony holds the key to the truth - but puts them both in danger.

events.name: Advances Yoga Class Level 3 - 4
events.venue: Yoga in Daily Life Centre, 23 Jessie St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Level 3 - 4 continues with more advanced postures that build on all the previous exercises learnt, if you have systematically followed them you will find these Asanas easy and energising.

The previous excercise have increased the flexibility of the spine, improved the body's physical condition and heightened awareness to the importance of relaxation. It has been emphasised that each exercise be practised slowly, coordinating movement with the breath, pausing motionless in each position and always with full concentration. Ideal for those who would like to learn the levels of the System YIDL.
Mondays 6 - 7.15pm, $20 or concession card or Monthly pass drop in class, no booking required, for experiences practitioners.

events.id: Musicking Together with Sam Manzanza-Evaro, Level 1, James Smith Corner, 65 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Musicking Together with Sam Manzanza
events.venue: Evaro, Level 1, James Smith Corner, 65 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Musicking Together is a free community music circle run by Sophie Sabri, a registered music therapist, in collaboration with different local musicians each month. The event takes place monthly at Evaro. It is open to anyone who has a love for music, and no musical experience is required. The venue is wheelchair accessible.

This month we will host famous Afrobeat Maestro Sam Manzanza for a night of African music. Sam will share some of his songs and we will run a drumming circle.

Musical instruments are provided. Come along to meet new people and play music without pressure.
"Musicking Together" is a space to create music together, to dance, to sing or simply listen to others. As long as we are in space, we are musicking.

How to find us? Evaro is located in the James Smith building, at the corner between Manners and Cuba Street. Enter the building and pass Rebel Sports to find the stairs. Go up to level 1 and follow the corridor on the left. You'll find Evaro to your left.

events.id: Are Prisons Obsolete? A future we make for ourselves.-3 Mulgrave St, Thorndon, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Are Prisons Obsolete? A future we make for ourselves.
events.venue: 3 Mulgrave St, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
People Against Prisons Aotearoa is an organisation that calls for a transformation in our approach: not just towards what we'd call criminalised behaviour and people, but towards organising the whole of society to support human flourishing.
The carceral system is an industry that does not produce a product, but a world of limited potential. While in the immediate future the cessation of harmful practices such as solitary confinement or abusive 'boot camps' for youth might not require alternatives, abolitionists like Ruth Wilson Gilmore argue that abolition is brought about by building something new. Some alternatives have already been attempted, such as restorative justice, but these programs are routinely discontinued even when they show signs of success. Other alternatives, such as Turuki! Turuki! https://www.beehive.govt.nz/.../2019-12/Turuki%20Turuki.pdf or #CareNotCages, are an imagined future, where education and community involvement transform lives for the better, in contrast to the so-called 'Justice System' limiting them far beyond reason.
Jade Winterburn, PAPA national outreach coordinator and Tāmaki branch secretary, will provide a short presentation followed by Q & A.
Join us online via Zoom
https://us02web.zoom.us/j/88369529154
Meeting chat link
https://us02web.zoom.us/launch/jc/88369529154

Join the IHC Wellington Association Committee for an informative evening exploring technology that supports independence and safety.

events.id: Paint and Wine Night - Rangitoto Sunrise-The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Rangitoto Sunrise
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar, 166 Willis St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.id: Saul Williams meets Carlos Niño-San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Saul Williams meets Carlos Niño
events.venue: San Fran, 171 Cuba St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Visionary poet, musician and cultural icon Saul Williams joins forces with acclaimed percussionist, producer and sonic curator Carlos Niño for their first-ever Aotearoa performances this June.

Together, the duo have earned a GRAMMY Award nomination for Best Spoken Word Poetry Album for their collaborative project Saul Williams meets Carlos Niño & Friends - a fully improvised live recording that captures the depth of their creative connection.

Renowned for his powerful fusion of spoken word, hip hop and alternative music, Saul Williams has built a multidisciplinary career spanning music, film and literature. From his breakout role in Slam to critically acclaimed albums like Amethyst Rock Star and The Inevitable Rise and Liberation of NiggyTardust!, he remains one of the most vital and genre-defying voices in contemporary culture.

Carlos Niño is celebrated for his expansive, collaborative approach to sound, connecting generations of forward-thinking artists through his work as a producer, composer and bandleader, including his ongoing project Carlos Niño & Friends.

For audiences in Aotearoa, this is a rare opportunity to experience two singular artists sharing the stage delivering a live performance shaped by improvisation, connection and decades of creative evolution.

events.id: Karori Friendship Club-St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Karori Friendship Club
events.venue: St Ninian's Uniting Church, 208 Karori Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tuesday mornings just got better!

If you enjoy a good chat, a decent cuppa, and the kind of laughter that makes you forget the time, the Karori Friendship Club is for you. We meet at St Ninian’s, 208 Karori Road, Karori, from 10 am to 12 pm, and for a koha you’ll be treated to morning tea and genuinely friendly company.

It’s a wonderful fit for seniors, people new to Karori, or any ESOL learners who would like a relaxed place to practise their language skills and settle into the community. Some weeks, there’s a board game or a puzzle on the go. Most weeks, it’s simply good conversation, shared stories, and the kind of connection that brightens your whole day.

No pressure, no fuss — just a warm welcome and a seat at the table. Come and see what makes this little Tuesday gathering such a special part of our community. You don't have to live in Karori to come; we have members from all over Wellington.

Ph: 476 7137 for more info or check out our website.

events.id: French Film Festival - Once Upon My Mother-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Once Upon My Mother
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Based on the bestselling autobiography by Roland Perez, this exuberant and heartrending film pays tribute to a love like no other – a mother’s love for her child.
In 1963, Roland is born to Esther, her sixth child in their close-knit Jewish immigrant family living in the Parisian suburbs. When Esther learns that Roland has been born with a club foot, she vows to ensure her son lives not just a normal life, but a fabulous one.

She enlists the help of a determined specialist and the pop songs of Sylvie Vartan (who makes a cameo in the film) to help Roland defy all expectations and learn to walk.

This joyful crowd-pleaser is vibrantly shot by Oscar-nominated cinematographer Guillaume Schiffman (The Artist) and features a powerhouse performance from Leïla Bekhti (Paris, je t’aime) as the fiercely devoted Esther.

Once Upon My Mother absolutely smashed the French box office with over 1.5 million admissions, so is sure to be popular at this year’s Festival. Book your tickets now to avoid missing out!

events.id: Spooky Tour of the Parliamentary Library-89 Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Spooky Tour of the Parliamentary Library
events.venue: 89 Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for some spooky fun, with our ghost tour of the Parliamentary Library. Tours are available:
9 June at 5:30pm
10 June at 5:30pm
11 August at 5:30pm
12 August at 5:30pm
Hear the dark secrets and spooky stories that lie within the walls of the oldest building on the Parliamentary precinct, plus, get a behind the scenes look at some of the spaces you don't usually see. Not for the faint hearted.
This event is full of scares and numbers are limited, so register now!
Please note, some areas of the route are narrow, and dark. There is also limited accessibility on this tour, and some parts are only accessible by stairs. Due to the content of some of the stories, this tour is for visitors aged 14 years+.
Please get in touch with tour.desk@parliament.govt.nz if you have any questions.
