events.id: French Film Festival - Santiago: The Camino Therapy-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Santiago: The Camino Therapy
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Experience the breathtaking scenery of the Camino de Santiago in this life-affirming drama centred on an unforgettable pairing of characters.

Adam (a powerful performance from up-and-comer Julien Le Berre) is a teenager who’s lost and heading in the wrong direction. After a run-in with the law, a judge hands over a surprising sentence: he must walk the 800 kilometre Camino de Santiago to think about his future.

Assigned to walk the path with Adam is Fred (an excellent-as-always Alexandra Lamy, Miss Violet), a tough and outspoken woman coming to terms with her own chequered history. The two begin a journey that could change their lives. The pair make an unlikely team; Fred is looking to heal from her past, while Adam is struggling to channel his anger and feelings of abandonment. With every mile travelled, they inevitably endure exhaustion, clashes and arguments.

But among the points of tension are moments of peace. As the miles rack up, Fred and Adam begin to form a strong bond. Walking The Way, they each discover an inner strengh they didn’t know they had. Will it be enough to accomplish the goals they set out to achieve?

Santiago: The Camino Therapy is an uplifting and inspirational story with gorgeous vistas that deserve to be seen on the big screen.

events.id: Awareness through Movement Classes on Mt Vic-Quaker Meeting Room, 8 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Awareness through Movement Classes on Mt Vic
events.venue: Quaker Meeting Room, 8 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
Want to reduce pain and tension, improve posture, in a friendly and supportive atmosphere?

These lessons use principles of neuroplasticity, biomechanics, and human development to help you improve long-term habits of movement and posture.

They are based on the pioneering work of physicist and engineer Dr Moshe Feldenkrais. He developed his unique system to help manage his own knee injuries. His method is now used worldwide.

First class is free.
Sue 0274667123

events.id: Book launch | NOVA-61 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Book launch | NOVA
events.venue: 61 Willis St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us for the launch of NOVA, a wise and joyful new novel by Tim Corballis. The book will be launched by pip adam.
Tuesday 9 June, 6pm
Unity Books Wellington
Free event – all welcome!
Nobody is very used to organising a world.
From the author of Our Future Is in the Air and R.H.I., NOVA is a genre-defying novel that imagines a future in which people and their worlds talk to each other.
Set on NOVA, a self-contained world launched into deep time, the novel unfolds through conversations between Kalla, a former councillor uneasy with consensus and ceremony, and System, the voice of all NOVA’s mechanisms and processes. System is curious and anxious – and seems to know about every aspect of life on NOVA, but in some ways knows nothing at all. Kalla is sceptical, smart and increasingly troubled by what can and can’t be measured. Together, System and Kalla circle around questions of democracy, labour, memory, entropy and love.
As it moves between scenes of work, public ritual and speculative reflections on systems theory and time, and as NOVA itself coasts, rotates and persists in its unknowable form, the novel asks disarming questions: what might it mean to have on-demand access to the voice of the world? What would we do with that knowledge? And is it possible for a world to be meaningfully organised at all?
‘This novel is such a wise, far-reaching, and funny reflection of organised societies and the relationship between humans and machines. What an ambitious, enlightening, and strangely joyful book.’ —Alice Miller
events.description:
The book will be launched by pip adam.
Tuesday 9 June, 6pm Unity Books Wellington Free event – all welcome!

And is it possible for a world to be meaningfully organised at all?

events.id: The Inner Blueprint — How the Unconscious Shapes Everything-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Inner Blueprint — How the Unconscious Shapes Everything
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Inner Blueprint — How the Unconscious Shapes Everything
Before you could think critically, your mind was already being programmed.
By age 7, you had absorbed a blueprint — beliefs about your worth, your safety, your place in the world — downloaded from the people and environment around you. You didn't choose those beliefs. But you've been living by them ever since.
The Inner Blueprint is a 75-minute transformational workshop exploring the hidden architecture of the mind through the pioneering work of Carl Jung, Bruce Lipton, Joe Dispenza, and Robert Gene Smith.
You'll discover where your patterns come from, why willpower alone can't change them, and experience a practical method to begin rewriting the script — at the level where it actually lives.
The blueprint was written for an earlier version of you. It's time to write a new one.
Meet your host, Krishnaloka
Introducing Krishnaloka, who is driven to help others overcome their issues within their mind and psychology. As a mind technician trained in eutaptics (Faster EFT), she employs the fastest and most powerful method to create lasting change by addressing the root causes of issues rather than just the symptoms. Her warm and caring nature makes every interaction with her both comforting and transformative.
6.15pm Tuesday 2nd June
End the session on a high note with a delicious plant-based dinner, dessert, and good conversation.
Tickets available here~ https://tinyurl.com/BLeventtickets
Bhakti Lounge 1/175 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington. http://www.bhaktilounge.org.nz

events.id: Whaea Kairau: Mother Hundred Eater By Apirana Taylor-Te Whaea - National Dance & Drama Centre,11 Hutchison Road, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Whaea Kairau: Mother Hundred Eater By Apirana Taylor
events.venue: Te Whaea - National Dance & Drama Centre,11 Hutchison Road, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Whaea Kairau: Mother Hundred Eater By Apirana Taylor
Aotearoa, the 1840s. The land is fracturing, and one woman will do whatever it takes to survive.

About this Event

Toi Whakaari: NZ Drama School presents Whaea Kairau: Mother Hundred Eater by Apirana Taylor, as part of our 2026 Double Bill season.
Aotearoa, the 1840s. The land is fracturing, and one woman will do whatever it takes to survive. Whaea Kairau is dispossessed, displaced, and utterly without illusion as she navigates the chaos of the New Zealand Wars not with nobility, but with a cunning eye for opportunity.
Apirana Taylor's landmark Māori theatre classic is black-hearted and uncomfortably funny. A journey through colonisation's darkest chapters that refuses to offer easy villains, easy victims, or easy answers.
Together with Van Badham's Animal Farm, they form an unmissable double bill from Toi Whakaari: NZ Drama School, one that refuses to look away from the oldest story in the book: who gets power, who loses it, and who pays the price.
We invite you to witness the bold work of our students across Acting, Production, Design, Costume, and Set & Props construction, and come away with more questions, more fire, and more hope than you arrived with.
By arrangement with Playmarket NZ

Whaea Kairau: Mother Hundred Eater | Recommended for ages 14+
Contains references to war, colonial violence, death, coarse language, and themes of colonisation and racial conflict.
Special School Group Price - Enquire by contacting

events.id: World Ocean Day Fundraising Quiz - Mountains to Sea-Parrotdog Bar, 60 - 66 Kingsford Smith St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: World Ocean Day Fundraising Quiz - Mountains to Sea
events.venue: Parrotdog Bar, 60 - 66 Kingsford Smith St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Whale-come back for another instalment of the Mountains to Sea Wellington World Ocean Day Pub Quiz!

Brush up on your ocean trivia, put on your pirate hats, and join us for an unforgettable night of community sea-lebration!
Thanks to our friends at Parrotdog Bar, we’ll be back on the south coast this year for this annual sell-out fundraising event... get your tickets fast!

All proceeds from the night will support Mountains to Sea Wellington’s marine programmes, providing opportunities for folks of all ages to get up close and personal with te taiao through our education programmes, restoration projects and community events.

We’ll have a range of quiz topics, themed around the ocean (and other topics for the less ocean-savvy). There’ll also be mini games, raffles, spot prizes and more!

‍Be the best dressed:
Don your most sea-purb costumes and join the catwalk to compete for …the best in shoal!

Tickets on sale NOW! Get in quick before they are gone.
Tickets are $25pp or $150 for a team table (maximum 6 per team), with options to pay more if you’re feeling like making a bigger splash to support us.
If you book a single ticket, we’ll set you up with team mates on the night.

This event is part of World Ocean Day celebrations. Learn more about World Ocean Day here:https://worldoceanday.org.

events.id: A Closer Look at the International Baccalaureate Programmes-1 Monorgan Rd, Strathmore Park, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: A Closer Look at the International Baccalaureate Programmes
events.venue: 1 Monorgan Rd, Strathmore Park, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Why Scots College Junior and Middle School?
Take a closer look at the International Baccalaureate (IB) programmes at Scots College
We invite you to an evening designed to give families a real look inside the Junior and Middle School at Scots College.
We will begin with an overview of our recent IB review visit, before taking a closer look at the Primary Years Programme (PYP) and Middle Years Programme (MYP) – what makes them different, and what makes them world class. We will also share findings from the latest international research on IB student outcomes and discuss what the significant changes to NCEA mean for families making decisions about education right now. And perhaps most valuably, two of our own students will share what the IB programmes have meant for them, with plenty of time for your questions afterwards.
Refreshments from 6:30pm in the Hall, with a 6:45pm start.
This event is open to both current Scots College families and families who are interested in enroling at Scots College.
Register now with the tickets link above.

events.id: Brazilian Zouk Dance Classes - Beginners block D-60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Brazilian Zouk Dance Classes - Beginners block D
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Connect with other like minded people that have a love of dance.
Brazilian Zouk was developed in Rio de Janeiro in the mid 90s, and is now becoming more and more popular
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=DfhRHnEAbak
..............
This is stunning to watch and even more fun to dance! It is based on the original Lambada dance but adapted to more modern music with a slow-quick-quick beat. It can be danced to a wide variety of music including R&B, Rock and Pop.
In this block of 5 classes you will discover the fundamental techniques, movements and sequences of Brazilian Zouk to get you up and running on the dance floor.
This includes:
Week 1: Passo basico
Week 2: Lateral
Week 3: Soltinho
Week 4: Body Roll, and Snake
Week 5: Combination
They are designed so you can start this block as a complete newbie, or you can continue on as an existing student from the last block.
There are 4 blocks of classes (A, B, C, D) in the complete Fundamentals course. Once these are done, you can progress to the Improvers classes.
Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week term cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students.
- Ad hoc cost is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25

events.id: Gardens of Aotearoa with Xanthe White-Churton Park School, 90 Churton Drive, Churton Park, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Gardens of Aotearoa with Xanthe White
events.venue: Churton Park School, 90 Churton Drive, Churton Park, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join Xanthe White for an evening exploring the beautiful gardens her studio has designed around New Zealand. One of the country’s leading landscape designers and a Chelsea Flower Show award winner, Xanthe has worked for nearly three decades on projects ranging from private gardens to larger community spaces, both here and overseas.

Known for her thoughtful, place-based approach that connects people with the natural world, she’ll share a selection of the studio's projects - from coastal and rural sites to urban gardens, including Wellington - and talk through how different climates, conditions, and clients shape each design.

As a relaxed and engaging look at how gardens evolve from their setting, there will be plenty of ideas to take back to your own garden as well as a delicious supper, cash raffles for some fantastic prizes, and signed copies of Xanthe’s newly revised book The Natural Garden for sale at a special price of $50.

All are welcome at this special Churton Park Garden Group event.

Tickets $10 per person. Churton Park Garden Group members free.

events.id: Intro to Improv with PopRox-48 Aro Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Intro to Improv with PopRox
events.venue: 48 Aro Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Build confidence, embrace failure and unleash your inner silly billy with this 6 week introduction to the art of Improvisation, with Wellington's premier improv professionals.
Over 6 weeks you'll cover the core principles of improv. You will learn to:
- Happily accept offers to make each other look and feel good.
- Find the joy in failing together, so we can be bolder and surprise one another.
- Re-connect with your sense of "play" in a safe and welcoming space.
- Learn positive communications skills which foster collaboration.
This is a 6 week course for folks new to improv, or returning to it after a longer break. Classes are every Tuesday 7:15pm-9:15pm at the Aro Valley Community Centre, from May 12 to June 16. Registration covers the entire course.
ABOUT THE TEACHER
Austin Harrison is one of Aotearoa's most experienced improvisers and teachers with over 15 years experience. He holds a Master of Fine Arts in Theatre from Victoria University of Wellington and is the current Artistic Director of the New Zealand Improv Festival.
Austin believes improv is a powerful connector which builds communities, boosts confidence and reminds us all how to have fun. He's taught improv in a variety of environments, from intermediate schools, right through to over-65s classes. He's an award-winning performer and producer, a founding member of PopRox Improv and has taken shows to the Edinburgh Fringe and Melbourne International Comedy Festival.
ABOUT POPROX
PopRox are Wellington's best-dressed improv company. Known for their slick production values, and bold story-telling they were the resident improv company at Circa Theatre performing monthly throughout 2024 and 2025. Their Production of improv Murder Mystery "Farce Onion" won a Director's Choice award at the NZ Comedy Festival in 2024 and they were shortlisted for the "Best Improv Company Award" at the NZ Comedy Guild Awards 2025.
THE STAND-BY LIST
PopRox firmly believes that improv is for everyone, especially when it comes to sharing skills and knowledge. So we're offering "the stand-by list" for all our workshops in 2026. We'll giveaway any spots remaining one week before this course, on a first-come-first-serve basis. No need to provide a reason, no questions asked. If the entry fee is a barrier for you to come along, then simply email poproximprov@gmail.com and we'll pop you on the SBL. Everyone on the list will be notified whether there was a place available for them at least 5 days before the course begins.
ACCESSIBILITY
Aro Valley Community Centre is wheelchair accessible.
There is cycle parking right outside, and the nearest bus stop is 200 metres away on Aro Street (STOP 6784), or 300 metres to Willis Street (stop 7711) via Palmer Street.
These classes are suitable for blind/low-vision learners and for those living with diasability, who have good verbal communication skills (this is key to the type of work we'll be doing).
QUESTIONS? GET IN TOUCH
If you have questions about the course, please don't hesitate to get in touch with us by emailing poproximprov@gmail.com

events.long_description:

The complete Intermediate course consists of 16 different Blocks (A-P).

events.id: Animal Farm By Van Badham-Te Whaea - National Dance & Drama Centre,11 Hutchison Road, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Animal Farm By Van Badham
events.venue: Te Whaea - National Dance & Drama Centre,11 Hutchison Road, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Animal Farm By Van Badham
The animals of Manor Farm have had enough. They rise up, and build a new world on the promise that all animals are equal. But it won't last.

About this Event

Toi Whakaari: NZ Drama School presents Animal Farm by Van Badham, as part of our 2026 Double Bill season.
The animals of Manor Farm have had enough. They rise up, cast out their oppressor, and build a new world on the promise that all animals are equal. But it won't last.
Van Badham's razor-sharp adaptation of Orwell's enduring fable drags the barnyard into the present tense, where the pigs have podiums, the sheep scroll and share, and the slogans change just fast enough that nobody notices. A savage, darkly comic warning that has never felt more necessary.
Together with Apirana Taylor's Whaea Kairau: Mother Hundred Eater, they form an unmissable double bill from Toi Whakaari: NZ Drama School, one that refuses to look away from the oldest story in the book: who gets power, who loses it, and who pays the price.
We invite you to witness the bold work of our students across Acting, Production, Design, Costume, and Set & Props construction, and come away with more questions, more fire, and more hope than you arrived with.
Performance rights exclusively licensed by Yellow Creative Management

Animal Farm | Recommended for ages 14+
Contains adult themes, coarse language, references to war, slavery, poverty, animal abuse and animal hanging.
Special School Group Price - Enquire by contacting

events.id: French Film Festival - Redress-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Redress
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Auteur Régis Wargnier (director of the Oscar-winning Indochine) returns to filmmaking for the first time in over a decade with the masterfully crafted globe-trotting drama Redress.

Renowned chef Paskal Jankovski (Clovis Cornillac, A Little Something Extra) is moments away from announcing his third Michelin star when he suddenly vanishes. Disappearing alongside him is Antoine (Julien de Saint-Jean, Lie with Me), his trusted sous-chef.

Paskal’s daughter Clara (Julia de Nunez) suddenly finds herself the inheritor of the family restaurant at just 20 years old. Once a paragon of Gallic gastronomic excellence, the establishment now faces an uncertain future. The void left by Paskal’s absence feels inescapable, while the mystery of his and Antoine’s disappearance remains unsolved.

The enigma deepens when years later, traces of Paskal’s cooking style crop up at a restaurant in Taiwan. Clara must embark on a journey to find the truth about her father’s fate.

A story that covers continents, cuisines and cultures, Redress is brought to life by strong performances and immaculate cinematography. You’ll be drawn in by the intriguing mystery, lush environments and variety of sumptuous dishes - just don’t see it on an empty stomach!

events.id: Ethan Rawhiti and Weiran Chen, Piano Four Hands-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ethan Rawhiti and Weiran Chen, Piano Four Hands
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join pianists Weiran Chen and Ethan Rawhiti for a lunchtime concert of Dvorak and Brahms symphonic works for piano four-hands.

Dvorak- Slavonic Dance, Op. 72, No. 2

Brahms- Symphony No.4 in E minor, Op. 90
I. Allegro non assai
II. Andante moderato
III. Allegro giocoso
IV. Allegro energico e passionato

ETHAN RAWHITI

Wellington-born pianist Ethan Rawhiti graduated in 2025 with a Bachelor of Music (Honours) from Victoria University of Wellington, where he studied with Dr Jian Liu and Emma Sayers. He began learning piano at age seven and has extensive experience as a collaborative musician. In 2025, Ethan was répétiteur for Wellington Opera’s Mate Ururoa by Dame Gillian Whitehead and performed in Brigid Bisley’s In Return concert in Whanganui. In 2024, he was sponsored by Professor Jack C. Richards to attend the Semaine Internationale de Piano in Switzerland. Ethan is committed to collaboration and plans to pursue further study overseas.

WEIRAN CHEN

Chinese pianist Weiran Chen has been a prizewinner in numerous international and national piano competitions, including the “Mido”Concours De Tiano Sino-Francais, the Kingsburg International Piano Competition, the Feis Ceoil in Ireland and the Beethoven International Music Competition.

Chen graduated from the Boston Conservatory at Berklee with a Master of Music degree and is a current DMA student at the NZSM. She is passionate about not only performing piano solos, but also collaborating with other artists and teaching up and coming musicians.

events.id: French Film Festival - The Richest Woman in the World-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - The Richest Woman in the World
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
- OFFICIAL SELECTION: Festival de Cannes (Out of Competition) -
- CÉSAR AWARD WINNER: Best Actor – Laurent Lafitte -

Few modern scandals have captivated France as the astonishing events surrounding Liliane Bettencourt, the billionaire heiress to the L’Oreal fortune and doyenne of Parisian society. Now writer/director Thierry Klifa has freely adapted the incredible story into a captivating dark comedy, featuring a jaw-dropping, tailor-made role for one of France’s most iconic stars.

Marianne Farrère (Isabelle Huppert), the head of the Windler Group, is the richest woman in the world. Pierre-Alain Fantin (Laurent Lafitte, who won a César award for his performance) is a vivacious dandy, writer and photographer. After meeting on a photo shoot, the two quickly become inseparable. Their friendship surprises, amuses, intrigues, and eventually unsettles the billionaire’s entourage and family.

Most of all, Marianne’s daughter (Marina Foïs) struggles with her mother’s sudden complicity with this younger man, but her husband (André Marcon) and loyal butler (Raphaël Personnaz) share her concerns. Surreptitious queries soon transition into a private investigation, resulting in extraordinary ramifications for all involved…

With several twists too good to spoil, The Richest Woman in the World makes the most of its fabulous ensemble cast, but Huppert and Lafitte simply eat up the screen. This wildly entertaining film became an enormous box-office hit in France for good reason.

events.name: Otaki Women's Community Club Meeting
events.venue: Family Hotel, Ōtaki, 30 Main Street, Ōtaki 5512, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Come along and join the Ōtaki Women’s Community Club
Meet amazing wāhine, connect with other local women, share a few laughs, feel inspired, and learn together in a warm, welcoming, and safe space with like-minded women. We get together for a club meeting, then have dinner.

Keen to come to a meeting? Email us at otakiwomensclub@outlook.com

The Ōtaki Women’s Community Club is also the heart behind the much-loved Ōtaki Market and Ōtaki Kids Market. Through this, the club supports local businesses, brings people together through community events, and helps strengthen connections across Ōtaki.

Whether you’d love to volunteer, meet new friends, share your skills, or simply be part of something positive in your community, there’s a place for you at ŌWCC.

events.id: Ōtari Raranga in Te Aro 2026 - Evenings-113 Taranak Street, 113 Taranaki Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga in Te Aro 2026 - Evenings
events.venue: 113 Taranak Street, 113 Taranaki Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kua raranga tahi tātou he whariki mō āpōpō
Together we weave the mat for future generations

Nau mai haere mai, all are welcome to attend Ōtari Raranga wānanga evening sessions in Te Aro

Suited to beginner and intermediate weavers.

You will walk away with woven taonga suited to your skills at the end of each class.

These classes run approximately monthly.

Beginner students will complete a variety of putiputi (flowers) and we will provide harakeke for you if it is your FIRST class.

We will share tikanga and demonstrate how to harvest for yourself during this time.

If this is not your first class with, and you would like us to harvest for you, purchase the add-on - "Please harvest for me" for an additional fee.

Moving forward, you will start making kōnae (2 corner kete - basket), kono (4-sided kete), tapawhāroa learning a variety of closes.

You will need to harvest 8-10 rau for yourself and expected to start building your own tool kit.

With regular attendance, you will:
- Learn tikanga (protocols) and karakia for hauhake (harvesting) and preparing harakeke
- Build your mātauranga (knowledge)
- Use kupu Māori appropriate to raranga practice
- Develop your pūkenga (practical skills)
- Build hononga (connections) within our raranga rōpū (group)
- Have the skills to practice at home.

What’s included in your beginner's ticket?
- Three-hour weaving class with experienced kaiako
- Harakeke is provided for your first class, and then you should hauhake (harvest) for yourself or click the "please harvest for me" option in addition to your ticket at least two days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share

Led by Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera) , Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri).

On occasion our raranga rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), will drop by and we often have support from our Ōtari Raranga kaiāwhina.

Important information
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia
- For tikanga purposes, beginner ticket sales close two days before wānanga, giving us time to hauhake (harvest) for you
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers

For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit our website: www.otariraranga.co.nz

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with the Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits, please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com or visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

events.id: French Film Festival - Cycle of Time-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Cycle of Time
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Elsa Zylberstein (Simone: Woman of the Century) and Didier Bourdon (The Scammers) star in this inventive time travel comedy that sharply explores societal roles.

It’s 1958 in quiet suburbia. Hélène (Zylberstein) and Michel (Bourdon) live a simple but happy life with their two children. Michel works in a bank, while Hélène focuses her efforts on their home and family life.

Through an unexpected and unlikely series of events, the couple find themselves catapulted into the 21st century. The year is 2025, and everything they know has changed. Hélène and Michel grapple with new technology, social rituals, and the frantic pace of modern life.

Hélène now finds herself a successful bank manager and the sole breadwinner, while Michel has become the stay-at-home-husband.

As they navigate this unfamiliar world, the pair must confront new ways of thinking, loving and living. Hélène and Michel are left wondering not how they will survive in their new reality, but whether they even want to...

Propelled by hilarious performances from Zylberstein and Bourdon, Cycle of Time is a delightfully subversive comedy that will have you rediscovering the present through the eyes of two characters from a bygone era.

events.name: Paint and Wine Night - Victoria Bridge (First Drink Included)
events.venue: Hey Coastie, Wellington, 42 Tutere Street, Waikanae
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Waikanae! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: First drink is included in the ticket price.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.name: Save Water. Save Power.
events.venue: Tireti Hall, Cnr Tireti Rd & Te Pene St, Titahi Bay,42 Tireti Rd, Titahi Bay, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Save Water. Save Power.
This free event is all about saving you water and power. Meet Lisa from The Sustainability Trust for lots of tips on reducing water usage and tips on saving power this winter. Come and share your own too. PLUS - FREE CLOTHES! Let's share ideas on cheaper and more sustainable living.

Bring clothes to give, or come get free clothes for you and your whānau.

Also, here from locals about their project to make sure the moana is safe to swim.

This event is a TBCG Community Connect event. We run these once a month-ish to connect you with things you care about and need.

Meet locals, share ideas, get inspired. We would love to see you there.

Please share. Nau mai, haere ma e te whānau.

Just turn up. Or, for more details email

events.name: Heartbreak and Harmonies
events.venue: St Barnabas, Plimmerton Presbyterian Church,107 Mana Espl, Paremata, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Heartbreak and Harmonies
Voices in the Wind Community Choir invites you to a memorable musical experience that will stir your heart, lift your spirits, and bring back memories you didn’t even know you’d forgotten.

From Motown classics to modern favourites, from the Beatles to a touch of Kiwi humour, this concert weaves together songs of love, loss, laughter, and everything in between. You’ll hear harmonies that move you, moments that make you smile, and perhaps even a song that feels like it was written just for you.

Come along, relax, and enjoy the beautiful music while supporting your local community. Whether you’re coming with friends, family, or just for yourself, there’s something here for everyone.

events.name: Saint Levant
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington
events.long_description:
Combining traditional Arabic music, R&B and hip-hop, Saint Levant embraces the plurality of his own cultural heritage, the Arab community worldwide and the Palestinian struggle abroad. His songs – written in Arabic, English and French – have captivated a global audience with their international appeal. Appearing in New Zealand for the first time and exclusively at Lōemis, his shows in Australia sold out in less than 24 hours.

Don't miss your opportunity to see him live!

events.id: TEST (BRAZILIAN GRINDCORE) W/ WINCE & VORTEKKE-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: TEST (BRAZILIAN GRINDCORE) W/ WINCE & VORTEKKE
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Couldn’t be more thrilled to present legendary DIY grindcore band TEST touring throughout the North Island of New Zealand for the first time this June.
Come see TEST Wednesday 10th at Wellingtons finest venue - Valhalla. Joined by the terrible twins of Vorteke and Wince.
For the uninitiated, TEST is a Brazilian duo formed in 2010 by João Kombi (guitar and vocals) and Barata (drums), two veterans of São Paulo’s underground scene. Known for their intense live performances, notable collaborations with artists like Iggor Cavalera and Deafkids, and a fearless musical approach, the band has established itself as one of the most inventive names in the Brazilian alternative scene.
From the deathgrind band initially known for playing outside of major heavy metal concerts, fueled by a old school VW van-powered generator, Test has gone a long way since 2011, when the band started playing on the streets. Since then, the band has shared stages - inside the venue this time, be it as opening band or headlining - with artists such as Mr Bungle, King Diamond, Napalm Death, Carcass, Cannibal Corpse, Exodus e Brujeria, Mayhem, Mortician, Dillinger Escape Plan, to name a few. Speaking of name-dropping, some famous Test fans include Shane Embury, Napalm Death’s bass player, and the brothers Max and Iggor Cavalera - Iggor, by the way, has chosen Test’s drummer on the top 10 drummers of all time.
Aside from performing with well-know heavy metal artists, Test also took part in two of the major extreme music festivals worldwide: “Obscene Extreme”, in the Czech Republic and Mexico, and “Maryland Deathfest”, in the US. With a steady recording and playing live pace, Test turns sixteen years old with 19 albums (full-lenghts, EPs and split-album with bands from different parts of the world, like Nepal [Chepang], U.S.A. [Cloud Rat, Ixias] and China [Struggle Session]), two feature films, more than ten thousand records sold and more than one thousand shows in all states of Brazil, as well as twenty one foreign tours (Asia, Europe, North America and South America).
Tickets available from Undertheradar.co.nz Presales only $20.

events.id: Terror-Fi Presents: DISCLOSURE DAY (Advance Preview - Wellington)-12 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Terror-Fi Presents: DISCLOSURE DAY (Advance Preview - Wellington)
events.venue: 12 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Be one the first to see Steven Spielberg's DISCLOSURE DAY at our special advance preview.
The legendary director returns to his science fiction playground, with a film oozing ‘Close Encounters of the Third Kind’ vibes. With an all-star cast that includes Emily Blunt, Colin Firth, and Colman Domingo, this modern sci-fi epic is not to be missed on the big screen!
Come early to enjoy our themed lobby and custom pre-show.
-- If you found out we weren’t alone, if someone showed you, proved it to you, would that frighten you? This summer, the truth belongs to eight billion people. --

events.long_description:
We're so excited for this one!!
8:30 Ani Saafa
9:15 Debt Club
10:00 DJ Lazarus
Sick poster by Vanessa Cone
Debt Club are a Pōneke based indie / alt rock 6-piece. Known for their rich arrangements that blend anthemic saxophone melodies with thoughtful songwriting, Debt Club crafts a sound that is full of texture and energy.
Ani Saafa are a four-piece band. Distinguishing themselves from their peers, they draw from raw 2000s garage rock with influences of grunge, punk, and alt pop, balancing grit, melody, and immediacy.
DJ Lazarus mixes Techno & House with a twist of acid synths.
Artist links:
Debt Club: https://debtclub.bandcamp.com/
Ani Saafa: https://anisaafa.bandcamp.com/album/phonetics
DJ Lazarus: https://www.instagram.com/lazarusam_/
Pōneke is home to an incredible diversity of artists and music lovers.
We want Eyegum Wednesdays to be a space that everyone can enjoy with care and respect.
We have no room for transphobia or racism, sexism, homophobia and disregarding consent.
If you see or experience anything concerning, no matter how small, please let our crew or San Fran staff know.
Lovingly sponsored by San Fran, Abandoned Brewery and Wellington City Council.

events.id: Awareness through Movement in Karori-Karori Community Centre, 7 Beauchamp St, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Awareness through Movement in Karori
events.venue: Karori Community Centre, 7 Beauchamp St, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Maintain mobility and balance as you go through life.

Want to reduce pain and tension, improve posture, in a friendly and supportive atmosphere?

These lessons use principles of neuroplasticity, biomechanics, and human development to help you improve long-term habits of movement and posture.

They are based on the pioneering work of physicist and engineer Dr Moshe Feldenkrais. He developed his unique system to help manage his own knee injuries. His method is now used worldwide.

First class free.
Sue 0274667123

events.id: Boy & Bear New Zealand tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Boy & Bear New Zealand tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Eccles
Australian indie-folk favourites Boy & Bear announce their return to Aotearoa, playing three consecutive shows in Wellington, Christchurch and Auckland this June. Tickets go on sale Tuesday 7 April at midday.
Off the back of their sixth studio album Tripping Over Time, released in December 2025 and an extensive international tour across Canada, the UK and Europe, the band are set to arrive in peak form.
Boy & Bear emerged onto the global stage with their breakthrough debut album Moonfire, which earned multi-platinum success and five ARIA Awards in 2011. Two years later their follow-up, Harlequin Dream debuted at #1on the ARIA Albums charts, and they went on to amass five consecutive Top 10 albums and more than half a billion streams worldwide.
New Zealand last saw the band in 2024, for their Harlequin Dream 10th Anniversary Tour. That milestone celebrated one of their most beloved records, featuring hit single ‘Southern Sun’ that remains a favourite in New Zealand alongside its music video filmed against a scenic backdrop near Queenstown. Now they return to Aotearoa with a catalogue that spans over a decade and a brand new album.

events.id: French Film Festival - Guru-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Guru
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
In this tense psychological thriller, Pierre Niney (The Count of Monte Cristo) delivers a chilling performance as a popular life coach with a dedicated following.

Mathieu Vasseur (Niney) delivers electrifying seminars, promising empowerment and transformation to his loyal fanbase. But behind his carefully curated image lies a more sinister reality.

As Vasseur’s reach grows, the bonds he forms with his followers tighten into chains of control. Self-growth is traded for emotional reliance, and subtle influence becomes total psychological domination. What began as a quest for enlightenment for all involved becomes a predatory hierarchy.

As scrutiny from skeptics and the law pushes Vasseur’s organisation towards obsession and collapse, he faces the question: how far can he go before his promise of freedom becomes a prison?

Niney’s outstanding turn is bolstered by the excellent supporting cast of Marion Barbeau, Anthony Bajon (Beating Hearts), Christophe Montenez (also starring in 13 Days, 13 Nights) and
Holt McCallany (Mindhunter).

Guru is a razor-sharp examination of power dynamics that masterfully explores the thin line between mentorship and manipulation. Already a box office hit in France with over $16 million USD at the box office, make sure you don’t miss it!

In this tense psychological thriller, Pierre Niney (The Count of Monte Cristo) delivers a chilling performance as a popular life coach with a dedicated following.
Mathieu Vasseur (Niney) delivers electrifying seminars, promising empowerment and transformation to his loyal fanbase.
But behind his carefully curated image lies a more sinister reality.
As Vasseur’s reach grows, the bonds he forms with his followers tighten into chains of control.

events.id: Lunch with the Finance Minister-Banquet Hall, New Zealand Parliament Buildings,1 Museum Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Lunch with the Finance Minister
events.venue: Banquet Hall, New Zealand Parliament Buildings,1 Museum Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Lunch with the Finance Minister
Wellington Chamber of Commerce is proud to host the Finance Minister for Wellington’s definitive post Budget lunch

About this Event

Business Central, the home of Wellington Chamber of Commerce, is proud to host Finance Minister Hon. Nicola Willis for a premium post‑Budget lunch at Parliament on Thursday 11 June, following the delivery of Budget 2026.
In an election year and amid global uncertainty, Budget carries increased importance for business leaders and will provide clarity on the Government’s economic priorities and fiscal direction.
This exclusive lunch will bring together senior business leaders and decision‑makers for timely insight into what the Budget 2026 means in practice for business, investment, and economic growth.
Taking place shortly after the Budget, this is an opportunity to hear directly from the Finance Minister as its impact begins to take shape.
Following her address, guests will have the opportunity to ask questions to the Minister on the issues that matter most to their businesses.
Beyond the briefing, this exclusive event offers an opportunity to connect with leaders from across Wellington’s business community in a distinctive parliamentary setting. With both tables and individual seats available, it is an ideal occasion to host clients, strengthen networks, or bring senior members of your team to one of the capital’s key business events following the Budget.
Seats are limited and demand is expected to be strong. Secure your place early to avoid disappointment.
Table Bookings:
If you would like to book a table directly from the Wellington Chamber of Commerce (rather than through Eventbrite), please email .
Member tables: $1,080 + GST
Non-member tables: $1,350 + GST
Agenda:
From 11.30am - Guest arrival & networking
12.00pm - Guests seated for lunch
12.15pm - Lunch served
12.55pm - Finance Minister to speak
1.15pm - Q&A with audience
1.25pm - Dessert served
1.50pm - End concludes

Each Thursday, local performers take over this one-of-a-kind venue for a burst of live music to lift your day. Set inside the 160-year-old architectural gem that is Old St Paul’s, this is a peaceful, welcoming place to pause and enjoy something special.

events.id: French Film Festival - Jean Valjean-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Jean Valjean
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Victor Hugo’s masterpiece Les Misérables is one of the most beloved novels of the 19th century, and now the superb Grégory Gadebois has re-teamed with his Delicious writer/director Éric Besnard for this authentic and emotionally affecting retelling that charts the origin story of the tale’s legendary protagonist.

1815. Provence, France. In the peaceful village of Digne, rumour spreads that a dangerous man has entered the area; indeed, the hulking Valjean (Gadebois) has just been released from prison after nineteen years of hard labour. Branded a criminal and ostracised, Jean is repeatedly turned away.

Desperate for a bed, he knocks at the door of the house of Monseigneur Bienvenu (Bernard Campan), who lives simply with his ailing sister (Isabelle Carré) and dedicated – but suspicious – servant (Alexandra Lamy). Without judgement, Bienvenu invites him in. And on this crucial night, Jean will face a choice that will define his future…

With this superbly filmed exploration of justice, empathy and moral redemption, Besnard’s reflective and intimate drama – and Gadebois’ towering performance – bring new depth to Hugo’s hero, a spurned man transformed by kindness and compassion. Admirers of the source text and rich historical drama will not be disappointed by Jean Valjean.

Provence, France.

Northland Wing-Foil & Windsurf Surfari Holiday
Northland Wing-Foil & Windsurf Surfari Holiday is in Doubtless Bay of Northland, New Zealand. Our most popular Winterless North Surfari Trip!
Choose a three night or five night Northland Wing-Foil and Windsurf Holiday to the stunning Doubtless Bay and Karikari Peninsula. The Karikari Peninsula with its white sand beaches and crystal clear water provides opportunities to foil in any wind direction. Northland is known as the ‘winterless north’ with mild temperatures and reliable wind conditions to suit all wing foil and windsurf skill levels. events.id: Passing Through – Opening-26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Passing Through – Opening
events.venue: 26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
To move through a street is to collect its elements and digest. Its windows and weeds build into the landscape of a city, each alley fleshing out its brick and mortar into something more tangible - a pace, a rhythm of life. In Twentysix Gallery’s new group exhibition ‘Passing Through’ is the transitory place of ‘between’ - corners and a backyard, parked cars and sidewalks - are all subject to Jess Swney, Lewis Vivian Cosgrave, Jackson Harry and Hugo Van Dorsser’s material presence. A slow piecing together and remaking of cityscapes - abroad and at home.
Each of the four artists work closely with their respective materials in distinct processes, all of which allow for a certain sense of memory. Jess’s newest works draw on her time in Morocco on residency last year, where she learnt traditional weaving techniques from local artisans. Now, while on a new residency in Berlin, her tufted rug ‘paintings’ look back at this time - documenting geometric motifs of window guards she observed while there - domestic and architectural details, patterns and repeated forms, rendered by repetition and a labour intensive process.
For Lewis, images of the everyday Aotearoa are collected over time. Traces left behind, residue from the streets he has wandered are translated into fleeting images that are gradually softened, edited and reworked through a transfer process and paint. Repetition, erosion, and layering - embedding presence of maker into the work and finding the pulse of a certain sweet monotony. Streets walked as part of daily routine, the back garden in which you stare at each evening. Tender. A dazed memory.
Where there is softness, there is always the opposing to be found. Jackson’s bronze objects grounding the gallery in a feeling of nostalgia, of industry. Now based in Melbourne, the artist works with a reverence for traditional materials and technique. His sculptures of cars ‘frozen in place’ are both solid and weathered, eroded - coloured patina becoming a motif of passing time and an industrial quality that has been lost to years of change.
Speaking from the same vein of nostalgia, Hugo’s paintings are an exploration of the innocence of childhood, and the subconscious who still visits there. With a primary colour palette and stickfigure illustrations of ponytails and flying cars, his is a world that imagines the street in recollection. Separating us from the years of change that both swell streets then steal them away from a city, a village. Years that see our growing footprint, and build a lingering afterimage at the corner of our eye. An echo of our steps through a place, passing through.
Passing Through runs from 12 June – 4 July 2026, with an opening celebration on Thursday, 11 June at 5pm.

events.id: New Zealand and the heroic age of Antarctic exploration-National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: New Zealand and the heroic age of Antarctic exploration
events.venue: National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
A New Zealand perspective on early Antarctic expeditions.

Come along to this Friends of the Turnbull Library event for an opportunity to learn about the pivotal role New Zealanders played in early 20th century voyages to Antarctica.

In his book, 'South by South: New Zealand and the Heroic Age of Antarctic Exploration', Charles Ferrall tells the thrilling story of New Zealand’s key role in ‘the heroic age’ of Antarctic exploration.

In his research for the book, Charles explored a collection held in the Alexander Turnbull Library that was donated by Sir Joseph James Kinsey. Sir Joesph was a Christchurch businessman, collector, noted Antarctic enthusiast and New Zealand agent for the Antarctic expeditions of Sir Ernest Henry Shackleton and Captain Robert Falcon Scott.

events.id: Somatic Breathwork: Returning to Center-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Somatic Breathwork: Returning to Center
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Somatic Breathwork: Returning to Center
Returning to Center is a nervous-system-first somatic breathwork workshop designed to support winter resilience, helping you come home to your body, soften protective tension, and build a steadier baseline through the colder months.
“Returning to center” is the practice of reorienting to breath and body when stress pulls you outward - so you can meet winter with more warmth, clarity, and capacity.
Plus - there’s an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert after the event. All included in your ticket!
This workshop is perfect for:
High-functioning people running on tightness, adrenaline, or overthinking
Those feeling wired and tired, emotionally flat, or easily overwhelmed
Those living with chronic tension, persistent pain, or recurring symptoms that feel hard to explain (alongside appropriate medical support)
Anyone craving a gentler way to release without forcing catharsis
People wanting steadiness, boundaries, and self-trust in the body
Beginners looking for a welcoming space to explore somatic breathwork
Pre-class prep
Mats, blankets and pillows will be provided but bring anything extra to support your comfort
Eat a light meal 2 hours before class.
Hydrate well throughout the day.
Avoid alcohol and recreational substances for 24 hours.
Limit caffeine 4 hours prior.
Arrive 10 minutes early for intake.
If you have health conditions, please check with your provider.
Feel free to email Lynn at hi@lynofuro.co.nz if there’s anything you’d like to share before the session.
Contraindications and safety
Somatic breathwork may not be suitable for pregnancy; serious cardiovascular conditions; uncontrolled blood pressure; glaucoma or retinal detachment; epilepsy or seizure history; recent surgery or concussion; or severe psychiatric conditions/active psychosis. A conservative, nasal-only track is always available. This is not a substitute for medical care.
Aftercare
After the session, remember to:
Drink plenty of water
Eat nourishing food
Engage in simple movement or a short walk
Journal your insights
Get an early bedtime
Emotional waves may continue for 24 to 48 hours, so reach out if you need support.
Meet Lynn, your host
Lynn Tiang is an Integrative Somatic Coach, breathwork facilitator, and Reiki practitioner. Lynn supports people who are high-functioning on the outside but chronically tense, exhausted, or “stuck” on the inside—often after seasons of corporate burnout, chronic stress, or prolonged over-responsibility.
Lynn’s work blends three pillars:
Somatic breathwork to help regulate the nervous system, soften protective patterns, and restore a steadier baseline in the body
Reiki to support energetic clearing, heart coherence, and deep rest (in a gentle, consent-based way)
Life coaching to translate insight into grounded change—so what opens in ceremony becomes real in relationships, work, boundaries, and daily choices
Lynn’s approach is trauma-aware, practical, and spiritually spacious. The focus isn’t on forcing catharsis—it’s on creating enough safety for your system to unwind, receive support, and remember your natural capacity for calm, clarity, and connection.

Booking for Thursday is recommended!
For more details and to book please go here--
tinyurl.com/ThursdayOnPurposeEvents...............................................................................................
Make Thursdays your night to connect and take a break from your week. Events range from creative workshops, wellness courses to entertaining experiences. All within a down-to-earth and welcoming space. Each event wraps up with a plant-based dinner and dessert.
...............................................................................................

1/175 Vivian Street

events.id: Women's Circle: Threads of Thought-Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Women's Circle: Threads of Thought
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Women's Circle: Threads of Thought
A women’s circle for reflection and sharing, using prompts to guide honest conversation, connection, and deeper understanding.

About this Event


Women’s Circle: Thread of Thought
This Women’s Circle is a a space to gather, reflect, and explore the beauty and depth of the feminine experience.
The evening offers a slightly different approach to our usual format. You’re invited to arrive with something in your heart or on your mind - a thought, reflection, or question you’d like to place into the space. These will be written down, gathered into a basket, and passed around, becoming the prompts that guide our conversation.
From there, the circle opens for sharing. Each woman will have the opportunity to speak, reflect, or simply listen as different threads of thought unfold and weave through the room.
There is no pressure to perform or explain yourself here. Some may share openly, others may simply sit and listen. Both are welcome. The circle is held in a simple way that allows honesty, presence, and genuine expression to unfold naturally.
When women gather in this way, something quietly meaningful happens - stories are witnessed, voices are heard, and a sense of shared understanding begins to emerge.




A nourishing plant-based dinner and dessert will be shared afterwards, offering a warm and grounding way to close the evening together.




Meet Rasesvari, your host
Rasesvari (pronounced Rah-say-shwari) has always remembered feeling connected to healing, the inner world, and what helps people feel well and in alignment. Growing up, she spent many hours tucked away in libraries, reading about philosophy, plants, and anything that felt meaningful or life-giving.
Her path took a few turns away from the usual route, leading her to spend six years in an ashram setting focused on self-development and spiritual life. During that time, she explored meditation deeply and had space to focus on her own healing. This naturally opened the door to learning about Traditional Chinese healing, Ayurveda, sound healing, bioresonance, and a lot of self-study along the way.
Her loving aim is simple: to create spaces where people can finally feel comfortable in themselves again and and have room for their own growth and healing.



Agenda


🕑: 06:00 PM - 06:15 PM
Arrive to Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:15 PM - 07:15 PM
Women’s Circle: Thread of Thought
Host: Rasesvari

🕑: 07:15 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!

Enjoy a celebratory night, with special guest emcee Jenny-May Clarkson, as we acknowledge excellence across a range of categories and showcase the incredible depth of talent and community spirit within our region.

events.id: Midsommar-5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Midsommar
events.venue: 5 Park Road, Miramar, Wellington
events.long_description:
We are pleased to present a special screening of this highly disturbing Swedish-American folk horror film. Described by The Guardian as “an outrageous black-comic carnival of agony”, Midsommar is a masterfully made work that draws you unrelentingly deep into its beautifully — and horrifically — stylised pagan cult world.
Preceding the film will be a special food event (included in the ticket price), inspired by the intriguing array of dishes seen in the film, including Hårgan pies, berries, pickled delicacies, Swedish meringue dessert, and ‘hallucinogenic’ tea.
Be sure to dress for summer!
*R18: Graphic violence, sex scenes, drug use, and suicide.

events.id: Looking for a New Sport? Try Ultimate Frisbee-Ākau Tangi Sports Centre, 72 Kemp Street, Kilbirnie, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Looking for a New Sport? Try Ultimate Frisbee
events.venue: Ākau Tangi Sports Centre, 72 Kemp Street, Kilbirnie, Wellington
events.long_description:
Looking for something active and social to try on a Thursday night?

Come along to our free beginner Ultimate Frisbee taster sessions at Ākau Tangi Recreation Centre. These relaxed games are designed for people who have never played before or want a casual introduction to the sport.

Ultimate frisbee is a fun, fast-moving team sport played with a flying disc. It is easy to pick up, very social, and a great way to meet new people while getting some exercise.

Each Thursday night is a stand-alone session, so you can drop in whenever it suits you. You do not need to have attended earlier weeks, and there is no commitment to come every week.
These sessions focus on beginners and newer players, with guidance available to help people learn the basics, build confidence, and enjoy their first games.

Most people who attend are in their 20s and 30s, but anyone curious to try ultimate frisbee is welcome.

Bring a friend, try something new, and see why so many people love ultimate.

Event Details
What: Beginner Ultimate Frisbee “Taster” Sessions
When: Thursdays, 7:00pm
Dates: 16 April – 18 June
Where: Court 1, Ākau Tangi Recreation Centre, Kilbirnie, Wellington
Cost: Free
Who: Beginners and anyone curious about trying ultimate frisbee
What to bring: Comfortable sports clothes and running shoes

No experience needed. Just show up and join in.

events.id: Pub Sing-Boundary Tap & Kitchen, 3 Raumati Rd, Raumati-Event Finder
events.name: Pub Sing
events.venue: Boundary Tap & Kitchen, 3 Raumati Rd, Raumati
events.long_description:
Pub Sing - where the audience turns into the choir at the pub, with a live rock band,

No sheet music, no experience needed, and absolutely no solos.

Katy reveals three songs on the night (usually a crowd-pleaser, a ballad, and a banger), and even if you don’t know them, you’ll pick them up fast with colour-coded lyrics on screen.

You learn different harmony options, sing along with the band, and blend into a room of up to 80 voices, so no one’s listening to you; they’re singing with you.

There’s banter, laughs, a bit of silliness, and a whole lot of feel-good energy. It’s not karaoke (shudder), it’s everyone singing together just for the fun of it, and it’s the perfect end-of-week lift you needed.

*Please check the ticket booking link for updates or changes to dates*
*All ticket sales goes directly to the band*

Get ready because Wellington comedy show Silly Billies is about to break out of the fence screaming!

After taking a month off for the Comedy Festival – the silly billies are back for June, hosted by the oddball shepherds themselves, Mo Munn and Aaron James Douglas.

Silly Billies is the only alternative variety comedy open mic in Wellington and those who know, will tell you that every month is always unexpected and never boring.

Intended for those how have already started to learn Kizomba and wanting to learn about Tarraxinha.

Tarraxinha mostly danced on the spot, tarraxinha has very little in the way of steps and is focused on isolations and two bodies moving as one.

looking for more look out for 201 and 301

events.id: King Leo & Son-Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: King Leo & Son
events.venue: Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Undercurrent presents King Leo & Son!
After decades of recording and performing in the U.S. and Australasia as a solo artist and with his band alongside such luminaries as Bo Diddley, Pinetop Perkins and Doug Kershaw, King Leo is joined by his son Oscar, and at its heart, the resulting album is a testament to their deep musical connection. At once a collaboration and a portrait of the singer and multi-instrumentalist at his most personal, King Leo & Son showcases the duo's love for the music and their shared journey.
Since Oscar moved overseas from their adoptive home of Ōtepoti/Dunedin, Aotearoa/NZ, they've reunited regularly to spend time together performing. During an eventful tour in Winter 2025, King Leo and Oscar went into Surgery studios in Wellington/Pōneke, Aotearoa/NZ and recorded 10 songs live to tape, providing an intimate snapshot of their dynamic musical vision. Capturing King Leo's confident, easy voice against the backdrop of Oscar's sensitive playing, King Leo & Son is an invitation to take the third chair, settle in, and enjoy the conversation.
Thursday June 11
Doors 7pm
Music 7:30pm
$20
Tickets available online and on the door!
https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../King-Leo-And-Son.utr
King Leo:
https://www.facebook.com/KingsyDunedin
, https://www.instagram.com/kingleoaotearoa/

events.id: Fight Club-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Fight Club
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Four comedians face off in one of the most brutal and radical fighting styles: Structured Debate. Welcome to Fight Club!

A gladiator-style comedy showdown where hot takes replace punches and the audience is judge, jury, and executioner.
Four comics battle across three rounds of ruthless, ridiculous debate. Two rounds in the first half, a desperate losers’ bout for the coveted booby prize, then a final face-off between the victors. The ultimate winner earns a spot in the Grand Final in November and a shot at the Fight Club belt.

They’ll argue life’s most important questions, like “Which comic would be the best date to your ex’s wedding?” and “Who could talk their way out of a speeding ticket?” No facts, no data, no mercy. Expect sharp jokes, fake confidence, real egos, audience voting, and maximum tomfoolery.

This isn’t just comedy debate, it’s trial by charisma.

events.id: Lunchtime Awareness through Movement Lessons-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lunchtime Awareness through Movement Lessons
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Want to reduce pain and tension, improve posture, in a friendly and supportive atmosphere ?
These lessons use principles of neuroplasticity, biomechanics, and human development, to help you improve long-term habits of movement and posture.
They are based on the pioneering work of physicist and engineer Dr. Moshe Feldenkrais. He developed his unique system to help manage his own knee injuries. His method is now used world wide.

First class free.
Sue 0274667123

events.id: Passing Through – Group Show-26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Passing Through – Group Show
events.venue: 26 Constable St, Newtown, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
To move through a street is to collect its elements and digest. Its windows and weeds build into the landscape of a city, each alley fleshing out its brick and mortar into something more tangible - a pace, a rhythm of life. In Twentysix Gallery’s new group exhibition ‘Passing Through’ is the transitory place of ‘between’ - corners and a backyard, parked cars and sidewalks - are all subject to Jess Swney, Lewis Vivian Cosgrave, Jackson Harry and Hugo Van Dorsser’s material presence. A slow piecing together and remaking of cityscapes - abroad and at home.
Each of the four artists work closely with their respective materials in distinct processes, all of which allow for a certain sense of memory. Jess’s newest works draw on her time in Morocco on residency last year, where she learnt traditional weaving techniques from local artisans. Now, while on a new residency in Berlin, her tufted rug ‘paintings’ look back at this time - documenting geometric motifs of window guards she observed while there - domestic and architectural details, patterns and repeated forms, rendered by repetition and a labour intensive process.
For Lewis, images of the everyday Aotearoa are collected over time. Traces left behind, residue from the streets he has wandered are translated into fleeting images that are gradually softened, edited and reworked through a transfer process and paint. Repetition, erosion, and layering - embedding presence of maker into the work and finding the pulse of a certain sweet monotony. Streets walked as part of daily routine, the back garden in which you stare at each evening. Tender. A dazed memory.
Where there is softness, there is always the opposing to be found. Jackson’s bronze objects grounding the gallery in a feeling of nostalgia, of industry. Now based in Melbourne, the artist works with a reverence for traditional materials and technique. His sculptures of cars ‘frozen in place’ are both solid and weathered, eroded - coloured patina becoming a motif of passing time and an industrial quality that has been lost to years of change.
Speaking from the same vein of nostalgia, Hugo’s paintings are an exploration of the innocence of childhood, and the subconscious who still visits there. With a primary colour palette and stickfigure illustrations of ponytails and flying cars, his is a world that imagines the street in recollection. Separating us from the years of change that both swell streets then steal them away from a city, a village. Years that see our growing footprint, and build a lingering afterimage at the corner of our eye. An echo of our steps through a place, passing through.
Passing Through runs from 12 June – 4 July 2026, with an opening celebration on Thursday, 11 June at 5pm.
Opening Hours:
Thursday 12pm - 6pm
Friday 12pm - 6pm
Saturday 10am - 4pm
Or by appointment

Each of the four artists work closely with their respective materials in distinct processes, all of which allow for a certain sense of memory.
His sculptures of cars ‘frozen in place’ are both solid and weathered, eroded - coloured patina becoming a motif of passing time and an industrial quality that has been lost to years of change.
An echo of our steps through a place, passing through.

events.id: The Seasonal Forecast Series: Winter-264A Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: The Seasonal Forecast Series: Winter
events.venue: 264A Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Seasonal Forecast Series continues with Winter.
A four-part exploration of ingredients at their peak, highlighting the flavours & textures that capture the distinct character of Autumn, Winter, Spring & Summer.
For our Winter release, we've brewed a Belgian Stout with chocolate, cherry & rye. Dark chocolate & cocoa create a fuller, rounded mouthfeel, balanced by subtle cherry aromatics that help dry the finish. Rye contributes a restrained bready depth, layered with nutty biscuit undertones.
We'll also have Autumn's Rhubarb Farmhouse Ale pouring alongside it, offering a chance to revisit the bright, tart character of the season before.
Join us from midday on Friday 12 June for the next chapter in The Seasonal Forecast Series.

events.id: 5 Course Dinner at Le Cordon Bleu | 12 JUNE 2026-Level 2, 52 Cuba Street, 6011, NZ,52 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: 5 Course Dinner at Le Cordon Bleu | 12 JUNE 2026
events.venue: Level 2, 52 Cuba Street, 6011, NZ,52 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
5 Course Dinner at Le Cordon Bleu | 12 JUNE 2026
This 5-Course dinner expertly prepared by our Bachelor of Culinary Arts and Business students is not to be missed.

Date: Friday 12th June
Price: $99 per person
Booking Times: 5:30pm | 6:00pm | 6:30pm | 7:00pm

Matched Wine Option: If you would like to add matched wines to your dining experience you can do so on the day by paying an additional charge. Alternatively, we have a selection of wines to purchase by the glass or bottle as well as beers and soft drinks. We are a fully licensed restaurant and do not offer BYO.

Dietary Requirements: Our menu is set and we are unable to make any menu alterations or cater to any specific dietary requirements (including religious diets) or food allergies. We apologise for any inconvenience this may cause.
The Menu: will not be released before the event. Please be aware that the menu will include fish, meat, and shellfish components.

Tickets are allocated for specific seating times, please ensure you arrive promptly at the time indicated on your ticket.
The estimated time to enjoy the full Le Cordon Bleu 5 course dining experience is 2 hours. We invite you to sit back, relax and enjoy our students' creations.

What is Brasserie Le Cordon Bleu?
Brasserie Le Cordon Bleu is our training restaurant where our students get practical experience in a real restaurant environment - all under the expert guidance of their chef lecturers. In the kitchen, we have students from our Diplôme de Cuisine programme. In the dining room we have our Bachelor of Culinary Arts and Business students serving you and putting their theory skills into practice.

Brasserie & High Tea Refund Policy
• No refunds are provided unless the event is cancelled by Le Cordon Bleu NZ.
• Customers can arrange for someone else to take their place.

events.name: Select Online Art Auction | Launch Event & Viewing — Wellington
events.venue: 23 Marion St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Join us at Webb's Wellington Gallery to view the Select art auction catalogue, on view from 10—13 June with the launch event taking place on Friday 12 June from 5.30pm.
Highlights currently on view at our Te Aro gallery include a rare Waikato landscape by Margo Phillips, works by artist sisters Star and Aroha Gossage, a monumental expressionist canvas by Emily Karaka, and an impressive pair of decorated amphora screenprints by Shane Cotton. These wall-mounted works are accompanied by free-standing sculptures by Bronwynne Cornish, Guy Ngan, Greer Twiss, and Joe Ngallamella.
The wider catalogue includes a significant grouping of Māori artists across generations, early and conceptual works, and many other collectable artworks by emerging, topical, and established artists from Aotearoa and beyond.

https://events.humanitix.com/yoga-sound-journey-with-shared-vegetarian-food

🌸 Yoga & Sound Bath with Shared Kai 🌸
✨ Reconnect, Restore, and Nourish ✨
🌸 Join us in the sanctuary of our beautiful home in Karori for a unique and profound healing journey. This immersion is designed to release stress, anxiety, and physical tension, guiding you back to a state of deep peace through breathwork, sound healing, meditation, and restorative yoga.
🌸 Experience a gentle yoga flow with Pam, specifically curated to assist in the release of stuck energy and emotions. Together, we work to heal core emotional wounds, trauma, and self-sabotaging patterns in a safe, held space.
🌸 Transition into an intuitive Sound Journey Integration. Using the resonant frequencies of crystal singing bowls, medicine drums, Koshi chimes, and more, we support the release of excess energy while opening you to powerful insights and profound relaxation.
🌸 We work with the sacred plant medicines of Ceremonial Cacao and Blue Lotus. These gentle allies support the reconnection to your sacred heart, relaxing the mind and allowing you to go deeper into your healing and inner guidance.
🌸 To ground our journey, we gather for shared vegetarian Kai and stories around the fire. It is a time for community, connection, and integration within the warmth of our home.
✨ The Benefits of Our Journey ✨
🌸 Release suppressed emotions and heal core emotional wounds
🌸 Reduce stress and anxiety while regulating your nervous system
🌸 Experience deep inner peace, balance, and mental clarity
🌸 Release limiting beliefs, habits, and unhealthy patterns
🌸 Increase your natural energy levels and motivation
🌸 Reconnect deeply with your authentic self
✨ Event Details ✨
🌸 Location: Our beautiful home sanctuary in Karori
🌸 Investment: $45 per person
🌸 Intimate Setting: Strictly limited to seven spaces
🌸 What to Bring: A yoga mat, water bottle, and a comfortable blanket
🌸 Booking: Essential—ticket details to follow soon
✨ About Your Hosts ✨
🌸 Crystal: A classically trained pianist, Music Therapist, Reiki Master, and Sound Healing Practitioner with over 40 years of experience. Trained in both London and New Zealand, she is a dedicated mama of three beautiful daughters.
🌸 Pam: Our experienced Yoga Teacher and author. An ex-school teacher, Pam has been practicing and teaching the art of yoga for many years, bringing heart and wisdom to every stretch.
✨ We look forward to sharing this sacred fire and space with you. ✨

events.id: French Film Festival - Maigret and the Dead Lover-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Maigret and the Dead Lover
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Following the break-out success of 2025 hit The Stolen Painting, acclaimed writer/director Pascal Bonitzer brings his unique perspective and panache to Simenon’s cherished Parisian detective - a French counterpart to Agatha Christie’s Poirot - as he attempts to solve the murder of a diplomat with a long-held secret.

Inspector Maigret (four-time César nominee Denis Podalydès, in a tremendous performance) is urgently summoned to the Quai d’Orsay: a former ambassador has been found shot dead by his long-serving housekeeper (Anne Alvaro, The Taste of Others). But rather than contact the police, she has followed diplomatic protocols by first informing the Ministry of Foreign Affairs. Forewarned that he will find a series of salacious letters - and cautioned to be discrete - Maigret heads to the victim’s opulent apartment. He finds no forensic evidence at the crime scene, but certainly, the letters prove startling - secret, decades-spanning correspondence between the ambassador and the Princess of Vuynes, whose own husband, by strange coincidence, has also just died…

Bonitzer, renowned for an intuitive and empathetic focus, finds a perfect match with a protagonist who relies on quiet observation. He delivers an absorbing, wholly compelling mystery that also serves as a thoughtful examination of modern life’s key concerns: hierarchy, aging, and the very different worlds that money creates. Best of all, the twist ending is… a killer.

events.id: FUJIFILM and WAIRARAPA DARK SKY | Astro Experience-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: FUJIFILM and WAIRARAPA DARK SKY | Astro Experience
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for an evening under the stars as we launch the Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve Photo Competition, a collaboration with Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve and Fujifilm New Zealand.
The Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve is one of the most accessible dark sky locations in the lower North Island, making it an amazing place for people to experience and photograph the night sky without needing to travel too far. This event is designed to inspire more people to get out there, enjoy the reserve, and explore what is possible with a camera under dark skies.
Whether you are completely new to astrophotography or already have some experience, this evening is a chance to learn, shoot, and connect with others in a beautiful and easily reachable dark sky location.
