events.id: FUJIFILM and WAIRARAPA DARK SKY | Astro Experience-Pae Tū Mōkai O Tauira Whare,23 North Soldiers Settlement Road, Featherston, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: FUJIFILM and WAIRARAPA DARK SKY | Astro Experience
events.venue: Pae Tū Mōkai O Tauira Whare,23 North Soldiers Settlement Road, Featherston, New Zealand
events.long_description:
FUJIFILM and WAIRARAPA DARK SKY | Astro Experience
Discover the wonders of Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve and learn astrophotography with Fujifilm experts

About this Event


Rock Arch, Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve
Photo Credit: Keith Molly




Nau mai, Welcome!

Join us for an evening under the stars as we launch the Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve Photo Competition, a collaboration with Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve and Fujifilm New Zealand.
The Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve is one of the most accessible dark sky locations in the lower North Island, making it an amazing place for people to experience and photograph the night sky without needing to travel too far. This event is designed to inspire more people to get out there, enjoy the reserve, and explore what is possible with a camera under dark skies.
Whether you are completely new to astrophotography or already have some experience, this evening is a chance to learn, shoot, and connect with others in a beautiful and easily reachable dark sky location.


Your evening
6:30 – 7:30pm | Discovery talk at Pae Tū Mōkai O Tauira Whare presented by Charlotte Harding (Dark Sky Reserve Project Coordinator), Keith Molloy and JM Beltran (Photographers)
7:30 – 8:00pm | Transition to Lake Wairarapa (optional)
8:00 – 9:30pm | Hands-on night photography session by the lake, with hot drinks available for participants (optional, additional ticket required, limited to 30 participants)


Who this is for
Anyone wanting to discover and learn more about the Wairarapa Dark Sky Reserve
People curious about astrophotography
Beginners wanting to learn the basics
Intermediate photographers wanting expert tips
Fujifilm users and anyone interested in night photography


Fujifilm gear support
Fujifilm New Zealand will provide selected gear to touch and try during the event.


What to bring
For the outdoor session, attendees should bring:
Camera
Warm clothing
Headlamp or torch
Optional: Empty memory card (Fujifilm gear will be available try during this session, bring your memory card to take home your images!)
Optional: Tripod


Weather
This event is weather-dependent.
Back-up date: Saturday 13 June 2026
If the weather is not suitable, registered attendees will be notified of any changes.




Pūtangirua Pinnacles, Dark Sky Reserve
Photo credit: JM Beltran


Agenda


🕑: 06:30 PM - 07:30 PM
Discovery talk at Pae Tū Mōkai O Tauira Whare

🕑: 08:00 PM - 09:30 PM
Lakeside photography session (additional)

events.name: Cantina Comedy
events.venue: Sunday Cantina,1 Renown Rd, Raumati South, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Cantina Comedy
Laugh away the winter cold on 10th July.

Join us at Sunday Cantina in Raumati from 7pm for a stand-up showcase bringing some heat to a cold winter night.

Twelve comedians. Five minutes each. Rapid-fire punchlines, bold new material, and just enough chaos to keep things interesting. If one joke doesn’t land, don’t worry — another comic is stepping up straight after.

Expect sharp one-liners, gloriously awkward confessions, and stories that probably should’ve stayed in the group chat. It’s fast, it’s fun, and it’s the perfect Thursday night excuse to get out of the house.

Grab your tickets, your friends, a cold drink, a warm meal and settle in for a night of laughs you'll be talking about for a month.

Tickets are just $10 — cheaper than a round of drinks and guaranteed to last longer.

events.id: Glow In the Dark Titiwai Tours - New Zealand Glow Worms-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Glow In the Dark Titiwai Tours - New Zealand Glow Worms
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A fascinating tour into the world of titiwai, New Zealand's glow worms, in Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā. This outdoor night-time tour takes about an hour, and all are welcome to join. If bringing children, we find it most suitable for those aged 7 years and older.
Meet at the Founders’ entrance to Wellington Botanic Garden ki Paekākā on Glenmore Street. Please bring your ticket, a torch, and an umbrella if it’s raining. We'll see you there!
This tour is operated by the Friends of Wellington Botanic Garden.
Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy
• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.
• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.
• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.
For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

events.id: WELLINGTON, NZ: Modern Raqs Sharqi Technique & Combinations Workshop-Thistle Hall Community Venue,293 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: WELLINGTON, NZ: Modern Raqs Sharqi Technique & Combinations Workshop
events.venue: Thistle Hall Community Venue,293 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
WELLINGTON, NZ: Modern Raqs Sharqi Technique & Combinations Workshop
Siobhan is a multi-award winning raqs sharqi (“belly dance”) artist, and the founder of Greenstone Dance Arts, based in the Netherlands. Her love of music is equaled only by her love of movement, making belly dance her perfect match. Her joyful movement and 1000-watt smile captivate audiences across the globe.

She has been invited to teach and perform across North America, Europe, and Oceania, and she is excited to be back in New Zealand to offer this one-off workshop in Wellington!

In this workshop, you'll learn some of Siobhan Camille's signature technique, and several combinations to take away and put into your own practice and choreography.

Don't miss out - this is Siobhan Camille's first time teaching in the North Island of New Zealand in over four years.

$45 NZD before June 1
$55 NZD thereafter

Please arrive on time to the Thistle Hall Community Venue for a 7pm start on Friday June 12. The address is:
Thistle Hall Community Venue
293 Cuba Street, Wellington, Aotearoa, NZ.

$45 NZD before June 1 $55 NZD thereafter Please arrive on time to the Thistle Hall Community Venue for a 7pm start on Friday June 12.

events.id: Big Sigh | Thomas Arbor-272 Taranaki St, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Big Sigh | Thomas Arbor
events.venue: 272 Taranaki St, Mount Cook, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kirikiriroa dream-folk duo joined by local collaborator Thomas Arbor.
-
Big Sigh is the dream-folk duo of Kirikiriroa’s Rachel Hope Peary and Nicholas Joseph Walsh (Deerpark) who are touring to celebrate the release of their debut EP, Tether (Sunreturn). The EP captures that first rush of creative momentum between the pair - a collection of the earliest songs born from a partnership that felt from the outset instinctive and inevitable. A beautiful blend of Intertwined harmonies, finger-picked acoustic guitar and the steady drone of harmonium.
-
Thomas Arbor is a multimedia composer, producer and audio engineer. In live performances he searches for sublime states, led by melody through lush, full-spectrum soundscapes of synth, samples and voice. Drawing on the organic complexity of nature using field recordings he sculpts them into evolving electronic forms grounded in deep harmonic resonance.

$20 via UTR
$25 door sales ($15 unwaged)
Special thanks to CNZ for supporting Pyramid Club's programme

events.id: Rum Jungle ‘Marginalia’ AU/NZ Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Rum Jungle ‘Marginalia’ AU/NZ Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Australian indie-rock outfit Rum Jungle return to Australia and New Zealand following a breakout few years that has seen the band sell out shows across the UK, Europe and North America while quietly building a global fanbase. Known for their infectious guitar hooks, restless energy and emotionally honest songwriting, Rum Jungle have become one of Australia’s most exciting live bands.
The tour arrives alongside new music from their forthcoming sophomore album Marginalia, including recent singles “Coal Dust”, “What’s Next” and the introspective new track “Lowlife”. Written during a period of reflection, the new material sees the band expanding their sound while retaining the sharp melodies and raw immediacy that first drew fans in.
Renowned for their electric live shows, Rum Jungle’s performances blur the line between indie rock urgency and sing-along pop instinct, the kind of gig where the room moves as one. With momentum building internationally and a new chapter of music underway, these shows mark the band’s biggest headline run across Australia and New Zealand to date.
San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.name: Greg Larsen: Unrelenting Ultra-Violence
events.venue: The Fringe Bar,26-32 Allen Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Greg Larsen: Unrelenting Ultra-Violence
Greg Larsen makes his long awaited return to Wellington!

About this Event

Hello I am Greg and I weigh more than 99 percent of all performers, so in terms of dollars spent per kilo you are already saving huge amounts by coming to see me.

I've won awards and stuff but who cares. What matters is that I am big and funny and I want to spend time with you.

In any case I hope you buy a ticket and watch me grub around and get mucky. If you are a real estate agent or you own one of those Ford Raptors then don't come. You are banned.

'Cuttingly perceptive sharp humour with hilarious results. A comic treat' - Glam Adelaide (2026)
'Definitely R Rated, definitely recommend' - The Age (2026)
'Not a peodophile.' Chortle (UK)

https://events.humanitix.com/little-monsters-june/tickets
We love techno.
We're back out from under the bed again, and as the solstice nears we realise how much we love these long, dark nights!
To celebrate we've summoned our friend VNESS in from the deep Auckland underground. She’s bringing her driving techno to attract all you little monsters into the darkness of the Afters. VNESS will be supported by BLVIR w FORTUNA and HALO, so dig out your darkest finery, it’s time to creep, slither, and crawl in search of bass.
See you in the shadows.
‘Listen to them, the children of the night. What music they make!’ (Stoker, 1897, Chapter 3).

Child Policy: Children under 3 years will be admitted free providing they do not occupy a seat (Sit On Lap Policy).

Te Whanganui-a-Tara comes alive with haka as 13 kapa take the stage, all vying for one of four coveted spots at Te Matatini 2027.

Witness the power, precision, and passion of our region's finest kapa haka as they compete for a place on the national stage. This is more than a competition - it is a celebration of whakapapa, reo, tikanga, and the enduring strength of kapa haka.

From spine-tingling moteatea to electrifying haka, this is kapa haka at its highest level - where every movement, every note, and every moment counts.

events.id: Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forest-Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Family Shiny - Akatarawa Forest
events.venue: Wellington
events.long_description:
Registrations open NOW
What to expect
This is a “Family Shiny” trip into the Akatarawa Forest.
Non members are welcome to join us but must register to obtain details of start time and location.
Vehicles must have a low ratio gearbox and must not be a 4wd car, station wagon or van, and if a soft top must have a roll cage.
Visitor Registration- $20.00 per driver
There is no trip fee for this trip however non-members pay a “Day Membership Fee” of $20. This is payable in Cash to the Trip Leader on the day.
The trip should be relatively easy as it needs to cater for people just starting out and all types of 4wd vehicles – but a good way to see if you want to get into 4wding and a chance to meet some of our club members (if you are unsure if your vehicle is suitable just ask).
No Dogs Allowed sorry
Once you have completed 3 Family Shinys you can join the club but can only participate in Family Shiny or Touring Class trips. Once you have also done a Training Day and had your vehicle inspected by one of our approved inspectors you can come along on our other more adventurous trips. To find out more on how to join our club go to https://ccvcwellington.wildapricot.org/page-1725861.
Like our Facebook page to find out about more trips and club nights.

events.id: Acing The Interview-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Acing The Interview
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Acing The Interview
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
People have skills which they want to offer to employers, however, low confidence and anxiety combined with a lack of interview experience results in them not gaining employment.
'Acing The Interview' is a 3-module one day workshop that provides practical, cutting-edge tools and techniques to prepare participants to 'Ace the Interview'.
The 3 modules are:
1. Resourceful You - Think
2. Managing You - Feel
3. Acing the Interview - Do
Explore how to manage your thoughts under pressure and control your critical voice. Understand how to manage anxiety and stress. Demonstrate your interview skills and use body language and well-formed responses to create a powerful and positive first impression.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Manage your thoughts and critical voice under pressure
-  Visualise and mentally rehearse for success
-  Recognise your emotional reactions and manage your emotions under pressure
-  Utilise techniques to manage anxiety
-  Demonstrate interview skills and deliver well-formed responses to questions
-  Understand the STAR model and other communication techniques and how and when to use them
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Bike Maintenance for Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bike Maintenance for Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bicycle Maintenance for Beginners
Have you recently started riding a bicycle and want to make sure you can take care of it properly? Or have you been riding for ages, but have always been scared to fix anything yourself?
Are you unsure of how to pump your tyres, or how much oil your chain needs? Find yourself fumbling for words when trying to describe that clunking sound to the guy in the bike shop?
This one day course lays bare the nuts and bolts of bicycle mechanics for absolute beginners, as well as those with a little experience. Through practical experience working with your own bicycle, you will find out what needs to be done to keep it running smoothly. We’ll cover what to look out for when things start to wear out, and how to check for problems, so you can be confident your bicycle is safe to ride. You will also learn how to fix some of the most common mechanical problems, and learn about the correct terminology and tools to use.
Those who feel intimidated in bike shops are especially welcome in this course. No question is a stupid question!
Bringing a bike to work on is essential, preferably one that you are riding regularly, because the best way to learn is by building familiarity with your own ride. E-bikes are welcome, although the extra weight can mean they are more challenging to work on. Regardless of whether your bike is in pristine condition, or desperately in need of TLC, it is welcome on the course – bikes showing a bit more wear can be useful for the learning of the whole group.
Bring your pushbike along on the day.  If you have the following, please bring them as well:  A multi-tool and pump; chain oil and rag; lightweight close-fitting gardening or rubber gloves.
Note: A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
(Please note this course is not exhaustive and is not a professional training course).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Name most parts of a bicycle correctly
- Perform a quick safety check on any bicycle
- Fix a flat tyre
- Adjust your bike to fit your body
- Oil and clean your chain
- Identify and fix some common brake problems
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Communicate Across Generations: Bridge the Divide-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Communicate Across Generations: Bridge the Divide
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Communicate Across Generations: Bridge the Divide
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
 Discover why different generations prioritise different things in their interactions, from family group chats and face-to-face conversations through to workplace attitudes. Learn to decode the nuances, bridge the divides, and foster more effective communication in your daily life.
Learning Outcomes
Deconstruct Communication Styles: You will be able to identify and describe the typical communication styles, preferences, and values of different generations, including Baby Boomers, Generation X, Millennials, and Generation Z.
Mitigate Conflict: You will learn to recognise and resolve common intergenerational communication conflicts by understanding their root causes and applying effective conflict resolution strategies.
Improve Collaboration: You will develop practical skills to adapt your communication approach to better engage with people from different generations, leading to enhanced understanding and connection.
Foster Inclusivity: You will be able to appreciate and leverage the unique perspectives and strengths that each generation brings to the table, creating more inclusive environments in your relationships.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks, and pen and paper to take notes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Masalas & Breads From India-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Cooking: Masalas & Breads From India
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Masala is the Indian word for curry. Spend the day making a range of curries both from scratch and by adapting spice packets, for family meals and special occasions. All ingredients provided.

Take a day and create a range of delicious and different Indian masalas, using both Manjula’s tried-and-true recipes but also some of the new spice mixes now available in Indian shops. You’ll also learn how to make a variety of breads (naan, roti and parathas) to serve with the curries.

By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
- Create several different masalas (curries)
- Make tasty Indian bread accompaniments
- Adapt ready-made spice mixes to enhance the flavours
- Use a range a different Indian spices with more confidence

All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dishcloth, tea towels and containers to take any leftovers home.

Presented by Manjula Patel.

events.id: Free Comic Book Day - Saturday 13 June-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Free Comic Book Day - Saturday 13 June
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Celebrate international Free Comic Book Day at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library (we are holding our celebrations a little later than everyone else, on Saturday 13 June). To celebrate our Comic Book Day, we have a mega giveaway, thousands of free comics to suit all ages and tastes will be available, there will be something for everyone.
Just pop along to Ngā Paiaka | the Bleachers from 9.30am and pick up a free comic.
Due to demand, we have a limit of two comics per enthusiast please.
Meet the comic writers and artists:
Meet one of your favourite local comics / graphic artists, Sam Orchard, Sarah Laing and Jem Yoshioka as they draw and talk about their work in these fabulous informal half hour demonstrations.
10am - 11.30 am, at Ngā Paiaka | the Bleachers
Sarah Laing is a multi-award-winning Wellington-based writer and illustrator her works include her graphic memoir Mansfield and Me, her comic anthology Let Me Be Frank and recently Sylvia and the Birds.
Jem Yoshioka, an illustrator and comic artist living in Wellington, New Zealand. Jem deftly weaving words and pictures together, Jem’s comics tell evocative and emotional stories with themes of belonging, place, and heritage. Jem’s works include the webcomic Circuits and Veins which has attracted a large following on Webtoon.
Sam Orchard is the Assistant Curator for the Cartoon and Comics Archive at the Alexander Turnbull Library. He is a comic artist and illustrator, who has been involved in community and youth development campaigns and practices for over a decade.
Drawing competition:
9.30- 12.30 at Ngā Paiaka | the Bleachers
Head over to our drawing competition tables and create your own work of art! Draw us your favourite character from a book, movie TV show – or perhaps a character from your own imagination. There’s a panoply of awesome prizes up for grabs.
Prize categories are:
Age 8 and under,
Age 9-13,
Age 14+
Winners to be announced later in the week.

Meet one of your favourite local comics / graphic artists, Sam Orchard, Sarah Laing and Jem Yoshioka as they draw and talk about their work in these fabulous informal half hour demonstrations.

10am - 11.30 am, at Ngā Paiaka | the Bleachers

Sarah Laing is a multi-award-winning Wellington-based writer and illustrator her works include her graphic memoir Mansfield and Me, her comic anthology Let Me Be Frank and recently Sylvia and the Birds.

Jem Yoshioka, an illustrator and comic artist living in Wellington, New Zealand. Jem deftly weaving words and pictures together, Jem’s comics tell evocative and emotional stories with themes of belonging, place, and heritage. Jem’s works include the webcomic Circuits and Veins which has attracted a large following on Webtoon.

Sam Orchard is the Assistant Curator for the Cartoon and Comics Archive at the Alexander Turnbull Library. He is a comic artist and illustrator, who has been involved in community and youth development campaigns and practices for over a decade.

Drawing competition:

9.30- 12.30 at Ngā Paiaka | the Bleachers

Head over to our drawing competition tables and create your own work of art! Draw us your favourite character from a book, movie TV show – or perhaps a character from your own imagination. There’s a panoply of awesome prizes up for grabs.
Prize categories are:

Age 8 and under,
Age 9-13,
Age 14+
Winners to be announced later in the week.

Meet the comic writers and artists: Meet one of your favourite local comics / graphic artists, Sam Orchard, Sarah Laing and Jem Yoshioka as they draw and talk about their work in these fabulous informal half hour demonstrations.
Jem Yoshioka, an illustrator and comic artist living in Wellington, New Zealand.
Jem deftly weaving words and pictures together, Jem’s comics tell evocative and emotional stories with themes of belonging, place, and heritage.

events.id: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Get the Job: Strategy, Skills & Self-Awareness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the Job: Strategy, Skills and Self-Awareness
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Equip yourself with the tools and strategies to thrive in today’s competitive job market. This course is designed to help job seekers at all stages across any industry. Whether you're applying for your first job, changing careers, or re-entering the workforce, this course is relevant for everyone because it requires you to self-reflect on your own journey. You’ll learn to reflect deeply on your goals and strengths while building practical skills to stand out from the crowd.
 You’ll leave with a set of practical tools, clear strategies, and a better understanding of what employers want, so you can step into your next opportunity with confidence.
 You’ll develop a tailored job search strategy that works for you. One that has purpose, highlights your transferable skills, sharpens your professional image, and helps you connect authentically with hiring managers. From crafting a digital-ready CV and setting job alerts, to working with recruiters and networking effectively, you’ll gain tools and insights to streamline the process.
 The course also addresses the emotional journey of job hunting. Learn to manage time and energy, stay adaptable, and build resilience as you face the highs and lows of searching for the right opportunity. You’ll explore how emotional intelligence, digital literacy, and critical thinking play essential roles in first impressions, interviews, and long-term career decisions.
 By the end of this course, you’ll be able to:
Identify and articulate your transferable skills.
Apply both emotional and intellectual strategies to mentor and coach yourself from within.
Develop a job search strategy that aligns with your goals.
Build confidence in presenting yourself professionally and authentically.
Develop a confident, authentic approach to your personal branding and professional networking.
Create and manage a job search plan using shortcuts like job alerts, recruitment agents, and focussed applications.
Maintain a polished digital presence and decide whether the use of professional platforms are for you (LinkedIn).
Understand how to navigate the emotional demands of job hunting with self-compassion, motivation, and adaptability.
Build and maintain a network of trusted referees who reflect your work ethic and potential.
 Whether you're starting from scratch or just need a refresh, this course offers a structured, encouraging path forward to help you land your next opportunity.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your session, if applicable.
 Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Like to be able to give massages to friends and family with a bit more knowledge and confidence? Join this introduction to the basics of massage, held over two sessions.

2 weeks, Sat 9:30 AM – 1:00 PM
Presented by Sue Field.

events.name: Photo Fundraiser
events.venue: 10 Davis St, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington City Rudolf Steiner Kindergarten is running a Photo Fundraiser on Saturday 13 June, and we’d love your support.
We’ve organised Dream Momentz — amazing Wellington‑based professional photographers — to come into the kindergarten for a special day of family and individual portraits. It’s a brilliant way to get beautiful photos and help our little charity‑run kindergarten at the same time.
Here’s what you get for just a $15 booking fee:
• One free professionally edited, high‑resolution digital image
• A free 15‑minute family photo shoot
• Access to very affordable photo packages starting from $69 if you’d like more images
• Extended whānau welcome — grandparents, cousins, aunties, uncles… everyone can join in. No restrictions.
Our children grow so quickly — those cheeky smiles, the way they play, the little expressions we adore… they change before we know it. This is such a lovely chance to capture those moments and keep them forever.
To book your timeslot get in touch by phone (04) 499 7394 or email arnica@wellingtonsteinerkindy.nz
Thanks for your support!

events.id: WOMP 3D Digital Design - An Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: WOMP 3D Digital Design - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
WOMP 3D Digital Design - An Introduction
WOMP is user-friendly design-software. Saving you both time and unnecessary stress, WOMP allows your creativity to shine as you create 3D designs.
Whether you want to design toys, figures, logos, product mock-ups, jewelry or other 3D things - then WOMP makes this much more accessible.
Held in a computer lab, this one-day course will explore WOMP and you will be able to use it to create your own 3D designs. You will also get information on where you can send your designs to have them 3D printed.
Please bring an idea of what you would like to work on during the day, and pen and paper to take notes.
Note –  You will be using the free version of WOMP for this workshop - it also has affordable subscription options if you choose to move beyond the free version.
By the end of this workshops you will:
Discovered how WOMP simplifies the 3D Design process
Be able to describe why WOMP is a good software choice
Be able to use WOMP for making designs
Know where you can send your designs to be manufactured
Note: All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Drypoint Printmaking Workshop-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Drypoint Printmaking Workshop
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Explore drypoint printmaking with experienced local print maker Kirsty White.

In this workshop you will

- Learn to etch a polycarbonate plate
- Use steel needle point tools and wire brushes to create line and texture that will hold ink.
- learn to ink up your plate
- use mull cloth to achieve desired tone
- print on damp printmaking paper
- run this print through a second stage using the relief process of inking up and printing from a natural textured block

events.venue: Te Whaea: National Dance and Drama Centre, 11 Hutchison Road, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
This free two-day workshop offers theatre training for young people aged 15-25 years.

Suitable for all levels of experience, this workshop is led by two professional teaching artists from the Massive teaching team, Stef Fink & Tane Pakeke-Patterson.

You will learn a wide range of foundational theatre skills and techniques, and explore different ways of making theatre pieces using your own ideas and stories.

The workshop runs from 10am to 4pm each day. Please arrive no later than 9.50am, so you are ready begin the workshop at 10am. The doors will be open from 9.30am so you're welcome to come early and join us for a game of four square beforehand.

Bookings are essential as this workshop fills up fast! Reserve your place by clicking the link below or copying into your browser, then click on the 'BOOK MY PLACE' button to go through to the enrolment form:
https://www.massivecompany.co.nz/workshops/massive-foundations-ages-14-24/events/wellington-june

Important: You must be available for both days of this workshop, and attend each day in full, to be eligible to enrol.

events.id: Stained Glass Suncatcher Day-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Stained Glass Suncatcher Day
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Create gorgeous simple leadlight suncatchers with Lisa Sinclair.

A super fun day-class, ideal as a stand-alone experience, or as a day to get stuck into projects with your tutor at hand.

Beginners choose from a range of achievable patterns and choose their colour palette. They will learn to cut the glass, and prepare it before foiling and soldering each piece, creating a simple elegant colourful glass project.

The aim of this one day class is to leave with a completed small leadlight glass item.

More experienced students may bring existing projects to work on in class - though emphasis will be placed on focusing on the same techniques of flat, nicely soldered simple designs.

This is a warm and friendly class with excellent support and learning for complete beginners and more advanced students. All tools are supplied, though you are welcome to bring your own tools and glass if you have them.

Your materials for your beginner project are supplied as part of your course fee.
Additional materials and tools are available to purchase.

Not suitable for children. Older teenagers (16+) are welcome.

About:
Escape to Martinborough this winter for a cosy, creative workshop, held at a gorgeous working studio in the heart of a boutique wine village.
These 'nature inspired' workshops are filled with mindful stitching, eco‑printing and indigo dyeing techniques, and will be tailored towards small groups, beautiful materials, and a slow, relaxed pace.

Workshops run Saturdays from June–Aug at Thunderpants , Naples St, Martinborough. Bookings are essential and spaces are limited, so get in quick to snaffle your spot!

Please email: steffwillmore@gmail.com to book or for more info call: 021 268 6748

- Saturdays at Thunderpants HQ - June, July & Aug
- All materials included -100% natural textiles & plant pigments
- Small groups, cosy atmosphere, hands‑on guidance by Steff Willmore, a local textile artist and sustainable arts practitioner.
- Tea, coffee and snacks provided at all workshops
- Lunch provided for 4–5 hr workshops (warming winter soup + crusty bread)
- Bookings essential - steffwillmore@gmail.com or call 021 268 6748
events.description:
13th June — Stitched Landscapes - 10 - 1pm - Build a mini landscape from fabric scraps - Intro to mindful stitching techniques - Learn on a small embroidery hoop (to take home) - Intro to hand embroidery stitches - Tea/coffee & snacks provided - $90 (all materials included) About: Escape to Martinborough this winter for a cosy, creative workshop, held at a gorgeous working studio in the heart of a boutique wine village.

events.long_description:
Your weekends, done properly.
From 13 June, every Saturday and Sunday from 10:30am – 2:30pm, Portlander is the place to be for a brunch experience designed to slow things down and savour the moment.
Whether it’s overdue catch-ups with friends, a relaxed date in the city, or simply carving out time for yourself, Portlander is the perfect place to settle in for the weekend.
Our à la carte brunch menu brings together elevated favourites, indulgent flavours, and beautifully crafted dishes designed to pair perfectly with bubbles, cocktails, and leisurely conversation.
Expect a vibrant atmosphere, exceptional service, and the kind of brunch that turns one coffee into a long afternoon. Add a glass of bubbles, explore our cocktail specials, and let the weekend unfold at its own pace.
Because the best weekends begin around a good table.
Weekend Brunch | Every Saturday & Sunday
Starting 13 June | 10:30am – 2:30pm

events.venue: 160 Wilton Rd, Wilton, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kua raranga tahi tātou he whariki mō āpōpō
Together we weave the mat for future generations

Nau mai haere mai, all are welcome to attend Ōtari Raranga wānanga (classes). You will walk away with woven taonga at the end of every class.

No experience is required and all materials are provided for your beginner classes.

In your first class you will learn a variety of woven putiputi (flowers). If you choose to continue your learning, your next classes will teach you kōnae (2 corner basket) and kono (4 sided basket) in. On completion of those classes and with practice you will have the skills to move on to other raranga taonga.

Location
Leonard Cockayne Centre, Otari-Wilton's Bush, 150 Wilton Road, Te Whanganui-a-Tara

Upcoming Dates
Sat 30 May 2026, 10:30am–1:30pm
Sat 13 Jun 2026, 10:30am–1:30pm
Sat 27 Jun 2026, 10:30am–1:30pm

For more information and to book your ticket please visit our eventfinder page.

We offer a flexible pricing structure to ensure cost is not a barrier to participation. If you would like to discuss your situation further please get in touch.

We thank Wellington City council for their continued support of our raranga programme.

Mauriora

events.id: Ōtari Raranga 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga 2026 - Pihinga (Beginners)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Kua raranga tahi tātou he whariki mō āpōpō
Together we weave the mat for future generations

Nau mai haere mai, all are welcome to attend Ōtari Raranga wānanga (classes).

These classes run approximately fortnightly.

No experience required and all materials provided. You will walk away with woven taonga at the end of every class.

During the first 2 classes, beginner students will complete a variety of putiputi (flowers).

We provide harakeke for you if it is your FIRST class and we will teach you how to harvest for yourself during this time and what to harvest for your next class.

After that, if you would like us to harvest for you, purchase the add-on - "Please harvest for me" for an additional fee.

Moving forward, you will start making kōnae (2 corner kete - basket), kono (4-sided kete), tapawhāroa and more.
You will need to harvest 8-10 rau for yourself and start building your own tool kit.

After completing 6 or more beginner classes (close together), you may be ready to move up to our intermediate classes. Please talk with our kaiako and kaiāwhina if you are interested.

With regular attendance, you will:
- Learn tikanga (protocols) and karakia for hauhake (harvesting) and preparing harakeke
- Build your mātauranga (knowledge)
- Use kupu Māori appropriate to raranga practice
- Develop your pūkenga (practical skills)
- Create taonga (treasures) for your whānau
- Build hononga (connections) within our raranga rōpū (group)
- Have the skills to practice at home.

What’s included in your beginner's ticket?
- Three-hour weaving class with experienced kaiako
- Harakeke is provided for your first class, and then you should hauhake (harvest) for yourself or click the "please harvest for me" option in addition to your ticket at least two days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share

Led by Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera) , Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri), and/or Anna Lees (Te Āti Awa, Kāi Tahu).
On occasion our raranga rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), will teach and we often have support from our kaiāwhina.


Important information
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia
- For tikanga purposes, beginner ticket sales close two days before wānanga, giving us time to hauhake (harvest) for you
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets
- We welcome children aged 10-14, but they should be accompanied by an adult with a ticket

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers
For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit our website: www.otariraranga.co.nz

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with the Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits, please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com or visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

events.id: Card Merchant Wellington - Catan Settlement Event #2 2026-Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Card Merchant Wellington - Catan Settlement Event #2 2026
events.venue: Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Card Merchant is excited to be part of the 2026 Catan Organized Play program here in New Zealand, providing an opportunity for players from all over the country to get involved, learn, and enjoy the game in a structured event setting.
These Catan Settlement Events are designed as an accessible entry point into organized play, giving players a chance to experience tournament-style games ahead of more competitive events later in the year.
Event Details:
Store: Card Merchant Wellington
Dates: 13th June 2026
Time: 11:00 AM
Entry Fee: $20
Event Structure:
Players will engage in multiple rounds to determine overall standings for the event.
Preliminary Round:
Each participant will play three separate games of base set Catan.
Opponents will rotate for each game.
At the conclusion of the preliminary round, players will be ranked based on their overall performance across all games.
Final Rounds:
Depending on player numbers, top-performing players may be split into final tables to determine overall placings.
If player numbers are lower, the event may instead consist solely of multiple Swiss-style rounds, with final standings determined by total points or wins.
This event is designed to balance structure with accessibility, giving players a clear tournament experience while keeping the environment welcoming and easy to engage with.
Prizes:
4x Summer Hex Kits
2x Gamegenic Catan Sleeves
2x Exit: Adventures on Catan
1x Catan Seafarers Duel
Whether you’re new to Catan or a long-time player, this is a great opportunity to get involved, meet other players, and take part in organized play. Join us at Card Merchant for a fun and welcoming day of Catan!

events.id: Indigenous Voices: A Third Spaces Collective performance-29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Indigenous Voices: A Third Spaces Collective performance
events.venue: 29 Waiapu Rd, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With the ngahere/forest backdrop of Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne, this special performance brings together artists from indigenous and diverse cultural backgrounds to create music grounded in connection, collaboration, and listening.
Third Spaces Collective explores what can emerge when artists share creative space shaped by indigenous values and approaches. Drawing on cultural difference, improvisation, storytelling, and ancestral traditions, the collective creates immersive new music that moves between worlds and traditions while remaining deeply rooted in place and identity.
Audiences can expect an evocative sound journey featuring taonga pūoro, Sámi joik, Brazilian percussion, flute, prepared double bass, and voice. Together, these artists weave contemporary and ancient sound practices into a living conversation about culture, belonging, and collective creativity.
This project is part of the Voice and Justice Third Spaces initiative at the Sibelius Academy, University of the Arts Helsinki, supported by the Erkko Foundation and Paloheimo Foundation.
This event will be held on the Heritage Lawn and is free for all visitors to attend but sanctuary entry fees apply.
Zealandia members get free entry as part of their membership.
POSTPONEMENT:

events.id: Indigenous Voices: A Third Spaces Collective performance-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Indigenous Voices: A Third Spaces Collective performance
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
With the ngahere/forest backdrop of Zealandia Te Māra a Tāne, this special performance brings together artists from indigenous and diverse cultural backgrounds to create music grounded in connection, collaboration, and listening.

Third Spaces Collective explores what can emerge when artists share creative space shaped by indigenous values and approaches. Drawing on cultural difference, improvisation, storytelling, and ancestral traditions, the collective creates immersive new music that moves between worlds and traditions while remaining deeply rooted in place and identity.

Audiences can expect an evocative sound journey featuring taonga pūoro, Sámi joik, Brazilian percussion, flute, prepared double bass, and voice. Together, these artists weave contemporary and ancient sound practices into a living conversation about culture, belonging, and collective creativity.

This project is part of the Voice and Justice Third Spaces initiative at the Sibelius Academy, University of the Arts Helsinki, supported by the Erkko Foundation and Paloheimo Foundation.

This event will be held on the Heritage Lawn and is free for all visitors to attend but sanctuary entry fees apply.
Zealandia members get free entry as part of their membership.

POSTPONEMENT:
If the weather is poor this event will be postponed to the same time the next day (Sun 14th). Should poor weather affect both days, the event will sadly be cancelled due to artist travel schedules.

Artist biographies

Hildá Länsman (Sápmi) is an internationally acclaimed Sámi joik singer and songwriter from Utsjoki in northern Finland. Raised within Sámi traditions of reindeer husbandry, duodji, and joik, Hildá combines ancestral vocal practices with contemporary and experimental music. She is known for her uniquely ethereal, shrilling, growling and clear voice and is a proud upholder of the Sámi culture and joik-tradition. Hildá has toured globally with award-winning groups including Solju, VILDÁ, and Gájanas.

Nathan Riki Thomson (Australia / Aotearoa) is a double bassist, multi-instrumentalist, composer, researcher, and Professor of Global Music and Community Engagement, leading the Global Music Programme at the Sibelius Academy in Helsinki. His work spans intercultural collaboration, improvisation, and community arts practice, shaped by decades of performance and teaching across Africa, Europe, Australia, and Aotearoa.

Adriano Itaúna Adewale (Brazil) is a percussionist, composer, educator, and researcher celebrated for his exploratory and deeply collaborative approach to music-making. Blending Brazilian traditions with contemporary improvisation, Adriano is known for his unconventional and exploratory approach to music, and for his ability to create magical soundscapes from the seemingly banal. He has composed for orchestra, theatre, and community projects and collaborated with artists around the world.

Jerome Kavanagh Poutama and Ruiha Turner (Aotearoa) are leading practitioners of taonga pūoro Māori and co-creators of the ORO ATUA, a kaupapa grounded in the healing power of ancestral Māori sound practices. Jerome is a Grammy Award-winning composer and performer whose work has brought taonga pūoro to audiences worldwide. Together, Jerome and Ruiha share deeply immersive performances that balance as taha wahine and taha tane while reconnecting people to whenua, wairua, and sound.

Livia Schweizer (Italy) is a Helsinki-based flutist, improviser, educator, and artistic researcher whose work explores intercultural collaboration through improvisation and experimental notation. Originally from Tuscany, Livia performs internationally across contemporary, experimental, and interdisciplinary music contexts, creating inclusive artistic spaces where diverse voices and traditions can meet.
events.description:
This event will be held on the Heritage Lawn and is free for all visitors to attend but sanctuary entry fees apply.
Raised within Sámi traditions of reindeer husbandry, duodji, and joik, Hildá combines ancestral vocal practices with contemporary and experimental music.
Blending Brazilian traditions with contemporary improvisation, Adriano is known for his unconventional and exploratory approach to music, and for his ability to create magical soundscapes from the seemingly banal.
Jerome is a Grammy Award-winning composer and performer whose work has brought taonga pūoro to audiences worldwide.
Livia Schweizer (Italy) is a Helsinki-based flutist, improviser, educator, and artistic researcher whose work explores intercultural collaboration through improvisation and experimental notation.

events.id: Workshop Series: Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama: POSTPONED-Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Workshop Series: Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama: POSTPONED
events.venue: Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington
events.long_description:
Vinyasa – Mai i te pō ki te ao mārama is a four part workshop series themed on the phases of the Māori creation story (te kore, te pō, te whaiao, and te ao mārama) as a metaphor and framework for confronting the cyclical nature of life. Through vinyasa practice, we explore and embody the phases of the creation story, drawing parallels to yogic wisdom and setting a foundation and spark of curiosity for the wānanga that follows. After a short break, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to further explore the deep wisdom of the creation story, learn more about the roles of the atua, and begin to unearth the relevance of it all to our own lives.

No prior experience of knowledge required. All are welcome - haere mai!

What to expect:
We will practice vinyasa flow together, sometimes incorporating meditation or breathwork. After a short break for showers/ lunch/coffee, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to explore the relevant aspects of the Māori creation story for that workshop.

What will people take away from the experience?
A deeper understanding and appreciation of the Māori creation story and how its wisdom can help guide us through life, learned through wānanga and discussion but also through the embodied practice of vinyasa yoga and by drawing parallels with yogic wisdom.

Why Enrol?
To enjoy challenging and fun vinyasa flows themed around the phases of the Māori creation story. To engage in a safe space to ask questions and learn more about the Māori creation story, the phases of creation, the atua, and how the story offers us a framework for moving through the cycles of human life, from darkness to enlightenment.

What to bring:
Bring an open mind, perhaps a journal to gather thoughts down after the workshop. Light kai/food/snacks , as options for buying food are quite limited on the weekends. Mat hire and complementary herbal tea are included in your ticket. Towels can be hired for $3 if needed.

Facilitated by Ani Morris (Ngāti Kahungunu, Te Āti Awa, Rangitāne, Ngāti Mutunga, Rongowhakaata, Ngāti Tūkorehe, Tūhoe, Ngāti Pāhauwera) & Jenny Oliver (RYT 200).
events.description:
Through vinyasa practice, we explore and embody the phases of the creation story, drawing parallels to yogic wisdom and setting a foundation and spark of curiosity for the wānanga that follows.
After a short break for showers/ lunch/coffee, we'll reconvene in a wānanga space to explore the relevant aspects of the Māori creation story for that workshop.
What will people take away from the experience?
To enjoy challenging and fun vinyasa flows themed around the phases of the Māori creation story.
To engage in a safe space to ask questions and learn more about the Māori creation story, the phases of creation, the atua, and how the story offers us a framework for moving through the cycles of human life, from darkness to enlightenment.

events.id: Découvrez Le Lot - Workshop & Tasting-Alliance Française Welington - French Cultural Centre,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Découvrez Le Lot - Workshop & Tasting
events.venue: Alliance Française Welington - French Cultural Centre,78 Victoria Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Découvrez Le Lot - Workshop & Tasting
Join us and learn more about this beautiful region!

About this Event

🌿 Tucked away in southwestern France, far from the crowds, lies Le Lot. A land of golden-stone villages perched on clifftops, deep limestone gorges, and a slow-winding river at the heart of it all.
This is the France of unhurried mornings and long lunches: the medieval lanes of Saint-Cirq-Lapopie, the dizzying cliffs of Rocamadour, and Cahors with its legendary "black wine." Local markets here brim with black truffles, walnuts and saffron.
Join Laura for a fun and immersive workshop (en français !) and step into the authentic flavours, landscapes and art de vivre of Le Lot.
And of course, we'll wrap up with a tasting of delicious traditional treats from the region!
🗯️ French A2/B1 level required
🗓️ Saturday, 13 June 2026, 1.30pm-3pm
📍 Alliance Française Wellington (78 Victoria Street, Wellington)
🎟️ $30 for AF members & $35 for non-members

and step into the authentic flavours, landscapes and art de vivre of Le Lot.

events.id: The Four Pillars of Excellence™ Workshop-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Four Pillars of Excellence™ Workshop
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
How to Move Forward When Life Feels Too Much

This is the first in-person Zammtopia workshop, designed to give you something practical, thoughtful, and immediately usable in your daily life.

If you have been moving from one demand to the next, holding a great deal and rarely getting a moment to hear yourself think, this workshop was made with you in mind.

It offers more than a breather. It gives you a space to stop, reflect, and recognise how much of your energy has been going into everything and everyone else. And it gives you a way to use some of that time and energy for yourself — to sort through what is happening, and learn how to carry the load and care for yourself better.

After the workshop, you will leave with a practical tool you can use immediately — one that helps you manage your load more clearly and begin putting some of your energy back into yourself.

And for those two hours, the workshop itself becomes part of that shift: a space set aside for you to pause, reflect, and acknowledge how much of your energy has been spent elsewhere. It is a chance to spend some of that energy on yourself, in a way that helps you carry life better and care for yourself better, too.

Limited Seats. Intimate Setting. Optimal Learning.

events.id: Writing Workshops for Rangatahi-Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Writing Workshops for Rangatahi
events.venue: Public Library Building, 65 Victoria St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
As part of the Out on the Shelves Campaign Month each year in June, InsideOUT Kōaro runs a Rainbow Storytelling Competition for young people. It aims to celebrate budding rainbow, takatāpui and MVPFAFF+ storytellers and encourage them to share their work.
These workshops, run by experienced writers and librarians, will support young writers working on a piece to enter this competition. Each workshop is being held in a different library and has a different focus, though the exercises and advice will be helpful and applicable to all kinds of writing. We recommend attending all three workshops to polish your piece for the Rainbow Storytelling Competition, though attendance at all three is not mandatory.
Workshop 1 - Creative non-fiction: Saturday 13 June, 2-3.45pm at Newtown Library
Workshop 2 - Poetry: Saturday 20 June, 2-3.45pm at Johnsonville Library
Workshop 3 - Fiction: Saturday 27 June, 2-4pm at Te Matapihi Central Library
For young writers aged 14-18.
The application deadline has been extended until Wednesday 3 June. Click the ticketing link for registration information.

events.id: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Waenganui (Intermediate)-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Raranga Raumati 2026 - Waenganui (Intermediate)
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ma te mōhio ka roa, ma te ora ka mōhio
Through learning there is life, through life there is learning

Nau mai haere mai, welcome to Ōtari Raranga Wānanga - Waenganui

Our kaiako will have made it clear to you when you can advance to this level from beginners.

If you are new to Ōtari but have experience please reach out before committing.

Projects are planned in advance of each wānanga to help us move forward together as a rōpū and awhi one another while working on similar taonga.
Please check your emails beforehand so you know what to bring.

Waenganui Wānanga are an important step toward joining our Tuakana Rōpū, who will often be attending these wānanga.
While they work on their own taonga projects, they are a valuable source of support for learners—happy to kōrero, share advice, and offer inspiration along the way.

Projects may include new closes for your kete timata, kete waikawa, puritanga, pīkau and pōtae.
We will teach you preparation, preservation and storage of harakeke - important especially over winter months. Additionally, we will induct you to the Ōtari Pā Harakeke when ready.

With regular attendance, you will deepen the mātauranga gained from beginner classes and share your learnings with others. (Refer to below for a reminder)

What’s included in your intermediate ticket?
- Three+ hours weaving class with our tuakana weavers and raranga rangatira.
- Limited access to tools, as you are expected to have your own kete mahi (tool kit).
- You are expected to bring your own harakeke.
- We encourage you to harvest for yourself, but if you are unable to harvest for class, we have ‘Please harvest for me’ options in addition to your ticket at least three days before class.
- Light refreshments are provided, and we encourage you to bring kai to share


Led by our raranga Rangatira, Frank Topia (Ngā Puhi, Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Haua), with Linda Lee (Ngāti Huia, Ngāti Raukawa, Ngāti Kurī, Te Rarawa, Te Aupōuri) and / or Irihāpeti Te Aho (Ngāti Porou, Ngāti Kahungunu, Ngāti Pauhawera)


Important information:
- Waenganui Rōpū are expected to harvest for themselves, but if something unexpected pops up we have add-on services available during the ticketing process. A minimum of 3 days notice is required.
- It is important that everyone turns up on time for karakia.
- At our discretion, tickets are fully refundable or transferable.
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class.
- We offer concession tickets for students, unwaged, seniors, and those with disabilities.
- It is important to us that our classes are accessible to all. If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets.

We mihi to our funders and sponsors, Wellington City Council, Wellington Gardens and the Ōtari-Wilton Bush Kaitiaki, Maverick Creative, Good Fortune Coffee and Wellington Apothecary.

For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit www.otariraranga.co.nz

Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers

Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting
- We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust. This enables us to continue to offer subsidised and free events to the community.
- Shared Lines Trust is registered with Charities Service; Shared Lines Trust (CC61762)
- All donations over $5 made to The Trust are tax-deductible
- For corporate gifting or individuals requesting tax credits please email SharedLinesTrust@gmail.com
- Visit www.sharedlines.org.nz for more information

- You are expected to bring your own harakeke.
- We encourage you to harvest for yourself, but if you are unable to harvest for class, we have ‘Please harvest for me’ options in addition to your ticket at least three days before class.
- If class numbers are too low, we may have to cancel (and will offer a refund or transfer), so please check your emails before class.
If ticket costs are a barrier for you, please visit our website to enquire about our reduced-cost or free tickets.
For more information about what to expect and what to bring, visit www.otariraranga.co.nz Follow us on Facebook or Instagram: Otari Raranga Weavers Donations, Tax Credits and Corporate Gifting - We accept koha to support Ōtari Raranga Weavers via Shared Lines Trust.

events.id: Concert Pianist Nikolai returns to Featherston on June 13-62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Concert Pianist Nikolai returns to Featherston on June 13
events.venue: 62 Bell St, Featherston 5710, Fernside, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A VERY SPECIAL PIANIST RETURNS TO NEW ZEALAND:
MULLED WINE CONCERTS PRESENTS NIKOLAI SARATOVSKY
Nikolai Saratovsky is an internationally acclaimed Russian concert pianist with an extraordinary repertoire. Mary Gow, organiser of the Mulled Wine Concerts series discovered Saratovsky in 2018 when he played in Brussels as a young pianist after succeeding in several important piano competitions in Europe. "I was amazed by his virtuosity" says Gow, a concert pianist herself, "He not only had a love for the music, he had all the technical skill-set you need to express yourself fully on the piano". She told him she came from New Zealand and he then told her that he and his Japanese-born wife had always dreamed of visiting New Zealand! As Gow notes, "from there, it was a short step to discussing possibilities..."
His effect on audiences here was remarkable - everyone who heard him knew that they were seeing and hearing something special. Since then, audiences around the world have come to the same conclusion. Saratovsky is now a professor of piano at the huge music school of Taiwan University where he combines teaching and academic research with his performing career, appearing in major concert venues worldwide. He also gives master-classes for aspiring international concert pianists.
Nikolai himself says this of his visits here: "This will be already the fifth time I am coming to NZ, and every time I feel extremely happy to come. I have lived and worked in Taiwan for more than 6 years. I have many memories from NZ. New Zealand is very different from Taiwan, and for me it's very important to "charge my inner spiritual battery". Coming to New Zealand is the ideal choice. Combination of meeting my lovely friends, conversations beside the fire place, fantastic sunsets, and music making makes this trip truly remarkable" He has chosen a wonderful set of music for his recital: "I chose this program by myself, because at this moment of life I prefer to play something that gives me a positive energy. I feel very compassionate with these music works, and they help me to recover during difficult times. I am happy to share this beautiful music with the audience, and I am looking forward for this fascinating musical journey". The concert will start with Western European romantic composers and conclude with pieces by Tchaikovsky and Rachmaninoff.
Mary Gow feels that his visit to New Zealand represents a wonderful opportunity for Wairarapa audiences to catch a star already well known in Europe, Russia and the Far East. "This concert I regard as one of the most important and for the North Island, not-to-be-missed!" Mulled Wine Concerts will sponsor his appearance at the ANZAC Hall in Featherston on Saturday 13 June 2026.
==============================
Mulled Wine Concerts presents a piano recital by Nikolai Saratovsky at 4:00 PM on Saturday 13 June, 2026 at the ANZAC Hall, 1 Bell St., Featherston.
Tickets: $30 adults ($10 students under 14).
Online sales: marygow@gmail.com. Tel: 021 101 9609 .

events.id: Little Prayers-National War Memorial Hall of Memories, Buckle Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Little Prayers
events.venue: National War Memorial Hall of Memories, Buckle Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Little Prayers is a song cycle being performed for the first time by Hannah Griffin and Norman Meehan, accompanied by a small chamber group.

Comprising texts composed by poet Bill Manhire, it is centred on Bill’s 2016 cycle ‘Known unto God’, commemorating the Battle of the Somme. In performance, that sequence is bookended by his poems ‘Huia’ and ‘Little Prayers’, composed shortly after the 2019 mosque attacks in Christchurch.

events.id: HiViz-Jamz presents The Sensory Overload-St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki,Beach Road, Paekakariki, Paekakariki , New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: HiViz-Jamz presents The Sensory Overload
events.venue: St Peters Village Hall, Paekākāriki,Beach Road, Paekakariki, Paekakariki , New Zealand
events.long_description:
HiViz-Jamz presents The Sensory Overload
HiViz-Jamz presents The Sensory Overload

HiViz-Jamz, are proud to bring one of our Highly Visible shows to St Peters Hall on Saturday 13th June with local Hutt Valley band ‘Something Like That’ plus the fantastic UK DJ, Hexadecimal and then to top it all off we have a wicked laser show and visuals.

That’s right, DJ, Band, Lasers, Lighting, Video and great sound…a Sensory Overload, coming to your town!

The band, ‘Something Like That’s most recent performance at St Peters as part of Coastal Rhythm’s was amazing and the crowd absolutely loved their blend of Funk, Rock, Rap, Reggae and all points in between spiced with a dash of good humor and excellent musicianship!

About HiViz-Jamz

HiViz- Jamz Have been working with bands, hip hop artists and DJ’s for the past 5/6 years. Mostly at our own Hutt Valley venue, either live streaming or putting on live gigs and Dance parties, all at a very high standard with our very own brand of Hospitality!

With many years in the industry, we are the same people behind NZ’s Pink Floyd Experience and bring a wealth of knowledge and experience to the local gigging scene.

Firstly and foremost, we are musicians and we are all about the artists. We work mostly with young upcoming artists who are ambitious and dedicated to their craft and offer our expertise and skills to help them put on quality gigs and learn the business. Also for us its great way to give back after 30 years of touring PFE having a wonderful time. We run regular events and jams so check out our youtube channel and see what you think

events.id: Michaela Tempers & Greta O'Leary (solo) play a Cozy Show at Vogelmorn-93 Mornington Road, Brooklyn, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Michaela Tempers & Greta O'Leary (solo) play a Cozy Show at Vogelmorn
events.venue: 93 Mornington Road, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Greta O’Leary and Michaela Tempers are banding together for a double bill on Saturday 13 June at Vogelmorn Upstairs. Join Greta and Michaela for a cosy evening of indie-folk music as they celebrate their recent releases, River Dark and Good Woman.
Greta O’Leary
Tāmaki Makaurau songsmith Greta O’Leary writes with a surreal clarity of vision, the line between art and artist, self and image - is razor thin. Raised on rolling hills in northern New Zealand, and coming of age anywhere but here, O’Leary is a writer with her own lens and language. Her breakthrough debut album ‘River Dark’ was released in 2025, landing at #9 on the Aotearoa Album Charts, it received heavy support from RNZ, Rolling Stone AU/NZ, PBS FM, the SRN and toured ten dates through Aotearoa and Victoria.
Michaela Tempers
Michaela Tempers is a writer and musician based in Te Whanganui-a-Tara, Aotearoa. While studying for her master’s in Creative Writing, she began writing a new collection of songs exploring the complexities of womanhood. These songs formed the basis for her debut EP, Good Woman, and feature mediaeval heroines, love, anxiety and death. The record’s six tracks are rich with incisive detail, performed with sincerity and wit.

events.id: The Wooden Box Band-Hilltop, 17 Upland Road, Kelburn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Wooden Box Band
events.venue: Hilltop, 17 Upland Road, Kelburn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Gate opens 6.30pm, Music starts 7pm, Saturday 13 June 2026.

Winners of the 2025 Aotearoa NZ Folk Alliance Best Original Album Award, Wellington’s beloved six-piece bring their latest album Broken River to Hilltop House Concerts on Saturday 13 June.

This funky folky eclectic unrelated family of musicians brings heartfelt NZ storytelling, layered vocals and dynamic live performance steeped in craft and connection.

Featuring Jessie Moss (vocals and guitar), Jeremy Desmond (guitar), Emily Clemmet (vocals, trumpet and fiddle), Sean Barker (drums), Murray Costello (bass) and Paddy Burgin (vocals, acoustic guitar, slide guitar and keyboards), the band are known as the capital’s “folk darlings,” blending rich harmonies with acoustic warmth and electric flair.

Their sound—evoking Wilco, The Mamas and the Papas and Fleet Foxes—draws on classic folk, Americana and indie influences. Formed in the mid-2000s, with a settled lineup for the past decade, they’ve released several albums including Old World, Far Far Away, and now Broken River.

Come along and enjoy this great gig in our special space, up close and personal.

These are private house concerts, not for profit, supporting our dedicated musicians. Visit www.hilltophouseconcerts.co.nz to reserve a seat. Suggested donation $30. Email confirmation will include address and details.

Teas, Coffee and Snacks provided. You're welcome to bring anything you'd like to eat or drink.
Parking available in the Coupon zones of Upland Rd & Glasgow St free after 6pm weekdays, and free all through the weekends. The Wilson Carpark at the top of the Cable Car charges 24/7.

events.id: Ōtari Glow Worm Tour-Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ōtari Glow Worm Tour
events.venue: Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush, 160 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington
events.long_description:
Take a guided night tour through Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush and discover some of the fascinating night-time residents on your way to see the titiwai/glowworms. Enjoy the tranquil beauty of the bush after dark and witness the magical glow of these unique creatures.

This outdoor night-time tour takes just over an hour.
The tour requires a moderate level of fitness; the route includes numerous steps and a steep incline to and from the glowworms.
No dogs, and not suitable for babies and young children.

Meet at the Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road. Wrap up in warm clothes, wear sturdy walking shoes, and bring a torch or head lamp.

Glow Worm Tour Ticket Refund Policy:
• No Refunds: We do not offer refunds for Glow Worm Tour tickets.
• Ticket Transfers: If you can’t attend your booked tour, with 2 weeks notice we can put you on a future tour, subject to availability.
• Weather Conditions: Tours operate rain or shine. In the event of extreme weather or unsafe conditions, we will reschedule your tour or provide a refund.

For any changes or enquiries, please get in touch with us in advance at Treehouse@wcc.govt.nz or call 04 499 1400.

This tour is operated by the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust. Join the Trust from only $20 a year and the glow worm tour is free, plus you’ll be supporting the amazing work the Trust does. Join today at owbt.nz/membership-donations/

events.long_description:
💃🕺 June Salsa Hutt Dance Party 💃🕺
May’s Salsa Hutt dance party was great!! 💃🕺😊 For those that missed out last month, and those that can come again this month, place this June party in your calendar:

Come dance the night away with your like-minded friends, and loads of classic Salsa songs from years ago, as well as a “dash” of Bachata and a “sprinkling” of Kizomba…..

• One fantastic wooden dance floor with a mix of Salsa, Bachata, and Kizomba music

• Easy to find venue off a main street in Petone (72 Cuba St)

• Plenty of parking in surrounding streets

• On main bus routes (#83 from Wellington, or #110 from Upper Hutt), or short 8min walk from Ava Train Station

• Water station provided, however bringing your own drink bottle is recommended. Also, BYO soft drinks, fruit juices, NON-alcoholic drinks, etc. (NB/ No alcohol allowed on premises!)

• Please wear high heel/stiletto caps to prevent damage to the venue’s wooden floor – For example, from https://www.beclickless.com/pages/faqs

• Only goes until 10pm so get there early to have a decent "fix" of dancing and socialising 😊

• Only $10.00 at the door (cash), or bank transfer (preferably at least 24 hours in advance):

Particulars/Code/Reference: “Your 1st and last name” - Salsa Hutt – “date of Salsa Hutt party”
******* (If you have prepaid on the night, please take a screenshot and show proof of payment at the door).

💃🕺 From 7pm to 10pm

💃🕺 Petone Salvation Army
Corner of Cuba and High Sts, Petone, Lower Hutt (Entrance from High St)

Email Kasia on or ring/text on 021 141 8116 for more details.

Created in mid 2014 by a passionate group of musos with extensive experience across various bands over many years. Brown Sugar is an exciting new project on a mission to deliver a professional and carefully tailored showcase of the most well known and exciting Rolling Stones songs from the past five decades to New Zealand venues.

events.name: HOICK W/ GNARLING, EURYDICE & WINCE
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
HOICK.
Valhalla
Doors - 8PM
$20
Forged late in the year 2020, deep in the bowels of Tauranga, New Zealand, the band HOICK brewed and spewed forth their very own unique brand of audio pollution to the world.
Though developed by nefarious means the groups yield from their riff farm has been inducing the stank face of approval from gig goers Island wide since the bands inception, and with their debut self titled album "HOICK" set to be released to the public April 3rd 2026, followed closely by an album release tour, the trio's distortion worship only looks to gain greater occupancy in the ears of true believers this year.
The three mix fast chaotic punk chordage with sections of slower, heavier more experimental sections of noise therapy. Think if the Bad Brains faster punky songs smoked some Black Sabbath but then got hit by a car crossing the road driven by Fugazi.
Catch the wreckage in a town near you soon and come celebrate an N.Z made musical release we can all be ashamed of.
Grab a copy of HOICK’s debut album on vinyl from Bandcamp or stream via on your favorite streaming service.

events.id: Soundscape Series: Lockie Bennett Trio-The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Soundscape Series: Lockie Bennett Trio
events.venue: The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Lockie Bennett is a jazz guitarist based in Wellington, who specialises in bop-oriented music. Lockie has performed and recorded regularly as both a leader and a side man with some of New Zealand’s top musicians. This drumless permutation of the trio features Alex Trask on alto saxophone and Elijah Mulheron on bass.

Opening act: to be announced

About The Mostly Music Soundscape Series

A curated series of intimate live music experiences showcasing the depth, texture and emotion of sound. Each performance invites you to immerse yourself in a new soundscape, informed by genre, sonic craft, and music as an art form. Designed for music lovers who want more than a background soundtrack, this series celebrates live performance with a diverse range of acclaimed performers.

Each event will also feature a short opening set from a local, young emerging artist — offering up-and-coming performers the opportunity to step into a professional live performance setting and share their music with a listening audience.

When: Monthly performances from May – October
Where: The Bond Store, Paraparaumu
What: Seated, intimate performances
Tickets: $25 per event, available via Eventfinda
