events.id: Terror-Fi Presents: THE DEATH OF ROBIN HOOD (Wellington Premiere)-12 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Terror-Fi Presents: THE DEATH OF ROBIN HOOD (Wellington Premiere)
events.venue: 12 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Be the first to see THE DEATH OF ROBIN HOOD at our special Wellington Premiere.
‘Logan’ meets ‘The Northman’ in a powerful reimagining of the classic tale, where director Michael Sarnoski (‘Pig’, ‘A Quiet Place Day One’) shoots an arrow into the heart of the legend. Hugh Jackman delivers a truly moving performance as the tortured hero, alongside the always brilliant Jodie Comer and an almost unrecognisable Bill Skarsgård.
“Grappling with his past after a life of crime and murder, Robin Hood finds himself gravely injured after a battle he thought would be his last. In the hands of a mysterious woman, he is offered a chance at salvation.”
Come to the cinema early to enjoy our themed lobby and custom pre-show.
Directed by Michael Sarnoski THE DEATH OF ROBIN HOOD stars Hugh Jackman, Jodie Comer, Bill Skarsgård, Murray Bartlett and Noah Jupe.

events.id: Air Force Band Charity Concert tickets-Southwards Theatre, Paraparaumu, Wellington-Ticketek
events.name: Air Force Band Charity Concert tickets
events.venue: Southwards Theatre, Paraparaumu, Wellington
events.long_description:
Presented by: Waikanae Lions Club and Royal New Zealand Air Force Band
The much loved Air Force Charity Concert returns for its 18th spectacular year! A firm favourite that regularly sells out, this is not to be missed — secure your tickets early to be part of this unforgettable afternoon.
This year’s concert promises an exciting and uplifting programme, featuring everything from iconic movie themes to timeless classics. Audiences will be treated to the full power and colour of bagpipes, drums, brass and woodwind, together with a stunning performance by the incomparable soprano LAC Barbara Graham.
Adding to the occasion, this concert marks a heartfelt farewell as Director of Music Squadron Leader David Gallaher takes the podium for his final performance with the RNZAF Band before embarking on new adventures back home in Christchurch.
Best of all, thanks to the hard work of the Waikanae Lions Club, every ticket purchased supports a wonderful cause, with proceeds going to Mary Potter Hospice.

events.id: Parangarahu Lakes Community Planting Day-Lower Hutt-Facebook
events.name: Parangarahu Lakes Community Planting Day
events.venue: Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Join us for a day of planting at these nationally significant lakes.
The Parangarahu Lakes are important to the surrounding environment and are home to native taonga. This includes a range of wetland plants, īnanga (galaxias maculatus), manu (birds), and our native freshwater mussel, the kākahi.
This planting day is perfect for those who enjoy a bit of physical activity, as you’ll be carrying plants and digging holes.
What to bring/wear:
• Sturdy boots or gumboots
• Long sleeves and pants, and a waterproof jacket are essential. Layering and wearing a hat or beanie is a good way to stay warm. This site can be very exposed, so it is a good idea to bring a spare change of clothes.
• Feel free to bring your own favourite spade and gloves.
• Bring your own water bottle, and snacks.
We provide:
• FREE transport from Burdan’s Gate, Eastbourne, along the rugged coast to the Lower Pencarrow Lighthouse at the harbour entrance and onto the entrance of the first lake. Please book your seat as we have limited numbers. No private cars will have access.
• Plants and planting tools, gloves.
• An experienced team leader, health and safety management and training.
Meeting point: 507 Muritai Road, Eastbourne, Lower Hutt.
Booking is essential to secure your seat on the bus to the lakes.
Follow this link to register: https://forms.office.com/r/VDGJcRJLww

Please book your seat as we have limited numbers.

events.id: 2026 NZ Card Collectors Show-Te Rauparaha Arena,Trust Porirua Aquatic Recreation Centre, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: 2026 NZ Card Collectors Show
events.venue: Te Rauparaha Arena,Trust Porirua Aquatic Recreation Centre, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
2026 NZ Card Collectors Show
The 5th edition of the New Zealand Card Collectors Show is back in 2026 and will take place in the massive Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua on Sunday 14 June.

It is the most comprehensive card collecting show in the country – over 90 vendors and 200 tables of cards covering all interests and periods (ultra-modern through to vintage).

All cards and collectors catered for, including sports cards (rugby, basketball, cricket, league, football, boxing, ice hockey, UFC etc), TCG (Pokémon, MTG, Yu-gi-oh, Dragonball Z, One Piece, Lorcana etc), trading cards, gum cards, trade cards, cigarette cards, stickers, postcards, as well as associated sports memorabilia, card accessories and card grading services.

Public admission fee:
$5 Adults
Under 18 free

Raffles - Prizes - Games - Live Breaks - Trading Space for Adults and Kids - Food Vendors - Free Parking - Free Valuations

Vendor tables (all 1.8m in length) range in price from $30 for the hobbiest/beginner through to $60 for the business professional. All info for these can be found on the 2026 NZ Card Collectors Show Facebook page.

For more table information/bookings please contact Dan or Steve ()
events.description:
2026 NZ Card Collectors Show The 5th edition of the New Zealand Card Collectors Show is back in 2026 and will take place in the massive Te Rauparaha Arena in Porirua on Sunday 14 June.
It is the most comprehensive card collecting show in the country – over 90 vendors and 200 tables of cards covering all interests and periods (ultra-modern through to vintage).
All cards and collectors catered for, including sports cards (rugby, basketball, cricket, league, football, boxing, ice hockey, UFC etc), TCG (Pokémon, MTG, Yu-gi-oh, Dragonball Z, One Piece, Lorcana etc), trading cards, gum cards, trade cards, cigarette cards, stickers, postcards, as well as associated sports memorabilia, card accessories and card grading services.
Public admission fee: $5 Adults Under 18 free Raffles - Prizes - Games - Live Breaks - Trading Space for Adults and Kids - Food Vendors - Free Parking - Free Valuations Vendor tables (all 1.8m in length) range in price from $30 for the hobbiest/beginner through to $60 for the business professional.
For more table information/bookings please contact Dan or Steve ()

events.id: Bike Reveal + Obsession With Dirt Museum-2019 akatarawa road Upper Hutt,2019 Akatarawa Rd, Akatarawa Valley, Upper Hutt 5372, New Zealand, Upper Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Bike Reveal + Obsession With Dirt Museum
events.venue: 2019 akatarawa road Upper Hutt,2019 Akatarawa Rd, Akatarawa Valley, Upper Hutt 5372, New Zealand, Upper Hutt
events.long_description:
Bike Reveal + Obsession With Dirt Museum
Look at this! We’re hosting another special one off event! This one is quite an emotional / special one for me…

For anyone who’s not aware by now unfortunately my brother look his own life about a month ago (still can’t believe I have to say that…) man every time I write that out I get the mega brain fart on what the hell do I say next!?

But pretty much this event is a fundraiser for us (the motorcycle collective) and my Sister in law and niece! We will have a Gold coin donation BBQ, along with our Motorcycle gear op shop to raise some money and all money made from this event will be split down the middle!

We will also be unveiling a new motorcycle that I can guarantee 99% of you haven’t heard of… limited edition bike and this is the only model in the whole of New Zealand!! So I promise you guys don’t wanna miss that out!

Plus “Obsession with dirt museum” will be open to browse around! I wanna say if i remember correctly about at least 20 vintage dirt bikes are in the museum! Damo the owner will be around to answer any questions yall have about the bikes and to tell you the history!

This event is free and open to everyone! Bikes come down the driveway, if you come in a car I’d suggest to park somewhere safely on the road and do not block any driveways or others in.

Also looking for photographers and videographers to help out! Reach out to me if you’re keen thanks!

events.id: Otaki Market - June-Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Otaki Market - June
events.venue: Otaki Market, 58 Main Highway, Ōtaki, Kapiti Coast, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Nau Mai Haere Mai, meet you at the Market.
Discover a vibrant outdoor market nestled on State Highway One in Ōtaki, just across from New World. Join us to greet our regular stallholders and special guests from the community.
---
PARKING
There is plenty of free parking available at the Otaki train station.
Main Highway is a perfect spot for customers to the market
60 Minute Parking zone behind Riverstone Cafe.

CASH / ATM
Some vendors have eftpos, and all take cash. There is an ATM located on the main highway.

FOOD VENDORS
And when hunger strikes, our lineup of food trucks and stalls is ready to tantalize your taste buds. From waffles to hangi, seafood chowder to fry bread, sausage sizzles, and a delectable assortment of rice, noodles, and home baking – a feast awaits.

BUSKER
We often have an awesome busker, who'll be playing chilled out middle of the road tunes.

- Art & Craft
- Native trees, flowers and plants
- Candles, soaps and wax melts
- Antiques and collectables
- Produce, including yummy honey!
- Pre-love clothing, shoes and accessories
- Tools for the home and garden
- Toys and books for tamariki

---
STALL HOLDERS
Would you like to be a stall holder?
Join our market family, fill out the enquiry form: www.otakiwomensclub.org/stall-holder

events.id: The Waikanae Beach Monthly Community Market-Waikanae Beach Community Hall, 22a Rauparaha St, Waikanae-Event Finder
events.name: The Waikanae Beach Monthly Community Market
events.venue: Waikanae Beach Community Hall, 22a Rauparaha St, Waikanae
events.long_description:
Enjoy a Sunday outing on the Kapiti Coast with your family and friends by visiting this great, friendly community market, where you will discover handmade treasures and local creativity by highly talented stallholders, where every piece tells a story.

For the kids:-
Bring the kids along so they can meet Helen with her huge range of awesome handmade children's dress-ups and doll clothes, etc, and Viv, an author and illustrator of Children's Books and an accomplished creator of Pet Portraits.

New Stallholder:-
Catherine with her yummy baking, including Cakes and Cupcakes.

Discover unique and quality items:-
You will find a huge variety of high-quality, reasonably priced gift ideas along with new exciting creations every month.

Meet up with your friends and family:-
Why not enjoy a family day out on the beautiful Kapiti Coast, meet up with a friend or 2, pick up some handmade treasures and gifts, then spend the rest of the day at other events and attractions, including a visit to our lovely beaches and cafes.

Don't miss it....Support local!!

events.id: Le Marché Français at GP Leeds St (MELTED CHEESE GALORE!)-7 Leeds St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Le Marché Français at GP Leeds St (MELTED CHEESE GALORE!)
events.venue: 7 Leeds St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
We love Le Marché Français. They're tres chic and one of the greats when it comes to fromage (cheese). It's always a treat having delicious French food on deck to class the place up, and it pairs so well with beer.
On Sunday 14 June, Le Marché Français is coming to Leeds St for the afternoon to present to you lucky folks their raclette baguettes - it's a Raclette Lunch!
Huge wheels of specialty French cow's milk cheese, slowly melted and then loaded straight onto fresh baguette, with a range of accoutrements on the side (cornichons, Dijon mustard, radish and Saucisson) so you can build each perfectly-balanced bite to your personal preference. Have you seen raclette IRL before? It's the closest food gets to performance art, and it takes skill and experience to execute.
Lucky for us, Le Marché Français are pros - you'll feel like you've been transported straight to fair Paris ("Paree") or a quaint village square in Provence. There's a fuel crisis on - let the world's delicacies come to you!
Sunday 14 June, from 12pm
$30 a head gets you a raclette baguette with sides + a drink (beer, wine and non-alcoholic options)
Vegetarian option available
No gluten-free, dairy-free or vegan options (it's cheese and bread!)
Bookings available, and recommended
Walk-ins welcome - but you'll be taking your chances
Book your spot now using the link above!

events.id: Air Force Band Charity Concert-Southward Theatre, Otaihanga Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Air Force Band Charity Concert
events.venue: Southward Theatre, Otaihanga Rd, Otaihanga, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Presented by: Waikanae Lions Club and Royal New Zealand Air Force Band

The much loved Air Force Band Charity Concert returns for its 18th spectacular year! A firm favourite that regularly sells out, this is not to be missed — secure your tickets early to be part of this unforgettable afternoon.

This year’s concert promises an exciting and uplifting programme, featuring everything from iconic movie themes to timeless classics. Audiences will be treated to the full power and colour of bagpipes, drums, brass and woodwind, together with a stunning performance by the incomparable soprano LAC Barbara Graham.

Adding to the occasion, this concert marks a heartfelt farewell as Director of Music Squadron Leader David Gallaher takes the podium for his final performance with the RNZAF Band before embarking on new adventures back home in Christchurch.

Best of all, thanks to the hard work of the Waikanae Lions Club, every ticket purchased supports a wonderful cause, with proceeds going to Mary Potter Hospice.

Each session is intimate (6–7 people), includes all materials, your own full-size candle, and a warm cup of tea.
2026 Dates (Sundays): 26 Apr • 10 May • 14 Jun • 26 Jul • 9 Aug • 13 Sep • 18 Oct • 8 Nov • 6 Dec (Festive) Time: 2:00 pm – 4:00 pm Price: $69 per person Private workshops are also available for birthdays, corporate events, and other special occasions — message us to book.
*All children under the age of 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver*

events.id: Activists, Allies & Beyond: 40 Years Since Homosexual Law Reform-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Activists, Allies & Beyond: 40 Years Since Homosexual Law Reform
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library for a free event celebrating the 40th anniversary of the Homosexual Law Reform Act 1986. We’ll be joined by Trevor Mallard, Linda Evans, Bill Logan, Onjei Bond, and host Gareth Watkins for a panel discussion exploring the social and political climate, and the long history of community activism that contributed to the landmark 1986 reform passing through Parliament.

The panel will discuss the political processes behind the bill and the community campaigns that emerged during a deeply divisive period in New Zealand’s history. We’ll hear about the personal impacts this had on members of the rainbow community, the resulting legacies of the reform, and the ways it continues to shape the lives and rights of queer people in Aotearoa today.

About the Speakers:
Trevor Mallard:
Trevor Mallard was an MP for 35 years and served as a Minister and Speaker. More recently he was our Ambassador to Ireland, is now retired and spends his time gardening, walking his dog and helping with grandchildren.
Trevor acted as “whip” for the Homosexual Law Reform Bill. His role was to keep a day by day count to help ensure the numbers were there to make sure it became law.

Linda Evans:
Linda Evans has been an activist in lesbian, feminist and gay politics in Te Whanganui-a-Tara since the 1970s. She was involved in the ongoing campaigns for homosexual law reform and anti-discrimination laws in the 1970s and 1980s.
Linda is a trustee and voluntary curator for Kawe Mahara Queer Archives Aotearoa.

Bill Logan:
In 1985 Bill Logan was a radical Wellington bookseller and involved in the Gay Switchboard and the response to the Aids pandemic. He became one of the organisers of the Homosexual Law Reform campaign to decriminalise sex between men. He remains engaged in socialist and community activism, and now works as a counsellor and marriage and funeral celebrant.

Onjei Bond:
Onjei Bond is a recent Master’s graduate in Museum and Heritage studies, interested in the queer history of Aotearoa.
He has recently finished a six-month research project focused on documenting and preserving the queer history of the Taranaki region – in collaboration with Pride Taranaki and Puke Ariki.

Gareth Watkins:
Gareth Watkins (he/him) founded the non-profit community website PrideNZ.com in 2009. It has grown into the largest online audio repository of local LGBTQIA+ Rainbow histories, with more than 1,000 recordings of events and life histories, alongside thousands of related news items dating back to the early 1900s. The material is freely available to a global audience. Gareth is also a co-founder of Walk Tours NZ, a volunteer group sharing Wellington’s queer history through free walking tours and digital storytelling. He is currently writing an AI-assisted musical marking 40 years since homosexual law reform in New Zealand, combining human storytelling with AI-generated production elements. Across his work, Gareth is interested in how archives, technology and creative practice can help keep community histories alive and bring them to new audiences.

Sunday 14 June, 2-3pm
Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library (65 Victoria Street)
Ngā Pou Ruahine, Level 2
Free event, all welcome


About the Speakers:
Trevor Mallard was an MP for 35 years and served as a Minister and Speaker. More recently he was our Ambassador to Ireland, is now retired and spends his time gardening, walking his dog and helping with grandchildren. Trevor acted as “whip” for the Homosexual Law Reform Bill. His role was to keep a day by day count to help ensure the numbers were there to make sure it became law.


Linda Evans has been an activist in lesbian, feminist and gay politics in Te Whanganui-a-Tara since the 1970s. She was involved in the ongoing campaigns for homosexual law reform and anti-discrimination laws in the 1970s and 1980s. Linda is a trustee and voluntary curator for Kawe Mahara Queer Archives Aotearoa.


In 1985 Bill Logan was a radical Wellington bookseller and involved in the Gay Switchboard and the response to the Aids pandemic. He became one of the organisers of the Homosexual Law Reform campaign to decriminalise sex between men. Onjei Bond is a recent Master’s graduate in Museum and Heritage studies, interested in the queer history of Aotearoa. events.long_description:
Klezmer Rebs at Futuna Chapel, Karori 2pm
The Klezmer Rebs return to Futuna Sunday afternoon 14 June after a stellar year. The Rebs bring their sometimes rollicking, sometimes delicate songs from the ‘old country’, hot off headlining at the Wellington Folk Festival and rousing sets at CubaDupa and Newtown Festival.

The Rebs will be featuring their new live album recorded at Futuna last year.

Always a unique musical event - catch some great world folk music and get home in time for your afternoon nap!

Tickets at the door

events.venue: Wild Wellbeing,347 Kapiti Rd, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Nourish Your Nervous System - with Nutritionist Lucy Edwards
Preventing Burnout: Nutrition + Breathwork Workshop
Join us for a calming and practical 1-hour workshop designed to help women better manage stress and prevent burnout before it takes hold.

This session blends science-backed nutrition with simple, effective breathwork techniques to support your nervous system, gut health, and hormones—so you can feel more balanced, energised, and resilient in your daily life.

What’s included:
✨ 30-minute nutrition talk�Learn how to nourish your body during times of stress, with a focus on:
Supporting your nervous system
Improving gut health
Balancing hormones
Practical strategies to regulate your stress response

✨ 10-minute Q&A�Ask questions and get personalised guidance.

✨ 15-minute breathwork session�(no experience needed)
Understand how breath impacts your body, then experience a guided practice designed to help shift you out of “fight or flight” and into a calmer, more grounded state.

🍵 Organic herbal teas�Enjoy specially selected teas to further support relaxation and nervous system health.
Feel free to bring a pen and paper if you’d like to take notes.

About your host;
Lucy Nutrition, founded by Lucy Edwards, offers a warm, holistic approach to health that blends science with real-life practicality. With over 20 years of experience in the health industry, Lucy is passionate about helping women feel energised, balanced, and back in control of their wellbeing.

Pricing:�Early Bird: $34.99�Regular: $49.99
📍 Held at Wild Wellbeing�Limited spaces available, bookings essential.

Tickets through Eventfinda (booking fee applies)
https://www.eventfinda.co.nz/2026/nourish-your-nervous-system-with-nutritionist-lucy-edwards/paraparaumu

Pay online; with NYNS as the reference and your name as the particular.

Account number:38-9017-0441911-00

events.id: Concert Pianist Nikolai Saratovsky plays in Paekakariki - JUNE 14-Beach Road, Paekakariki-Facebook
events.name: Concert Pianist Nikolai Saratovsky plays in Paekakariki - JUNE 14
events.venue: Beach Road, Paekakariki
events.long_description:
A VERY SPECIAL PIANIST RETURNS TO NEW ZEALAND:
MULLED WINE CONCERTS PRESENTS NIKOLAI SARATOVSKY
Nikolai Saratovsky is an internationally acclaimed Russian concert pianist with an extraordinary repertoire. Mary Gow, organiser of the Paekakariki-based Mulled Wine Concerts discovered Saratovsky in 2018 when he played in Brussels as a young pianist after succeeding in several important piano competitions in Europe. "I was amazed by his virtuosity" says Gow, a concert pianist herself, "He not only had a love for the music, he had all the technical skill-set you need to express yourself fully on the piano". She told him she came from New Zealand and he then told her that he and his Japanese-born wife had always dreamed of visiting New Zealand! As Gow notes, "from there, it was a short step to discussing possibilities..."
His effect on audiences here was remarkable - everyone who heard him knew that they were seeing and hearing something special. Since then, audiences around the world have come to the same conclusion. Saratovsky is now a professor of piano at the huge music school of Taiwan University where he combines teaching and academic research with his performing career, appearing in major concert venues worldwide. He also gives master-classes for aspiring international concert pianists.
Nikolai himself says this of his visits here: "This will be already the fifth time I am coming to NZ, and every time I feel extremely happy to come. I have lived and worked in Taiwan for more than 6 years. I have many memories from NZ. New Zealand is very different from Taiwan, and for me it's very important to "charge my inner spiritual battery". Coming to New Zealand is the ideal choice. Combination of meeting my lovely friends, conversations beside the fire place, fantastic sunsets, and music making makes this trip truly remarkable" He has chosen a wonderful set of music for his recital: "I chose this program by myself, because at this moment of life I prefer to play something that gives me a positive energy. I feel very compassionate with these music works, and they help me to recover during difficult times. I am happy to share this beautiful music with the audience, and I am looking forward for this fascinating musical journey". The concert will start with Western European romantic composers and conclude with pieces by Tchaikovsky and Rachmaninoff.
Mary Gow feels that his visit to New Zealand represents a wonderful opportunity for Kapiti audiences to catch a star already well known in Europe, Russia and the Far East. "This concert I regard as one of the most important and for the North Island, not-to-be-missed!" Mulled Wine Concerts will sponsor his appearance at the Paekakariki Memorial Hall on Sunday, June 14.
==============================
Mulled Wine Concerts presents a piano recital by Nikolai Saratovsky at 2:30 PM on Sunday 14 June, 2026 at the Paekakariki Memorial Hall, The Parade, Paekakariki.
Tickets: $35 adults ($15 students under 14).
Online sales: marygow@gmail.com. Tel: 021 101 9609 .

"This concert I regard as one of the most important and for the North Island, not-to-be-missed!" Mulled Wine Concerts will sponsor his appearance at the Paekakariki Memorial Hall on Sunday, June 14.
OTHER CONCERTS
Saturday June 13 at 4 pm. ANZAC HALL, Featherston
Wednesday June 17 at 7.30 pm Nelson Centre Musical Arts - Tickets from NCMA and Eventfinda
Thursday June 18 at 7.30 pm Blenheim Whitehaven Theatre Tickets from Eventfinda
==============================

Mulled Wine Concerts presents a piano recital by Nikolai Saratovsky at 2:30 PM on Sunday 14 June, 2026 at the Paekakariki Memorial Hall, The Parade, Paekakariki.

Tickets: $35adults ($15 students under 14).
Online sales: marygow@gmail.com. Tel: 021 101 9609 .
Info: www.mulledwineconcerts.com, or FaceBook: Mulled Wine Concerts

Ticket outlets (please note: cash only) : Magpie Paramata, Mana Esplanade; Finn's Hotel, Paekākāriki; Moby Dickens Bookshop, Paraparaumu Beach; Jenoa, 2 Mahara Place, Waikanae.
events.description:
Mary Gow, organiser of the Paekakariki-based Mulled Wine Concerts discovered Saratovsky in 2018 when he played in Brussels as a young pianist after succeeding in several important piano competitions in Europe.
"I was amazed by his virtuosity" says Gow, a concert pianist herself, "He not only had a love for the music, he had all the technical skill-set you need to express yourself fully on the piano".
Nikolai himself says this of his visits here: "This will be already the fifth time I am coming to NZ, and every time I feel extremely happy to come.
Combination of meeting my lovely friends, conversations beside the fire place, fantastic sunsets, and music making makes this trip truly remarkable" He has chosen a wonderful set of music for his recital: "I chose this program by myself, because at this moment of life I prefer to play something that gives me a positive energy.
ANZAC HALL, Featherston Wednesday June 17 at 7.30 pm Nelson Centre Musical Arts - Tickets from NCMA and Eventfinda Thursday June 18 at 7.30 pm Blenheim Whitehaven Theatre Tickets from Eventfinda ============================== Mulled Wine Concerts presents a piano recital by Nikolai Saratovsky at 2:30 PM on Sunday 14 June, 2026 at the Paekakariki Memorial Hall, The Parade, Paekakariki.

events.id: French Film Festival - Just an Illusion-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Just an Illusion
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
From Eric Toledano and Oliver Nakache, the award-winning directors of The Intouchables and A Difficult Year comes Just an Illusion, a heartwarming and brilliant comedy set in the suburbs of Paris in 1985.

The film follows Vincent (an incredible debut performance from Simon Boublil),
a boy standing on the precipice of his 13th birthday and his Bar Mitzvah - a milestone that feels more like an identity crisis than a celebration.

Vincent’s mother Sandrine (Camille Cottin, Three Friends) tries to hold the family together with her job as a secretary, while his father Yves (Louis Garrel, also starring in Couture) grapples with unemployment and a wounded ego.

Caught between his parents’ frequent arguments and a distant older brother, Arnaud (Alexis Rosenstiehl), Vincent must find his own rhythm in a family that is out of sync.

Simon Boublil is being hailed as a major discovery for his performance as Vincent, carrying this nostalgia soaked tale with a winning combination of vulnerability and dry humour. Meanwhile, French cinema stalwarts Cottin and Garrel deliver explosive but grounded chemistry.

Directors Toledano and Nakache blend the infectious energy of a classic 80s soundtrack with their signature brand of heartfelt humour, for their most personal film to date. Book in quick to avoid disappointment!
events.description:

Caught between his parents’ frequent arguments and a distant older brother, Arnaud (Alexis Rosenstiehl), Vincent must find his own rhythm in a family that is out of sync.
Simon Boublil is being hailed as a major discovery for his performance as Vincent, carrying this nostalgia soaked tale with a winning combination of vulnerability and dry humour.

events.id: Jian Liu (Solo Piano)-St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jian Liu (Solo Piano)
events.venue: St Andrews on the Terrace, 30 The Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
Jian Liu is Deputy Head of School, Associate Professor, and Head of Piano Studies at New Zealand School of Music, Victoria University of Wellington. He is a regular performer for Sunday Concerts as a soloist and member of various chamber music groups.

The concert explores how composers from different times and cultures paint their worlds in sound. Works by NZ composers (Lilburn, Fisher, Farr, Whitehead, Ritchie and Farquhar) are contrasted with those of Chinese composers. The journey begins with landscapes and turns to childhood and memory.

Douglas Lilburn From the Port Hills
Huang Hu Wei 黄虎威 Pictures from Bashu (selections) 巴蜀之画 (选曲)
Tan Dun 谭盾 Eight Memories in Watercolour (selections) 八幅水彩画的回忆 (选曲)
Salina Fisher Three Short Pieces
Gareth Farr Love Songs
Zhang Zhao 张朝 Three Songs from the Mountains of Southern Yunnan 滇南山谣三首
Gillian Whitehead Lullaby for Matthew
He Lu Ting 贺绿汀 Lullaby 摇篮曲
Anthony Ritchie Caroline Bay Suite
Ding Shan De 丁善德 Children’s Suite: Happy Holidays (selections) 儿童组曲《快乐的节日》(选曲)
David Farquhar Sonatina
Wang Li San 汪立三 Sonatina 小奏鸣曲

This concert is presented in association with Chamber Music NZ

events.name: Sunday Roast
events.venue: The Customhouse, 17 Whitmore St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Sundays were made for this…..
From 14 June, Portlander invites you to slow down and feast, with the return of the great Sunday tradition, a hearty roast shared over good conversation, great wine, and incredible food.
Each Sunday, our chefs will showcase a different premium roast, with meats rotating weekly to celebrate the season and the craft behind every cut. Expect everything that makes a proper Sunday roast unforgettable: perfectly cooked meat, golden roast potatoes, rich gravy, seasonal sides, and all the comforting touches that turn a meal into an occasion.
To finish, enjoy a decadent dessert as part of our two-course experience, all for $60 per person.
Whether it’s a family tradition, a catch-up with friends, or simply an excuse to indulge during the colder months, Sunday Roast at Portlander is winter dining done properly.
Available every Sunday from 14 June.
Bookings recommended.

events.id: French Film Festival - What is Love?-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - What is Love?
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Marguerite (Laure Calamy, How to Make a Killing, AF FFF25) sees no reason to refuse her ex-husband Fred (Vincent Macaigne, Three Friends, AF FFF25) when he asks for an official Catholic Church annulment of their marriage. They share a child, she has since rebuilt her life with a new partner and a teen daughter, and she is even genuinely pleased to learn that Fred hopes to remarry Chloé, the woman who now shares his life.

To satisfy the Church authorities, the former spouses must prove that their marriage was destined to fail from the beginning. What begins as a simple administrative formality slowly turns into an unexpected emotional journey. As Marguerite and Fred revisit old memories, moments of tenderness, and the cracks that once separated them, emotions they believe long-resolved begin to resurface.

What follows is a touching and witty family comedy about the strange afterlives of relationships, the surprises that linger after separation, and the subtle power of second chances that can shift everything.

The latest film from Fabien Gorgeart (Diane Has the Right Shape, AF FFF18), it is a warm and insightful exploration of love’s complexities and the fragile possibilities of new beginnings, carried by humour, humanity, and a glow that guarantees smiles as you exit the cinema.

Rhythm Output presents: Bryan Gee (WLG)
Aotearoa, get ready — the legendary Bryan Gee touches down in Wellington for an exclusive Sunday session at Afters, bringing the unmistakable V Recordings flavour to the capital.

For over three decades, Bryan Gee has shaped the sound of jungle and drum & bass worldwide. From discovering scene‑defining artists to championing soulful rollers, Bristol funk, and deep UK jungle pressure, his sets are a masterclass in energy, history, and pure dancefloor joy.

On Sunday 14 June, Rhythm Output transforms Afters into a low‑lit, high‑vibe haven for heads who know. Expect warm basslines, classic cuts, unreleased gems, and that signature Bryan Gee selection that turns a room inside out.

A rare chance to catch a true icon in an intimate space built for proper sound.

events.id: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)-39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Bailamos Bachata! Latin Dance Party (with free lesson)
events.venue: 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Please note: the event is free but it can only continue if we support the Southern Cross by buying food or drinks

Wellington’s favourite monthly Bachata Latin dance party

Join us for an awesome mix of bachata, focusing on the best sensual tracks and remixes, along with salsa and a smattering of kizomba and zouk. We welcome everyone, from the absolute beginner to the most experienced dancer. We’re all about the joy of social dancing together.
And what makes a free party with great people even better? A free beginners’ bachata lesson with some of Wellington‘s best Latin dance teachers. If you’ve never danced bachata, Latin dance or even any other dance style before then this is the perfect lesson for you. We will have you dancing like a pro in no time!
Bailamos Bachata is hosted free of charge by the Southern Cross. They love the atmosphere we bring but we also need to support them in return. We’re asking everyone attending to purchase soft drinks, food or alcohol from the bar. It’s vital we show our gratitude.
1800-1830 social
1830-1900 free lesson
1900-2100 social
Q&A
Q. What is bachata?
A. Bachata is a popular and fun Latin couples dance. It started in the Dominican Republic, then travelled to Europe and the rest of the world. It now includes a wide range of music styles and dance influences and is still changing and evolving.
Q. I haven’t danced before, is that a problem?
A. Not at all! That’s exactly what the lesson is for – everyone has to start somewhere and we’re a very welcoming crowd for beginners.
Q. Is bachata a difficult dance to learn?
A. Its origins as a party/community dance mean the basics can be learnt quite quickly, so you’ll be enjoying yourself in no time.
Q. Who are the capital bachata collective?
A. We are the combined Wellington dance schools and people teaching bachata and we really want to share this dance with everyone. We love bachata, we love our community and we want to share it with you
￼

🇮🇹 Festa Italiana 🇮🇹
🇮🇹 Festa Italiana 🇮🇹

For one night only, Bhakti Lounge transforms into a bustling little corner of Italy.
Expect delicious food, colourful mocktails, live mantra music~kirtan, dance, and a lively social atmosphere that feels more like an Italian street festival than a restaurant.
You'll find great company, plenty of laughs, and one of the most affordable nights out in town.

🧆 Arancini Balls

🥗 Roast Vegetable Salad with Balsamic Vinaigrette

🍹 Italian Mocktails 🍋 Limoncello, 🌅 Sicilian Sunset

A wisdom talk exploring The Renaissance and the Search for the Self, a journey into one of history's most creative periods and what it can teach us about living with purpose today.

Come hungry. Bring your dancing shoes. Meet some good people.

Taste Italy. Join the mix. Stay a while.
Food. Music. People.

Sunday June 14th 6pm

events.name: Songwriters' Showcase #112
events.venue: Moon, 167 Riddiford Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
A great lineup for the June Songwriters’ Showcase featuring Wellington legend Ali Whitton, Karli McGray and Helena Faust and Friends. Your chance to hear 3 of the city’s best songwriters for the price of one.
The show will kick off about 7pm, following the conclusion of Moon’s Songwriters’ open mic (which will run from 4:00pm.). Ali Whitton will be onstage from 7:00pm, followed by Karli at 7:45pm, with Helena closing the show from 8:15pm.

Tickets $15 from Eventfinda or at the door, but with a special price of $10 for folk who have played at the open mic.

events.id: Lydia Lunch-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lydia Lunch
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Lydia Lunch
Performing the songs of Suicide & Alan Vega

Lunch first met Suicide — the duo of late vocalist Alan Vega and multi-instrumentalist Martin Rev — in the 1970s, after moving from Rochester to New York City as a teenager. She formed a lifelong bond with the pair, often joining them on stage to perform ‘Frankie Teardrop’, and later recording a duet for Vega’s solo album Sniper.

Since Vega’s passing in 2016, Lunch has performed several Suicide tribute shows, but has never brought them to New Zealand.

events.id: Feast Your Eyes | Ferris Bueller's Day Off-5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Feast Your Eyes | Ferris Bueller's Day Off
events.venue: 5 Park Rd, Miramar, Wellington 6022, New Zealand
events.long_description:
40th Anniversary Screening
--- Ferris Bueller (Matthew Broderick) has an uncanny skill at cutting classes and getting away with it. Intending to make one last duck-out before graduation, Ferris calls in sick, "borrows" a Ferrari, and embarks on a one-day journey through the streets of Chicago. On Ferris' trail is high school principal Rooney (Jeffrey Jones), determined to catch him in the act. ---
Upgrade your ticket to the FEAST YOUR EYES (FYE) experience which includes themed drink and dessert.
At Feast Your Eyes, cinephiles and foodies will be treated to a movie and food experience in the cinema at the perfect after dinner event!
Admit only tickets are $15, but you can upgrade to a Feast Your Eyes tickets for $45. All Feast Your Eyes tickets include a themed dessert* and cocktail to enjoy during the film.
The upstairs bar will open at 7:15pm for cocktail and dessert collection. The pre-show kicks off at 7:40pm and the feature begins at 7:50pm.
*No dietaries catered for.

events.id: LYDIA LUNCH performs the songs of SUICIDE & ALAN VEGA | Meow (as part of Loemis), Wellington-17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: LYDIA LUNCH performs the songs of SUICIDE & ALAN VEGA | Meow (as part of Loemis), Wellington
events.venue: 17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
New York - No Wave Queen, Lydia Lunch – returns to Australia to perform the songs of synth – punk band, Suicide and Alan Vega.
Lydia will be joined on stage by Andrew Coates of much loved Melbourne electro trio, Black Cab to commemorate the 10th anniversary of Alan Vega’s passing.
Lunch originally met Suicide – the duo of late vocalist Alan Vega and multi-instrumentalist Martin Rev – in the 1970s, when Lunch moved to New York City from Rochester as a teenager. She formed a lifelong bond with the pair, often joining them on-stage to perform the song ‘Frankie Teardrop’ and recording a duet as part of Vega’s solo album Sniper. Since Vega’s passing in 2016, Lunch has performed several Suicide tribute shows, most often with Marc Hurtado.
Having previously never performed the show in Australia, Lydia surprised fans on a night off in Melbourne during her 2024 Spoken Word tour with an impromptu appearance at the Tote. It sold out in 3 days so she decided to bring it back for Vega’s anniversary in 2026.
Passionate, sexual, confrontational and bold, Lunch, Vega and Rev are all cut from the same grimy cloth of 1970s New York. This show is more than a tribute. It’s an offering.

events.id: MON Wellington - Empower Her Networking - Money Mindset & Wealth Building-The Arborist Rooftop Bar,166 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: MON Wellington - Empower Her Networking - Money Mindset & Wealth Building
events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar,166 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
MON Wellington - Empower Her Networking - Money Mindset & Wealth Building
Empower Her, female networking group that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences

About this Event

WELLINGTON - The Arborist Rooftop Bar
Monthly Topic: Money Mindset & Practical Wealth Building
Hosted by:
Money is one of the most loaded topics in business, and yet it's one of the least talked about. This month we're changing that. We're looking at the beliefs that shape how we earn, charge, spend, and save, and exploring what practical wealth building actually looks like for women in business. Because financial confidence isn't just about knowing the numbers. It's about understanding the story you've been telling yourself about money, and deciding whether it's still one worth keeping.
Want to get your ticket for FREE?
Are you a woman in business looking for a supportive community to help you connect, grow, and learn?
As an Inner Circle member, you can attend as many of our networking hubs as you like for FREE! This includes our hubs across Auckland, NZ, Australia and the UK. Enjoy unlimited access to our netwokring events and take full advantage of the community and networking opportunities.
Join the Inner Circle to unlock even more benefits:
Monthly Masterclasses: Learn from industry experts on topics that will elevate your business and mindset.
Accountability Calls: Stay on track with our supportive community and achieve your goals.
Personal Branding Opportunities: Create your own masterclasses and shine as an expert.
Exclusive Discounts: Enjoy special rates on Empower Her events and live workshops.
Vibrant Community: Share your journey, get support, and celebrate your successes with empowering women.
now and become part of a wonderful community of women in business who are ready to build their dream life and business.
Empower Her is a global female networking group, that offers a welcoming and safe environment for women of all business backgrounds and experiences.
Whether you're just starting out with new business ideas or have years of experience under your belt, all are welcome! Empower Her provides a safe space where you can share your business with others, connect with like-minded women in business, and prioritise collaboration over competition.
Our groups value the power of connection, and we believe that together, we can achieve more than we ever could alone. That's why we're committed to fostering a supportive community that empowers each member to reach their full potential. Our groups are focused on connection, inspiring stories, celebrating each other's successes and sharing our vulnerable moments.
Join our free community
The Inner Circle
Follow us on Instagram
Empower Her Co-Founders Victoria and Charlotte are highly passionate and dedicated to empowering female leaders in the business world. By joining Empower Her, you will not only expand your professional network but also gain access to a supportive community of like-minded individuals who are committed to helping each other succeed.Find out more about us and check up our upcoming events on our website.

events.id: Rewa Rewa Station Craft Day-Rewa Rewa Station, 166 Rewa Rewa Road, Tinui, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Rewa Rewa Station Craft Day
events.venue: Rewa Rewa Station, 166 Rewa Rewa Road, Tinui, Masterton
events.long_description:
Our on-farm Craft Open Days are aimed primarily at knitters, spinners, crocheters and other fibre crafters.

Nestled in the small rural community of Tinui is Rewa Rewa Station. Operating as a hill country Sheep & Beef breeding farm with a strong focus on NZ Wool & Fibre. Romney, Corriedale cross Romney, Polwarth & Gotland sheep provide the fleeces to create fibre products for crafters, which are sold via the Rewa Rewa Station website, and at Fibre Markets. Our Studio Shop is also open during Craft Days or by appointment outside of these days.

Our Craft Days allow you to work on your own project, seek advice, learn a new fibre skill or maybe just spend time with fellow fibre crafters while potentially ticking off a project or two in the process.

Please bring your knitting, crocheting, spinning, hand sewing, or any project you are working on and enjoy a drive in the country!

Coffee, tea and snacks provided, but bring your lunch and stay awhile.

Everyone is welcome, the more the merrier!

Note: Craft Days are primarily held on the 3rd Saturday of every mont,h but occasionally we do have to juggle the dates.

events.id: French Film Festival - Leave One Day-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Leave One Day
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Making waves in 2025 as the first-ever debut feature selected to open the Cannes Film Festival, Amélie Bonnin’s joyously entertaining musical romance stars break-out singer/songwriter Juliette Armanet as a talented Parisian chef forced to reconnect with her small-town upbringing during an unexpected trip home.
Cécile (Armanet) – a fan-favourite Top Chef winner – is about to finally realise her dream and open her own restaurant when her father, the cook at a small rural truck stop, falls unwell.

Cécile must deal with a return to her roots that is more complicated than she imagined; when she runs into her teenage first love (César Award-winner Bastien Bouillon), old memories and sensations resurface... and then her fiancé, who senses something’s wrong, shows up.
Evocatively depicting regional France and the complications of family life with tenderness and wit, Leave One Day interweaves music naturally into the story, blending several classics with more recent hits by artists such as Stromae and Céline Dion.

Armanet, herself one of France’s most popular contemporary artists, performed John Lennon’s “Imagine” during the Paris Olympic Games’ opening ceremony and proves to be a shining discovery, in this warm-hearted and completely endearing gem.

events.id: French Film Festival - Couture-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - Couture
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
Angelina Jolie turns in a career-best performance in this immaculate drama set in the high stakes fashion world.

Taking place during the frenzy of Paris Fashion week, Jolie plays American filmmaker Maxine Walker, commissioned to direct a film for a prestigious label.

After receiving sudden, life-changing news, Maxine crosses paths with two other women: Ada (Anyier Anei), a young South Sudanese model who has escaped her predetermined destiny to make a name for herself, and makeup artist Angéle (Ella Rumpf, Marguerite’s Theorem) who quietly dreams of a different life.

While their backgrounds couldn’t be more different, the three women form a strong bond. Amid the glamorous scenes of haute couture, each must make choices small and significant,
and confront major career decisions.

Anchored by Jolie’s memorable portrayal, the intertwining journeys of these three women are supported by excellent turns from Louis Garrel (The Three Musketeers) and Vincent Lindon (Another World).

events.id: Descendents tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Descendents tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: SBM Presents
Celebrating 30 Years of Everything Sucks – June 2026
So Cal punk royalty Descendents return to Australia and New Zealand this June for the Run Down Under tour, celebrating 30 years of Everything Sucks.
Originally released in 1996, Everything Sucks remains a pivotal release not only in the band’s catalogue, but in the genre they helped define. The album captured the urgency and directness that has underpinned Descendents’ influence since their beginning way back in 1978.
While the tour marks the anniversary of Everything Sucks and will focus heavily on tracks from the album, fans can of course expect a career-spanning set, including live staples that have cemented Descendents as one of punk rock’s most consistent and respected acts.

events.id: French Film Festival - A Nun in the City-The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: French Film Festival - A Nun in the City
events.venue: The Screening Room, 435 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description: After twenty years of devotion to her convent, Sister Lucie’s (Marilou Berry) quiet life is upended when she is separated from her sisterhood during a group outing. While trying to find her way home, she spots a familiar face on a television screen: her teenage sweetheart, now accused of a high-profile murder. Convinced of his innocence, she makes the impulsive decision to try to help him. Lucie’s disappearance has caused panic back at the convent, and the formidable Mother Superior Henriette (Isabelle Nanty, Amélie) sends two nuns to track her down and bring her back. A Nun in the City is a heartwarming, comedic story where faith, freedom and past love collide with surprising results.

events.id: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting at Kumutoto Forest, Kelburn-Rubix Bar, Salamanca Rd, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Awa After Dark: Spotlighting at Kumutoto Forest, Kelburn
events.venue: Rubix Bar, Salamanca Rd, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Explore your local awa with freshwater experts from our Wai Connection team at this spotlighting event at Kumutoto Forest, Kelburn. With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi to your local waterway to ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat. Learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and some of the ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa. Due to the delicate nature of this awa the observations will be stream-side as we walk along the banks and bridges of the stream.
This event is in collaboration with the Kahotea Kaitiaki, a restoration group that has been working on restoring and enhancing the biodiversity within and around the Kahotea awa.
Book here: https://events.humanitix.com/awa-after-dark-spotlighting...
Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.
This event is free, but bookings are essential. These events are very popular, please let us know if you have booked and can no longer make it so we can reallocate your spot.
Outdoor event:
This event is outdoors, please dress appropriately (gumboots and warm clothing recommended). If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.
Location:
The streams we spotlight often don't have an 'address'. On the day before the event, we'll email you all the details you need to find our exact meeting location on the night.
What is spotlighting?
Ever played spotlight with a fish? Heaps of our native freshwater critters are nocturnal. Scientists often visit freshwater areas at nighttime with a torch, it’s the best time to see exactly who’s living in our awa!

events.long_description:
Awa After Dark: Spotlighting at Kumutoto Forest, Kelburn
Explore your local awa with freshwater experts from our Wai Connection team at this spotlighting event at Kumutoto Forest, Kelburn. With your gumboots and a torch, head off on an evening hīkoi to your local waterway to ‘spotlight’ and observe freshwater species in their natural habitat. Learn about the health of our waterways, freshwater conservation efforts in your neighbourhood, and some of the ways that you could become a kaitiaki of your local awa. Due to the delicate nature of this awa the observations will be stream-side as we walk along the banks and bridges of the stream.

This event is in collaboration with the Kumutoto Restoration Project, a restoration group that has been working on restoring and enhancing the biodiversity within and around the ngahere and awa at Kumutoto.

Book here: https://events.humanitix.com/awa-after-dark-spotlighting-at-kumutoto

Awa After Dark events take place in the early evening, they’re perfect for the whole whānau.

This event is free, but bookings are essential. These events are very popular, please let us know if you have booked and can no longer make it so we can reallocate your spot.

Outdoor event:
This event is outdoors, please dress appropriately (gumboots and warm clothing recommended). If the weather is really bad, we'll let you know we’re cancelling via the email you've provided us.

Location:
The streams we spotlight often don't have an 'address'. On the day before the event, we'll email you all the details you need to find our exact meeting location on the night.

What is spotlighting?
Ever played spotlight with a fish? Heaps of our native freshwater critters are nocturnal. Scientists often visit freshwater areas at nighttime with a torch, it’s the best time to see exactly who’s living in our awa!

What is Wai Connection?
Wai Connection is a national programme delivered by Mountains to Sea Wellington aimed at connecting local communities to their waterways to improve them. We support communities and mana whenua with the knowledge, tools and expertise to monitor and care for their local freshwater places.

events.id: Bloomsday 2026-25 Tinakori Road, New Zealand 6011,25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Bloomsday 2026
events.venue: 25 Tinakori Road, New Zealand 6011,25 Tinakori Rd, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bloomsday 2026
Join us to celebrate Bloomsday!

June 16 is the day on which James Joyce’s groundbreaking Modernist novel, 'Ulysses', is set. In Dublin there is a whole festival dedicated to Bloomsday and it is marked all around the world with events just like this one.

Cherie Jacobson, Director of Katherine Mansfield House & Garden, will share her experience of Bloomsday in Dublin last year, and Jane Connolly, Ambassador of Ireland, will share a reading from Ulysses. Food and music are important in 'Ulysses', so we’ll also be offering a taste of both.

Participants in Bloomsday often dress up, from a simple straw boater like James Joyce wore or the bowler hat sported by his character Leopold Bloom, to full Edwardian garb. So don’t be shy about dressing for the occasion!

Katherine Mansfield and James Joyce met for a cup of tea in Paris; now you can meet in the birthplace of one Modernist icon, to celebrate the work of another.

This event is free but registration is required via our website due to limited space in the house. Please note that this event will take place upstairs, see the Accessibility page of our website for more information.

In partnership with the Embassy of Ireland, New Zealand.

events.long_description:
Community Conversation with Richie Hardcore
We’re proud to partner with Ōrongomai Marae, Kōkiri Whatu team, and Greg Finnigan to bring educator and advocate Richie Hardcore to Upper Hutt for an inspiring, powerful conversation on leading healthy relationships in a social media world.

Richie inspires communities, especially young people, to challenge stereotypes, to rethink masculinity and mental health, and to embrace resilience.

Free event. Kai (food), tea, and coffee provided – with thanks to Ōrongomai Marae and Kōkiri Marae.

events.id: Kelly Moran-Old St Paul's, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Kelly Moran
events.venue: Old St Paul's, 34 Mulgrave St, Pipitea, Wellington
events.long_description:
An accomplished and highly sought-after composer, Moran has collaborated with visionary contemporaries including FKA Twigs, Yves Tumor, The Avalanches, Kelsey Lu, Oneohtrix Point Never, and classical musician Margaret Leng Tan. Her unique strand of experimental piano compositions which conjure hypnotic textures and dramatic compositional arcs have traversed end of year lists across classical, avant-garde and metal genres.

Her newest critically acclaimed album "Moves in the Field" is out now. The New York Times said of the album "a softhearted but steel-skinned set of pieces that are as rapturous as a waterfall or as delicate as vapor". Kelly makes her debut New Zealand peformance at Lōemis festival.

events.id: Red Rocks Night Walk-Owhiro Bay, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Red Rocks Night Walk
events.venue: Owhiro Bay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Come along with us to walk the South Coast of Wellington. Park in the main car park at the end of Ōwhiro Bay Parade (where freedom campers stay)
Dogs are welcome on this walk. We will head out and return back to where we started from.
The photos (not mine) show just how amazing it can be along this rugged stretch of coast so let’s hope for stunning weather and clear skies.
The must haves on this walk is a good head torch and comfortable walking shoes.
Poop bags if you are bringing a dog.
Chloe and I look forward to seeing you in the carpark ready to go at 6pm
I have included a map where to park in the event photos for those who need it.

events.id: DONT LOOK NOW-BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: DONT LOOK NOW
events.venue: BATS Theatre,1 Kent Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
DONT LOOK NOW
Following a successful and critically acclaimed season last year, Don't Look Now is a collection of short plays about a changing climate. Focusing on empowering community, they ask: how can we make an impactful difference to the climate crisis? 🫵

Performed in different styles, including absurdist and naturalistic drama, the plays follow multiple perspectives you’d be able to see within our own community: from a man facing the impending doom of rising waters in a small Pacific nation, a nurse arrested for attending a demonstration, to an elderly woman protesting the felling of a majestic native tree.

When the dynamics of families and communities struggle with the mixed messages from the media and from those in power, we see the potential of theatre to start discussions, to open up new possibilities, to imagine solutions and a better future together.

As part of the Don’t Look Now season, join us for two FREE mid-season events, including:

📚 Don’t Look Now: Book Launch on Wednesday 17th June at 5:15pm and a post-show
🎙️ Open Forum on Thursday 18th June where the Theatre for Climate Team will host an open panel discussion about the climate crisis, featuring a panel of environmental experts and national political figures.

ORIGIN has carved their legacy with a sound that blends technical extremity, old-school brutality, and forward-thinking composition — influencing an entire generation of death metal bands.

events.name: Watercolour Paint and Wine Night - Watercolour Hydrangea
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Turn wine into watercolour! Take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other, and let’s paint a watercolour masterpiece!

Join us for a beginner's watercolour painting event full of fluid colour mixing and delicate brushwork where you’ll craft your own bespoke watercolour artwork. One of our watercolour experts will guide step-by-step through the watercolour process and will encourage you to add your own unique touch.

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality watercolour paper, watercolour paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night, you will leave with your own watercolour painting!

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.id: The Marriage of Figaro-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Marriage of Figaro
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Written when Mozart was operating at the top of his powers, The Marriage of Figaro is considered one of the great operas. New Zealand Opera’s production in 2026 promises to bring the 240-year-old masterpiece to life in a sumptuous setting. 
Picking up narratively where The Barber of Seville left off, The Marriage of Figaro is renowned for its wit and musical sophistication. It follows Figaro and his bride-to-be, Susannah, on their wedding day. Figaro’s employer, Count Almaviva, tries to seduce Susanna, so the engaged couple seek revenge. The cast boasts an unbelievable wealth of talent from Aotearoa and Australia, including runner-up at the 2022 Lexus Song Quest Felicity Tompkins, Julien Van Mellaerts, and James Clayton. 
Trans-Tasman creative duo Lindy Hume and Tracy Grant Lord take the helm with direction and design while Orchestra Wellington and conductor James Judd lead from the pit. This season offers opera at its most gorgeous, its most vivid, its most unforgettable.

events.name: Hindi Language Classes
events.venue: Sheila's Indian Kitchen, Ashton Fitchett Dr, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn Hindi language in 8 to 12 weeks. Travelling to India? Hindi will help you explore and give you invaluable experience of incredible India.

The lesson is approximately 90 mins long one on one teaching, and I teach anyone according to your ability. Can you please let me know how much do you know about the Hindi language and why do you want to learn.

Example: are you planning to travel to India? Or just interested in learning a new language?
We teach basic writing, reading and speaking in 6 sessions, and also teach more advanced levels if anyone wants to learn more. Classes are available everyday. Day and time can be arranged according to our availability.

If the time and cost of these lessons are prohibiting you from learning the language then please let me know and I can definitely work something out for keen Hindi learners.

Email: hindiclass@xtra.co.nz
Txt: 02102697038
events.description:

We teach basic writing, reading and speaking in 6 sessions, and also teach more advanced levels if anyone wants to learn more.
Classes are available everyday.

events.id: Art Classes - Term 2 - Creative Kid's Art Lab - 2026-Art Lab, 6 Rainbow Court, Raumati-Event Finder
events.name: Art Classes - Term 2 - Creative Kid's Art Lab - 2026
events.venue: Art Lab, 6 Rainbow Court, Raumati
events.long_description:
Sculpt Animals from Clay - This term we continue with animals as our inspiration. We will use clay and I will show you how to make an animal of your choice. It will get fired in the kiln (clay oven) after which you will paint them with glaze (clay paint made of glass) and you can take it home.

Website: creativekids.co.nz

Classes start from May 13 or 14th, 2026 - Wed or Thurs   (3.30 - 5.15pm) for 4 non-consecutive classes (this allows for clay to be baked in the kiln and returned)

Wednesday : 5 - 8 yr olds
Thursday: 9 - 12 yr olds

Theme: Sculpt Animals from Clay

This is a non-consecutive set of four classes for juniors (5-8yrs) and seniors (9-12yrs). Check the dates.

    For more details visit the website... www.creativekids.co.nz/classes

Payment is by credit card (no fees) - Visa/Mastercard

Refunds up to 7 days before

events.id: Boost Your Business: Building Your Business & Future Wealth-The Dowse Art Museum and Dowse Square,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Boost Your Business: Building Your Business & Future Wealth
events.venue: The Dowse Art Museum and Dowse Square,45 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Boost Your Business: Building Your Business & Future Wealth
Boost Your Business: Building Your Business & Future Wealth with the team from ANZ

About this Event

Boost Your Business Series – Lower Hutt 2026
Free Workshop Series for Business Owners, Entrepreneurs & Our Wider Community
Being self-employed comes with freedom and opportunity, but it also comes with the responsibility of planning for your future, your family, and your long-term financial goals.
Join us for Part 2 of our Business Finance Workshop Series with the team from ANZ, as they take a deeper dive into building financial stability, planning ahead, and creating pathways toward goals like home ownership and long-term wealth while running your own business.
This practical and empowering session is designed to help business owners better understand how to position themselves financially for the future while balancing the realities of self-employment.
Session Four (PART 2): Building Your Business & Future Wealth with the team from ANZ
Come and learn directly from industry professionals about the key financial skills every business owner should know to help grow and strengthen their business.
What you’ll learn:
– Planning financially for your future as a business owner
– Understanding pathways toward home ownership while self-employed
– Building healthy financial habits and long-term goals
– Understanding lending, financial readiness, and what banks look for
– Tools and strategies to support financial wellbeing for you and your family
Whether you’re newly self-employed, growing your business, or planning for your family’s future, this session will provide practical insights and guidance to help you move forward with confidence.
Who Should Attend:
– Small business owners
– Side hustlers and aspiring entrepreneurs
– Self-employed individuals and contractors
– Families wanting to build toward future financial goals
– Anyone interested in strengthening their financial future
Time:
Doors open: 5:30PM
Workshops: 6:00PM – 7:30PM
Networking: 7:30PM-8:00PM
Cost:
Free for our business community
Food & Refreshments:
Enjoy dinner provided by the amazing team at Amilagi’s Catering, come straight from work and we’ll take care of the rest.
Proudly Delivered By:
Wellington Pasifika Business Network
in partnership with Hutt City Council and WellingtonNZ

events.id: The Birds of Netherfield (Austen Role Playing)-The Green Man Pub, Cnr of Willeston & Victoria Sts, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Birds of Netherfield (Austen Role Playing)
events.venue: The Green Man Pub, Cnr of Willeston & Victoria Sts, Wellington
events.long_description:
Play Good Society (a Jane Austen role playing game) with your Facilitator, Johnny Bowtie.

Injecting a little whimsy into this year's diversity theme, we look to the works of Kenneth Graham, Beatrix Potter, & Brian Jacques to provide a diverse yet familiar society for this session.

In Bird Society, as in Austen, the mating dance revolves around courting a suitable match (of any species) to marry & lay eggs with – one who hopefully brings land, wealth, and social standing. Here gentlebirds earn income from their estates, rather than sullying their feathers with coarse labor. Seeds are the principal currency amongst birdkind.

Ticket includes game play and table snacks. Drinks & further food is additional.

events.id: Café Scientifiqué — How Nature Inspires Modern Medicine-Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Café Scientifiqué — How Nature Inspires Modern Medicine
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for a Café Scientifique hosted by Pūtaiao ki te Pāpori—School of Science in Society.

Drawing on Pacific Island knowledge and western scientific approaches, a chemist and a biologist share insight about the potential of marine organisms for the development of modern medicines.

Featuring Dr Helen Woolner and Associate Professor Rob Keyzers.

Café Scientifique is a place where, for the price of a cup of coffee or a glass of wine, anyone can come to explore the latest ideas in science and technology in a relaxed, friendly environment.

The evening will start with a short introduction from the speakers, followed by a question-and-answer conversation.

Doors open at 5.30 pm, talks begin at 5.45 pm. All welcome—free entry, nibbles provided.

Please note: anyone under the age of 18 must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian.
For further information, please contact scienceinsociety@vuw.ac.nz.

events.id: The Racket Lounge-Bedlam and Squalor, Upstairs, 18 Garrett Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Racket Lounge
events.venue: Bedlam and Squalor, Upstairs, 18 Garrett Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Grab a spot on our couch and enjoy the noise at The Racket Lounge - a chill classical music series showcasing up-and-coming Pōneke musicians.
If you’ve never been to a classical concert before, or if you have and now you want to see talented emerging musicians experimenting and playing what they want to play, then The Racket Lounge is for you. Over the past two seasons we’ve seen classical covers, jazz ensembles, video game soundtracks, original compositions, loop pedals, poetry and much more. Our 2026 season is shaping up to be just as epic, plus with your tickets starting at just $5 you can afford to take a chance on something new.
Each concert will feature a different line up of musicians and genres, to be announced a week before each session. If you’re a musician looking to perform at The Racket Lounge, then head to the New Zealand String Quartet’s website to see how to apply.
So, we’ll see you at Bedlam and Squalor to make a racket!
The Racket Lounge is created and presented by the New Zealand String Quartet Trust - Te Rōpū Tūrū o Aotearoa, New Zealand’s longest-running professional string quartet. With support from Wellington City Council.
Our ticket prices range from $5 to $100, so just pick the ticket price you feel like paying. At The Racket Lounge, we believe everyone should have access to high-quality but affordable art. If you're a bit strapped for cash right now, but still want a good night out, no worries! Just book yourself a $5 ticket! If you can afford to pay a little more and want to do some good for your community, then pick one of our higher ticket prices and rest assured that your dollars are going towards funding great, accessible art in your city.
All proceeds from ticket sales will go directly into presenting the next Racket Lounge, including paying musicians and artists, hiring instruments and marketing costs. Plus, by choosing a higher price point, you're ensuring that we're able to continue to offer tickets at a lower price point for those who need it.
PLEASE NOTE: Bedlam & Squalor is a licensed venue, so under 18s must be accompanied by a parent or legal guardian. Everyone must bring ID.

This class is run by the owner operator of a Wellington based Engineering firm with over 20 years of Welding experience. Participants will get to learn the theory and safety aspects of welding before gaining experience MIG welding Tee Joints, Lap Joints and But Joints. Some of the joints will then be cut through to inspect and learn more about how to improve their quality and strength.

Only 6 students per class.

What people are saying about this class...

Tim Blair's intro to welding course is 10/10. Concise, considered, and targeted course material and practice for the beginner to give you confidence for self-directed learning.

_______________________________________________________________________________

Did a beginners welding course with Tim and it is absolutely worth it. Gives you good base knowledge of what a good weld is and whatba bad one is and why and gives you the confidence to give it a go. Good tips and tricks as well with proper hands on leaning. Thanks Tim and MakeRoom Wellington!

_______________________________________________________________________________

Tim Blair is an excellent tutor. Pace was perfect, content excellently presented, really approachable. Incredible patience and feedback. Really recommend for women especially wanting to learn the art of welding, Tim's a good guy!
I'll be back for advanced lessons.

events.id: Quiz Night-Eastbourne Sports & Services Club, South End Tuatoru Street, Eastbourne, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Quiz Night
events.venue: Eastbourne Sports & Services Club, South End Tuatoru Street, Eastbourne, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Come join us for the monthly ritual (third Wednesday of the month) on a Wednesday night at Eastbourne Sports & Services Club, where you and your crew will negotiate five rounds of amazing trivia with Gee Quiz.

Your team's street cred, life smarts and common sense will go a long way to ensuring success on the night.

Add to the mix delicious food and an incredible vibe, it's the place to be on a Tuesday night with plenty of time for laughter, banter and merrymaking!

The quiz kicks off at 7pm and runs for roughly 2 hours. So, grab your buddies and come on down and join us for one of Eastbourne's best quiz nights – ESSC style!

events.id: Alex Trask Quintet-Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Alex Trask Quintet
events.venue: Garibaldi Club, 118 Tory St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The Undercurrent presents the Alex Trask Quintet!
Local saxophonist Alex Trask presents a set of original music for two horns and rhythm. Inspired by folk music from around the world and infused with jazz performance practice, these tunes have a simplicity in common. Free improvised sections contrast carefully arranged melodies, allowing creativity and structure to coexist. The fiddle-esque melodies and straightforward harmonies create an open and inviting sound that changes depending on the musicians on stage. Performing this music as a group invites each member to paint their private imaginings, creating a unique collective folklore.
This is the first of a two gig series where the band will be exploring some new compositions. On this gig the band will perform some previously unheard songs, come and catch the debut of this new set.
Wednesday June 17
Doors 7pm
Music 7:30pm
$25 ($10 unwaged)
Tickets available online and on the door!
https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../Alex-Trask-Quintet.utr
Alex's Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/alexmtrask/

events.id: Mei Semones-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Mei Semones
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Brooklyn-based Mei writes songs in both English and Japanese, celebrating a passion for life, family, music, and more, sentiments which can be heard on her sublime debut album Animaru.

Through continuously honing in on her signature fusion of indie rock, bossa nova, jazz and chamber pop in a way that highlights her technical prowess on guitar, the 24-year-old Brooklyn-based songwriter and guitarist is quickly establishing herself as an innovative musical force.

"A potent talent" – Clash Magazine

events.id: NZ Opera | The Marriage of Figaro-St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: NZ Opera | The Marriage of Figaro
events.venue: St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Love, lies and glorious chaos...

NZ Opera’s 2021 season of The Marriage of Figaro – the world-premiere of a sumptuous staging by the superstar trans-Tasman creative duo of Lindy Hume and Tracy Grant Lord – remains a high-water mark in the company’s history. For its centrepiece mid-year season, with a cast boasting an almost-unbelievable wealth of talent from Aotearoa and Australia, NZ Opera is thrilled to finally bring this incredible opera to the fair city of Te Whanganui-a-Tara Wellington.

Picking up narratively where The Barber of Seville left off, The Marriage of Figaro is renowned for its wit and musical sophistication – truly an example of the legendary Wolfgang Amadeus Mozart operating at the height of his powers.

With an outstanding principal cast joined on stage by the Freemasons Foundation New Zealand Opera Chorus and accompanied by Orchestra Wellington – conducted, for the first time, by James Judd – this season offers opera at its most gorgeous, its most vivid, its most unforgettable.

events.id: Eyegum (free) Wednesdays: Bella Mae & awkwatch + Charmort-171 Cuba Street, Wellington-Facebook
events.name: Eyegum (free) Wednesdays: Bella Mae & awkwatch + Charmort
events.venue: 171 Cuba Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Welly cold may be setting in but this line-up is HOT!
8:30 Bella Mae
9:15 awkwatch
10:00 Charmort
Bella Mae: Solo project of rapper-producer Piero Grosman, Bella strays the beaten path in search of sincerity. On the fringes of trap, folk, lo-fi and indie his music remains consistent in it's tight lyricism and romantic nature.
awkwatch arrives with a presence that's unmistakably his own. Whether riding sparse, bass-heavy production or something more off-kilter, awkwatch leans fully into shaping the future sounds rather than chasing trends.
You love dancing. You loving moving. You love charmort.
Artist links:
Bella Mae: https://www.instagram.com/bellamaelovesu/
Awkwatch: https://www.instagram.com/awkwatch/
Charmort: https://www.instagram.com/charmort4u/
Pōneke is home to an incredible diversity of artists and music lovers.
We want Eyegum Wednesdays to be a space that everyone can enjoy with care and respect.
We have no room for transphobia or racism, sexism, homophobia and disregarding consent.
If you see or experience anything concerning, no matter how small, please let our crew or San Fran staff know.
Lovingly sponsored by San Fran, Abandoned Brewery and Wellington City Council.
