events.long_description:
Hutt City Night Market -18 June 2026
Experience the sights, smells, and flavors of a traditional night market right in the heart of Lower Hutt this autumn. 🍂

The Hutt City Night Market is an international celebration of food, bringing together a delicious mix of cuisines from around the world.

Stroll through bustling stalls, grab a cozy bite (or three), bring your friends and family, stay for a picnic, and enjoy the crisp autumn air in a vibrant community setting.

@wellingtonstreeteats

See you there under the autumn skies! 🍁

events.long_description:
Northland Wing-Foil & Windsurf Surfari Holiday
Northland Wing-Foil & Windsurf Surfari Holiday is in Doubtless Bay of Northland, New Zealand. Our most popular Winterless North Surfari Trip!
Choose a three night or five night Northland Wing-Foil and Windsurf Holiday to the stunning Doubtless Bay and Karikari Peninsula. The Karikari Peninsula with its white sand beaches and crystal clear water provides opportunities to foil in any wind direction. Northland is known as the ‘winterless north’ with mild temperatures and reliable wind conditions to suit all wing foil and windsurf skill levels. The perfect winter break to give yourself a welcome vacation.
Our Surfari Trips are all inclusive, meaning you don’t need to do a thing except book the trip and turn up!
Quality accommodation
Single, double or twin share available. Limited to 6 guests.

Local knowledge
We ensure you will be at the right spot at the right time.
Self Drive
You have the freedom to travel as it suits you.
Fly in?

Photos

events.id: Emotional Mapping: Making Sense of Your Emotional Patterns-175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Emotional Mapping: Making Sense of Your Emotional Patterns
events.venue: 175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Emotional Mapping: Making Sense of Your Emotional Patterns
Have you ever noticed that when emotions arise, you tend to react in the same ways over and over again?
Some of us shut down, disconnect, or tell ourselves we’re “fine.”
Others overthink, become emotionally overwhelmed, or feel responsible for everyone else’s feelings.
These are emotional patterns, learned ways of relating to emotions that helped us cope in the past, but can leave us feeling anxious, reactive, disconnected, or exhausted.
Most of us were never taught how to understand emotions in a healthy way.
Instead, we learned to avoid feelings, get stuck in them, or stay hyper-aware of the emotional states of others.
Emotional literacy offers another path.
It helps us recognise what we’re feeling, understand the needs underneath those feelings, and respond with more awareness and self-compassion.
In this workshop, we’ll explore common emotional patterns, what they can look like in everyday life, and how we can begin shifting them over time.
You’ll leave with:
•⁠ ⁠a clearer understanding of your emotional patterns
•⁠ ⁠practical tools to help you respond differently to emotions
•⁠ ⁠greater awareness of feelings, needs, and nervous system responses
•⁠ ⁠simple ways to build more emotional steadiness and self-empathy
This is a warm, supportive space to better understand yourself, connect more deeply with your emotions, and leave with tools you can actually use.
Plus - your ticket includes a plant-based dinner & dessert! You’ll get to eat good food in good company and have a wholesome evening
Meet your host, Chanchala:
Chanchala combines her background in psychology, education, and coaching to help people make sense of their emotions. She loves sharing practical ways to meet feelings, build clarity, and navigate overwhelm with confidence and self-compassion.
................................................................................................
Booking for Thursday is recommended!
For more details and to book please go here--
tinyurl.com/ThursdayOnPurposeEvents...............................................................................................
Make Thursdays your night to connect and take a break from your week. Events range from creative workshops, wellness courses to entertaining experiences. All within a down-to-earth and welcoming space. Each event wraps up with a plant-based dinner and dessert.
...............................................................................................
Bhakti Lounge
1/175 Vivian Street

events.id: Tanzanian Dinner-Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Tanzanian Dinner
events.venue: Le Ciel Bakery, 100 Tory Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Tanzanian Cuisine Dinner – Returning for a Limited Season

After winning Best Event at last year’s WOAP, our celebrated Tanzanian Cuisine Dinner is back — for two weeks only.

Experience an evening designed for discovery, comfort, and generous sharing. Guests are invited to order as many dishes as they wish from a specially curated menu showcasing classic Tanzanian flavours, traditions, and vibrant spices. This is not a set menu, but a relaxed dining experience that encourages exploration at your own pace.

To complement the food, an optional pairing menu featuring wines, beers, and thoughtfully crafted non-alcoholic beverages will be available each evening.

Event Dates:
May 7th, 8th, 9th
May 14th, 15th, 16th
June dates added due to high demand!

Time:
6:00 PM – 8:30 PM

Join us for a warm, flavour-driven dining experience that quickly became one of our most loved and awarded events.

Seats are limited.

We are offering a tasting menu with 5 tasting plates for $62 per person, or the main event, a 5-course dinner for $82 per person.

OPTION 1: TASTING MENU
5 Tasting Plates — $62 per person
Option to order extra plates at additional cost!

Course 1: Mshikaki ya Ng’ombe
Charcoal-grilled beef skewers
Marinated in garlic, ginger, lemon and pili pili.
Served with fresh kachumbari (tomato–onion salad).

Course 2: Viazi Karai
Spiced potato fritters (Zanzibar style)
Golden fried, dusted with masala,
served with tamarind-coconut chutney.

Course 3: Maharage ya Nazi
Slow-simmered red kidney beans in coconut cream
Fragrant with turmeric and cardamom.
Served with turmeric basmati rice.

Course 4: Kuku wa Kupaka
Grilled chicken in rich coconut curry sauce
Charred first, then simmered in spiced coconut gravy.
Served with chapati.

Course 5: Mandazi
Traditional doughnut
with mango passion curd.

OPTION 2: FIVE-COURSE DINNER
Plated Culinary Experience — $82 per person

This version is more structured and elevated — designed like a progressive tasting journey from coast to mainland.

Amuse-Bouche
Mini Mandazi
Lightly spiced coconut doughnut
with mango and passionfruit curd.

Entrée
Zanzibar Seafood Supu
Prawns and fish in coconut broth
with lime leaf and coriander.

(Vegetarian alternative: Zanzibar mix)

Intermezzo
Tamarind & Lime Sorbet
A bright palate cleanser.

Main Course

Nyama Choma (Chef’s Selection)
Charcoal-grilled lamb or beef
served with fragrant Swahili pilau rice,
kachumbari and pili pili sauce.

(Vegetarian option: Mchuzi wa Mboga – seasonal vegetable skewers)

Course 4: Kuku wa Kupaka
Grilled chicken in rich coconut curry sauce
Charred first, then simmered in spiced coconut gravy.
Served with chapati.

Dessert
Spiced Chocolate & Cardamom Mousse
with candied coconut and sesame shard.

Course 4: Kuku wa Kupaka Grilled chicken in rich coconut curry sauce Charred first, then simmered in spiced coconut gravy.

events.id: Glow in the Dark Paint Party - A Glowy Night-Leroy's Bar, 2 Plimmers, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Glow in the Dark Paint Party - A Glowy Night
events.venue: Leroy's Bar, 2 Plimmers, Wellington
events.long_description:
Ultra violet lights and neon nights in Wellington. Prepare to experience Paintvine like you’ve never experienced it before – in the dark!

Paintvine After Dark is New Zealand’s original glow in the dark paint party – a completely unique night out where creativity, neon paint, and UV lights come together for an unforgettable experience. Watch your artwork come to life as fluorescent colours glow under the lights and your canvas transforms before your eyes.

Our artist guides you step-by-step, so absolutely no art experience is needed. Whether you’re planning a date night, organising a catch-up with friends, or just keen to try something different, Paintvine After Dark is all about good vibes, glowing paint, and outrageous fun from start to finish.

It’s part art class, part neon party, and absolute fun! Who knew you could have so much fun in the dark?

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, neon acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit. All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night you will leave with your own fluro painting and a fun memory to go with it.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

Whether you’re planning a date night, organising a catch-up with friends, or just keen to try something different, Paintvine After Dark is all about good vibes, glowing paint, and outrageous fun from start to finish.
Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Having freshly released his latest orchestral album 'Elegant Calamity', played on jazz radio stations across the globe, flamboyant saxophonist / multi-instrumentalist and Selmer Paris Artist Oscar Lavën presents a kaleidoscopic array of fresh new original music written for jazz quintet, featuring a heavyweight band that are equal parts sugar-sweet and ferocious:

events.id: Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Melanie Bracewell is back with a brand-new hour-long show. It took a long time to write because she got distracted by a Sudoku puzzle. Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying is about procrastination. Come along (don’t be late) to hear the Kiwi comic’s hilarious observations about avoidance. The UK comedy guide Chortle described Melanie as a “phenomenal storyteller”. Expect some wild stories as she dissects why she ‘puts things off’ for another day. 
Stand-up comedy, radio announcing, radio producer, script writing — Melanie Bracewell has found success in multiple disciplines. Her TV appearances include ‘The Cheap Seats,’ ‘Taskmaster’ (NZ), ‘Have You Been Paying Attention,’ and ‘Thank God You’re Here.’ She has won multiple awards as well, including the Billy T Award for the country’s best emerging comedian in 2018. The same year, she won Breakthrough Comedian of the Year at the NZ Comedy Guild Awards.
events.description:
Melanie Bracewell is back with a brand-new hour-long show.
It took a long time to write because she got distracted by a Sudoku puzzle.
Melanie Bracewell — Dilly Dallying is about procrastination.
Come along (don’t be late) to hear the Kiwi comic’s hilarious observations about avoidance.
The UK comedy guide Chortle described Melanie as a “phenomenal storyteller”.

events.id: REforms: Shayne P. Carter and the NZSO-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: REforms: Shayne P. Carter and the NZSO
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
It’s an amalgamation of two musical worlds, and the result is set to be mesmerising. Shayne P Carter has been active on the New Zealand popular music scene since 1979. The singer-songwriter has penned many great tunes. Now, some of those tunes are getting an orchestral treatment thanks to the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra. REforms reimagines ten of Shayne’s songs in magical orchestra colour. 
With arrangements by Tane Upjohn Beatson and conducted by Hamish McKeich, the concert promises to enhance Shayne’s songs through a new lens while keeping their original integrity. Classic songs like Randolph’s Going Home and If I Were You sit alongside new numbers like Left To Defend. Songs from Shayne’s solo album Offsider also feature. 
Shayne is best known for leading indie rock band Straitjacket Fits from 1986 to 1994, and rock band Dimmer (1995–2012). He is a member of the New Zealand Music Hall of Fame and the recipient of several Aotearoa Music Awards.

events.name: Are You Smarter Than A Drag Queen? - Trivia Night
events.venue: Scotty and Mal's Cocktail and Lounge Bar, 176 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join Rosa Violeta and Yuri Guaii, star of RuPaul’s Drag Race Down Under and The Boulet Brothers’ Dragula, as we finally answer the age-old question: Are You Smarter Than a Drag Queen?

This is a trivia night unlike any other.. packed with fabulous prizes, outrageous drag shows, chaotic banter, and questions that’ll have you questioning everything.

Grab your smartest (or stupidest) friends for a night of camp, cocktails, and competition.
Expect stunning looks, unhinged lip-syncs, and maybe even a heated debate over who really invented the Pavlova.

Think you’ve got what it takes to win? There’ll be prizes, bragging rights, and chaotic glory up for grabs - and not just for the trivia champs.
Whether you nail the answers or charm your way through the madness, you might just walk away with something fabulous… or hilariously useless.

Doors open: 6pm
Trivia starts: 6.30pm
Trivia Finishes: 9:00PM.

Seats are limited, so snatch yours now and don’t miss the most glamorous quiz night of the season!

events.long_description:
Explore Te Waka Huia Wellington Museum after hours with a special late‑night opening. Wander through the museum in a calm and relaxed atmosphere, check out the exhibits, and enjoy activities on offer.
Slip on a pair of headphones and create your own experience with our silent soundtracks, curated by DJ Marika. Choose your music. Choose your volume. Move at your own pace as you explore the museum after dark.
Get creative with craft activities, join a guided tour of our temporary exhibition, unwind in our chill-out spaces and catch up with old friends or make new ones.
This event is open to anyone wanting to explore the museum in a relaxed and friendly environment. It may feel particularly welcoming to sensory sensitive, neurodivergent and Takiwātanga Autistic visitors. To support visitor comfort, audiovisual displays will run at reduced volume. Sudden sound or visual changes will be clearly signposted.
Whether you’re coming on your own or with friends, you’ll be made welcome by our friendly staff.
Under 16-year-olds must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.

events.id: Speed Dating 45-59 and 60+-Kanteen Cafe, 7 Waterloo Quay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Speed Dating 45-59 and 60+
events.venue: Kanteen Cafe, 7 Waterloo Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Come join us for a fun night of Speed Dating! It’s a fun and safe way to meet other genuine singles looking for a real connection. We will have a private function space for this event. Price includes a drink and delicious food at the break!

Speed Daters will be split into 2 age groups which will run simultaneously. There will be an opportunity to mix with the other group during the break.

When you arrive you will get a name label and your complimentary first drink, and will have time to settle in and mingle a little. You will be Speed Dating with around 10 people over the course of the evening. You will have 7 minutes to chat to each person. You then decide if you want to meet your Speed Dates again and tick interested on your form if you do. Only if the feeling is mutual will we email you each other’s contact details.

Speed dating does not have to be stressful or frightening; you will be pleasantly surprised how fun and positive it is.

Our event is designed to be inclusive and foster open, honest communication. Not everyone you meet will be your type, but we expect you to be polite. You only have to chat to them for seven minutes!

events.id: Waenga-Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Waenga
events.venue: Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington
events.long_description:
Created by Hariata & Tamati Moriarty and directed by Jim Moriarty, 'Waenga' centres on a young wahine Māori who is arrested and finds herself in a police holding cell. Alongside her journey is Peyton, who is coming to understand what a Tangata Tiriti partnership is and Grayson, a young Māori lawyer navigating his role in the system.

Performed by an ensemble cast, including well known rangatahi Hariata and Tamati Moriarty, the play is presented in Te Rākau’s unique Theatre Marae framework, creating space for collective reflection and shared experience.

Te Rākau is the longest running Māori theatre company in Aotearoa founded in 1989.

Recommended age 12+ contains swearing and depicts some violence. Children under 12 can accompany for free with a paying adult.

events.id: REforms: Shayne P. Carter and the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra-Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Wakefield Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: REforms: Shayne P. Carter and the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, 111 Wakefield Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
The heft and glory of the NZSO collide with the Shayne P. Carter songbook for the world premiere of this formidable work. Ten tracks ‘reformed’ in orchestral technicolour while maintaining the integrity of the originals.

Classic songs like Randolph’s Going Home and If I Were You spar with new numbers like Left To Defend. Guitar and orchestra do battle on the mighty Crystalator. Songs from Carter’s solo Offsider album expand from their original two-finger piano primitivism into widescreen epicness. Other songs glower with the drama of orchestral restraint.

With arrangements by Tane Upjohn Beatson and conducted by Hamish McKeich, REforms is a singular and purposeful artistic statement; an immersive and all-encompassing performance from a world-class orchestra and one of Aotearoa’s greatest singer/songwriters.
events.description:

events.id: CHAT PILE (USA) & BEASTWARS-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: CHAT PILE (USA) & BEASTWARS
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sludgy noise superstars CHAT PILE have announced their first ever Australia & New Zealand tour for June 2026. Fusing elements of post-punk, nu metal, grunge, they have earned a cult following for their eclectic take on what they simply deem ‘noise rock.’ 
Formed in 2019, the band took their name from piles of chat, byproducts of mining which are commonly found throughout northeast Oklahoma. Their adopted stage names Raygun Busch, Luther Manhole, Stin and Cap’n Ron were conjured as a way of avoiding identification from previous employers.
Following the band’s self-released debut ‘This Dungeon Earth’, they were signed to The Flenser in 2020 and released their debut album ‘God’s Country’. The album received broad acclaim, including ‘Best New Music’ on Pitchfork. 
Their second full-length record ‘Cool World’ was releasing in 2024. They went on to headline Roadburn Festival 2025 and tour extensively, earning a reputation for an incendiary live show.
Joining Chat Pile in Sydney, Melbourne, Brisbane, Auckland & Wellington is Kiwi sludge metal band BEASTWARS. The veteran group originally formed in the late 1980s and have abided by one steadfast maxim ever since: obey the riff. It’s time. 
Dig into Chat Pile this June.

Sludgy noise superstars CHAT PILE have announced their first ever Australia & New Zealand tour for June 2026.
Fusing elements of post-punk, nu metal, grunge, they have earned a cult following for their eclectic take on what they simply deem ‘noise rock.’ Formed in 2019, the band took their name from piles of chat, byproducts of mining which are commonly found throughout northeast Oklahoma.
Their adopted stage names Raygun Busch, Luther Manhole, Stin and Cap’n Ron were conjured as a way of avoiding identification from previous employers.
Following the band’s self-released debut ‘This Dungeon Earth’, they were signed to The Flenser in 2020 and released their debut album ‘God’s Country’.
The album received broad acclaim, including ‘Best New Music’ on Pitchfork.

events.id: MOVEMENT (SECOND EDITION)-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: MOVEMENT (SECOND EDITION)
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Edgy music for edgy people.

Alternative sounds of the Eighties and beyond to make you move.

Your host is DJ Matai, known for sets spanning vintage goth, new wave and post-punk. Expect to hear the classic and the obscure. Not only a selector, but a genuine alternative music fanatic, he is also known for running Razored Raw and drumming in bands such as No Sector and Maced.

Joined by guests:

DJ Nightmare: Three decades deep and still dragging the dancefloor into the dark — DJ Nightmare is a fixture of the scene and a genuine authority on all things goth, post-punk, industrial, cold wave, new romantic, angular electronic, neo goth and synth pop, with a blast beat or two thrown in for the brave. Known to his people as Ol' Nighty, he has spent years as a promoter, radio host, and noise terrorist, and every set carries that hard-won knowledge: equal parts floor-filler and deep-cut excavation, crafted to pull even the most committed wallflowers out of the shadows. Expect the unexpected. Expect the dark, Expect to dance.

DJ Shade is an 80s deep-cut enthusiast. Digging up rare gems from New Zealand alongside obscure B-sides that span electro, new wave, post punk dancefloor cuts. Expect a carefully curated set of grooves and underground energy guaranteed to move the floor. Beyond the decks, Shade is also an active musician, performing in bands such as Whirlwind and The Mermmen.

Brought to you with the support of Creeps Record Parlour!

events.id: The Keeper's Dance: an Evening of Butoh-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Keeper's Dance: an Evening of Butoh
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
With Lime presents

Two bodies. Three Butoh dances. Infinite transformations.

Here’s something you don’t often see: two artists on opposite sides of the world building a work together — over Zoom. Rosemary Candelario is in Austin, Texas. Miki Seifert is in Wellington. For months they’ve been meeting across time zones to plan, imagine, and develop The Keeper’s Dance. They won’t be in the same room — or the same country — until Rosemary arrives in Wellington on 11 June. Then, just eight days before opening night, they’ll visit five Wellington lighthouses together and let the land shape the work.

This is how Butoh travels.

Butoh is visceral and cathartic, ethereal and poetic. A Butoh performance is not merely seen, but experienced and felt.

Followed by a post-show Q&A.
events.description:
With Lime presents Two bodies.
Three Butoh dances.
Infinite transformations.
They won’t be in the same room — or the same country — until Rosemary arrives in Wellington on 11 June.
A Butoh performance is not merely seen, but experienced and felt.

events.id: Garage Sale Presents: Caru and Brandn Shiraz-Moon Bar,167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Garage Sale Presents: Caru and Brandn Shiraz
events.venue: Moon Bar,167 Riddiford St, Newtown, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Garage Sale Presents: Caru and Brandn Shiraz
AFTER A HUGE RUN OF SHOWS OVER SUMMER, CARU & BRANDN SHIRAZ BRING THEIR MUCH HYPED LIVE SHOW BACK TO THE MIGHTY TE-WHANGANUI-A-TARA. PLAYING AT MOON BAR FOR ONE NIGHT ONLY!

PRESENTED BY GARAGE SALE

JOINED BY AN ALL-STAR CAST OF LOCAL SELECTORS:
⟡ CLOVA
⟡ NOYER & YOUNG GHO$T
⟡ ULTRAVIOLET

⌁₊˚⊹ ⊹˚₊⌁

Caru and Brandn Shiraz join forces to celebrate the sounds of UK Garage. The pair combined their unique musical styles with the 2025 release of their EP ‘Back 2 Back’, a project inspired by the pioneers of pirate radio. Their music combines both artists' respective styles to create a fresh take on the classic 2000’s dance genre, with Brandn Shiraz’s boundary pushing MC skills effortlessly complementing Caru’s refined production. The duo have had notable sets across Aotearoa, with a recent summer tour which showcased the fusion of their energy at festivals such as Northern Bass, The Others Way, Cuba Dupa and Twisted Frequency, and most recently opening for Bakey and Capo Lee.

⌁₊˚⊹ ⊹˚₊⌁

This night will be curated by Garage Sale, one of the capital’s longest running garage nights, who have chosen a range of the finest DJs in town to keep the vibes flowing. Opening things up we have Noyer and Young Gho$t the masterminds of Garage Sale, who will be easing things in with some selections across UKG, funky and grimey beats accompanied by Ghost’s bars.

Warming up for the headliner we have CLOVA, a rising star and local legend who recently released her debut album ‘Spiral’ to widespread applause. For Garage Sale she will hone in some of her favourite tracks, new and old, delivering her unique take on the sound.

Finally on closing duties we have none other than Ultraviolet, making his Garage Sale debut. A seasoned veteran with releases on Big Fresh, Echo Train and Club Unique, UV is one of the country’s best kept musical secrets, and we can’t wait to see what he pulls out the bag for this set.

Tickets are available online for an extremely reasonable $20+bf. Lock in now and prepare for a massive night of big tunes and vibes!
events.description:
Garage Sale Presents: Caru and Brandn Shiraz
⌁₊˚⊹ ⊹˚₊⌁ Caru and Brandn Shiraz join forces to celebrate the sounds of UK Garage.
The pair combined their unique musical styles with the 2025 release of their EP ‘Back 2 Back’, a project inspired by the pioneers of pirate radio.
Their music combines both artists' respective styles to create a fresh take on the classic 2000’s dance genre, with Brandn Shiraz’s boundary pushing MC skills effortlessly complementing Caru’s refined production.
⌁₊˚⊹ ⊹˚₊⌁ This night will be curated by Garage Sale, one of the capital’s longest running garage nights, who have chosen a range of the finest DJs in town to keep the vibes flowing.

events.id: Auditions - Social Climbers by Roger Hall-Atlas House, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Auditions - Social Climbers by Roger Hall
events.venue: Atlas House, 22 Ghuznee St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A group of female teachers, along with one reluctantly accompanying daughter, set off on a weekend bushwalking trip in the mountains to escape it all. But when torrential rain and a washed-out bridge leave them stranded in a remote hut for three days, tensions begin to rise.
The women’s carefully constructed social facades begin to crumble as they challenge each other, strip away professional distance, and grow into a bond of support, understanding, and friendship. This insightful and witty situation comedy explores truth, consequences, and unexpected revelations through humour, honesty, and emotional depth.
The play delves into the complexities of relationships, resilience, and the bonds of female friendship. With rich, relatable roles for women and a compelling mix of comedy and heartfelt moments, this is a wonderful opportunity to bring dynamic, authentic female characters to life on stage.
We’ll be looking to cast women who will enjoy the creative process and work collaboratively. Strong on-stage chemistry and the ability to connect authentically with others will be key.
If you’re interested in auditioning you can find more information and fill out the form at https://www.stagecraft.co.nz/auditions
KEY DATES
Callbacks if needed will be Sunday 21st June and the performance dates are Wednesday 23rd September - Saturday 3rd October.
ROLES
Maxine (The Drama Queen): 50s-60’s. Outgoing, flamboyant, and boisterous. While she can be snide and opinionated, her character undergoes a convincing emotional journey.
Kath (The Lovable Older Teacher): 50s-60’s. The “old girl” of the group. Loveable, grounded, and no-nonsense.
Emily (The Eternal Doormat): 40’s-50’s. A put-upon woman who has been a “doormat” her whole life.
Sinead (The Stroppy Daughter): Mid 20’s. Emily’s daughter. Often immature and eye rolling while harbouring a major “bombshell” revelation for her mother.
Susan (The Sensitive Idealist): Late 30s-50s Initially the cheerful “Pollyanna” of the group until the pressure-cooker environment reveals that her supposedly perfect world is actually falling apart.
Annie (The Frazzled School Counsellor): 30s-50s A calming, soothing presence throughout the play who uncovers the often-forgotten hardships and emotional weight of her profession.
events.description:

The play delves into the complexities of relationships, resilience, and the bonds of female friendship.
The “old girl” of the group.
Emily (The Eternal Doormat): 40’s-50’s.
Emily’s daughter.

events.id: Microsoft Excel - Advanced-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Microsoft Excel - Advanced
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Microsoft Excel - Advanced
For those who have completed the Intermediate course or equivalent - you must be competent using Excel formulas and formatting.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
All computer courses are run in the school computer labs and you are required to use the school desktops for the duration of the course (you are not able to use your own laptop).  Some of the courses may require you to login onto the network. 
By the end of the course, you'll have had an introduction to the more advanced features of MS Excel, including how to:
• Manage lists: sort and filter
• Create a pivot table from an existing set of data
• Create custom date formats
• Analyse data: goal seek, data tables and scenario manager 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Thai Home-Style-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Thai Home-Style
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Thai Home-Style
Thai cuisine and cooking has a key secret - the secret is that authentic Thai food balances key flavour elements. Combining sweet, salty, bitter, sour and spicy, Thai cuisine creates sumptuous and exciting flavour combinations. Come along to this half-day cooking course and learn to make some home-style Thai dishes.
Not suitable for vegetarians. All ingredients included in course fee, you just need to bring your apron, a couple of tea towels, a dishcloth and containers to take leftovers home in.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Make some  Thai dishes at home with more confidence
Use ingredients/spices/herbs to match with dishes
Apply some aspects of the Thai eating style at home or when visiting Thailand
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique And Preparation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Get the Job: STARR Interview Technique And Preparation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Get the best results in job interviews. Learn how to approach behavioural questions and impress the interview panel. Ace interviews using the potent Starr method. Use it to your advantage by answering competency focused questions with winning replies.

Meet the demand for excellence in the job market head on. Learn how to utilise the Starr technique in the way that recruiters use it. That is, to define and seek out the skills and qualities of the best candidate. Master the Starr skills and feel confident in your next job interview.

Familarise yourself with the skills needed to ace your next interview. Learn the STARR method as an approach to responding to behavioural based questions. Understand how to use concrete examples from your life as proof that you possess the experience and skills for the job you are applying for. You’ll develop the key interview skill of being able to share your accomplishments through real-life examples.

In this course, you are taught how to identify specific example situations that are unique to you. You’ll learn how to confidently talk about the tasks involved and describe how you completed the key task and met its challenges. We’ll also consider the context of jobs you are interviewing for and how to choose examples that best show the desired skills interviewers are seeking.

By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Develop a higher level of confidence in approaching job interviews.
- Identify the different components of the STARR acronym and apply them to the job interview context.
- Identify what behavioural based questions are and why recruiters use them.
- Successfully answer behavioural based interview questions by applying the STARR technique.
- Develop skills in using real life examples to show transferable skills in the workplace.

Note: You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the free course fee option.  A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

events.id: Jewellery Making - Vintage Silver Charms-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Jewellery Making - Vintage Silver Charms
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Whether you want to commemorate a special occasion, make friendship bracelets with your BFF, or craft a unique gift, you’ll come out of this workshop with jewellery to be treasured for years to come. You’ll not only produce fantastic charms but gain essential silversmithing skills such as soldering, shaping and polishing, as well as learning top tips for how to create stunning and meaningful jewellery on a budget.
 This workshop is ideal for both beginners and those returning to further develop their skills. Charm bracelets are back in fashion and these make wonderful gifts for any occasion.
 Everything you need is provided to make up to 3 silver charms. You can bring your own bracelet to adorn with the charms you make, or if you choose you can purchase one of our top-quality NZ made silver bracelets directly from the tutor in class ($25 cash please if you wish to purchase a bracelet).
 By the end of the course you’ll be able to:
Design and create a set of 3 silver charms using fine jewellery-making techniques
Use a jeweller’s saw to cut sheet silver into simple shapes
Use jeweller’s files and sandpapers to refine silver forms hammers and steel punches to imprint letters, symbols and textures into sheet silver
Use jewellery pliers to bend silver hoops and/or a soldering torch to connect charms to your bracelet
Use polishing tools to shine and finish your silver jewellery creations
Please wear closed-in footwear suitable for a workshop environment. bring a work apron if you have one or wear clothes you don’t mind getting a little grubby.  Remember to bring your reading glasses if a have them.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mindful Awareness – One-Day Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
 “If we don’t know what we’re doing, how can we do what we want?”
 A hands-on, one-day workshop blending mindfulness, neuroscience, and self-awareness to help you feel better, relate better, and live with more clarity and purpose.
 What you’ll get:
Simple tools to soothe stress and calm your system
Strategies to respond wisely instead of reacting on autopilot
Insight into your feelings, needs and inner patterns
Cutting-edge knowledge of the Vagus nerve and how it shapes your wellbeing
An intro to Parts Work: understand the different parts of you and how they work together
Practical ways to build creativity, clarity and genuine connection
Skills to feel stronger in yourself, your relationships and your work life
Everyday tools for better health, resilience and emotional balance
 Real tools. Real change. Backed by science, grounded in practice.
 Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks, and pen and paper to take notes.

events.long_description:
A hands-on, one-day workshop blending mindfulness, neuroscience, and self-awareness to help you feel better, relate better, and live with more clarity and purpose. (NZ Citizens and residents free with photo ID, all others pay $149).

Mindful Awareness – One-Day Workshop

Course is free for NZ citizens and residents (please bring photo ID when attending workshop). If you are not a NZ citizen or resident a fee of $149 will apply, please phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.

“If we don’t know what we’re doing, how can we do what we want?”

A hands-on, one-day workshop blending mindfulness, neuroscience, and self-awareness to help you feel better, relate better, and live with more clarity and purpose.

What you’ll get:

Simple tools to soothe stress and calm your system
Strategies to respond wisely instead of reacting on autopilot
Insight into your feelings, needs and inner patterns
Cutting-edge knowledge of the Vagus nerve and how it shapes your wellbeing
An intro to Parts Work: understand the different parts of you and how they work together
Practical ways to build creativity, clarity and genuine connection
Skills to feel stronger in yourself, your relationships and your work life
Everyday tools for better health, resilience and emotional balance
Real tools. Real change. Backed by science, grounded in practice.

Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch and snacks, and pen and paper to take notes.

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Neurodiversity Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Neurodiversity Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Neurodiversity Workshop
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
This one-day workshop introduces you to the topic of neurodiversity and considers some of the key conditions that people live with. We look at:
•ADHD – Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder
•Autism
•The “Dys family” – dyslexia, dysgraphia, dyscalculia, dyspraxia (Development Coordination Disorder)
•OCD – Obsessive Compulsive Disorder
•Tourette’s Syndrome
Tik Tok creator and public speaker on neurodiversity, Nigel Sutton, will introduce the subject and facilitate you in discovering the definitions and impacts of each of the key kinds of neurodiversity. Working together with other participants, you will consider many key commonalities that those living with various neurodivergent conditions  experience. We will expose the realities of being neurodivergent in a “neurotypical world” and this will allow you to develop empathy and practical strategies to support neurodivergent people to be their best. 
You will be able to ask anything you want about neurodiversity.
Find out from those offering “lived experience” what it is really like to have a different kind of brain. This one day workshop is: ”Neurodiversity in a Nutshell” – whether you know a little or a lot, whether you are a relative, employer, partner, friend, or just interested to know more about neurodiversity – this workshop is for you! 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Neurodiversity Workshop-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Neurodiversity Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Neurodiversity seems to be a hot topic of discussion lately. Famous singers such as Lewis Capaldi and Sia and actors such as Ryan Gosling and Channing Tatum, have discussed their neurodiverse conditions. Lockdowns and social media have driven many adults to have startling realisations about their own neurodiverse conditions. It seems suddenly – all at once – the world has become aware that 15-20% of people have different types of brains. (Free for NZ citizens/residents, all others pay $99).

Description
This one-day workshop introduces you to the topic of neurodiversity and considers some of the key conditions that people live with. We look at:

-ADHD – Attention Deficit Hyperactivity Disorder
-Autism
-The “Dys family” – dyslexia, dysgraphia, dyscalculia, dyspraxia (Development Coordination Disorder)
-OCD – Obsessive Compulsive Disorder
-Tourette’s Syndrome

Tik Tok creator and public speaker on neurodiversity, Nigel Sutton, will introduce the subject and facilitate you in discovering the definitions and impacts of each of the key kinds of neurodiversity. Working together with other participants, you will consider many key commonalities that those living with various neurodivergent conditions experience. We will expose the realities of being neurodivergent in a “neurotypical world” and this will allow you to develop empathy and practical strategies to support neurodivergent people to be their best.

New Zealand’s leading dyslexia expert Mike Styles, will provide an interactive and participatory session to assist you to understand and support a family member or friend who has dyslexia or a similar learning difference.

You will be able to ask anything you want with our neurodivergent hot seat session.

Find out from those offering “lived experience” what it is really like to have a different kind of brain. This one day workshop is: ”Neurodiversity in a Nutshell” – whether you know a little or a lot, whether you are a relative, employer, partner, friend, or just interested to know more about neurodiversity – this workshop is for you!

events.id: Self Defence for Women-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Self Defence for Women
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Gain more confidence in your everyday life - learn ways to be strong and safe in many situations by using verbal, psychological and physical self-defence.

By the end of the course, you'll:
- Feel more confident in any form of harassment or frightening situation
- Have practised using a strong voice as a first response to fear and learned to use your body as a weapon
- Have had fun while feeling safe to share experiences that may have had an impact on your confidence
- Have developed safety strategies for particular aspects of your life and learned ways to talk to others about safety issues
- Have increased your confidence and knowledge of dealing with various forms of attacks

(This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk. While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk).

Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly.

Wear loose clothing and no socks or stockings as there are times when bare feet are required. Please let the tutor know of any previous or current injuries or health concerns that may have an impact on your participation.

Wellington High School
Presented by Sue Lytollis
$135 incl. GST
 This course is held over 2 Saturdays

events.id: Speaking To An Audience-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Speaking To An Audience
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Need to “step up” and speak to an audience as part of your working life, and find it difficult? This course will equip you with strategies to assist and provide opportunities to build your confidence in this role.

Build your skills and confidence in speaking to an audience, whether it’s for a work presentation, a more formal speech, or part of a performance. In this experiential course, we’ll explore and practise skills to be better prepared to speak, build rapport with your audience, and deliver content effectively.

By the end of the day’s session, you’ll be given:

Essential elements of a great talk - from impromptu talks to formal presentations.
Tools to prepare, organise, practice, and deliver a meaningful, persuasive, and effective presentation.
Actions that build rapport with your audience.
Strategies to maximise the impact of your words, voice, and body language.
Mindsets and actions to reduce stage fright and appear more confident.
Memory techniques and strategies for impromptu speaking.

Note: You must be a NZ citizen or resident to qualify for the free course fee option. A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.

Courses need a minimum number to go ahead, so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by emai,l so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: The Digital Minefield: AI, Misinformation, and Detox-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: The Digital Minefield: AI, Misinformation, and Detox
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
Together, workshop participants will demystify how artificial intelligence actually works, acknowledge some of the harms of this new technology, learn about the characteristics of mis/disinformation, build resilience against fake news, and explore why our brains weren’t built for the infinite scroll. You will walk away from the workshop with a better understanding of the hidden forces shaping your screen, equipped with real-world tools to cut through the digital noise.
  Dr. Andrew Chen is a leading New Zealand specialist at the intersection of digital technology ethics and public policy. With a PhD from the University of Auckland in Computer Systems Engineering, Andrew brings a deep technical understanding and applies it towards systemic issues and interventions. With extensive experience advising public agencies and private companies on governing emerging technologies, Dr. Chen translates complex technical concepts into clear, accessible insights for the community. He is passionate about helping people understand technology's societal impacts, and thinking more deeply about what’s happening behind the scenes, so we can all navigate today’s complex digital landscape.
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Are you feeling overwhelmed by the non-stop flood of AI headlines, viral deepfakes, and notifications on your phone? The Digital Minefield: AI, Misinformation, and Detox is a practical, engaging workshop designed to give you a deeper understanding of the modern digital world, building your confidence to engage with the technology and know how to put it down. (NZ citizens and residents free with ID, others pay $120.)

Description
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents. Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.

If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.

You will walk away from the workshop with a better understanding of the hidden forces shaping your screen, equipped with real-world tools to cut through the digital noise.

Dr. Andrew Chen is a leading New Zealand specialist at the intersection of digital technology ethics and public policy. With a PhD from the University of Auckland in Computer Systems Engineering, Andrew brings a deep technical understanding and applies it towards systemic issues and interventions. With extensive experience advising public agencies and private companies on governing emerging technologies, Dr. Chen translates complex technical concepts into clear, accessible insights for the community. He is passionate about helping people understand technology's societal impacts, and thinking more deeply about what’s happening behind the scenes, so we can all navigate today’s complex digital landscape.

events.id: Exploring Figure & Form-Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Exploring Figure & Form
events.venue: Inverlochy Art School, 3 Inverlochy Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this course we will study the human figure through drawing and sculpture. Both mediums use close observation as a focus and encourage the study of the curves, angles, and subtleties of the human body to accurately depict it.

Students will be guided in experimentations with size and volume, as well as exercises that will sharpen one's attention to detail, improving an artist's overall observational skills and enhancing their artistry across all subjects and styles.

In this course you will -

- Learn how to imbue a sense of the models personality into your drawing and sculpture
- Learn different approaches to rendering the surface of your sculpture
- Explore traditional working methods in both drawing and sculpting

Course fee includes model, tutor, air-dry modelling clay, necessary tools & equipment

events.id: Ice Rink-Queen's Wharf, Queen's Wharf, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ice Rink
events.venue: Queen's Wharf, Queen's Wharf, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wellington Ice Rink at Queens Wharf

Paradice EVENTS returns to Wellington this winter with the Wellington Ice Rink at Queens Wharf. Located on Wellington’s waterfront, the pop-up ice rink transforms Queens Wharf into a seasonal skating destination for visitors of all ages.

Now in its seventh year in Wellington, the event offers a winter skating experience in the heart of the city, featuring music, lighting effects, and sessions suitable for both experienced skaters and first-time visitors.

The rink measures 30 metres by 15 metres and provides approximately 400 pairs of ice skates for hire.
Throughout the season, a range of themed sessions will be held, including:
• Student Wednesdays – Midweek skating sessions with student-focused pricing
• Skate & Date Thursdays – Evening sessions with music and ambient lighting
• Groovy Fridays – Top 40 music sessions to start the weekend

Wellington Ice Rink is suitable for families, groups, students, corporate outings, and casual visitors looking for a winter activity on the waterfront.


Whether visiting for a daytime skate or an evening session under the lights, Wellington Ice Rink offers a seasonal experience in one of the city’s most iconic locations.

Wellington
20 June – 19 July
Location: Queens Wharf
10 am – 10 pm daily
Ice Skating Prices
Adults $26 (13 years plus)
Children $21 (5-12 years)
Pre schoolers $16 (3 and 4 year olds)
Family pass $70
Student night Wednesday 7pm – 10pm $18 Student ID required
Please note: all skaters are required to wear a helmet or Paradice safety approved headband when skating.

Risk Disclosure
Please bear in mind that ice skating and ice sliding is an extreme sport which can result in harm or injury. The risks and hazards include, but are not limited to tripping, falling, collisions and cold exposure. By participating in the activities offered by the Wairakei Estate Ice Rink and MB Century Ice Slide you acknowledge these risks could result in injury and you skate/slide at your own risk.

Keep Yourself Safe
The wearing of a helmet or Paradice approved safety headband is compulsory and we recommend the wearing of gloves. Children 6 years and under only have the option of wearing a helmet not the safety headband. Please follow our rink rules and the advice of staff
Please note minimum age for ice skating is 3 years old.

Tickets are available inside the rink and online www.paradiceevents.co.nz
For more information
Contact Paradice Events
09 281 5645 or 027 233 0972 | events@paradice.co.nz

events.id: Ngaio Repair Cafe - Don’t chuck it. Fix it.-Ngaio Union Church, 3 Kenya St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Ngaio Repair Cafe - Don’t chuck it. Fix it.
events.venue: Ngaio Union Church, 3 Kenya St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Don’t chuck it. Fix it.

Free community service.

What sort of things can our skilled volunteers help you with?

Bring:
- socks and learn how to darn
- sewing & mending
- household items
- electrical items

events.id: Open Day at Otari Montessori Preschool-Otari Montessori School, 166 Wilton Rd, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Open Day at Otari Montessori Preschool
events.venue: Otari Montessori School, 166 Wilton Rd, Wellington
events.long_description:
Otari Montessori Preschool
Where children grow into confident, capable, and caring people — through play, exploration, and real-world discovery.

We warmly invite Wellington families to come and explore Otari Montessori Preschool at our Open Day.

Come and meet our wonderful teachers, explore our environment, learn about our holistic and well-rounded programme, and see your child in action. Children will have the chance to engage with a variety of discovery activities, as well as explore our purpose-built outdoor deck and playground.
This is also a lovely opportunity to meet our warm parent community and enjoy a conversation over a warm drink and a snack.

At Otari Montessori, we are committed to delivering high-quality early childhood education, supporting our tamariki in their interests and learning about the world around them, while building strong foundations for social-emotional development.

We are also lucky to be situated in one of the most beautiful natural environments in Wellington, with Wilton’s Bush right on our doorstep for children to explore and enjoy.

As a result, our preschool environment supports children to:
• feel happy and secure coming to preschool
• build friendships and connect with others easily
• focus, persist, and stay engaged in what they do
• take initiative and grow in independence
• show kindness, empathy, and care for others

Our programme offers:
• learning through hands-on experiences, purposeful play, time outdoors, and plenty of free play with rich opportunities for social interaction with peers
• a rich and diverse holiday programme featuring arts, environmental exploration, and physical activities, including interactive Kelly Sports games
• weekly music sessions
• weekly bush walks
• a strong transition pathway: Playgroup → Preschool → Primary
• quarterly parent information sessions

This is a relaxed opportunity to visit, ask questions, and get a feel for whether Otari Montessori could be the right fit for your child and family — whether you are new to Montessori or already familiar with its core principles.

Please register your interest if you can, so we can plan for numbers — but you’re also very welcome to pop in on the day, even if you haven’t registered:
https://www.otaripreschool.school.nz/contact-us

We’d love to welcome you.

events.id: Vietnamese Crab Noodle Soup - Bánh Canh Cua-9 Batchelor Street, Newlands, Wellington 6037,Newlands Community Centre, 9 Batchelor St, Newlands, Wellington 6037, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Vietnamese Crab Noodle Soup - Bánh Canh Cua
events.venue: 9 Batchelor Street, Newlands, Wellington 6037,Newlands Community Centre, 9 Batchelor St, Newlands, Wellington 6037, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Vietnamese Crab Noodle Soup - Bánh Canh Cua
Join Viet Hub NZ for a comforting winter cooking workshop featuring Bánh Canh Cua, a rich and hearty Vietnamese crab noodle soup known for its thick, silky noodles and flavourful seafood broth.

In this hands-on session, you will learn how to:

• Prepare and season a traditional crab-based broth

• Work with thick tapioca or rice noodles

• Balance flavours and textures with crab meat and fresh herbs

• Present the dish beautifully, Vietnamese style

Bánh Canh Cua is a beloved Southern Vietnamese dish, often enjoyed as a nourishing family meal. Its naturally thick broth and delicate sweetness from crab make it perfect for cooler days.

Participants will cook together, share stories, and enjoy tasting the finished bowl as a group.

To help keep these classes sustainable and support ongoing community activities, there will be an additional $10/person (13+ years old) contribution if you pay online. Walk-ins are accepted but the fee would be $12/person (13+ years old).

This helps cover:

• Facilitator effort and preparation

• Admin and coordination work

• The ability to continue running and expanding community workshops

Please prepare to show your booking evidence before the class

This workshop continues our collaboration with I Love Viet Food and is made possible thanks to the generous support of:

• Hutt City Council

• E Tū Whānau

• Pomare Taita Trust

• Te Mako Community Centre

• Newlands Community Centre

Spaces are limited to ensure a quality, interactive experience.

Come warm your hands, learn a new skill, and celebrate Vietnamese heritage through food.

events.id: Wellington Ice Rink-51 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Wellington Ice Rink
events.venue: 51 Customhouse Quay, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Paradice EVENTS returns to Wellington this winter with the Wellington Ice Rink at Queens Wharf, running from Saturday 20 June to Sunday 19 July. Located on Wellington’s waterfront, the pop-up ice rink transforms Queens Wharf into a seasonal skating destination for visitors of all ages.
Now in its seventh year in Wellington, the event offers a winter skating experience in the heart of the city, featuring music, lighting effects, and sessions suitable for both experienced skaters and first-time visitors.
The rink measures 30 metres by 15 metres and provides approximately 400 pairs of ice skates for hire.
Throughout the season, a range of themed sessions will be held, including:
Student Wednesdays – Midweek skating sessions with student-focused pricing
Skate & Date Thursdays – Evening sessions with music and ambient lighting
Groovy Fridays – Top 40 music sessions to start the weekend
Wellington Ice Rink is suitable for families, groups, students, corporate outings, and casual visitors looking for a winter activity on the waterfront.
Whether visiting for a daytime skate or an evening session under the lights, Wellington Ice Rink offers a seasonal experience in one of the city’s most iconic locations.

events.id: On Target-292 Boomrock Rd, Ohariu Valley , Wellington-Facebook
events.name: On Target
events.venue: 292 Boomrock Rd, Ohariu Valley , Wellington
events.long_description:
On Target is your chance to try something completely new, build on your existing skills, or get back into shooting in a safe, supportive and seriously fun environment.
Hosted at the beautiful Boomrock Wedding & Events Venue, this women-only experience gives you the opportunity to try shotgun shooting and axe throwing, guided by experienced professionals who make learning feel relaxed, confidence-building and empowering.
It’s not about being perfect – it’s about backing yourself, having a go, and discovering what you’re capable of alongside a group of incredible women doing exactly the same.
Your ticket includes a platter with light snacks, a complimentary drink after shooting, 10 clay targets, and 10 axe-throwing attempts – we will make sure you can really settle in, experiment and enjoy the experience without pressure.
After the session, stay on and celebrate your wins at the fully licensed bar with your new Wild Chix friends – because confidence earned absolutely deserves to be toasted.

events.long_description:
Our first Pop Up in 2026

SOULFUL HANDCRAFTED JEWELLERY
Contemporanea will be holding a Pop Up shop in Wellington!
For one day only, Contemporanea will be holding a Pop Up shop within the Wellington Apothecary.
Contemporanea brings you soulful, handcrafted jewellery by female artists worldwide - pieces that celebrate connection, belonging, and self-expression.
Ethically made and rich with meaning, each creation is a reminder that you are seen, valued, and part of something bigger
Join us on Saturday 20 June, 11am–3pm at the Wellington Apothecary.

Dani
Founder and Curator
Contemporanea

events.name: Brooklyn Community Market

events.long_description:
Call into the Brooklyn Market and catch up with friends and neighbours while browsing through a wide variety of stalls, including jewellery, plants, crystals, crafts, resin and vintage goods, home baking, knitted and crocheted toys, Eco products and lots more.

Delicious food available from the kitchen with ready-to-eat homemade pies, rolls, toasted sandwiches and sweet treats, plus coffee, tea, juices and a sausage sizzle on the deck.

Andrew, our popular knife sharpener, will be on-site sharpening knives, scissors and gardening tools.

There is a large, fun playground outside to keep the kids entertained.

The market is under cover and runs rain or shine.

Email: market@brooklyncommunitycentre.org.nz

events.id: Belmont planning community drop-in: Lower Hutt-Lower Hutt-Facebook
events.name: Belmont planning community drop-in: Lower Hutt
events.venue: Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Greater Wellington is developing a long-term plan to guide how Belmont Regional Park is cared for, used, and invested in over the next 50 years. This community drop-in is part of early engagement to help shape that plan.
Drop-in to learn about what the plan will cover, like tracks and access, restoration, landscapes, and visitor experiences. Share what you think is important for the future of Belmont Regional Park.
Whether you use the park often, live nearby, or simply value it as a regional open space, your perspective can help shape the future of Belmont Regional Park.
This is an informal session with the project team. You can come along at any time, ask questions, learn more about the project, and share your thoughts and ideas for the future of Belmont Regional Park.
Find out more about master planning: www.gw.govt.nz/belmontplan

events.id: International Yoga Day - Community Event-Lotus Yoga Centre & Retreat,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: International Yoga Day - Community Event
events.venue: Lotus Yoga Centre & Retreat,91 Ruapehu Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
International Yoga Day - Community Event
A Kundalini Yoga and Gong Bath workshop to celebrate International Yoga Day

About this Event

To celebrate International Yoga Day, I’m offering an annual community class — a welcoming space for anyone curious about Kundalini Yoga, or simply wanting to move, breathe and reset.
This special 1.5 hour session includes:
• A beginner-friendly Kundalini Yoga class
• Breathwork to energise and centre
• Deep relaxation
• A short, soothing gong bath to integrate

Whether you’re completely new to yoga, returning after a break, or already practising, this class is designed to be accessible and supportive.


💛 Community Ticket — $15 per person
This discounted price (normally $35) is my way of giving back and making the practice accessible for anyone who has been curious to try.

🎟 Bookings essential

Bring a friend.
Come on your own.
All are welcome.

💫No prior experience needed. Just come as you are.
💫Bring an open heart, a yoga mat, blanket, water bottle and anything you need to feel cozy + supported.

If you want to pay by cash on the day or by bank transfer, please send an email to to book your place.

events.id: New Zealand’s Unusual Plants and Their History - Guided Walk-Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: New Zealand’s Unusual Plants and Their History - Guided Walk
events.venue: Begonia House, 101 Glenmore St, Kelburn, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Have you ever wondered why so many of New Zealand's plants are different from those in other countries? We will explore some of the answers on this 90-minute walk starting at the City Lookout beside the Cable Car terminus and descending to the Founders’ Gate in Glenmore Street.
Meet at the Cable Car Entrance Lookout, Upland Road, Kelburn.
Cost: $10 or Free for Friends – bookings required (you’ll get the option to choose your ticket type after clicking ‘Buy Tickets’)
Join the Friends for only $30 a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits.

events.id: House Candle Holder Clay Workshop-Maungaraki Community Centre, 135 Dowse Drive, Maungaraki, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: House Candle Holder Clay Workshop
events.venue: Maungaraki Community Centre, 135 Dowse Drive, Maungaraki, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Make a magical clay house which can be used with real or fake candles. We will use my hand drawn templates, white clay, slab building and modelling and imprinting techniques, to produce stunning cream houses. This class also includes the option of adding some dashes of colour using underglazes and coloured slips.

Add birds, cats, window boxes or whatever inspires to your creation. By themselves they are gorgeous, and when they have a candle inside, they bring warmth and magic to a space.

Make either 1 medium house (13cm x 7cm x 10cm) or 2 mini houses (9cm x 5cm x 8cm), with the option of making extra houses for an additional fee. Around 5 weeks after the class these will be fired and glazed using a clear glaze, creating a gorgeous art piece to display in your home or gift to another.

Booking is essential, either using the eventfinda ticket link (booking fees apply) or this link: https://forms.gle/wk2NKbsJ5BEoqn6n6 (no ticketing fees) or contact Cate at clpates@hotmail.com

events.venue: Our Lady's Home of Compassion, 2 Rhine St, Island Bay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Venerable Meri Hōhepa Suzanne Aubert was one of the most remarkable women in Aotearoa New Zealand’s history: a tireless nurse, social reformer, and Catholic nun who dedicated her life to the poor, the sick, and the forgotten. Founder of the Sisters of Compassion, she bridged Māori and Pākehā worlds with deep respect, practical care, and extraordinary courage. This talk brings to life a woman whose compassion reshaped communities and whose legacy still speaks powerfully today.
As part of "Rua Tau Pūaroha | Celebrate Compassion 2026", commemorating 100 years since the passing of Venerable Meri Hōhepa Suzanne Aubert, you are warmly invited to an afternoon with acclaimed biographer and award-winning writer/historian Jessie Munro as she brings to life the story of this extraordinary woman - her life, work, influence and enduring legacy.
Jessie Munro is the author of 'The Story of Suzanne Aubert', the highly commended biography that won the Book of the Year award in the Montana Book Awards in 1997. For her talk, Jessie has chosen four fascinating elements:
- the early New Zealand narrative
- our tightly compressed history
- a meeting of cultures
- a women’s agency.

‘ Always alert, and responsive to her context, Suzanne lived nearly seven decades here in Aotearoa New Zealand, amidst fast-changing societal and political times and places. The strands of her active, wide-ranging engagement are interwoven with our historical fabric.’ - Jessie Munro
This free and informative event invites you to discover a woman of deep faith, sharp intelligence, and relentless compassion whose story remains profoundly relevant.
All are welcome and there is ample parking available.

Founder of the Sisters of Compassion, she bridged Māori and Pākehā worlds with deep respect, practical care, and extraordinary courage.

events.long_description:
Try your local cycle route
Build confidence riding in the city on a guided bike tour designed to help you get comfortable with local routes and street changes. You will practise key cycling skills, learn how to use new cycling infrastructure, and discover interesting features along the way.
These relaxed, supportive rides are organised by Wellington City Council and led by the experienced instructors from Greater Wellington Regional Council's Pedal Ready.

events.id: Card Merchant Wellington - 3-Pack Crack, Shuffle & Play Side Event (FAB National Championsip 2026)-Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Card Merchant Wellington - 3-Pack Crack, Shuffle & Play Side Event (FAB National Championsip 2026)
events.venue: Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
For the Saturday of Nationals weekend at Card Merchant Wellington, our featured afternoon side event is the Crack, Shuffle & Play, Guardian Special event.
Kicking off at 3:00pm, this three-round tournament is all about fast games, fresh packs, and pure fun.
Here’s how it works:
Each player will receive nine booster packs on entry.
At the beginning of each round, you’ll:
• Open three packs
• Shuffle the contents together
• Play that round using only the cards you just opened
That means over the course of the event, every player is guaranteed to crack at least nine packs.
Entry: $60 per player
Prize Structure:
• 1 additional booster pack per round win
This event will use the Guardian Special Pack product from Flesh and Blood, making it a unique and exciting way to experience the game during Nationals weekend.
Whether you’re looking for something fun after the main event, trying your luck with fresh pulls, or just keen to play something different, Crack, Shuffle & Play is the perfect way to spend your Saturday afternoon.
Spots will be limited based on seating.

events.id: Card Merchant Wellington - On-Demand Constructed Side Events (FAB National Championship 2026)-Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: Card Merchant Wellington - On-Demand Constructed Side Events (FAB National Championship 2026)
events.venue: Regency Apartments, 49 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
To give players as much opportunity as possible to play the games they love, Card Merchant Wellington will be running On-Demand Classic Constructed Side Events throughout the entire Nationals weekend.
What does “on-demand” mean?
As soon as we have eight players ready to go, we’ll fire a three-round Classic Constructed event using those players as a unique pod. No waiting around for scheduled start times, when eight players are ready, we kick things off.
This keeps games flowing all weekend long, whether you’ve finished your Nationals run or are just popping in to get involved.
Entry: $20 per player
Prize Structure:
• 1 booster pack per win
• Go 3–0 and walk away with 3 packs
• Even if you don’t win a match, you’ll still receive at least 1 pack
These events will run across both Saturday and Sunday of Nationals weekend.
We’re looking forward to an incredible Nationals weekend at Card Merchant Wellington.

events.id: Side Walker: Extra-Dimensional Improvised Tours-Corner of Cuba & Left Bank, Corner of Cuba & Left Bank, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Side Walker: Extra-Dimensional Improvised Tours
events.venue: Corner of Cuba & Left Bank, Corner of Cuba & Left Bank, Wellington
events.long_description:
Side Walker. Improvised magical walking tour where the audience chooses the route.

Discover the extra-dimensional secrets hidden in your urban landscape on this 45 min pedestrian adventure; made up on the fly by award winning improviser George Fenn.

Meet at the corner of Cuba Street and Left Bank. The audience should arrive prepared to move across multiple blocks with clothing appropriate for the weather of that day.

This show debuted in 2017 in the NZ Fringe and is back after a 6 year hiatus.

George Fenn is a genre bending comedy theatre maker based in New Zealand who specializes in audience interaction. While best known for their Clown/Burlesque "Sexy Ghost Boy" Trilogy, they are a fixture in the New Zealand improv scene. Notable contributions include running the Christchurch "Late Night Knife Fight," "Router Sidewalker" an Improvised Street Tour, and "Solo Yeet," an hour-long solo improv show. See less

events.id: CBK Social Club Beer Bong Tournament!-The Crooked Elm,25 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: CBK Social Club Beer Bong Tournament!
events.venue: The Crooked Elm,25 Laings Road, Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
CBK Social Club Beer Bong Tournament!
Keen for a good time? Grab a mate and join our Beer Pong Tournament at the Crooked Elm

About this Event

CBK Social Club is all about getting people out, meeting others, and actually doing something fun, not just talking about it.
Join us at the Crooked Elm for our Beer Pong Tournament, where you and your teammate will go head-to-head with other teams for the chance to win a bar tab.
Expect:
🍻 Good vibes + great people
🏓 Competitive (but friendly) beer pong
💧 Hydration support from Daeli to keep you going
🎉 A fun, social atmosphere whether you’re playing or just there for the vibes
Teams of 2 required - grab your partner and get ready to compete.
Come for the tournament, stay for the drinks, and leave with a few new connections.
Want to come solo?
If you don’t have a teammate, email us or DM us on insta cbksocialclub and we’ll do our best to match you with another player.
Once we’ve found you a partner, we’ll send you a link to book your team entry ticket ($50 for 2 players).
👉 Matching is not guaranteed, but we’ll try our best!!
CBK Social Club. Plans, people, and a place to belong.

Refund & Transfer Policy
Tickets are eligible for a full refund if requested at least 14 days before the event date.
Refund requests made within 14 days of the event are not eligible for a refund, however tickets can be transferred to another person.
If the event is cancelled by CBK Social Club, a full refund will be issued.
To request a refund or transfer, please contact:
As part of our commitment to responsible service under the Sale and Supply of Alcohol Act 2012, this event will be run with host responsibility obligations.

Participants must be 18+ with valid ID, and we’ll be actively monitoring alcohol consumption to ensure everyone enjoys the event safely. We reserve the right to refuse service and remove intoxicated and disorderly guests, and food and non-alcoholic options will be available throughout the event.

We want everyone to have a great time, and the rules are strict and enforceable by law.
