events.id: Sound Healing Experience with Claudio at Pause Yoga 20th of June 2026-Pause Yoga,28 Moana Rd, Days Bay, Lower Hutt 5013, New Zealand, Lower Hutt-All Events In
events.name: Sound Healing Experience with Claudio at Pause Yoga 20th of June 2026
events.venue: Pause Yoga,28 Moana Rd, Days Bay, Lower Hutt 5013, New Zealand, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Sound Healing Experience with Claudio at Pause Yoga 20th of June 2026
Join us for this special evening of harmonising pure sounds, inspiring music and relaxation in the beautiful Pause Yoga studio in Eastbourne.
Give yourself a peaceful moment and space of quiet awareness. Discover the power of your own voice, mantra and silence working to find inner peace and contentment. Bring an intention, get comfortable and let the sound waves take over. Invite the sound to take you into a journey within, into your heart, to rest, to release physical, mental and emotional tension.

Surrender to the sound of Gong, Tibetan and Crystal Bowls, Native American flutes, Harmonium, Sitar ,Esraj, Bells etc..Claudio brings high quality amplification so you can feel the sound in your body and take into a immersive experience.

Feel free to bring your own pillow and blanket to assure comfort, the studio provides mats, bolsters, blankets and eye pillows.


Contribution:

Early bird tickets $45 first 8 bookings
General admission :$49


Booking link:

https://www.claudioescutia.com/events/sound-healing-experience-with-claudio-at-pause-yoga-13


About Claudio: Claudio is a 3rd generation Multinstrumentalist Musician from Mexico City now based in Wellington. Claudio comes from a musicians family he started playing music since very early years. When he finished music school as a classical guitarist he moved to Montreal, Canada and explored new genres such a Jazz, World Music, and Spiritual Music. Traveling he discovered Indian Music and the Yoga Tradition and they became a very important influence on his career and lifestyle, and allowed him to study Music and Yoga with teachers in Europe and Asia. He learnt to play the Tibetan Singing Bowls in Kathmandu Nepal and Varanasi India.He explored many instruments and music styles such as Classical Guitar, Sitar, Esraj, Bansuri, Harmonium, Flutes, Gongs and Voice.Claudio is an active Sound Healing practitioner and performer. He has been playing the Tibetan bowls for more than 10 years now.

-Website:

www.claudioescutia.com

-SoundCloud

https://soundcloud.com/claudioescutiasounds

-Instagram

https://www.instagram.com/claudio_yoga_sound_healing

-Facebook

https://www.facebook.com/soundhealingyogamusicclaudio

events.id: Winter Solstice Bike Rave-Te Ara Tupua Pathway,Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Winter Solstice Bike Rave
events.venue: Te Ara Tupua Pathway,Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Winter Solstice Bike Rave
Main Event

When: Saturday 20 June, meet from 5.30pm
Where: Te Ara Tupua, Petone end
Ride departs: 6pm, finishing at Glover Park for a beverage and a yarn.

It’s time to light up Te Ara Tupua.

Join us for a winter Bike Rave along Te Ara Tupua as we roll from Petone back into the city in a glorious movement of lights, good tunes, rave-adjacent outfits and weird wheeled creations.

Main meet-up will be at the Petone end of Te Ara Tupua from 5.30pm, then depart at 6pm for a cruise southbound along Te Ara Tupua back toward Wellington, finishing at Glover Park.
.
.
.
Warm-Up Group Ride

When: 4pm
Where: HNRY Stadium forecourt
Departs: 4.30pm for Petone

Got the fam, or simply want to get the legs moving early? Join the warm-up roll-in from the HNRY Stadium forecourt. We’ll ease into the evening, warm up the dance vibes with the little ones, and bring out the beats before heading towards Petone.

This part of the ride is family friendly. For anyone riding one-way with kids, trains run back toward town every 30 minutes from Petone.
.
.
.
What to Bring
• Lights!
• Optional - Speakers with an aux port. We’ll have FM receivers if you want to tune in and be part of the noise.
• Warm layers
• A bike or other wheeled creation
• Your finest, weirdest, most rave-adjacent outfit

events.id: Live Art: Ossia-Toi Mahara, 20 Mahara Place, Waikanae-Event Finder
events.name: Live Art: Ossia
events.venue: Toi Mahara, 20 Mahara Place, Waikanae
events.long_description:
Ossia (Italian for “alternate musical passage”) is a newly formed ensemble built on the idea of musical exploration. Featuring Jess Deacon (vocals), Jessica Murphy (keys, melodica), Robert Kingi (guitar, bass), and Cam Steel (drums, percussion), Ossia creates space for music to evolve beyond its original form — stretching into improvisation, reinterpretation, and moments of spontaneous connection.

Blending jazz-adjacent textures with soul, folk, groove, and a touch of sass, Ossia invites audiences into an intimate live music experience where curiosity leads the way. This is music that listens, responds, and breathes in the moment: familiar songs reimagined, unexpected turns embraced, and musical conversations unfolding in real time.
This event is part of Toi MAHARA’s Live Art programme.

About Ossia:
Jessica Murphy — keys, melodica
Jazz and contemporary pianist and vocalist Jessie Murphy delivers smooth, captivating performances with a soulful, dynamic sound. Born in Nelson and trained at the Christchurch School of Jazz, Jessie blends a rich musical background with heartfelt performances that move effortlessly across genres, creating an engaging live music experience.

Jess Deacon — vocals
Jess Deacon is a cross-genre vocalist and songwriter whose music draws from jazz, folk, soul, and classic rock. An engaging and intuitive performer, her love of music began with an eclectic musical upbringing. Her studies have included cello, piano, guitar, composition, and jazz performance, shaping a creative practice that is both technically diverse and deeply expressive. Jess has fronted multiple bands, been commissioned as a composer, recorded on a variety of projects, and performed at festivals and events across Aotearoa, bringing curiosity, vulnerability, and musical storytelling to every performance.

Robert J. M. Kingi — guitar, bass
Based in Taitoko (Levin), Robert J. M. Kingi is a guitarist and bassist whose music traverses the soulful landscapes of blues, jazz, country, and gospel. Originally from Darwin, Australia, and of Māori and American descent, Robert brings a unique cultural perspective to the Horowhenua music scene.

A graduate of Whitireia with a Bachelor of Applied Arts in Music, Robert combines academic discipline with the instinctive flair of a seasoned performer. His recent work explores the use of live looper technology, allowing him to weave complex musical textures on the spot. From the intimacy of local cafés to the energy of regional festivals, Robert delivers performances that are technically impressive and deeply rooted in the joy of musical expression.

Cam Steel — drums, percussion
Cam Steel is a versatile drummer and percussionist known for his instinctive groove, dynamic musicality, and ability to shape immersive rhythmic soundscapes. A graduate of the Whitireia School of Music, Cam has spent years performing across Wellington’s diverse music scene, moving fluidly between jazz, soul, folk, funk, and experimental music settings.

Whether anchoring a groove or exploring more textural and atmospheric terrain, Cam approaches rhythm as both foundation and conversation — bringing energy, sensitivity, and spontaneity to every performance. His playing is deeply collaborative, driven by a love of improvisation and the shared experience of live music in the moment.

Her studies have included cello, piano, guitar, composition, and jazz performance, shaping a creative practice that is both technically diverse and deeply expressive. Jess has fronted multiple bands, been commissioned as a composer, recorded on a variety of projects, and performed at festivals and events across Aotearoa, bringing curiosity, vulnerability, and musical storytelling to every performance.

Robert J. M. Kingi — guitar, bass
Based in Taitoko (Levin), Robert J. M. Kingi is a guitarist and bassist whose music traverses the soulful landscapes of blues, jazz, country, and gospel. Originally from Darwin, Australia, and of Māori and American descent, Robert brings a unique cultural perspective to the Horowhenua music scene.

A graduate of Whitireia with a Bachelor of Applied Arts in Music, Robert combines academic discipline with the instinctive flair of a seasoned performer. His recent work explores the use of live looper technology, allowing him to weave complex musical textures on the spot. From the intimacy of local cafés to the energy of regional festivals, Robert delivers performances that are technically impressive and deeply rooted in the joy of musical expression.

Cam Steel — drums, percussion
Cam Steel is a versatile drummer and percussionist known for his instinctive groove, dynamic musicality, and ability to shape immersive rhythmic soundscapes. A graduate of the Whitireia School of Music, Cam has spent years performing across Wellington’s diverse music scene, moving fluidly between jazz, soul, folk, funk, and experimental music settings.

Whether anchoring a groove or exploring more textural and atmospheric terrain, Cam approaches rhythm as both foundation and conversation — bringing energy, sensitivity, and spontaneity to every performance. His playing is deeply collaborative, driven by a love of improvisation and the shared experience of live music in the moment.
events.description:
Live Art: Ossia Ossia (Italian for “alternate musical passage”) is a newly formed ensemble built on the idea of musical exploration.
Featuring Jess Deacon (vocals), Jessica Murphy (keys, melodica), Robert Kingi (guitar, bass), and Cam Steel (drums, percussion), Ossia creates space for music to evolve beyond its original form — stretching into improvisation, reinterpretation, and moments of spontaneous connection.
This is music that listens, responds, and breathes in the moment: familiar songs reimagined, unexpected turns embraced, and musical conversations unfolding in real time.
An engaging and intuitive performer, her love of music began with an eclectic musical upbringing.
A graduate of Whitireia with a Bachelor of Applied Arts in Music, Robert combines academic discipline with the instinctive flair of a seasoned performer.

events.name: Spotlight ON: OTTO
events.venue: Otto Champagneria & Beer Hall,8 Courtenay Pl, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Spotlight ON: OTTO
Wellington, welcome to the inaugural showcase of Spotlight Music.

We are taking over Otto on Featherston Street for an upscale, one-off winter crossover event—transforming a beautiful CBD space into a deep, atmospheric dance sanctuary.
We have meticulously engineered the lineup to deliver a continuous, evolving sonic journey from doors-open to the final track:

The Lineup💃🎶💃

The Groove:
Hawke's Bay powerhouse Mokai’s Finest lands in the capital alongside her namesake, the formidable Louloulou, laying down a heavy foundation of soulful, driving house music.

The Lift:
Fortuna takes control of the room, injecting her signature, high-energy, uplifting flavor to shift the dancefloor into high gear.

The Weight:
We are incredibly proud to welcome heavyweight Tiger Balm to the Spotlight booth. Expect a masterclass in house music driven by his notoriously thick, heavy, low-end basslines that will test the absolute limits of our sound system.

The Shadows:
Closing out the night, Matt Adam steps up to deliver a rare, specially crafted techno set, pushing the room into dark, late-night territory.

The Production:
This isn't a standard club night. We are retrofitting Otto's stunning room with a custom-engineered, heavy-hitting sound stacks and a deliberate, low-lit ambient light installation.

🎟️ TICKETS ARE OFFICIALLY LIVE — LINK IN BIO.

events.id: SOLD OUT: Dark Sky Experience @ Wainuiomata Regional Park-Wainuiomata Recreation Area,Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: SOLD OUT: Dark Sky Experience @ Wainuiomata Regional Park
events.venue: Wainuiomata Recreation Area,Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
SOLD OUT: Dark Sky Experience @ Wainuiomata Regional Park
Bring the family along for a magical night under the stars at Wainuiomata Regional Park and help us celebrate the opening of the brand-new Dark Sky Museum!

Set away from city lights, Wainuiomata Regional Park offers a superb dark sky location for stargazing. This special FREE dark sky experience begins with the official opening of the Dark Sky Museum, where you’ll learn about the wonders of the night sky and why protecting our dark skies from light pollution matters.

After exploring the museum, step outside to meet expert stargazers from Wellington Astronomical Society who will guide you through the night sky. With the help of telescopes, you’ll be able to spot stars, planets, constellations, and other celestial highlights visible on the night, including favourites of the southern sky.

Marshmallow toasting, hot drinks and a fun glow wall activity will be available to keep everyone warm and entertained between telescope viewings, making this a relaxed and family-friendly evening for all ages.

Come along to discover the beauty of the cosmos, learn something new, and celebrate this exciting new dark sky destination—no experience needed.

Session 1: 6.15pm – 7.45pm (SOLD OUT)
Session 2: 7.45pm – 9.15pm (SOLD OUT)

This event is now SOLD OUT, if you would like to go on the waitlist please email

A NIGHT OUT AT THE ROXY
25th Anniversary
Movies give you endorphins. Endorphins make you happy. Happy people don't miss out on A Night Out at The Roxy. Whether you're comfortable using legal jargon on everyday life, or prefer to stomp your last-season Prada shoes instead, this is for you.

*Themed drinks can be purchased on the night
Presented in 4K

Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show in Blenheim: Fun for All Ages!
Experience the Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show Where You Become the Star of the Show!

As seen on Seven Sharp (TVNZ) and going viral on TikTok, you’re in for a treat! Comedy Hypnotist Haiming Jiang is bringing his hit Comedy Hypnosis Show to your area. Get ready to laugh hysterically – and even join in, if you're up for it! It’s a night of unforgettable fun.

Haiming blends hypnosis and comedy to entertain the audience while showcasing the power and benefits of hypnosis through live audience participation. With his unique cultural background and deep understanding of both Western and Oriental cultures, this is a must-see event for anyone curious about hypnosis and keen for a good laugh.

Come and discover how this talented Chinese hypnotist uses hypnosis to both entertain crowds and deeply relax participants.
Experience the hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show – where you could become the star of the night! (Only if you’re keen – no pressure!)

events.id: Sound Therapy, Gong Bath with Hammocks-The Yoga Studio, 6/149 Karori Rd, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sound Therapy, Gong Bath with Hammocks
events.venue: The Yoga Studio, 6/149 Karori Rd, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join Nick for this therapeutic Gong Bath to unwind from the stress of your day/week/year, while being supported and held by your choice of a hammock. Hammocks and gongs aren't a common mix, but they work perfectly by allowing you to rest at ear level with the gongs, while having the extra comfort of not having a hard floor beneath you. If you prefer, there is space for floor seating along the edges of the studio.

If you haven't been to an event like this before, some of the benefits come from the sounds assisting the brain to reach deeper, more restful brainwave states, while the steady drones invite the nervous system to relax into the rest & digest mode. The sounds themselves are unlike anything else really, and sound like some kind of rich, harmonic, electronic music. Nick uses three medium-large Paiste gongs, full moon Tibetan singing bowls, monochord, zither, percussion, and a singing pyramid, and strucutres the set using his extensive training in Mindful Somatic Therapy and Music studies.

Nick has been producing and playing with music since his earliest teens, having graduated from The New Zealand School of Music, and later with a University qualification and work experience in experiential Psychology, combining those two worlds with events like these throughout Wellington and the lower North Island for the past 2.5 years.

If you live with any of these, please talk to your Doctor first, to make sure the event will be suitable:
-have a pacemaker or other implanted medical device
-live with epilepsy or a sound-sensitive condition
-are in the first trimester of pregnancy
-are experiencing severe or unmanaged mental health challenges (such as psychosis, schizophrenia, or PTSD)

Please bring along anything that'll help you feel more comfortable (pillow, eye mask, blanket, or earplugs if you live with tinnitus).

events.name: Walt Quizney @ 1852, Wellington
events.venue: 55 Cleveland St, Brooklyn, Wellington 6021, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hakuna Matata, it means no worries for the rest of your days. And we've got Saturday TBC 2026 all figured out for you!
Join us at 7pm for a Supercalifragilisticexpialidocious evening of Quizney themed trivia at 1852 Pub & Kitchen, Wellington!
Sport your best costume, brush up on your movie knowledge (including Pixar) and get ready to put all those years of watching "Quizney" to good use. Oh, did we mention we're having a sing-a-long round where you'll need to remember the lyrics of your favourite song lyrics?
In true Gee Quiz fashion, we'll also have fantastically-fun bonus games including our legendary but stressful Alphabetical round, where your team will negotiate 26 fast-fire questions as the time tick down, plus Last Prince and Princess Standing and The Line Up!
This quiz would not be complete without you, so book tickets today. A team ticket (up to 6 players) is $90).
Booking link: https://www.trybooking.com/nz/YMK
***Minors must be accompanied with parent or legal guardian***

events.long_description:
Pitch n Faders Presents reece_nz
We now return to your regularly scheduled programming...

Pitch n Faders are super excited to have our next guest. Long time welli stalwart and friend of the family, reece_nz!

Diving headfirst into the Wellington underground rave & club scene in the late 90s
reece became a foundational DJ of the capital, with his insatiable thirst for
new, exotic soundscapes securing him residencies at many esteemed
bars and clubs (like Vespa, Tatou, Phoenix, Runners, BacktoOurs) while performing
at numerous events and festivals (like The Gathering, Alpine Unity, Taniwhas Den)
making him one of Aotearoa's enduring DJs and sharing the booth with NZ and International legends alike
reece could also be found Slinging Wax at several Record Stores and continues to do so via his new project 3AM Vinyl. Currently residing in the central Art-centric town of Whanganui, reece continues to weave NZ
a tapestry of full spectrum House & Techno, generating electric dancefloors in dimly lit basements, shimmering bars, clubs and summer festival stages.

As always FREE ENTRY
The beats will be rollin and the drinks flowin so come join us for a shimmy!!

C-26 is a sizzling seven piece salsa band from Wellington. They are a great mix of South American and Kiwi professional musicians who bring vibrant and energetic music to the Wellington scene.

Their sound and style is inspired by the salsa classics from Cuba Puerto Rico and Colombia. Featuring the first female Latino salsa singer in Wellington, Anayibi Loboa from Colombia, C-26 is the Salsa purist's band, fully live, with no backing tracks, C-26 produces a sound and feel built from the classical combination of horns, timbales, congas, guiro, trumpet, saxophone, piano and bass that defines the authentic salsa experience.

Music from 9:30

events.venue: Ivy Bar and Cabaret, 63 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Feeling k*nky Wellington? Well boy does Ness Quick have a show for you...

Get ready to let your freak flag fly at Birthday Suit: A Kinky Cabaret. Ness Quick has invited some of Wellington's horniest drag performers to live out their fantasies (and yours *wink*) on the Ivy Bar stage. Prepare for themes of SEX, ROLEPLAY, BDSM AND MORE. There won't be a dry eye or a dry seat in the house, especially those in the splash zone.

Featuring many beloved Wellington performers, Birthday Suit goes on to explore what kink means in a queer community, and how sex and intimacy are one of the many languages which queer people chose to express themselves. From the hanky code to trans phone sex operators, kink lives and breathes within the queer community, and for tonight, it'll come alive on stage. So be prepared to be turned on and be taken on the ride of your life with BIRTHDAY SUIT.

Hosted by Ness Quick
Featuring:
Vixen Temple
Fetishini
Amoeba Geezer
Judas Kiss
Robin Yablind
Levi
Silica Gel

Doors open at 9:15pm for 10pm start. NO STAG DO's
PLEASE NOTE: Customers with disabilities requiring seating, please do not hesitate to reach out to myself, Ness Quick @thonly_nessquick on Instagram. We will arrange seating or you at the standard ticket price

events.name: Strand Painters and Decorators KSW Club Champs!
events.venue: Kartsport Wellington
events.long_description:
Strand Painters and Decorators KSW Club Champs!
The next race day is nearly upon us, it's KSW Club Champs!

Join us on Sunday June 21 for a full day of bucket and kart racing at Kaitoke!

We’ll be running F4/F5 in two grades based on your time around the track:
• Buckets A Grade
• Buckets B Grade

If you’re not a KSW member you can still race!

Sign up link: https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSd_0jPfmC1r3Vhz6d6qMX_xPnoewvnBExVTFX6IbS-NUSeIHQ/viewform?pli=1

Spectators are free to come along to enjoy a good day at the track, everyone’s welcome! Just make sure you have your wits about you, keep yourself safe!

Tentative morning timings:
Track opens: 7am
Sign in: 7:45am – 8:30am
Drivers/Riders briefing: 8:45am
Racing starts: 9am
Schedule - TBC

KSW will have the café running throughout the day with snacks, lunch, drinks, and beersies at the end of the day!

Senior officials for the day are myself as Clerk and Dane as Steward with a new Steward in training, Georgia, coming to check our bucket community out for the first time!

If you're interested in volunteering, now or in the future, let us know and we can show you what it's like on this side of the race fence and help you get started!

See you there!

events.id: 10th Grade Tournament-Simon Street, Timona Park, Feilding, New Zealand 4702,Feilding United Football Club, Simon St, Feilding 4702, New Zealand, Palmerston North-All Events In
events.name: 10th Grade Tournament
events.venue: Simon Street, Timona Park, Feilding, New Zealand 4702,Feilding United Football Club, Simon St, Feilding 4702, New Zealand, Palmerston North
events.long_description:
10th Grade Tournament
10th Grade Tournament

Timona Park, Feilding | Hosted by Feilding United AFC Juniors

Feilding United AFC invites all 10th Grade teams registered to play in Central Football Manawatū to attend the 2026 10th Grade Festival in Feilding.

There is a 10th Grade Cup to be won, with runner-up medals awarded for second place. A minimum of eight teams is required for both awards to be presented.

No academy teams or tournament specific teams to enter.

WHEN & WHERE

The festival will be held on: Sunday 21 June 2026
(Postponement date: Sunday 28 June 2026)

📍 Timona Park, Feilding – Home of Feilding United AFC Juniors

· 8:30am – Coaches Meeting at the Feilding AFC Gazebo

· 9:00am – First games kick off

REGISTRATIONS

Registrations must be completed via Tournify using the link below:

https://tournifyapp.com/live/feildingafc10thgradetournament

Registration fee: $110 per team

The first 16 teams registered by 8th June 2026 will secure a place in the tournament.

RULES

Tournament rules will follow the current Central Football Regulations for Youth Leagues, which can be viewed here:

https://www.sporty.co.nz/asset/downloadasset?id=7eabcc5d-0023-4a42-9383-cb93c07e4814

DRAW & FORMAT

· Teams will play a minimum of four games.

· Games will consist of:

o 2 x 10-minute halves

o 2 -minute halftime

Points System

· Win = 3 points

· Draw = 1 point

The team with the most points will win their group.

If teams are tied on points, placings will be determined by:

1. Goal difference

2. Most goals scored

3. Toss of a coin

If a semi-final or final ends in a draw, the match will go directly to a penalty shootout. No extra time will be played.

REFEREES

Referees will be provided for most games. However, if a referee is unavailable, teams may be asked to provide a suitable person to control one half of the game.

The referee’s decision is final.

POSTPONEMENTS / CANCELLATIONS

The tournament will proceed regardless of weather conditions unless:

· Manawatū District Council closes the grounds, or

· A Health & Safety risk is identified by the Feilding United AFC Junior Football Committee.

Please remember that everyone involved in organising and refereeing this tournament are volunteers. Without them, this event could not take place.

Enjoy the day and the opportunity to watch the kids play the beautiful game!

CONTACT

For any questions or concerns, please email Blair at:

A minimum of eight teams is required for both awards to be presented.
· Teams will play a minimum of four games.
Referees will be provided for most games.
However, if a referee is unavailable, teams may be asked to provide a suitable person to control one half of the game.

events.id: Glass Jewellery-Making - 4-week Block-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Glass Jewellery-Making - 4-week Block
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Join us for this fabulous block of classes in glass jewellery-making!

The course runs from Sun 21 June for 4 weeks till Sun 12 July, 10am-1pm.

This class is a great way for absolute beginners and those with some experience to get into working with glass to produce beautiful beads that can be used in a myriad of ways.

Students will learn correct tool use and gain a solid understanding of the lampwork process. They will also learn some simple ways of incorporating their beads into wearable jewellery.

Course cost includes set materials, extra glass available to purchase.

This class involves gas torches and molten glass, people under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

We are only taking 8 students per class so book now!

events.id: BrainPlay: States of Matter - Hands-on science for young kids-Ngaio Scout Group | SCOUTS New Zealand, 30 Waikowhai street, Ngaio, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: BrainPlay: States of Matter - Hands-on science for young kids
events.venue: Ngaio Scout Group | SCOUTS New Zealand, 30 Waikowhai street, Ngaio, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
*** Exploring how the world changes ***
In our upcoming States of Matter workshop, children experiment with solids and liquids through hands-on activities and unusual materials that don’t always behave the way we expect.

As they stretch, pour, mix and test different substances, they begin to notice patterns, compare behaviours, and explore how materials can change under different conditions.

It’s less about “learning science facts” and more about:
→ Why does this feel different?
→ How can something act like both a solid and a liquid?
→ What happens if we change it?

These kinds of experiences help children develop curiosity, observation skills, early scientific thinking, and confidence in exploring how the world works.
They also support a deeper learning skill: becoming comfortable with uncertainty. When children experiment, they practise making predictions, testing ideas, noticing unexpected results, and trying again. This builds flexible thinking, problem-solving confidence, and the ability to see “I don’t know yet” as the beginning of discovery — not a failure.

Arrival & discovery activities: 11-11.30am
Workshop: 11.30am-12.30pm
Snacks & social time: 12.30-1pm

Limited to 8 children (ages 3½-6yo)

Location: Ngaio Scouts Hall, 30 Waikowhai street
Cost: $59, all materials, snacks & drinks included

***
At Radiant Minds we offer engaging, highly experiential discovery workshops for curious young kids in Wellington. Small-group, expertly facilitated sessions across STEM, art, sound & language — encouraging curiosity, confidence & critical thinking.

events.id: First Burials Tour-76 Old Karori Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand-Facebook
events.name: First Burials Tour
events.venue: 76 Old Karori Rd, Karori, Wellington 6012, New Zealand
events.long_description: From 1840 to 1900 Wellington went through an extraordinary period of development and change. A small rugged beach under cliffs was transformed into NZ’s major port, NZ’s largest commercial centre and the seat of government. The Friend’s walking tour 'First Burials - an introduction to the oldest parts of Karori Cemetery' visits the graves of the industrialists, merchants, artists, doctors, lawyers, teachers, politicians, engineers, architects and scientists who made all this happen. We bring to life the early history of Wellington by telling the story of their experiences and exploits. The tour visits the old Public, Anglican, Catholic, and Servicemens Cemetery sections that lie at the centre of the Cemetery. This is a relatively compact area and the tour, though taking up to 2 hours, does not involve any difficult terrain.

events.id: Conservation Kōrero: Roots of Resilience-ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Conservation Kōrero: Roots of Resilience
events.venue: ZEALANDIA Eco-Sanctuary, Waiapu Road, Karori, Wellington
events.long_description:
Climate anxiety is becoming increasingly common, particularly among people who care deeply about the future of the environment and society.

This thoughtful Conservation Kōrero explores how we can navigate uncertainty without becoming consumed by fear, helplessness or burnout. Through reflections on resilience, values and the restorative effects of nature, Dr Ivan Lin discusses how confidence can emerge from staying present and taking meaningful action aligned with what matters most.

Dr Lin is a medical doctor, speaker and workshop facilitator with experience delivering public and professional wellbeing programmes focused on resilience, emotional wellbeing and the relationship between people and the natural world.

This event will be held in the Pāteke Room on the Visitor Centre, with doors opening at 1.45pm. Parking is available outside the Visitor Centre, along Waiapu Road, and at our overflow carpark on Birdwood Street. Zealandia's free shuttle will be running it's usual schedule.

Photo: Scott Langdale

events.id: Silver Clay Taster-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Silver Clay Taster
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Join us to try out this amazing technique and make a gift for yourself or your loved ones :-)

Precious Metal Clay is an incredible product made from metal particles mixed with an organic binder. When your pieces are shaped - just like a modelling clay - they are then dried and fused in the kiln. That process takes only a few minutes so there is plenty of time in this 3-hour class to complete your projects.

Beginners start with simple pendants/earrings. It is much easier to start with flat projects so we leave the rings for the advanced class!

Jewellery pieces can be textured and shaped however you like, and if you don't like your piece simply squish it and start again - Precious Metal Clay is the ultimate recyclable material!

Included in the course fee is 5 grams of fine silver clay, enough to make a pendant or earrings, or a collection of charms - it's up to you.

Extra findings such as earring hooks and chains will be available to purchase (you can also make your earring hooks if you are feeling adventurous).

This class is great for kids also but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany any person under the age of 16.

events.id: Textured Bracelets-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Textured Bracelets
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
This lively, hands-on workshop is designed for complete beginners, but it’s also a great opportunity for more experienced students to enjoy a creative session making their own unique bangle!

Everyone will leave with a beautifully finished bracelet of their own preferred texture.

We’ll begin by experimenting on a sample strip of copper, where you’ll practice creating textures using hammers, punches and stamps. Once you’re confident, you’ll move on to your main project: a 3mm wide flat strip bracelet in either sterling silver or copper, crafted entirely by you from start to finish. Along the way, you’ll learn essential jewellery-making skills including measuring, texturing, soldering, shaping, and finishing.

Students are free to personalise their bangle with textures or stamped designs. This could be a simple hammered finish, decorative patterns, or meaningful words, names, dates, or poetry lines - making your bracelet truly one of a kind.
Beginners will work with the 3mm strip provided, ensuring a rewarding project that’s manageable to complete. Wider metal is much harder to form and solder, so we are learning to walk before we can run ;)

Copper is included in the course fee; if you wish to work in silver, you can purchase it on the day.

Experienced students may choose to challenge themselves by making a wider bracelet from a flat strip or by creating additional bangles in silver wire. (Please note: extra silver for wider bangles is available to purchase at the workshop for experienced students only, and let us know if you wish to go wider so we can make sure we have the strip in stock.)

This workshop is a taster class: perfect for anyone seeking a fun, creative experience with a beautiful result, or for those looking to dip their toes into jewellery making and build foundational skills.

events.id: Winter Solstice - Nourishing Cacao Ceremony & Golden Winter Sun Activation-Inner City Sanctuary,18-32 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Winter Solstice - Nourishing Cacao Ceremony & Golden Winter Sun Activation
events.venue: Inner City Sanctuary,18-32 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Winter Solstice - Nourishing Cacao Ceremony & Golden Winter Sun Activation
You’re warmly invited to this Winter Solstice gathering on the afternoon of Sunday 21st June.


A space intentionally created for solace and comfort - with cosy blankets, cushions, candlelight, and warmth to gently hold you like a soft cocoon.

An opportunity to slow down, settle your nervous system, and feel supported and nourished through the depths of winter’s longest night.

This two-part experience begins with Alexandra guiding you through gentle movement and a ceremonial cacao journey to bring warmth as you unwind, ground into the present moment, and reconnect within.

From this centred space, Nicola will lead you through a reflective meditation and Golden Winter Sun Activation, welcoming the returning light.

This Winter Solstice gathering weaves together:

• Ceremonial-grade cacao to open the heart and support relaxation
• Gentle movement to soften and nurture the body
• Sacred drumming
• Intention setting ritual
• Guided meditation and activation journey
• Reflection, grounding, and deep rest

Arrive just as you are and leave feeling connected and replenished.


Even within the darkest night, there is wisdom to receive as the light slowly begins to return. ✨

IMPORTANT INFORMATION:
• Wear comfortable clothing — warm, cosy socks too!
• Bring a cushion or two and a blanket. The more comfy you are, the better. (The venue has some yoga mats, cushions, and blankets available to use.)
• Bring an eye mask or eye pillow if you have one — a few spares will also be available
• Please arrive 15 minutes early to settle in
• Bring an open heart and mind

When you arrive at the Hipsta shop, the friendly staff will welcome you and show you where to go.

events.id: O Vere Beata Nox – O Truly Blessed Night-Erskine Chapel, 31 Avon Street, Island Bay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: O Vere Beata Nox – O Truly Blessed Night
events.venue: Erskine Chapel, 31 Avon Street, Island Bay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Settle into the rich acoustics of Erskine Chapel for this concert of deep listening, sacred voices, and transformation, as we celebrate darkness and the turning of time on the night of the winter solstice.

Featuring the immersive ambient electronica sounds of Tonic Collective; a taonga pūoro quartet with Riki Gooch, Al Fraser, Sam Palmer, and Ruby Solly; and a very special one-off appearance by Orchestra of Spheres performing on gongs, chimes and other instruments.

They are joined by Baroque Voices singing rarely heard works of 12th Century abbess St. Hildegard of Bingen.

This is a vigil of sound, depth and stillness — a space to rest, reflect, and be carried by sound through the depth of winter’s darkest night.

Live visuals created by Erica Sklenars (aka Lady Lazer Light).

– Music Schedule –
Tonic Collective (in the ballroom, between sets, and at close)
Taonga pūoro quartet: Riki Gooch, Al Fraser, Sam Palmer, Ruby Solly (45 mins)
Orchestra of Spheres (45 mins)
Baroque Voices (45 mins)
Grand Finale (15 mins)

Food and drink available at the venue.
This is a licensed event, U18s must be accompanied by a parent or guardian.
The evening will see the audience alternating between live acts in the upstairs chapel and the ambient electronica in the downstairs ballroom.

events.id: Monday Eve 8 Week Block - Mixed Levels-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Monday Eve 8 Week Block - Mixed Levels
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Our popular weeknight block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment. This class will be held at our new waterfront location, Market Lane!

This class is a mixed-level class, allowing beginners to work alongside advanced students. It is the best way for beginners to get into jewellery-making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

Beginners start with basic ring-making and stone-setting in copper or brass. Students will learn measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering. Then we move on to making a practice-ring set with a Cabochon stone (cabochon means it has a flat bottom and a smooth domed top) in a Bezel Setting. As you work through your practice ring and setting, you can move on to working in silver and produce another ring with a cabochon stone that you really love.

Level 2 students learn to solder more competently on their own using chainmaking as a way to develop their fine soldering skills. They may create a simple stone setting or ring alongside this challenge project so they have something to hone their skills that they are familiar with, as well as taking on a new skill.

Level 3 and above students are encouraged to work on their design skills and try new and challenging techniques. They should prepare to have one or two simple projects they can work on self-directed in class, alongside a challenge project.

This class fee does NOT include materials for any student.

Beginners should expect to spend at least $60 on materials for their ring making project.
All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!

All metals and stones as well as quality findings and chains are available for purchase each week in the studio. Students are encouraged to keep their silver scrap and find ways to use it thriftily in future projects!

A note for young makers: those over 12 years are welcome in this class, but anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.id: Adult Class - LED Neon Sign-Evans Bay Intermediate School, 14A Kemp St, Kilbirnie, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Adult Class - LED Neon Sign
events.venue: Evans Bay Intermediate School, 14A Kemp St, Kilbirnie, Wellington
events.long_description:
4 Weeks
Mon 18th May 7pm-9pm
Mon 25th May 7pm-9pm
Mon 8th June 7pm-9pm
Mon 15th June 7pm-9pm

OR

4 Weeks
Mon 22nd June 7pm-9pm
Mon 29th June 7pm-9pm
Mon 6th July 7pm-9pm
Mon 13th July 7pm-9pm

During this course everyone will get to design and build their own awesome LED Neon Sign. The sign can be up to 50cm x 40cm and there are a range of colours available.

The design for the sign will be completed using the open source program 'Inkscape'. The frame will then be cut out of clear acrylic using our laser cutter.

The neon strip can be cut and soldered together to make sharp corners or bent into reasonably tight curves as shown in the images. Once all the neon pieces are positioned they will be glued then soldered together and powered by a standard 12 Volt wall adaptor (which is provided). There is also the option of incorporating a small dimmer circuit that allows you to control the brightness of your sign.

Only 6 Students Per Class.

Some prior soldering experience would be beneficial.

events.id: Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick-Wellington Chamber Of Commerce,NTT Tower, Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick
events.venue: Wellington Chamber Of Commerce,NTT Tower, Level 13, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick
Beehive to Business with Chlöe Swarbrick

About this Event

With the election approaching, understanding how different political perspectives shape policy and direction has never been more important for business.
We’re pleased to welcome the next speaker in our Business to Beehive Leaders Series: Chlöe Swarbrick, Co-leader of the Green Party.
This series brings party leaders to our members for open conversations about their priorities, perspectives, and policy direction.
Join us on 23 June where Chlöe Swarbrick will share her views on the forces shaping New Zealand’s future, and the role business can play within a changing economic, environmental, and social landscape.
Hear about:
The Green Party’s priorities heading into the election
Key themes shaping their policy approach
Their perspective on the opportunities and challenges facing New Zealand businesses
How they see the role of business in building a more sustainable and resilient economy
This is the third in our series of high-value discussions designed to give our members direct access to political leaders and a broader understanding of the landscape ahead.
Join us for a thoughtful and engaging discussion as you navigate the lead-up to election day.
Agenda:
7:15am- Standing breakfast, networking
7:45am- Address
8:10am- Q&A
8:30am- Event concludes
Can't make it? Click here to register for the free livestream. Sign up and watch at a time convenient to you.
Part of the ongoing Beehive to Business series, this event is proudly made possible through our partnership with Wellington-based consulting firm Allen + Clarke.

events.long_description:
Art n Social for Autistic Adults
Tomorrow is Art n Social, an art group for autistic adults to come and do art for themselves, it's not a class, it doesn't need to be art that someone else wants you to do. There are options but they are there to create your own art. Support people are welcome, art options available are: colouring (for when creating is simply picking colours), painting (acrylic and watercolours), felting, jewelry making, something with wool (we have 1 crochet hook), pens, pencils, air dry clay.
There are even some DnD trees to decorate for the DnD sessions.

events.id: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making in 8 weeks for Beginners
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
This class is beginner level only, with each student following the class project.

It starts Tue 23 June and runs for 8 weeks ill Tue 11 August, 5.30pm - 8pm.

Our popular weeknight classes are the best way for beginners to get into jewellery-making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

The stone-setting process teaches you many fundamental techniques in one project!

We start with basic ring-making and stone-setting in copper or brass. Students will learn measuring, texturing, shaping, cutting and soldering.

Then we move on to making a practice ring set with a Cabochon stone (a cabochon has a flat bottom and a smooth, domed top) in a Bezel Setting.

As you work through your practice ring and setting, you can move on to working in silver and produce another ring with a cabochon stone that you really love. This way, you will learn the basics as you go on your practice ring, before taking the next step in costly silver.

This class has all materials included in the class fee!

Each student receives a carefully curated materials pack including copper or brass, sterling and fine silver for your ring-making projects, as well as two stones from our supply so you get a good stone for your level of experience.

We have loads of gorgeous stones of a range of colours, and they are all appropriate for beginner settings.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time, please email us to arrange this ;)

events.id: Gauntlet Prom: The Musical-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Gauntlet Prom: The Musical
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
“Get your gauntlets - it’s the most important night of your lives!”

At Pinnacle Academy, future heroes and villains prepare for epic downtown battles. To secure their top choice of sponsor, they’ll need a compelling rivalry. And what better place to throw down the gauntlet and secure the nemesis of their dreams than prom?
Featuring a talented team of local Wellington Creatives, Gauntlet Prom is a campy, high-energy comedy musical about the pressures of superhero high school; who to team up with, and how to find your one true nemesis.
Will the school power rivalry finally throw the gauntlet and make it official? Will the lone hero ever find a nemesis? Will the reputation of the academy survive its graduating class?

Described by audience as “Extremely witty and queer, a delightful play on romantic, highschool and superhero tropes.” Gauntlet Prom is the Queer, Comedy, Musical, High school, Superhero show that you never knew you needed to see.


Show Duration - 60 Minutes

events.id: Hadestown: Teen Edition-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Rd, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Hadestown: Teen Edition
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Rd, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Kapiti College is excited to present Hadestown: Teen Edition, giving local audiences the opportunity to experience this internationally acclaimed musical live on stage.

Hadestown: Teen Edition blends modern folk and jazz-inspired music with ancient Greek mythology. It follows Orpheus and Eurydice, a young couple trying to make things work in a tough world, alongside Hades and Persephone, who rule over a dark, industrial underworld. When Eurydice is pulled into that world, Orpheus risks everything to bring her back, relying on nothing but his music and his belief in her. It’s a story about love, hope, and the choices we make.

The show is a huge team effort, with around 100 students involved. While many take the stage, others are working behind the scenes on lighting, sound, set design, and stage management. This helps to bring the entire production to life and gives students valuable experience both on and off stage.

--

For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email kiaora@teraukura.nz or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

events.id: OWE - a New Aotearoa Play!-BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: OWE - a New Aotearoa Play!
events.venue: BATS Theatre, 1 Kent Tce, Wellington
events.long_description:
A MUST-SEE new Aotearoa play written by Jack McGee and directed by Campbell Wright!

Adria’s transmission is shot, and she can’t afford to fix it. Her girlfriend, Mel, offers to pay. Mel doesn’t have the money. She asks her Aunt Jennifer. Jennifer is squeezed with the mortgage. Her son Steven owes her a few grand. Maybe she can get that back?

Loosely inspired by La Ronde, Arthur Schnitzler’s 19th century satire about interlinking lovers, Owe follows twelve individuals down a chain of debt and difficult conversations. With crackling dialogue, a sense of humour, and one hell of an ensemble, Squash Co’s newest work casts a wide lens on the cost of living crisis.

The play weaves a sincere tapestry of human interdependence in a world of shrinking wallets, narrowing worldviews, and closing borders. With twelve, short, eclectic scenes, Owe moves fast and keeps you wanting more.

️"Pōneke's brightest young theatre-makers - and honestly, thank god they're here" - Theatreview (on previous work)

OWE
23 - 27 June | 7pm (incl. additional 4pm matinee on 27 June) https://bats.co.nz/whats-on/owe/

events.id: Stereolab tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Stereolab tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Banished Music & Strange News
The panic is over! Anglo-French avant-pop group Stereolab are indeed returning to Aotearoa in June, hot on the mechanical heels of last year’s brilliant album Instant Holograms on Metal Film. This was the band’s first studio album in fifteen years, and followed their celebrated reunion in 2019. The Guardian hailed the album as “a radiant return”, and Rolling Stone fizzed over the band’s “gliding grooves, wowing-and-fluttering synthesizers, and lyrics that elegantly pine for more”. They’ve got a cult following for a very good reason, and we’re beyond astonished to have them as part of our winter line-up!

events.venue: Te Papa Tongarewa,55 Cable St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Uhi Tai: Te Reo Māori in Business 2026
Uhi Tai brings together leaders, innovators and practitioners from across Aotearoa to explore how te reo Māori is shaping the future of business, technology, sport, tourism and brand identity. Over two days, speakers from across sectors will share practical insights into how organisations are embedding te reo Māori into strategy, customer experience, innovation and everyday operations.

Whether you are beginning your reo journey or are already integrating te reo Māori into your work, Uhi Tai offers an opportunity to learn, connect and be inspired by those leading this kaupapa.

The theme for Uhi Tai: Te Reo Māori in Business 2026 is ‘Ihu ki te kei, we all have a part to play’, speaking to the idea that there's room for everyone on a canoe, and there are opportunities for everyone to contribute to the journey.

Registrations are now open, get your tickets now e hoa mā.
Kia Kaha te Reo Māori | Te Taura Whiri i te Reo Māori

events.id: Lost Wax Casting - 6 Weeks Course-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax Casting - 6 Weeks Course
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
A fantastic course that's full of fun and inspiration!

It starts on Wed 25 March and runs for 6 weeks till Wed 29 April, 5.30pm - 8pm.

Create interesting designs in wax, then send them off to be professionally cast in metal. Once your pieces are back, you will be taught how to cut, file and shape them into wearable pieces.

Level 1 students: Learn to work in both soft and hard jewellers' wax and send your waxes away to be cast. When they return, you will learn how to clean them up into finished jewellery and/or incorporate them into jewellery in multiple ways. We start with rings, but encourage students to think outside the box!

Level 2 students: Advance your carving skills and learn to set stones in your waxes. This can be done either by creating claws and other settings or trying the cast-in-place technique - an organic, exciting process.

Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.

Not included are the casting and metal costs, which vary from student to student depending on what they want to make. Small castings can cost around $7 - $10, and metals are between $0.35 per gram for bronze - $4 per gram for silver. Please see details on our Regular Classes page.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, PayPal or Afterpay on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time, please email us to arrange this ;)

events.venue: Hey Coastie, Wellington, 42 Tutere Street, Waikanae
events.long_description:
The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Waikanae Beach! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: First drink is included in the ticket price

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.id: Wānaga Matariki-Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Wānaga Matariki
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa, 55 Cable St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wānanga Matariki: Matariki Herenga Waka is a special Matariki evening kōrero exploring the traditions, knowledge and future of waka voyaging in Aotearoa and across the Pacific.

Presented by the crew of Ngahiraka-Mai-Tawhiti and Te Kura Waka, this free public wānanga brings together experienced navigators, cultural leaders and storytellers to share insights into traditional celestial navigation, ocean knowledge, waka hourua traditions, and the intergenerational revival of indigenous voyaging practices.

Through personal stories, cultural perspectives and discussion, speakers will reflect on the past, present and future of waka navigation, and the role of mātauranga Māori in reconnecting communities with whakapapa, te ao Māori and the moana.

The evening features respected speakers and facilitators including Tainui Stephens, Manihera Forbes, Hinerapa Rupuha and Tamahau Tangitu, each bringing extensive experience in Māori storytelling, cultural leadership, traditional navigation and Pacific voyaging.

Suitable for adults, educators, cultural practitioners and anyone interested in Māori knowledge systems, navigation traditions and contemporary indigenous storytelling.

Booking is recommended.

This event is wheelchair accessible. Please note that variable lighting may be used during the event.
events.description:
Wānanga Matariki: Matariki Herenga Waka is a special Matariki evening kōrero exploring the traditions, knowledge and future of waka voyaging in Aotearoa and across the Pacific.
Presented by the crew of Ngahiraka-Mai-Tawhiti and Te Kura Waka, this free public wānanga brings together experienced navigators, cultural leaders and storytellers to share insights into traditional celestial navigation, ocean knowledge, waka hourua traditions, and the intergenerational revival of indigenous voyaging practices.
Through personal stories, cultural perspectives and discussion, speakers will reflect on the past, present and future of waka navigation, and the role of mātauranga Māori in reconnecting communities with whakapapa, te ao Māori and the moana.
The evening features respected speakers and facilitators including Tainui Stephens, Manihera Forbes, Hinerapa Rupuha and Tamahau Tangitu, each bringing extensive experience in Māori storytelling, cultural leadership, traditional navigation and Pacific voyaging.
Suitable for adults, educators, cultural practitioners and anyone interested in Māori knowledge systems, navigation traditions and contemporary indigenous storytelling.

events.name: Stereolab (UK) w/ Glass Vaults
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington
events.long_description:
The panic is over! Banished Music and Strange News are impossibly excited to be hosting iconic Anglo-French avant-pop group Stereolab for two very special shows in Aotearoa this June.

Fans who saw the Strange Universe: Winter series announcement yesterday will have seen the band confirmed to play Powerstation on Thursday 25 June, but Wellington fans can breathe a sigh of relief with the announcement of a standalone Stereolab show at Meow Nui, Wednesday 24 June.

‍Returning on the mechanical heels of last year’s brilliant album Instant Holograms on Metal Film. This was the band’s first studio album in fifteen years, and followed their celebrated reunion in 2019. The Guardian hailed the album as “a radiant return”, and Rolling Stone fizzed over the band’s “gliding grooves, wowing-and-fluttering synthesizers, and lyrics that elegantly pine for more”.
‍
“..hailed as an inspiration by everyone from Deerhoof to Tyler, the Creator” - The Guardian

Stereolab have a cult following for a very good reason, with Banished and Strange News beyond astonished to have secured one of their all time favourite acts for these two hotly anticipated shows.

events.id: Between the Lines: Private Lives with New Zealand String Quartet-Ngā Pou Ruahine, Level 2, Te Matapihi, 65 Victoria Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Between the Lines: Private Lives with New Zealand String Quartet
events.venue: Ngā Pou Ruahine, Level 2, Te Matapihi, 65 Victoria Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This event will take place in Ngā Pou Ruahine on Level 2 of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, Wellington Central Library.

The theme of our June musical book club is private lives.

Between the Lines is a monthly early-evening chamber music series and book club that brings music and literature into conversation. Presented in the beautiful setting of Ngā Pou Ruahine in Te Matapihi, the series invites audiences to listen, think, and talk. No prior knowledge of music or literature is required, just a curious mind.

Taking place monthly from April to October on the final Thursday of each month, Between the Lines has been co-created by New Zealand String Quartet, Orchestra Wellington, and New Zealand School of Music. Each month these organisations will take turns presenting a talented group of musicians to perform music and host discussion on a different literary theme. From devil’s bargains to language games, the obsessive tortured artist to the intimate private lives of writers and composers, this book club will delve deep into themes that span across artforms.

Each session will feature carefully selected music and a reading list highlighting the theme of the month. So, arrive solo or bring your book club to Te Matapihi for a new kind of conversation.


The theme of our June musical book club is private lives.

Music to be performed:
Amadeus Mozart | String quartet Kv. 465 “Dissonance”, 1st movement
Leoš Janáček | Intimate Letters, 3rd movement
Dmitri Shostakovich | String Quartet No. 8, 1st & 2nd movement
Felix Mendelssohn | String Quartet No.6, Op.80, 1st movement
Pyotr Tchaikovsky | String Quartet No. 1, Op. 11, 2nd movement
Florence Price | "Adoration"

Optional reading list is available on the New Zealand String Quartet website.

Music and literature both have the power to transport an audience deep into the interior private lives of the composer and author. For our June musical book club, Between the Lines, which will take place on the anniversary of the publication of Anne Frank’s diaries in 1947, the New Zealand String Quartet have devised a programme of music that explores the ‘Private Lives’, secret thoughts, and hidden desires of four composers. Mozart, Janáček, Shostakovich, Mendelssohn, Tchaikovsky and Price each encoded their music with secrets, imbuing their music with a personal significance often at odds with their public personas. Now, by reading their letters, alongside performing their music, we can learn more about these composers’ personalities and appreciate their thoughts, fears, and hopes.

The reading list for ‘Private Lives’ brings together a variety of primary sources – the letters and diaries of artists, writers, composers and people living through extraordinary and difficult times – alongside fiction that utilises diary and letter forms.


Explore the rest of the series:

Thursday 30 April, 5:30pm - Orchestra Wellington - The Art of Conversation (first & second sessions to take place on Ngā Paiaka/ Bleachers on Te Papa Whenua/ Ground Floor)
Thursday 28 May, 5:30pm - New Zealand String Quartet - Obsession (first & second sessions to take place on Ngā Paiaka/ Bleachers on Te Papa Whenua/ Ground Floor)
Thursday 25 June, 5:30pm - New Zealand String Quartet - Private Lives
Thursday 30 July, 5:30pm - Orchestra Wellington - The Devil's Bargain
Thursday 27 August, 5:30pm - New Zealand String Quartet - Outsider
Thursday 24 September, 5:30pm - NZSM - Te Kōkī - Theme to be announced
Thursday 29 October, 5:30pm - Orchestra Wellington - Language Games

events.id: Jewellery-making on Thursdays, 8-week block, Level 2+-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making on Thursdays, 8-week block, Level 2+
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Our popular weeknight block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment. This class will be held at our new waterfront location, Market Lane!

The course starts on Thursday 25 June and runs for 8 weeks till Thursday 13 August, 5.30pm-8pm.

This class is designed for students who already have some skills and are ready to develop their craftsmanship in a lively, warm, and supportive environment. Guided by our highly skilled tutors, you’ll build confidence, refine your techniques, and gain the satisfaction of making jewellery that reflects your personal style.

If you’re brand new to jewellery making, we recommend beginning with our Tuesday Evening 8-Week Beginners’ Block, which gives a solid foundation in essential techniques before moving on to this class.

At Level 2, the focus is on precision and technique. Students explore the art of chain making, a classic jewellery making project that develops fine soldering skills and accuracy in construction. This project is an excellent way to build confidence with the torch and create a piece of jewellery that is both functional and beautiful. The finished chain can become a pendant, bracelet, or necklace, or you may choose to adapt it into a design of your own.

At Level 3 and above, the emphasis shifts toward creativity and design development. Students at this stage are encouraged to take on more complex projects, experiment with new and challenging techniques, and bring their own design ideas to life. With the guidance of our skilled and supportive tutors, you’ll learn how to push your skills further, problem-solve during the making process, and refine your eye for detail and design.

Evening classes are not just about the technical side of jewellery making - they are also a chance to slow down, enjoy the process, and be part of a creative community. Many students return term after term, finding the rhythm of regular making both mindful and rewarding.

Please note: this class does not include a materials allowance, giving you the flexibility to choose your own metals, stones, or other materials. You only pay for what you use, making it easier to customise your projects and work at your own pace.

Our booking system asks for full payment at the time of registration (via credit card, PayPal, or AfterPay). If you don’t have access to these options or would like to pay in instalments, please email us and we’ll happily arrange an alternative.

events.long_description:
Yoga Nidra: Winter Solstice
Yoga Nidra: Winter Solstice

On this threshold moment, as nature gently calls us inward, we join together to honour the longest night of the year.

In this special Yoga Nidra, we respect the beauty of the pause. You’ll be guided into a deeply restful state between waking and sleeping, where the body can fully release, the nervous system can unwind, and the mind can settle into spacious awareness.

Through breath, body sensing, and subtle imagery, we’ll rest in the richness of the dark - not as something to move through,
but as something to be held by.

This is a practice of deep nourishment, quiet reflection, and inner listening

Come wrapped in warmth, bring anything that helps you feel held, and allow yourself to be guided into profound rest.

There will be an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert afterwards - all included in your ticket!



Meet Nicola, your host
Nicola of Namaste Nicola is a dedicated yoga teacher with over 20 years of experience teaching across Australia and New Zealand. With more than 300 hours of training with international teachers, her approach is gentle, soulful, and deeply grounded in yoga philosophy. Nicola creates a calm and nurturing space where students can slow down, rest deeply, and reconnect with themselves through practices such as yoga nidra, hatha, and yin yoga.

events.name: Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness
events.venue: 175 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,175 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness
Cultivating Calm: Positive Psychology & Mindfulness

Develop the tools you need for navigating stress, anxiety, and overwhelm.

Mary Rose Dias - Positive Psychology Practitioner (MSc), Yoga Therapist, and Certified iRest®️ Meditation Teacher - will be leading a nourishing and practical evening exploring how mindfulness and positive psychology can help us cultivate greater calm, resilience, and inner steadiness.

Life can be demanding, and when stress builds in our system, it can become difficult to access clarity, ease, and perspective. While we can’t always remove life’s challenges, we can learn to shift our relationship with stress and develop tools that help us meet it with greater awareness and self-compassion.

Through guided meditation, reflection, and experiential practices, you’ll be introduced to accessible tools to help you:
•⁠ ⁠Regulate stress and settle anxiety
•⁠ ⁠Connect with a deeper sense of inner safety and steadiness
•⁠ ⁠Clarify intentions and reconnect with what matters most
•⁠ ⁠Access practical resources for greater calm and wellbeing in everyday life

Together, we’ll explore grounding practices drawn from mindfulness, meditation, and positive psychology, beginning and ending with guided meditation to support integration and deep rest.

This is more than just developing a mindset, you’ll be leaving with something practical to draw upon long after the evening ends.

Open to all - no prior meditation experience needed.

Plus - there is an amazing plant-based dinner & dessert included in your ticket! Stay back for the good food & great company.

Meet Mary Rose
Mary is a Wellington-based holistic therapist, meditation teacher and yoga practitioner whose work gently weaves together evidence-based psychology, ancient yogic wisdom, meditation practices and a whole lot of unconditional love and respect for each person’s unique journey. With over 15 years of experience in wellbeing, counselling, coaching, yoga and meditation, she is deeply passionate about creating nurturing spaces that support healing, self-discovery and inner calm.

Her heart-felt calling is to support others in accessing greater health, healing and happiness. While based in her birthplace of Aotearoa New Zealand, Mary’s dedication to wellness has taken her around the world, including Hawaii, Australia, Ireland, the Czech Republic and the UK, where she has trained with internationally recognised teachers in yoga, meditation and positive psychology. Through private sessions, workshops, courses and retreats, she offers a grounded, compassionate and holistic approach that honours both modern psychology and timeless wisdom traditions.

events.id: Forest & Bird talk - 100 Years Old and Counting ...-The Garden Room at St Peter's on Willis, 170 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Forest & Bird talk - 100 Years Old and Counting ...
events.venue: The Garden Room at St Peter's on Willis, 170 Victoria St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Come and hear from … Anthea McClelland (past Chair of Forest & Bird Manawatū branch) who will guide you through 100 years of Forest & Bird - how it started and how it grew over the years, with a few anecdotes popped in along the way.

Anthea joined Forest & Bird Manawatū in early 2002 and in 2007 was voted onto the Branch Committee as Secretary and then in 2014 as Chair. She stepped down as Chair in 2024 and now serves on the Branch Committee – that’s over 24 years involvement with the Society!

Our Wellington Branch Chair, Kate Littin will also give a brief on what’s happening presently and what’s coming, and delve into the important role that Wellington and Wellingtonians played in Forest & Bird's first steps and how you can carry the legacy forward. We look forward to seeing you there!

- ADMISSION FREE - KOHA WELCOME -

events.long_description:
RQP presents Showquest & TOI, a night celebrating creative rangatahi as they bring their ideas to life on stage.

Showquest is a nationwide performing arts programme and a platform for youth voices to be heard. Be amazed by original performances combining Art, Music, Dance, Drama, Culture & Technology, as students design, produce and perform their stories in celebration and recognition by their community.

Following the Showquest stage performances, prepare to be WOWed with TOI, a show-stopping wearable art showcase featuring bold, imaginative garments by emerging student designers. 

Watch friendly inter-school rivalries come to life as students explore the issues that shape their world. Join friends and wh?nau for an unforgettable night supporting local talent on the big stage.

Showquest and TOI are proudly presented by RQP, champions for young creators and producers of Smokefreerockquest, Smokefree Tangata Beats, Rockshop Bandquest, OnScreen and Stills.

RQP presents Showquest & TOI, a night celebrating creative rangatahi as they bring their ideas to life on stage.
Showquest is a nationwide performing arts programme and a platform for youth voices to be heard.
Be amazed by original performances combining Art, Music, Dance, Drama, Culture & Technology, as students design, produce and perform their stories in celebration and recognition by their community.
Following the Showquest stage performances, prepare to be WOWed with TOI, a show-stopping wearable art showcase featuring bold, imaginative garments by emerging student designers.
Watch friendly inter-school rivalries come to life as students explore the issues that shape their world.

events.id: Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show in Upper Hutt: Fun for All Ages!-Whirinaki Whare Taonga,836 Fergusson Drive, Upper Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show in Upper Hutt: Fun for All Ages!
events.venue: Whirinaki Whare Taonga,836 Fergusson Drive, Upper Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show in Upper Hutt: Fun for All Ages!
Experience the Hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show Where You Become the Star of the Show!

As seen on Seven Sharp (TVNZ) and going viral on TikTok, you’re in for a treat! Comedy Hypnotist Haiming Jiang is bringing his hit Comedy Hypnosis Show to your area. Get ready to laugh hysterically – and even join in, if you're up for it! It’s a night of unforgettable fun.

Haiming blends hypnosis and comedy to entertain the audience while showcasing the power and benefits of hypnosis through live audience participation. With his unique cultural background and deep understanding of both Western and Oriental cultures, this is a must-see event for anyone curious about hypnosis and keen for a good laugh.

Come and discover how this talented Chinese hypnotist uses hypnosis to both entertain crowds and deeply relax participants.
Experience the hilarious Comedy Hypnosis Show – where you could become the star of the night! (Only if you’re keen – no pressure!)

events.venue: The Arborist Rooftop Bar,166 Willis Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington Speed Dating for ages 26-44 by Cheeky Events New Zealand
Get ready for a night of fun and connection at Wellington's hottest speed dating event!

About this Event

Let’s ditch the apps together and embrace real connections. Say goodbye to the dating apps and hello to your very own Cheeky Love Story! 💖
🍸 What’s included: Your first drink is on us—let’s toast to great company!
👇How It Works:
Before the event:Make sure to download the Cheeky Events App (free on Google Play & the App Store) to set up your profile and upload a photo.
P.S. Don’t worry—it’s not a dating app! We’ve skipped the chat feature because we’re all about real, in-person connections.
During the event:
Check in to grab your name tag and free drink token. 🍻
Arrive on time to enjoy 15 minutes of casual pre-mingling.
Ladies stay seated while men rotate every 5 minutes—don’t forget to rate your matches in the app! 👍👎
Take a quick 15-minute break before the next round.
After the rotations, feel free to stick around for some extra socializing!
After the event: Stay for extra mingling and check the app for your cheeky matches 12 hours later! 📱💖
For more details, check out our blog: What to expect
Who knows? You might just find your next spark! ✨ Our IRL singles events (single mixers and speed dating events) are happening across Australia, New Zealand, London, and NYC! 🌏

About Cheeky Events
We host fun, inclusive singles events across Australia, New Zealand, London, and NYC! Whether you’re in your 20s, over 40, looking for love, or just looking to meet new people, our events are designed to bring people together. Whether you’re seeking romance, new friendships, or even networking, we’ve got something for everyone!
👉 Follow us on Instagram:@cheekyeventsaustralia @cheekyeventsukandireland @cheekyeventsnewzealand @cheekyeventsukandireland @cheekeventsus
Visit our website: www.cheekyevents.net or download the Cheeky Events app to get started.
We can’t wait to see you at one of our events soon! 🕺💃

events.id: Engelbert Humperdinck — The Celebration Tour-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Engelbert Humperdinck — The Celebration Tour
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
He has a unique voice that has charmed millions of fans around the world. Engelbert Humperdinck returns to Wellington in June 2026 for The Celebration Tour. After 60 extraordinary years, the legend continues to woo with crowd favourites. Audiences can expect hits like Release Me, The Last Waltz, A Man Without Love, Can’t Take My Eyes Off of You, and Quando, Quando, Quando. 
Over the past six decades, Engelbert has sold over 140 million records. His achievements include 64 gold albums, 35 platinum albums, four Grammy nominations, a Golden Globe, and stars on the Hollywood Walk of Fame, Las Vegas Walk of Fame, and Leicester Walk of Fame. Recordings span everything from romantic ballads to movie theme songs, disco, rock, and even gospel.

events.id: Te Wehi — I’m Home NZ Album Tour-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Te Wehi — I’m Home NZ Album Tour
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A debut album and a first-ever headline tour of New Zealand and Australia make 2026 a huge year for Te Wehi. The Kiwi singer has found fame for his mix of reggae, soul, and country. He’s carved out his own sound and, in the process, connected with a genuine fan base across the country. All 15 songs on the album will feature in the concert. They are rooted in real-life stories, cultural pride, and deep whakapapa . His down-to-earth approach and humble outlook make his songs resonate with emotional honesty and authenticity.   
Some would argue that his self-produced sound and sincere approach have propelled him to the number one place on the Aotearoa Music Charts throughout 2025. This is on top of taking his live performances to the main stages at Homegrown and Rhythm & Vines, and supporting L.A.B on their recent AU and NZ tour.

events.long_description:
Transporting New Zealand National Conference (60th Anniversary)
Early bird registrations are officially open for our National Conference in 2026! 26 - 27 June at Parliament, Wellington.

Don't miss out! The early bird rate ends 28 February 2026, or while allocations last.

Our focus areas will include the political landscape ahead of the 2026 general election, the future of technology and artificial intelligence, and managing costs in the current economic climate.

We are also excited to be hosting the Road Freight Industry Awards, and celebrating success across the entire industry. Nominations are open now at www.transporting.nz/conference/nomination-form

For more information or to register, visit www.transporting.nz/conference
