events.id: Matariki: Bringing our stars to life — A hands-on learning experience-National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Matariki: Bringing our stars to life — A hands-on learning experience
events.venue: National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
Nau mai, te whetū o te tau! Join us for our special Matariki event.

Using a whāriki star map and special glowing pīataata (star globes), come and share the magic of Matariki mā Puanga this Hōtoke season. We will sit around a giant 7 metre mat, learning about the different qualities of each whetū (star) and what they relate to in nature.

It is beautiful to make the star globes glow in different colours, respresenting the Matariki star cluster. We’ll mihi, sing and tuku karakia to our loved ones who have passed and make wishes to Hiwa-i-te-rangi for the year ahead!

Booking is not required — you can just turn up on the day.

events.id: Waltz of My Heart-Carterton Events Centre, 50 Holloway Street, Carterton-Event Finder
events.name: Waltz of My Heart
events.venue: Carterton Events Centre, 50 Holloway Street, Carterton
events.long_description:
Step into a world of sweeping melodies, soaring voices, and the graceful rhythm of the waltz in this unforgettable concert Waltz of My Heart, a celebration of one of the most beloved musical forms in history.

From the iconic Viennese waltzes of Johann Strauss to modern interpretations such as The Anniversary Waltz and The Tennessee Waltz, this enchanting programme showcases the enduring beauty and emotional power of the waltz across generations and genres. Whether heard in grand ballrooms or intimate theatres, this music continues to captivate and enchant.

Taking centre stage are our four magnificent tenors whose rich, expressive voices breathe new life into classic and contemporary waltzes alike. Joining them is fabulous baritone John Bayne plus stunning Amanda Atlas, whose powerful soprano and magnetic presence bring depth and drama to every note.

This is a concert of melody, magic, and masterful singing — where every phrase is infused with passion and every performance is unforgettable. Let yourself be swept away by the romance, elegance, and enduring charm of the waltz.

Featuring:
Bonaventure Allan-Moetaua
Benjamin Makisi
Derek Hill
Kalauni Pouvalu
Amanda Atlas
John Bayne
Paul Carnegie-Jones

NZ Tour 02 June 2026 - 03 July 2026
Concerts Start 11am
General Admission Seating
Doors Open 10am – Complimentary morning tea included.

Phone: 0508 266 237
Email: bookings@operatunity.co.nz

events.id: Mid-Winter Christmas (Lunch/Dinner) 2026-Tirohana Estate Vineyard, 42 Puruatanga Rd, Martinborough-Event Finder
events.name: Mid-Winter Christmas (Lunch/Dinner) 2026
events.venue: Tirohana Estate Vineyard, 42 Puruatanga Rd, Martinborough
events.long_description:
An annual tradition in Martinborough and the Wairarapa, Tirohana Cellars' Restaurant will be transformed into a magical Christmas winter wonderland, with twinkling fairy lights, mulled wine, Christmas trees, evocative music, the warmth of the open fire - and who knows, we might even get some snow in the vineyard!? :)

There is a festive menu where Christmas turkey and all the trimmings will be on offer (with options for vegetarians/special diets). But there are plenty of other options on the menu available for those who do not want to indulge in Turkey and ham off the bone, plum pudding and other favourites.

Lunch is three courses (starter, main, dessert) for $69 pp and Dinner is $75 pp for five courses (amuse bouche, starter, main, dessert, petit fours)

A wonderful lunch or dinner for a couple or a group, this Tirohana institution is something you won't want to miss. If you're feeling particularly festive, why not dress to suit the occasion - we'll be running a competition on our Facebook page for the best Midwinter photo.

Reservations are essential to secure a table for lunch or dinner.

To book a table please call 06 306 9933 or email makeabooking@tirohanaestate.com or book online https://www.tirohanaestate.com/restaurant/book-a-table/.

Winter Ales Fest is BACK for 2026!

Enjoy a unique selection of brand new winter beers, from NZ's best small and micro scale brewers.

Winter Ales Fest 2026 will again be held at the amazing Whisky & Wood event space, conveniently located at 60 Cambridge Terrace (above Sweet Axe Throwing Co).

events.id: Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store - Distillery-The Bond Store,21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store - Distillery
events.venue: The Bond Store,21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store - Distillery
Re-use, Re-Purpose, Beautify with Gold!! Come along and learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Sip & Kintsugi.

About this Event

Come and experience this unique Sip & Kintsugi workshop at the lovely Bond Store Distillery. You will get a gin tasting tray on arrival with three different gins with tonic. And why not come a little earlier and grab a bite to eat beforehand. (Must be 18+ yrs of age)
Learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Workshop. You can bring along a piece of your own pottery to restore in this beautiful style. (I'll also have a few as back up if you don't have your own piece) (All Levels)
Friday 26th June - 5:30pm - 7:30pm
Kintsugi
It's called kintsugi (金継ぎ), or kintsukuroi (金繕い), literally golden (“kin”) and repair (“tsugi”). This traditional Japanese art uses a precious metal – liquid gold, liquid silver or lacquer dusted with powdered gold – to bring together the pieces of a broken pottery item and at the same time enhance the breaks.
You will take away your wonderful finished work, plus your knew skills which will enable you to further your own Kintsugi in your own time.
This class is not just for budding or accomplished artists. It is for everyone to enjoy a relaxing day of art and design with Ema.
What you will take away:
A finished artwork piece
A understanding of Kintsugi
Knowledge and History of this beautiful art form.

Where: The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham St, Paraparaumu, Kapiti Coast 5032. NEW ZEALAND
When: Friday 26th June
5:30 - 7:30pm

&t=6s

events.id: Lost Wax in 3 - Soft Wax - Friday Nights-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax in 3 - Soft Wax - Friday Nights
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Over three sessions, two weeks apart, create stunning jewellery using the age-old lost wax technique. This is an excellent opportunity for a best friends' outing or a self-care date with yourself!

In the first and second session, you'll learn how to work with jeweller's soft wax to create impressions of natural textures and creatures like seashells, leaves and feathers. We can also use moulds created using Fimo, and press soft wax into them, which will result in amazing pieces!

Then there’s a week's break before the third session, to allow time for everyone's waxes to be cast. The third session covers how to trim, clean and finish your casts, turning them into original jewellery ready to wear!

More advanced students can use these sessions to complete projects in a class setting.

Included in the class fee are all your different types of waxes, sandpaper and courier costs.

Not included are the casting and metal costs which vary from student to student depending on what sized piece they want to make and the type of metal they choose. Small castings typically cost around $6.65–$12, with metal costs ranging from approximately $7 to $30, depending on whether you use brass, bronze, or silver.

events.id: Lost Wax in Three - Mitsuro Hikime Wax-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Lost Wax in Three - Mitsuro Hikime Wax
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Discover the magic of Mitsuro Hikime — the beautiful Japanese technique that uses a special stretchy wax to create flowing, organic textures unlike anything else in jewellery-making.

Over three inspiring sessions, you’ll learn how to transform this fascinating material into stunning wearable pieces using the traditional lost wax casting process.

Mitsuro Hikime wax behaves very differently from standard jeweller’s wax. Warm and pliable, it can be stretched, twisted and pulled into delicate ribbons and natural textures that resemble flowing water, bark, roots or molten metal. The results are beautifully organic and wonderfully unpredictable, making every piece completely unique.

During the first two sessions, you’ll learn how to handle and shape this traditional Japanese wax, exploring different techniques to create rings, pendants or small sculptural objects full of movement and texture. Once your designs are complete, your waxes will be professionally cast in the metal of your choice.

There is then a one-week break before the final session to allow time for casting. In the third class, you’ll learn how to cut, file, sand and finish your cast pieces, transforming them into polished jewellery ready to wear, gift or treasure.

This course is suitable for complete beginners as well as more experienced makers wanting to experiment with this exciting and expressive technique. More advanced students are also welcome to use the sessions to develop independent projects in a supportive studio environment.

Included in the class fee are all Mitsuro Hikime waxes, consumables, sandpaper and courier costs.

Please note that casting and metal costs are not included, as these vary depending on the size of your piece and the metal selected. Small castings generally cost around $6.65–$12, with metal costs ranging approximately from $8–$30 depending on whether you choose red bronze, white bronze or silver.

This class is great for young folks aged around 12 and up. Anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.id: Sip & Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store Distillery-The Bond Store,3C Magrath Ave, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Sip & Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store Distillery
events.venue: The Bond Store,3C Magrath Ave, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Sip & Kintsugi Workshop in Wellington // The Bond Store Distillery
Come and experience this unique Sip & Kintsugi workshop at the lovely Bond Store Distillery. You will get a gin tasting tray on arrival with three different gins with tonic. And why not come a little earlier and grab a bite to eat beforehand. (Must be 18years +)

Learn the magical art of Kintsugi with Artist Ema Frost in this new Workshop. You can bring along a piece of your own pottery to restore in this beautiful style. (I'll also have a few as back up if you don't have your own piece) (All Levels)

Friday 26th June - 5:30pm - 7:30pm


Ema has been teaching Kintsugi for 5+ years and regularly travels to Japan to study and learn new techniques. Ema's illustrations and ceramic works sit in many of our countries best galleries, and whose products and merchandise sell in the most stylish design stores and gallery shops. Her style is enchanting - quirky, feminine and colourful, and clearly influenced by her travels throughout the world, usually off the beaten track.

Have you ever wanted to try Kintsugi.... It's an ancient Japanese art form where you fix broken pottery with gold - making its imperfections its beauty. - A truly wonderful philosophy to live by. Nothing is ever really broken but new life and meaning is brought back making your piece a work of art and truly something special. You can also apply this technique on various materials like glass, wood, greenstone and shells.

Kintsugi
It's called kintsugi (金継ぎ), or kintsukuroi (金繕い), literally golden (“kin”) and repair (“tsugi”). This traditional Japanese art uses a precious metal – liquid gold, liquid silver or lacquer dusted with powdered gold – to bring together the pieces of a broken pottery item and at the same time enhance the breaks.

You will take away your wonderful finished work, plus your new skills which will enable you to further your own Kintsugi in your own time.

What you will take away:
A finished artwork piece
A understanding of Kintsugi
Knowledge and History of this beautiful art form.

This traditional Japanese art uses a precious metal – liquid gold, liquid silver or lacquer dusted with powdered gold – to bring together the pieces of a broken pottery item and at the same time enhance the breaks.

events.name: Avalanche Safety Talk
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Inc., 4 Moncrieff St, Mount Victoria, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
What do pizza and avalanches have in common..? They are both on the agenda for our next section night!
This week, we have our Avalanche Awareness Talk.
Whether you are a skier, boarder, climber or hunter we hope this evening inspires you to refresh or start building your avalanche knowledge and rescue skills so you are able to enjoy our beautiful backcountry safely.
We encourage you to invite others that you know that frequent the alpine regions too as the information presented is an essential part of safe travel.
There'll be pizza and a chance to mingle with other keen adventurers
we look forward to seeing you there!

Photo Credit: Peter Laurenson

events.name: Brazilian Zouk - Improvers - Intro to tilted turns
events.venue: 60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Come and join us for Improvers Zouk Block D (5 weeks).
We have some great new content for you this year and will be building on foundational movements.
The complete Improvers course consists of 6 different Blocks (A-F).
Each block focuses on a different aspect and technique of Brazilian Zouk and so they can be completed independently of each other.
Our recommendation is to complete all 6 Blocks before progressing onto the Brazilian Zouk Deep Dive classes.
In Improvers Block D we will introduce you to tilted turns, as well as some exercises on Floor craft and building a social dance.
===Prices and payment:
- Prepaid 5 week Block cost is $90 waged or $75 for unwaged/students
- Ad hoc cost per class is $20 waged or $18 unwaged/students
Online payments can be made to Adam Polaczuk
Acct: 03-0531-0559348-25
Details: Your name and ZoukImpD

Classes are in Footnote dance studio, 60 Dixon Street

events.id: New Zealand School of Dance - Choreographic Season 2026-Te Whaea: National Dance and Drama Centre, 11 Hutchison Road, Newtown, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: New Zealand School of Dance - Choreographic Season 2026
events.venue: Te Whaea: National Dance and Drama Centre, 11 Hutchison Road, Newtown, Wellington
events.long_description:
The season lives in the space between who we are, who we think we are, and who we become when witnessed.

NOTE TO SELF is a full-length choreographed performance, created from original short works, devised by NZSD 3rd-year contemporary dance students. Each work has been independently created, then thoughtfully woven together to form one shared performance experience.

Together, these works sit inside the tender and uneasy space of being seen. They ask what it means to be viewed, to be perceived, and how that gaze can shape, distort, reveal, or challenge our sense of self. The season explores the body not simply as something to look at, but as something that knows, remembers, senses, absorbs, and reveals.

Running through the work is a deep humanness - vulnerability, discomfort, identity, observation and the quiet negotiation between who we are privately and who we become when witnessed.
events.description:
The season lives in the space between who we are, who we think we are, and who we become when witnessed.
NOTE TO SELF is a full-length choreographed performance, created from original short works, devised by NZSD 3rd-year contemporary dance students.
Each work has been independently created, then thoughtfully woven together to form one shared performance experience.
Together, these works sit inside the tender and uneasy space of being seen.
They ask what it means to be viewed, to be perceived, and how that gaze can shape, distort, reveal, or challenge our sense of self.

events.id: WBS Society night featuring Charlotte Yates-Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: WBS Society night featuring Charlotte Yates
events.venue: Abandoned Taproom Petone, 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
In a professional musical career of nearly three decades, Charlotte Yates has worn a number of hats, both literally and figuratively. She was part of the comic-musical trio When The Cat’s Been Spayed, with their fruit-festooned headgear and high-camp renditions of Kiwiana classics such as ‘Taumarunui On the Main Trunk Line’.

She conceived and directed the large-scale recording and stage projects Baxter, Tuwhare and Ihimaera, based around the works of three of this country’s major literary figures. She has managed festivals and worked in different roles as an arts professional.

Yet looking back, writing and performing original material has been the thread that runs through her work. “I never thought, ‘I’m going to be a singer-songwriter for the rest of my life’,” says Yates. “I didn’t have the grand master plan.” In 2017 she released her seventh solo album, Then the Stars Start Singing.

events.id: Weekend Laughs with James Mustapic and Sameena Zehra-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Weekend Laughs with James Mustapic and Sameena Zehra
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Weekend Laughs at Fringe Bar is a two hour comedy showcase with some of the country's favourite performers. Each month's line up will feature a guest MC, plus comedians from around the capital and the country, doing their best to bring the big laughs!

With MC James Mustapic
James is an award-winning comedian known for Winning Celebrity Treasure Island, as well as his hit TVNZ+ series Abandonment Issues. James made his start in comedy with his online show Shorty Street Scandal back in 2014, and started stand up comedy in 2015. Since then he's been a regular performer across the NZ comedy circuit, and has also appeared on hit TV shows such as Have You Been Paying Attention NZ, The Comedy Gala, and Guy Mont's Spelling Bee.

and Sameena Zehra
Sameena is an award-winning storyteller, comedian, writer, director, political satirist, intersectional feminist and humanist with a unique take on the world; equally at home exploring the individual experiences of daily life and the larger issues of the world we live in. She is also a blues singer-songwriter, extreme doodler and Level 2 chocolate taster who was once headbutted by a llama while climbing Machu Picchu.

more acts to be announced!

This event is helping fundraise for KidsCan -supporting Kiwi kids in hardship so they can learn, participate, and thrive

events.id: Body Ocean - June 26th - Wellington @Meow-Meow,17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Body Ocean - June 26th - Wellington @Meow
events.venue: Meow,17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Body Ocean - June 26th - Wellington @Meow
BODY OCEAN — WELLINGTON - 26th June
Where: Meow 🐾
Tickets from $25 +BF
Wellington! We heard you… Something's been missing, you want more house music in this beautiful city?! 🌊 so here it is, Body Ocean!

Fronted by Jeremy Glenn (one half of The Upbeats) with creative input from Nik Roos (formerly of Noisia), the project has quickly carved out its own lane, blending deep production with a strong live presence that hits just as hard in the club as it does on a system.

With releases across Night Bass, Black Book Records, STMPD RCRDS, Dirtybird, Mad Decent and more, Body Ocean has earned support from Jamie Jones, Martin Garrix, Oliver Heldens and Claptone 🎶 Their 2025 run saw appearances at Snow Machine, Rabbit Eats Lettuce, and Slander’s Star Base Festival in LA.

Now heading into 2026, they arrive off the back of new music on Night Bass, including the upcoming single “Listen to Your Teacher” with Benni Ola 🚀

Support TBA

Tickets via Flicket 🎟️

events.id: Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Negotiation Skills for Successful Outcomes
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
What’s the Goal?
To arm you with the strategies, skills, and mindset to walk into any negotiation and leave with the outcome you desire.
What You'll Discover:
Crack the Code on People: Spot different types of negotiators and tailor your approach using the DISC methodology.
Gear Up for Success: Build a killer prep list with intel on your opponent and a clear inventory of your strengths.
Own the Room: Master the art of negotiation strategy, use space to your advantage, and know the unspoken rules.
Discover Your Edge: Tap into your natural negotiation style and make it work for you.
Learn from the Masters: Unlock game-changing tips and theories from the biggest “gurus” in the field.
Practice Makes Perfect: Get hands-on with exercises that turn theory into action.
Ready to negotiate like a pro? This workshop will show you how to make your case, win people over, and seal the deal.
You must be a NZ citizen or resident to register in this course for free and you must provide photo ID when you attend. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Jewellery-making On Saturday Mornings - 6 Weeks, Level 2+-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Jewellery-making On Saturday Mornings - 6 Weeks, Level 2+
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Our popular weekend block classes are the best way for keen makers to get into jewellery making in a lively, warm and friendly environment.

This class is for regular students at Ōtaki who have already completed at least a beginner's 6 week block as a minimum.

The block starts Sat 27 June and runs for 6 weeks till Sat 1 August, 10am-1pm.

Students will be supported by Vaune and the occasional guest tutor to further their jewellery making skills in the areas that interest them.

This class is a Fabrication focus. Students who have already taken a class in enamelling or wax work can continue their work in this class but the format does not allow for beginner lessons in these things.

You can purchase metals and materials for your projects as you go - in the studio.
Advanced students are encouraged to retain their silver scrap and learn to work thriftily with materials, using scrap for future projects.

Our booking system asks for payment to be made via credit card, Afterpay or PayPal on booking. If you do not have access to these payment methods or need to pay your course fee over time please email us to arrange this ;)

events.id: The Royal New Zealand Ballet presents Whānau Day 2026-St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Royal New Zealand Ballet presents Whānau Day 2026
events.venue: St James Theatre, 77-87 Courtenay Place, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bring your whānau and spend the day exploring creativity, discovering the world of dance, and celebrating the joy of being uniquely you at St James.

Join us for a vibrant and inspiring Whānau Day at St. James, a special event designed to bring families together through dance, storytelling, and creativity. For the first time, this much-loved day will take over Theatre’s multiple spaces across Levels 1 and 2, the stage, and the Hospitality Suite, offering a wide range of interactive experiences for all ages.

This year’s event is inspired by the beautiful children’s ballet The Bomb, creating a day full of imagination, movement, and self-expression.

About The Bomb

Based on the award-winning book by Sacha Cotter, illustrated by Josh Morgan, The Bomb tells a powerful and uplifting story about finding your own voice. It follows a young boy determined to master the perfect “bomb” dive. With the help of his Nan, a former champion, and through learning to trust himself, he discovers that true success comes from embracing what makes him unique.

This meaningful story has captured the hearts of readers across New Zealand and has received multiple prestigious awards in 2019, including the Margaret Mahy Book of the Year.

https://www.facebook.com/watch/?v=389888618397391

A Day for the Whole Whānau

With most activities completely free and something to engage every age group, Whānau Day is a welcoming and inclusive event for the whole community. Whether your child is trying dance for the first time, getting creative with crafts, or watching live rehearsals, there is something to inspire everyone.

events.id: Truffle Hunt and Taste Experience-Le Gra Vineyard, 42 Loopline RD 1, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Truffle Hunt and Taste Experience
events.venue: Le Gra Vineyard, 42 Loopline RD 1, Masterton
events.long_description:
The winter truffle season runs from June through August, making it the ideal time to join and watch skilled dogs in action and savour the rich, earthy flavours of freshly unearthed truffles.

This Truffle hunting experience has you enjoying a relaxed mid-morning hunt through Fiona and John’s truffle orchard of Oak and Hazel trees - Akura Truffles. Accompanied by their trained truffle dogs, they want to share this short but wonderful season and experience with you.

When you've found some of these treasured delicacies, pop around the corner to Le Gra Vineyard and Winery for a fun and interactive lunch inspired by these little black beauties.

You'll learn how best to infuse the truffle flavour into your food, taste some of the Opaki's finest and freshly hunted in delectable dishes, and, if you so choose pair them with local wines.

There are only 10 spots per hunt, ensuring a truly intimate and real hunting and tasting experience. Don't miss out; choose the date to suit your diary now.

Don’t let your best friend miss out on all the fun!

Treat your dog to the ultimate Doggy Day Out at Kai Tahi in Porirua, from 10am – 3pm on Saturday, 27 June 2026. A fun day out for your dog and its human, filled with wagging tails, friendly faces, and heaps of dog friendly fun.

80s Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco! 🎉
Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing! 🎧🕺✨

events.id: Bike Repair Workshop for Beginners - No Experience Needed-Kilbirnie/Lyall Bay Community Centre, 56/58 Bay Road, Kilbirnie, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Bike Repair Workshop for Beginners - No Experience Needed
events.venue: Kilbirnie/Lyall Bay Community Centre, 56/58 Bay Road, Kilbirnie, Wellington
events.long_description:
Learn to fix common issues and keep your bike running smoothly!

Got a bike but not much experience with repairs?
Keen to learn how to look after it and fix common issues?

Bikespace’s friendly bike mechanics are coming to Kilbirnie to guide you through the basics in a relaxed, supportive environment.

• Perfect for beginners and those with a little knowledge
• Learn how to look after tyres, brakes, chains, gears, and more
• Friendly, experienced, patient community mechanics
• First part of the session: Step-by-step explanation of how things work and how to fix or adjust them
• Second part of the session: Hands-on time to work on your own bike with support available, or watch and learn from others
• No equipment or technical know-how needed – just bring yourself. Also bring your bike if you’d like to apply what you’ve learned during the second part of the evening
• Tea and biscuits provided

Recommended from 13 years and older. Younger participants may benefit from parent support – please note that the first 45mins are observing and learning and require a level of focus and patience.

Cost: Registration is $15. At the end of the workshop, you’ll receive a $15 voucher to spend at your local bike shop, Burke Cycles at Kilbirnie. Perfect incentive to stock up on what you need to keep your bike running smoothly!

Find us in the hall at Kilbirnie Lyall Bay Community Centre.
We look forward to seeing you!

Questions? Email bikespace@wcc.govt.nz

events.id: GrandPrix Landmower Racing-Molewood Orchard, 17 Mole Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: GrandPrix Landmower Racing
events.venue: Molewood Orchard, 17 Mole Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Start your engines, it’s time for one of the Festival of Christmas’s newest iconic events, the Lawnmower Grand Prix!

Watch as brave (and slightly competitive) speedsters take to the track in a high-speed showdown of modified mowers, questionable tactics, and plenty of festive spirit. From souped-up speed machines to wildly twisty chicanes, anything can happen once the flag drops.

Expect:
• Fast laps and fierce competition
• Noise
• Plenty of laughs (and maybe a few breakdowns)
• Serious bragging rights up for grabs

Whether you’re racing or just cheering from the sidelines, it’s guaranteed chaos, camaraderie, and classic small-town fun.
Don’t miss it — this is lawnmowing like you’ve never seen before.

Head to the Farley Ave door, follow the noise, grab a hot drink, and witness some racing before the Festival Big Switch.

Many thanks to the North Island Mower Racing Association for this event.

events.id: Textured Bracelets-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Textured Bracelets
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
This lively, hands-on workshop is designed for complete beginners, but it’s also a great opportunity for more experienced students to enjoy a creative session making their own unique bracelet.

Everyone will leave with a beautifully finished bracelet of their own preferred texture.

We’ll begin by experimenting on a sample strip of copper, where you’ll practice creating textures using hammers, punches and stamps. Once you’re confident you’ll move on to your main project: a 3mm wide flat strip bracelet in either sterling silver or copper, crafted entirely by you from start to finish. Along the way, you’ll learn essential jewellery making skills including measuring, texturing, soldering, shaping, and finishing.

Students are free to personalise their bangle with textures or stamped designs. This could be a simple hammered finish, decorative patterns, or meaningful words, names, dates, or poetry lines - making your bracelet truly one of a kind.

Beginners will work with the 3mm strip provided, ensuring a rewarding project that’s manageable to complete. Wider metal is much harder to form and solder, so we are learning to walk before we can run ;)

Copper is included in the course fee, if you wish to work in silver you can purchase it on the day.

This workshop is a taster class: perfect for anyone seeking a fun, creative experience with a beautiful result, or for those looking to dip their toes into jewellery making and build foundational skills.

Important Note for Young Makers: Kids aged 12 and over are welcome to attend, but anyone under the age of 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio if you wish to make more.

events.long_description:
80's Day Disco: Wellington 🪩
Tickets selling fast → https://www.fatsoma.com/e/vb6j5pqr/la/qwmu

Get Ready to Groove at the Ultimate 80s Day Disco! 🎉

Step into a time machine and relive the magic of the 80s with a day filled with the biggest disco anthems and synth-powered hits of the decade. Whether you’re a true 80s fanatic or just here for the nostalgia, this party is all about electric vibes, funky beats, and non-stop dancing! 🎧🕺✨

What to Expect:

🎶 Nonstop 80s Classics: Dance to the timeless hits that defined the decade—from disco grooves to rock anthems and synth-pop bangers.

🕺 Retro Dance Floor Energy: Show off your best moves under the dazzling glow of neon lights and shimmering disco balls.

📸 Totally Rad Photo Ops: Capture the moment with throwback props, vintage backdrops, and all the 80s glam.

🌟 Feel-Good Vibes All Day Long: Celebrate the music, the energy, and the nostalgia with great people and iconic tunes.

Let’s make it a day to remember—80s style! 🎶💿✨

Expect to hear music from:

Michael Jackson ⭐ Madonna ⭐ Prince ⭐ Whitney Houston ⭐ Queen ⭐ George Michael ⭐ David Bowie ⭐ Tina Turner ⭐ Elton John ⭐ Phil Collins ⭐ Cyndi Lauper ⭐ Bon Jovi ⭐ The Police ⭐ Bruce Springsteen ⭐ Duran Duran ⭐ Journey ⭐ Billy Joel ⭐ Lionel Richie ⭐ Eurythmics ⭐ Depeche Mode ⭐ Aerosmith ⭐ U2 ⭐ The Cure ⭐ Van Halen ⭐ Hall & Oates ⭐ AC/DC ⭐ The Smiths ⭐ New Order ⭐ Fleetwood Mac ⭐ The Bangles ⭐ Rick Astley ⭐ Spandau Ballet ⭐ Roxette ⭐ Simple Minds ⭐ Guns N’ Roses ⭐ Billy Idol ⭐ Tears for Fears ⭐ Bryan Adams ⭐ A-ha

FAQ’S

🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?

Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.

🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etc

Yes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.

🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?

Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.

🗣️ Is the venue accessible?

Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.

🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?

Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.

🗣️ Are your events 18+?

All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.

🗣️ I have more questions?

If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)

Refund Policy:
In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

events.id: Greytown Lands Trust Big Switch Opening Night-Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Greytown Lands Trust Big Switch Opening Night
events.venue: Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Kick off the festivities on Saturday 27 June with a spectacular rural celebration. Main Street turns into a Town and Country show of sorts, where you can cheer on your favourite backyard hero in our Ride on Lawnmower Grand Prix, play old school games, and once it gets dark, see the magical lights get switched on for the first time, and enjoy the much-loved Light Up Tractor Parade as it rolls through the village.

What to expect:
- Entertainers throughout the village
- Artisan Night Markets with arts, crafts, food, and festive treats
- Outdoor dining at Truckstop, The White Swan Garden Bar and Food Truck Central
- The first switch-on of magical Christmas lighting
- The beautiful Community Christmas Tree, featuring adorable Ruru Morepork's decorated by local children. Thanks to Greytown Lands Trust.
- Snowfall returns — by popular demand - thanks FreshChoice Greytown! Every 30 mins from 6pm - 8pm
- spin the wheel to win a meat pack

events.id: Greytown School Country Food Fair-Greytown School, 73 East St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Greytown School Country Food Fair
events.venue: Greytown School, 73 East St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Join us at Greytown School for a country-themed food fair on Opening Night! Just a few steps away from Main St (follow signs through Stella Bull Park), the school children will have delicious, family-friendly food, drinks and sweet treats for sale with proceeds going towards a Shade Solution for the Greytown School Playground.

The event will kick off at 3pm with games, vintage machinery and tractors that kids can sit on - so head into town early to nab a carpark and relax while your kids enjoy their safe, fenced school grounds. The school will have seating, toilets, pram parking for the entire event (if you don’t wish to navigate Main St with it) and even a cosy parents’ room for feeding babies.

Grab a yummy dinner from the stalls, then head to Main Street to watch the Light Up Tractor Parade at 6pm.

events.id: Oyster Saloon with Martin Bosley-Aperitif Wine Bar, 101 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Oyster Saloon with Martin Bosley
events.venue: Aperitif Wine Bar, 101 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Martin Bosley is back in the Aperitif courtyard for two delicious nights only to bookend the Festival. Join the team at Aperitif Wine Bar & Eatery as the legend himself returns to work his oyster-shucking magic while you sip on crisp Champagne or a warm mulled wine.

Rug up, gather your crew, and stay toasty by our outdoor heaters as you soak up the festive atmosphere and live music in the courtyard, right amongst the action. No bookings required – just turn up and enjoy!

3pm - 7pm (unless sold out prior) Saturday 27 June and Saturday 25 July only.

events.id: Sarah A Parker: Book Signing - Ballad of Falling Dragons-Hedleys Bookshop, 150 Queen St, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Sarah A Parker: Book Signing - Ballad of Falling Dragons
events.venue: Hedleys Bookshop, 150 Queen St, Masterton
events.long_description:
We are so excited to host internationally bestselling author, Sarah A Parker to celebrate the release of her latest book, The Ballad of Falling Dragons!

Sarah is the author of the epic fantasy romance series: Moonfall and Crystal Bloom which have sold millions of copies internationally and are New York Times Bestsellers.

Sarah is currently touring the world for the release of Ballad of Falling Dragons – the second book in the Moonfall series, and we are incredibly lucky to be included as a stop on her tour. This event will be particularly special as Sarah grew up in Masterton!

All of Sarah's books, including The Ballad of Falling Dragons and some special editions, will be available to purchase and have signed on the day.
**To keep things fair for everyone, we kindly ask that only books pre-ordered from Hedley's or purchased on the day are brought in for signing.**

This is a free event but bookings are required.

events.id: TruckStop!-Main Street, Greytown, Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: TruckStop!
events.venue: Main Street, Greytown, Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Truckstop Winter Edition is back on Friday and Saturday nights throughout the festival.
This little hub of tasty goodness gives great dining for the whole family, tucked away from the hustle and bustle of Main Street. Every weekend, Dale Keith will be serving up his famous smashed burgers, Mao & Co will bring the zing with their delicious Asian street food, and Goodtimes really do make times good with flash flatbreads that pack a flavour punch. This year, in the ‘sweet spot’ corner, Mrs Blackwell will be selling New York-style cookies.
Truckstop will also be the official festival central station for mulled wine! Grab a bite, a mulled wine and cosy up next to the brazier, trucker style!

events.id: Food Truck Central-Main Street, Greytown, Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Food Truck Central
events.venue: Main Street, Greytown, Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Treat your taste buds every Friday and Saturday night during the 2026 festival, with a vibrant lineup of food trucks serving up crowd favourites and new delights. An extra night added for Thursday 9 July, before Matariki!

From authentic tacos and freshly made crepes to Japanese sushi bowls and slow-cooked meats, there’s something for every craving. If you’re leaning sweet, expect cakes, doughnuts, churros and plenty of decadent treats - plus the return of glow-in-the-dark candy floss for a little festival magic.

Find the action under the festoon lights outside the Town Hall, in Stella Bull Park, and at Truckstop.

events.id: Paint and Wine Weekend Afternoon in Kāpiti - Banksy Balloon Girl-The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu-Event Finder
events.name: Paint and Wine Weekend Afternoon in Kāpiti - Banksy Balloon Girl
events.venue: The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Join us for a fun adults’ painting class at The Bond Store – a vibrant distillery in the heart of Paraparaumu, known for its award-winning New Zealand craft spirits. No painting experience is needed – your friendly artist will guide you step by step in a relaxed, judgement-free environment.

This event is all about creativity, conversation, and having a great time while unleashing your inner artist.

Your ticket includes a gin tasting tray, giving you the chance to sample some of The Bond Store’s specialty gins while you paint.

Whether you’re coming with friends, planning a date night, or flying solo, this is your chance to unwind, get creative, and experience the joy of painting with Paintvine.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

Join us for the Grand launch of Feilding in Lights - Manawatū's Matariki Winter Festival, a month-long celebration that lights up Feilding through creativity, culture and community

• Tractor parade

• Community Light Project on display
• Lighting of the Clocktower

events.name: The White Swan Gin Garden Bar

events.long_description:
Greytown’s best-kept winter secret is back. Step into The White Swan’s Gin Garden Bar, a cosy mid-winter pop-up brimming with festive charm, crackling fires, live music, and winter warmers crafted with small-batch mulled gin from Greytown Distilling Company.

Perfect for a relaxed afternoon catch-up or a post-dinner sip, the Gin Garden is your go-to weekend destination this July.
events.description:
Greytown’s best-kept winter secret is back.
Step into The White Swan’s Gin Garden Bar, a cosy mid-winter pop-up brimming with festive charm, crackling fires, live music, and winter warmers crafted with small-batch mulled gin from Greytown Distilling Company.
Perfect for a relaxed afternoon catch-up or a post-dinner sip, the Gin Garden is your go-to weekend destination this July.

events.id: Experience Snowfall on Main Street, with FreshChoice Greytown-Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Experience Snowfall on Main Street, with FreshChoice Greytown
events.venue: Greytown Town Hall, 89 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Glorious snow will be falling once again outside the Town Hall, thanks to FreshChoice Greytown.

A firm festival favourite, this magical moment arrives right on cue every Friday and Saturday night throughout the festival (plus Thursday 9 July, the day before the Matariki public holiday). Who knew the forecast could be this reliable?

Set to music and lights, enjoy a little northern hemisphere White Christmas magic right here in Aotearoa. Strike your best snowy selfie pose, grab something delicious from the food trucks, keep an eye out for Ruru and his friends and get ready for a flurry of fun.

The forecast is looking very convincing. Snow will fall every half hour from 6pm to 8pm, like clockwork.

events.long_description:
Bring a fresh, fun twist to your market day with our new Car Boot Sale at the Ōtaki Market! This is a great opportunity to clear out pre-loved items, share hidden treasures, and connect with bargain hunters in a relaxed, community-focused space.

Whether it’s clothing, toys, books, household goods, furniture, tools, garden tools or bric-a-brac, if it’s in good condition and ready for a new home, it’s welcome at the car boot. Location: South end of the carpark
Spaces available: Five car spaces
Cost: $10 per car
10am-2pm

events.id: Sunday Morning Jewellery-making In 4 Weeks - Mixed Level-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sunday Morning Jewellery-making In 4 Weeks - Mixed Level
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
Spend your Sunday mornings immersed in creativity, learning the timeless craft of jewellery-making in our warm, welcoming studio.

It runs from Sun 28 June for 4 weeks till Sun 19 July, 10am-1pm.

This short course is the perfect introduction for complete beginners, guiding you step-by-step through the essential techniques used by jewellers. You’ll start by working in copper or brass, building confidence as you learn to cut, heat, shape and texture metal. From there, you can choose to move into silver and bring your ideas to life in more refined pieces.

A beautiful textured ring band is an ideal first project, but once you’ve mastered the basics, the possibilities really open up—create earrings, pendants, or small bespoke pieces that reflect your personal style. You’ll learn how to solder using a jeweller’s torch, refine your finishing techniques, and polish your work to a professional shine. It’s a hands-on, deeply satisfying process, and there’s nothing quite like wearing something you’ve made yourself.

These Sunday sessions are also perfect for returning students and more experienced makers who want dedicated studio time, guidance on more ambitious projects, and the chance to be part of a friendly, creative community.

Materials are purchased as you go, so you can tailor your projects to your budget and ideas. Beginners typically spend around $50–$70 on metals over the course.

This block is ideal for teens and young creatives aged 12 and up. Please note: anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.id: Glass Bead-Making Taster-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Glass Bead-Making Taster
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Join us for this fabulous introduction to working with colourful molten glass!

This class is a great way for absolute beginners to get a taste of working with glass to produce beautiful beads that can be used in a myriad of ways, including making a unique gift for your loved one (or yourself!).

As one of the earliest forms of human adornment, beads have long functioned as a way to express individuality, and what better way to do that than to learn to make your own unique beads?

Students are shown correct lampworking techniques to produce several decorative beads in just 3 hours! How many beads you make is up to you; some students make up to 10 while others make fewer using trickier techniques.

Kids over 12 (or so) are welcome to take the class, but anyone under 16 must be accompanied by an enrolled parent or caregiver.

events.id: Improv Workouts- Undisputed Champions w. PopRox Improv-Toi Poneke Arts Centre,61 - 69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Improv Workouts- Undisputed Champions w. PopRox Improv
events.venue: Toi Poneke Arts Centre,61 - 69 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Improv Workouts- Undisputed Champions w. PopRox Improv
Work your improv muscles with monthly "workouts" hosted by PopRox. Each month's workout will have a specific focus, with an emphasis on reps over theory. Our "Undisputed Champions" sessions are aimed at highly skilled improvisers with strong performance experience.

If you're not sure which workout is best for you, take our wee quiz linked below or get in touch. We're happy to offer some guidance.
https://opinionstage.com/page/d55e4c73-8faf-4d9c-9910-320b2dd00d6e

OPEN SESSION DATES:
Sunday 25 Jan
Sunday 22 Feb
Sunday 22 March
Sunday 26 April
Sunday 24 May
Sunday 28 June
Sunday 26 July
Sunday 23 August
Note: No September session due to NZIF
Sunday 25 October
Sunday 22 November

SEASON PASS:
Book a season pass to get access to all 10 Undisputed Champion sessions for the whole year at a reduced rate. Please note these are paid out straight away so refunds are not available for season passes. We'll reduce the pricing of these as the year goes on, so feel free to try out a session with a standard pass first.

STAND-BY LIST
PopRox firmly believes that improv is for everyone, especially when it comes to sharing skills and knowledge. So we're offering "the stand-by list" for all our workshops in 2026. We'll giveaway any spots remaining on the Friday before each Workout, on a first-come-first-serve basis. No need to provide a reason, no questions asked. If the entry fee is a barrier for you to come along, then simply email and we'll pop you on the SBL. Everyone on the list will be notified whether there was a place available for them 2 days before the relevant workshop.

events.id: Silver Clay Taster-Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Silver Clay Taster
events.venue: Toi Pōneke Arts Centre, 3-6 Market Lane, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us to try out this amazing technique and make a gift for yourself or your loved ones :-)

Precious Metal Clay is an incredible product made from metal particles mixed with an organic binder. When your pieces are shaped - just like a modelling clay - they are then dried and fused in the kiln. That process takes only a few minutes so there is plenty of time in this 3-hour class to complete your projects.

Beginners start with simple pendants/earrings. It is much easier to start with flat projects so we leave the rings for the advanced class!

Jewellery pieces can be textured and shaped however you like, and if you don't like your piece simply squish it and start again - Precious Metal Clay is the ultimate recyclable material!

Included in the course fee is 5 grams of fine silver clay, enough to make a pendant or earrings, or a collection of charms - it's up to you.

Extra findings such as earring hooks and chains will be available to purchase (you can also make your earring hooks if you are feeling adventurous).

This class is great for kids also but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany any person under the age of 16.

events.id: Stories Behind Some of Aotearoa’s Most Interesting Plants-Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Stories Behind Some of Aotearoa’s Most Interesting Plants
events.venue: Tāne Whakapiripiri - Ōtari Visitors Centre, 150 Wilton Road, Wilton, Wellington, Wellington
events.long_description:
Why is speargrass so sharp? Why does the parapara have such sticky seeds (hint, little birds beware). Join Te Papa science researcher and evolutionary biologist Lara Shepherd on an exploration around Ōtari’s plant collection, she’ll reveal all sorts of fascinating facts about our native species.

Meet at Tāne Whakapiripiri, Ōtari Visitor Centre. It is easy walking and family groups are welcome. We ask that dogs stay at home for everyones comfort and a smooth tour experience.

This is part of the Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush Trust’s annual guided walks programme. This year, they are on the fourth Sunday of the month, March – November, to celebrate 100 years of Ōtari-Wilton’s Bush.
Join the Trust for only $20 for an individual or $30 for a double/family for a year and all the walks will be free, as well as other great benefits. www.owbt.nz/memberships-legacies/

Ōtari 100: Celebrating 100 years of native plants, conservation, and community. 1926-2026.

events.long_description:
Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
Join us for Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze, where you can sip, glaze and decorate your own custom Tumbler

About this Event


Pottery Social: Raise a Glaze
Join us in person for a fun and laid-back afternoon of creativity and good vibes. Whether you're a pottery pro or just starting out, this social is all about raising a glass and enjoying some creativity together. Bring your friends, get your your creativety flowing, and let's make some unique pieces while sharing laughs and good company. Don't miss out on this awesome chance to connect and create!
You'll be glazing your very own a handmade tumbler made my Mud + Woman Pottery.





Pottery Class Terms & Conditions


Bookings: All class bookings are non-refundable 7 days prior to event.
Changes & credits: If something comes up and you can’t make it, just reach out — we’re happy to offer a voucher so you can join another class instead.
The nature of clay: Working with clay is a beautiful but imperfect process. Each piece goes through drying and firing, and sometimes cracking, warping, or breakage can happen despite our best care. It’s all part of the magic (and mystery) of pottery.
Finished pieces: While we do everything we can to look after your creations, we’re unable to guarantee the final result or offer refunds or remakes for pieces that don’t make it through the process.

events.name: Paint & Sip with Mandy Emerson
events.venue: The Offering, 65 Main St, Greytown
events.long_description:
Enjoy a fabulous night of creativity and great company paired with delicious food & refreshments!

Mandy Emerson is an established NZ artist from Riversdale Beach in the Wairarapa. She started her paint workshops over a year ago and they have been such a success she continues to be fully booked out.

It is The Offering's absolute pleasure to host her here in Greytown. Enjoy a glass of bubbles on arrival, select a delicious dish from the curated menu and settle in for some creativity. The workshop will then pause for dinner.

Mandy's work is mixed media, feminine with florals and she has won awards for her works and exhibits all over New Zealand.

Her biggest success came from exhibiting at the NZ ART SHOW in Wellington which she still continues to be a part of and also attending ARTMELBOURNE in Australia. Visit her website www.mandyemerson.com

events.name: The Snow Supper at The White Swan
events.venue: The White Swan, 109 Main Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
This winter, step into something truly magical.

In the heart of Greytown, experience The Snow Supper, an enchanting outdoor dining experience on the deck of The White Swan, where soft snowfall, glowing lights, and award-winning food and drink come together beneath the Wairarapa’s famed dark skies.

Set on the front deck of this much-loved local, you will dine under drifting flurries at the iconic starlit hotel, with festive feel-good music as the backdrop. Grab a cosy blanket, and enjoy a beautifully curated menu, all delivered with the warm, welcoming hospitality The White Swan is known for. Inclues a perfectly spiced mulled gin to complete the mid-winter experience.

Running Sunday to Thursday throughout the New Zealand Festival of Christmas, this is a distinctly local take on mid-winter Christmas, elevated, atmospheric, and designed to be shared.
Available: Sunday – Thursday evenings only between 28 June - 23 July 2026

Location: The Captain’s Dining Room, or outdoor deck (dependent on weather)

Menu: Set menu – $99 per person

Choice of 2 entrées / 2 mains / 2 desserts

Includes a warming mulled gin on arrival

Payment: Pay on arrival

Please note: Snow Supper is a special experience and only available on selected nights with limited capacity.

Tickets available soon via The White Swan: https://thewhiteswanhotel.co.nz/table-booking

events.id: AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage-9 Sunset Parade, Plimmerton,9 Sunset Pde, Plimmerton, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage
events.venue: 9 Sunset Parade, Plimmerton,9 Sunset Pde, Plimmerton, Porirua 5026, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
AUDITIONS for Lettice and Lovage
Lettice and Lovage by Peter Shaffer is a hilarious, satirical comedy about Lettice Douffet, an eccentric, history-obsessed tour guide who embellishes facts for tourists. When a strict preservationist, Lotte Schoen, fires her for lying, the two clash—only to form an unlikely bond over a shared disdain for the modern world.

· Lettice Douffet
An extravagant, imaginative, and highly theatrical former tour guide whose mother travelled France acting in Shakespearean plays. An expert on Elizabethan cuisine and medieval weaponry.

Requires extremely strong comedic acting. Maggie Smith won a Tony for this role!

Stage age 30-60.

· Lotte Schoen
A stern, strait-laced, and unbending inspector from the Preservation Trust. Underneath her brisk, bureaucratic exterior lies a deeply disappointed idealist and secret champion of radical architecture preservation.

Stage age 30-60.

· Mr. Bardolph/Visitor to Fustian House
Stiff-upper-lip, buttoned-down British attorney who is reluctantly dragged into one of Lettice’s extreme historical reenactments.

Requires excellent comedic acting.

Stage age 25+.

· Miss Framer/ Visitor to Fustian House
Lotte’s timid, and obedient secretary.

Stage age 30+.

· Surly man/ Visitor to Fustian House
Hostile history buff and visitor to Faustian House.

Stage age 25+.

· Visitors to Fustian House

Email for the audition sides.

Show run 2-12 September
Rehearsals will be 3 times a week in July and August.

events.id: Waltz of My Heart-Hutt City Church, 22 Marsden St, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Waltz of My Heart
events.venue: Hutt City Church, 22 Marsden St, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Step into a world of sweeping melodies, soaring voices, and the graceful rhythm of the waltz in this unforgettable concert Waltz of My Heart, a celebration of one of the most beloved musical forms in history.

From the iconic Viennese waltzes of Johann Strauss to modern interpretations such as The Anniversary Waltz and The Tennessee Waltz, this enchanting programme showcases the enduring beauty and emotional power of the waltz across generations and genres. Whether heard in grand ballrooms or intimate theatres, this music continues to captivate and enchant.

Taking centre stage are our four magnificent tenors whose rich, expressive voices breathe new life into classic and contemporary waltzes alike. Joining them is fabulous baritone John Bayne plus stunning Amanda Atlas, whose powerful soprano and magnetic presence bring depth and drama to every note.

This is a concert of melody, magic, and masterful singing — where every phrase is infused with passion and every performance is unforgettable. Let yourself be swept away by the romance, elegance, and enduring charm of the waltz.

Featuring:
Bonaventure Allan-Moetaua
Benjamin Makisi
Derek Hill
Kalauni Pouvalu
Amanda Atlas
John Bayne
Paul Carnegie-Jones

NZ Tour 02 June 2026 - 03 July 2026
Concerts Start 11am
General Admission Seating
Doors Open 10am – Complimentary morning tea included.

Phone: 0508 266 237
Email: bookings@operatunity.co.nz

events.id: Founder Lecture: A thousand words: Photographic archives in Aotearoa-National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Founder Lecture: A thousand words: Photographic archives in Aotearoa
events.venue: National Library of New Zealand, 70 Molesworth Street, Thorndon, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join us for the annual Friends of the Turnbull Library Founder Lecture with Auckland Museum’s Catherine Hammond, who will share insights from her work with major photographic archives across Aotearoa.

Catherine's Founder Lecture will discuss the role and impact of photographic archives as complex repositories of memory and research.

Currently Director of Collections and Research at Auckland War Memorial Museum Tāmaki Paenga Hira, Catherine has more than 20-years’ experience working with artists’ archives in major cultural institutions in Aotearoa.

She will discuss the recent book and touring exhibition, 'A Different Light: First Photographs of Aotearoa', and reflect on archives and meaning-making in examples ranging from Marti Friedlander to Frances Hodgkins, feminist photographic practice and whānau portraiture.

Light refreshments will follow the lecture.

Seats are limited, book your spot here: https://events.humanitix.com/catherine-hammond-founder-lecture-2026-a-thousand-words-photographic-archives-in-aotearoa

The concept is simple - take a brush in one hand, a drink in the other and let’s paint a masterpiece!

Join us for a creative night of drinking and painting in the heart of Eastbourne, Wellington! No previous painting experience necessary. Get your creative juices flowing and your brush strokes going!

Drinks: No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks are available for purchase.

Food: A delicious menu will be available to purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website.

events.id: Treasury Guest Lecture with Vangelis Vitalis (MFAT): IN-PERSON attendance-The Treasury, level 3, 1 The Terrace,1 The Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Treasury Guest Lecture with Vangelis Vitalis (MFAT): IN-PERSON attendance
events.venue: The Treasury, level 3, 1 The Terrace,1 The Terrace, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Treasury Guest Lecture with Vangelis Vitalis (MFAT): IN-PERSON attendance
The international context for New Zealand trade policy – where we were and where we are – and how do we respond?

About this Event

Join us for the next lecture in our new seminar series
The golden weather for New Zealand trade policy - from 1995 to roughly 2017 - is well and truly over. The world trading system is more turbulent, uncertain and challenging, but New Zealand is well placed to navigate this new world trade disorder.
We will, however, need to double-down on three inter-connected ‘pillars’ of trade policy: rules; resilience and innovation.
This presentation - by MFAT’s Deputy Secretary Vangelis Vitalis - will explain what this means and how it can help us prevent the jungle from growing back.
About this event
This will be a hybrid event. Please use this site to register if you plan to attend it in person.
If you would like to attend it virtually, please use THIS LINK to register.
About the presenter
Vangelis Vitalis is Deputy Secretary, Trade and Economic at the Ministry of Foreign Affairs and Trade. He is New Zealand’s Chief Negotiator for the New Zealand-India Free Trade Agreement and has led or helped conclude several major trade agreements, including those with the European Union, CPTPP, ASEAN-Australia-New Zealand, China, and Malaysia.
He previously served as New Zealand’s Ambassador and Permanent Representative to the World Trade Organisation in Geneva, and has also been Ambassador to the European Union and NATO. He has held key international leadership roles through APEC and CPTPP host years, bringing deep experience in trade policy and global economic diplomacy.
About this seminar series
New ideas, robust evidence, and insights from leading thinkers are crucial to strengthening the Treasury’s economic analysis and policy advice. Our new guest lecture theme — Navigating Global Shifts: Resilience, Challenges and Opportunities for New Zealand — invites us to reflect on how Aotearoa New Zealand can navigate an increasingly complex and interconnected global environment.
This series explores the major global forces that shape our economic landscape, including evolving trade relationships, geopolitical developments, and the global movement of people, capital, and ideas. Through these discussions, we aim to deepen our understanding of how New Zealand can build economic resilience, respond effectively to uncertainty, and harness our regional and international partnerships to support economic performance, productivity, and our living standards.
Our speakers explore strategies for mitigating the impacts of global disruptions — from pandemics and climate‑related events to geopolitical tensions and supply‑chain shocks – while also examining potential opportunities presented by rapid technological shifts, digital economy, and global innovations, to name a few. The series considers the role of government in supporting economic transitions, strengthening national resilience, and ensuring that New Zealand remains well‑positioned to meet emerging global opportunities. By bringing together diverse perspectives, this series seeks to inspire new ways of thinking about New Zealand’s economic future and to support informed, forward‑looking policymaking.
Please note that the views, opinions, findings, and conclusions expressed in the Treasury Guest Lecture Series are those of the individual presenters. They do not necessarily reflect the views of the New Zealand Treasury or the New Zealand Government.
For updates and our latest research visit: www.treasury.govt.nz
Keep in touch
If you would like to be the first to hear about our upcoming events in the Treasury Guest Lecture Series, please SUBSCRIBE to stay up to date.

events.long_description:
Step into a world of sweeping melodies, soaring voices, and the graceful rhythm of the waltz in this unforgettable concert Waltz of My Heart, a celebration of one of the most beloved musical forms in history.

From the iconic Viennese waltzes of Johann Strauss to modern interpretations such as The Anniversary Waltz and The Tennessee Waltz, this enchanting programme showcases the enduring beauty and emotional power of the waltz across generations and genres. Whether heard in grand ballrooms or intimate theatres, this music continues to captivate and enchant.

Taking centre stage are our four magnificent tenors whose rich, expressive voices breathe new life into classic and contemporary waltzes alike. Joining them is fabulous baritone John Bayne plus stunning Amanda Atlas, whose powerful soprano and magnetic presence bring depth and drama to every note.

This is a concert of melody, magic, and masterful singing — where every phrase is infused with passion and every performance is unforgettable. Let yourself be swept away by the romance, elegance, and enduring charm of the waltz.

Featuring:
Bonaventure Allan-Moetaua
Benjamin Makisi
Derek Hill
Kalauni Pouvalu
Amanda Atlas
John Bayne
Paul Carnegie-Jones

NZ Tour 02 June 2026 - 03 July 2026
Concerts Start 11am
General Admission Seating
Doors Open 10am – Complimentary morning tea included.

Phone: 0508 266 237
Email: bookings@operatunity.co.nz

events.long_description:
From Anxiety to Alignment
Explore how to move from anxiety to alignment. Discover nervous system insights and yoga wisdom for inner peace in an overstimulated world.

About this Event


Tuesday Wellbeing Seminars:
From Anxiety to Alignment: The Nervous System & the Search for Happiness

In a world of constant stimulation, anxiety and inner restlessness have become increasingly common. Explore the nervous system, the mind, and the deeper human search for happiness and fulfilment. Discover how to reconnect with the lost art of inner peace in an overstimulated world. Through practical insight and timeless yoga philosophy, learn what it truly means to move from anxiety into alignment.



The seminar will be followed by a delicious plant-based dinner together, all included in your ticket!

events.id: Gates to Memory - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance: CANCELLED-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Gates to Memory - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance: CANCELLED
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
*TICKETS TO THIS EVENT ARE CURRENTLY ON HOLD*

Gates to Memory is a contemporary dance work that premiered at the Kia Mau Festival 2025. The work explores the connection with entities and embodies the messages, wants, and connection with our physical world.

Gates to Memory invites audiences to witness the intimate act of remembering—of reclaiming lost stories and forging new ones through embodied performance. It is a call to honour those who came before, to hold space for those yet to come, and to respect the ones who will always be.

The strength of this work lies in the unspoken but strongly felt spiritual presence that comes from openness to access messages that are not ours but from ancestors from spirit worlds. To the creators, these messages come in the form of music, movement, and intuition.

Gates to Memory is performed and directed by Caribbean and Brazilian afro-descendant artists Ella Williams and Stela Dara, lighting designer Elekis Poblete and CreaTech sound designers Kevin Licorish and Jeanelle Frontin . All who wish to connect with their spirituality and the cultural intertwining of traditions and entities.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance 2026.

OPEN
DANCE

An open invitation to participate, witness and begin the festival together.

The Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance opens with a collective gathering of movement at The Hannah Playhouse.

This one-hour improvised movement sharing brings together dancers, artists and movement practitioners from across Pōneke.

Part performance, part meeting place, this open event celebrates coming together through movement, participation and exchange. Audiences are welcome to witness the unfolding improvisation, or join the space themselves.

Free to participate. Free to attend.

events.id: Paintvine Uncanvased™- Nude Life Drawing-Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Paintvine Uncanvased™- Nude Life Drawing
events.venue: Southern Cross Garden Bar Restaurant, 39 Abel Smith St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Undress your creativity in this fun, relaxed and easy to follow beginners nude life drawing workshop.

Over the course of two hours, our expert Paintvine artists will guide you through a series of life drawing exercises designed to introduce you to different methods of drawing, all while you and your friends enjoy a vino or brew. You’ll have access to a wide range of drawing tools and materials to explore your creative side in this experimental workshop.

After a couple of drinks, a few cheeky winks and a good laugh or three you and your friends will bid adieu to your model and head home with a series of unique and fun drawings to remember this creatively curious night out!

This is a popular event, so get your tickets before they sell out!

Drinks & Food

No drinks are included in the ticket price, but drinks and food are available for purchase throughout the event.

What is Paintvine?

It’s a fun social art class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own artwork in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends.

We provide all the materials you need. All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). All our events are in licensed venues, so you can purchase drinks to sip as you create! At the end of the night, you will leave with your own collection of unique drawings.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

Celebrate Mid-Winter Christmas at Q Space with connection, warmth, and a festive lunchtime gathering.
As the days get shorter and the temperature drops, we’re creating a cosy seasonal moment inside the space. This mid-year celebration is designed to bring members and the wider business community together for relaxed connection and a shared winter experience.
Step away from your desk, enjoy the atmosphere, and take a break in good company.

We look forward to welcoming you!

events.id: Welly Silent Book Club-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Welly Silent Book Club
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Silent Book Club is a global community of readers with 2,000 chapters in 60+ countries. There's no assigned reading — it's bring your own book.

More than a million members gather in person, online, and in destinations around the world to read together and swap stories. All readers are welcome!

A typical SBC meetup looks something like this:

5:00-5:15pm - People arrive, order drinks/food, share what they're reading
5:15-6:45pm - Quiet reading
6:45-7:00pm - Optional socialising, or just keep reading

The club will be reading on the second floor of Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, under the Earth Mothers (the three large panel art pieces across the ceiling and down the wall that faces the entrance of Ngā Pou Ruahine).

The #1 Ladies Night Out, Breaking Records Worldwide MagicMen Australia is taking over the world, breaking attendance records across Australia, New Zealand, Europe, U.S.A, South America and Canada, including over 6,500 screaming ladies at a single Ladies Night.

This is global entertainment at its peak.

Returning to New Zealand, the show presents TikTok and world-famous Instagram superstar Will Parfitt, with a combined following of over 20 million.

Get ready for a night you’ll never forget

Where entertainment meets energy and where records keep getting broken.

events.id: Pubble - Escape Room meets Pub Quiz-The Old Bailey, 101 Lambton Quay, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Pubble - Escape Room meets Pub Quiz
events.venue: The Old Bailey, 101 Lambton Quay, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pubbles have the goal of reconnecting people. One screen, your squad and an evening of social adventure!

A mix between the puzzles and lateral thinking of an escape room, mixed with the social, free experience of a pub quiz, Pubbles are designed to have something for everyone. They allow everyone to shine in a game where teamwork wins out over general knowledge and 'either you know it or you don't' traditional quizzes. Each Pubble is set to a different theme, so you could be a group of pirates one month, and astronauts the next. It’s not about what you know, it’s about what you see. Decode images, crack pattern, and beat the timer (and the other teams)

Perfect for meeting people, reconnecting, building bonds and having a laugh - Pubbles are free so it's never been easier to enjoy a midweek catch-up with mates!

To learn more, visit pubble.co.nz/players to learn why you might just love this brand new concept

When creating art, is the process more important than the product - or the product more important than the process? In this lively debate, local Wellington artists go head to head on a question that often plagues the creative mind. Expect diverse perspectives and a few friendly disagreements.
