events.id: You, Me, Everybody - Midnight Album Tour-Whisky & Wood, 60 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: You, Me, Everybody - Midnight Album Tour
events.venue: Whisky & Wood, 60 Cambridge Terrace, Wellington
events.long_description:
You, Me, Everybody hit the road in 2026 for a national tour celebrating Midnight, their new album exploring the moment where one day ends and the next begins. Recognised as finalists for Best NZ Folk Artist at the 2021 Aotearoa Music Awards, the group has earned widespread acclaim, including a global audience after their song “Stranger” featured in Netflix’s Sweet Tooth.

Founded by brothers Laurence (vocals and guitar) and Sam Frangos-Rhodes (mandolin, fiddle and vocals) and featuring Nat Torkington on Banjo, Rob Henderson on double bass and Kim Bonnington on vocals and guitar, You, Me, Everybody has built a reputation for pushing bluegrass beyond its borders, blending virtuosic musicianship with contemporary folk and modern string-band innovation. Midnight captures the tension and possibility of life’s turning points, brought to life onstage through rich harmonies, bold instrumental interplay, and great storytelling.

“Acoustic, drumless, genius” - Roger Bowie

The Midnight Tour sees the band bring their new album to Whāngarei as part of a 12-date run across Aotearoa.
events.description:
You, Me, Everybody hit the road in 2026 for a national tour celebrating Midnight, their new album exploring the moment where one day ends and the next begins.
Recognised as finalists for Best NZ Folk Artist at the 2021 Aotearoa Music Awards, the group has earned widespread acclaim, including a global audience after their song “Stranger” featured in Netflix’s Sweet Tooth.
Founded by brothers Laurence (vocals and guitar) and Sam Frangos-Rhodes (mandolin, fiddle and vocals) and featuring Nat Torkington on Banjo, Rob Henderson on double bass and Kim Bonnington on vocals and guitar, You, Me, Everybody has built a reputation for pushing bluegrass beyond its borders, blending virtuosic musicianship with contemporary folk and modern string-band innovation.
Midnight captures the tension and possibility of life’s turning points, brought to life onstage through rich harmonies, bold instrumental interplay, and great storytelling.
“Acoustic, drumless, genius” - Roger Bowie The Midnight Tour sees the band bring their new album to Whāngarei as part of a 12-date run across Aotearoa.

About this Event


Build the mindset, confidence and consistency that drive long-term business success
Success in business development is never just about strategy — it’s about mindset, communication, resilience, and the habits you build every day.

Every delegate will complete a personalised one-page Personal Performance Plan, including:
Strengths to lean into
Mindset shifts to work on

events.long_description:
Learn to create your very own voluminous, oversized crepé paper flower. A sculptural statement piece that’s equal parts art and joy.

In this immersive workshop, you’ll be guided through every step of the process:
Cutting, curling, shaping, and assembling each petal by hand. It’s a meditative, rewarding craft that requires patience, but with the right techniques (and a few of my favourite tricks!), you’ll be amazed at what you can create.

What’s included:
– A selection of curated colour options where you choose your favourite in advance, and I’ll prepare your paper for you
– All materials provided
– Step-by-step guidance in a relaxed, welcoming setting
– Your own stunning floral sculpture to take home
- Cocktail on arrival
- Canapès to enjoy while creating
- Enjoy a cuppa and sweets after the workshop!

Book now through https://www.ventana.co.nz/upcoming-workshops/p/giant-crepe-flower-greytown-msd

events.id: MATA MATA - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: MATA MATA - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
AWAKENING THE TANIWHA, HONOURING THE RECLAMATION. MATA MATA is an offering through the act of solo dance. A summoning of the ancestral spirits that reside deep within the awa. As the taniwha stirs just beneath the surface, memory begins to flow from skin into whenua. Awakening the blood memory that is present here. Becoming a sacred ancestral ritual, allow yourself to slip into this journey of returning. Acknowledging the physical body as a vessel to conjure spirit, mana, and memory. MATA MATA is the honouring of Whakapapa and identity and the holder of space to witness the sacred act of awakening this reclamation.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance 2026.

About the artist:
Harrison Cook is a contemporary dancer, choreographer, and writer of Māori (Kāi Tahu) and Pākehā descent, born and raised on Ngāti Hauā Whenua in the Waikato region of Aotearoa. He is currently based on the lands of the Whadjuk Noongar people in Boorloo (Perth). Harrison is a graduate of the Western Australian Academy of Performing Arts, completing his Bachelor of Arts Dance (2023) and Bachelor of Performing Arts Honours (Awarded a First Class Honours) (2024). During his honours research year, working under the mentorship of Jo Pollitt, he developed and produced his thesis work ’Te Wakahuia: Coming to Know Lineage through Dance Improvisation and Archival Poetics’ which included a body of movement and film work under the name ‘HōtokeWynter ~ ComingToKnow.’ This experience of producing this written and movement practice left Harrison feeling pulled to continue this ongoing artistic enquiry into whakapapa, ancestral memory, and the uncovering of the body as a living archive. His developing choreographic practice centres on research-based improvisation, exploring relationships between land and water, ancestral voice, and embodied lineage. Harrison’s solo work MATA MATA has become a sacred part of his practice and artistic offerings and has been developed and supported through the PILOT Choreographic Program with Enneagon Movement and will premiere as part of The Blue Room Theatre’s 2026 Annual Season through the EVOLVE Program with STRUT Dance. His artistic development has been supported through residencies with The Blue Room Theatre and PICA (Perth Institute of Contemporary Arts), where Harrison was given time and space to continue developing his ongoing practice and project 'THE RETURN'. Through his evolving practice, Harrison continues to explore and understand the connecting to dance becoming a sacred site for remembering, reconnecting, and embodying the stories of our past.

events.id: No One Rides Alone - Film Screening with Q and A-Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: No One Rides Alone - Film Screening with Q and A
events.venue: Penthouse Cinema, 205 Ohiro Rd, Brooklyn, Wellington
events.long_description:
Special screening of No One Rides Alone the latest documentary from Silvereye Films.

Can someone with Multifocal Motor Neuropathy (MMN) ride the route of the Tour de France? That’s what Clive Phillips and four fellow veterans set out to discover during the Tour de MMN 2025. Following the 1955 Tour de France route, the team set out to see if they could ride in the wheel tracks of the first British Tour de France team

Would they overcome the challenges of the Alps, Mont Ventoux and the Pyrenees? Or would the impacts of this rare disease on Clive’s motor function and strength bring him and the team to a halt?

From Making the Most of Now and the team at Silvereye films behind the award winning “One Day Ahead”. No One Rides Alone, follows the team as they ride around France, connect with the rare disease community and learn more about MMN and the impacts it and conditions like it have.

Featuring double Paralympic gold medalist Ben Watson and Italian para-triathlete Ilaria Brugnoli, No One Rides Alone is a journey of discovery and an intimate portrait of how with team work, positivity and a willingness to be vulnerable we can achieve the seemingly impossible.

Set against the majesty of the French landscape and a remarkable historical achievement. No One Rides Alone tells an inspiring story that has lessons for us all whatever challenges we face. The film aims to raise awareness of MMN, inspire the rare disease community and raise funds for MMN Research.

events.venue: Footnote Dance, 60 Dixon Street, New Zealand 6011,60 Dixon St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Konpa Gouya 101
Konpa, or also know as Compas or Kompa is a carribean dance from Haiti.
Two styles Dirèk and Gouya.
Dirèk music often a little faster and with more steps.
Gouya is slow and often dance with little steps.

Here will will be learning Konpa Gouya due to it's similar nature to Tarraxinha and as the music if often played at Kizomba Festivals overseas.

events.id: Pub Sing-The Empire, 175 Jackson St, Petone, Lower Hutt-Event Finder
events.name: Pub Sing
events.venue: The Empire, 175 Jackson St, Petone, Lower Hutt
events.long_description:
Pub Sing - where the audience (you) turns into the choir at the pub, with a live rock band.

No sheet music, no experience needed, and absolutely no solos.

Katy reveals three songs on the night (usually a crowd-pleaser, a ballad, and a banger), and even if you don’t know them, you’ll pick them up fast with colour-coded lyrics on screen.

You learn different harmony options, sing along with the band, and blend into a room of up to 80 voices, so no one’s listening to you; they’re singing with you.

There’s banter, laughs, a bit of silliness, and a whole lot of feel-good energy. It’s not karaoke (shudder), it’s everyone singing together just for the fun of it, and it’s the perfect end-of-week lift you needed.

*Please check the booking link for updates or changes to dates*

*Tickets are $10pp, and all proceeds go directly to the band!

events.id: The 25th Annual Putnam County Spelling Bee-Niblick Hall, Niblick Lane,, Porirua - Mana-Event Finder
events.name: The 25th Annual Putnam County Spelling Bee
events.venue: Niblick Hall, Niblick Lane,, Porirua - Mana
events.long_description:
Welcome to “The Bee!”

A hilarious take on the high-octane pressure of competitive spelling.

Six “tween-agers” battle it out for the glorious title of champion or perhaps a consolation juice box. Each with their own worries and pressures, they seek comfort amongst the complexities of perfectly arranged letters.

With its rapid-fire witty script and spellbinding music, “The Bee” hums out a beautiful coming-of-age story with more meaning than the word run*

The 25th Annual Putnam County Spelling Bee is a musical comedy and winner of 7 awards for its off-Broadway production and nominated for six Tony Awards, (winning two, including Best Book)

The Bee also invites selected audience members to take part in the spelling competition creating totally unpredictable, one show only, performances.

Come see what the buzz is all about!

Run time: 2 hours plus 20 min intermission
Contains sexual references, moments of bright lighting and haze effects
Recommended for mature audiences
Licenced Bar

(*Run has 645 distinct but related uses as a verb alone according to the Oxford English Dictionary)

events.id: A Person Shaped Thing - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: A Person Shaped Thing - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
A Person Shaped Thing is a new solo contemporary dance work by Trinity Maydon, exploring the fragile architecture of Self Perception/Presentation - the process of strategically concealing or revealing information in order to influence others' and our own perceptions on ourselves. Even the person you believe yourself to be only exists for you.

We consciously select different versions of ourselves in different rooms. Each version is carefully calibrated. Each gesture considered. A quiet choreography of revealing and concealing.

Drawing on sociologist Erving Goffman’s dramaturgical theories of human interaction, the work examines life as a performance: frontstage and backstage, audience and actor and the constant maintenance of expression control where we learn to play roles before we even realise we are performing them.

The attentive pupil performs attentiveness
until the performance consumes the listening.

In A Person Shaped Thing, identity is not something created, but selected — assembled from external influences, expectations, and the environments we move through. We shapeshift to fit the spaces we enter, adjusting the mask, refining the image, editing the self.

But the performance is exhausting.

What happens when the masks begin to slip?
What remains when the roles fall away?

As the work unfolds, the body becomes a site of tension — caught between authenticity and artifice, between who we are, who we think we are, and who we believe we should be. In the search to “fit into one’s own skin,” we risk becoming estranged from it entirely.

There is no singular, stable self.
At the crux, we are something harder to name —
An unidentifiable, faceless creature.

Blurring the boundaries between performer and performed

Does the performance ever really end?

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance.

About the artist:
Trinity Maydon is a contemporary dance artist originally from Ōtepoti and a graduate of the New Zealand School of Dance. Since graduating in 2024, she has been taking her first steps as a freelance artist, exploring and shaping her unique voice within the art form of dance.

Trinity has always been drawn to the ways in which we use our bodies to tell stories, how we can shape ourselves to physicalize our ideas and express them to the world. She is deeply interested in the lived experience of movement and the connection between mind and body, constantly exploring how embodiment can be a tool for both personal expression and collective storytelling.

Since graduating, Trinity has had the privilege of collaborating on two rave installations with Untitled Warehouse Project— 'Entry' and 'Omnia' (2025). She has also worked alongside Jeremy Beck, teaching throughout Dunedin and performing in Company Beck’s 'Commentary of Dreaming' as part of the 2025 Dunedin Arts Festival.
In 2026, Trinity was immersed in the Fringe Festival, performing in Lyndon Foley’s 'Our Dreaming Bodies' and and was a choreographer in Wellington Community Ballet Theatre's 'Four Questions' in her piece 'Is Santa Claus Real'. She also performed as a guest artist at the Performance Arcade in 'Four Strings – Four Limbs' with Alex Cooper.

A Person Shaped Thing is Trinity’s first full-length work and she is excited to be debuting it as part of this year’s festival.

A Person Shaped Thing is a new solo contemporary dance work by Trinity Maydon, exploring the fragile architecture of Self Perception/Presentation - the process of strategically concealing or revealing information in order to influence others' and our own perceptions on ourselves.
As the work unfolds, the body becomes a site of tension — caught between authenticity and artifice, between who we are, who we think we are, and who we believe we should be.
About the artist: Trinity Maydon is a contemporary dance artist originally from Ōtepoti and a graduate of the New Zealand School of Dance.
Since graduating in 2024, she has been taking her first steps as a freelance artist, exploring and shaping her unique voice within the art form of dance.
A Person Shaped Thing is Trinity’s first full-length work and she is excited to be debuting it as part of this year’s festival.

events.id: Deafheaven & Nothing-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Deafheaven & Nothing
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
Deafhaven return to NZ with special guests Nothing
American black-metal trailblazers Deafhaven announce their return to New Zealand this July, presented by Banished Music. The band will perform two exclusive headline shows in Auckland and Wellington, joined by special guests Nothing.
Formed in the San Francisco Bay Area in 2010, Deafhaven have spent the past decade and a half pushing at the emotional and sonic boundaries of modern metal. From their debut Roads to Judah, to the genre-defining breakthrough Sunbather, and subsequent records like New Bermuda, Ordinary Corrupt Human Love, and Infinite Granite, Deafhaven have continually redefined the limits of heavy music while confronting themes of alienation, addiction, family history, and self-perseverance.
The tour follows the release of their 2025 album Lonely People With Power. Their sixth album and first for Roadrunner Records arrives as both a culmination and a reckoning , an album that embraces the band’s full identity after years of evolution. Produced by Justin Meldal-Johnsen (Beck collaborator and producer of Infinite Granite), the record captures Deafhaven’s innate ability to merge melody with brutality, and pain with poetry. Through reflections on masculinity, inherited trauma, family, and self-perception, the album grapples with the idea that while we can’t fully escape our past or our nature, we can choose how we move forward. Ultimately, Lonely People With Power stands as one of Deafhaven’s most expansive and human statements.
The album has drawn praise from Pitchfork, Rolling Stone, The Line Of Best Fit, Kerrang and more, and features collaborations with Interpol’s Paul Banks and Boy Harsher’s Jae Matthews.
Revered for their transcendental live shows, Deafhaven have toured the world over, performing at festivals such as Coachella and Primavera Sound, and sharing stages with artists ranging from Slipknot and Knocked Loose to Chelsea Wolfe and Mono. They’ve received a Grammy Award nomination for Best Metal Performance and continue to command a reputation as one of the most compelling live acts in heavy music.
Joining them are American shoegaze outfit Nothing, whose latest release A Short History of Decay has been described by Pitchfork as “fresh and exciting.” Their fifth album and first for Run For Cover Records further expands the band’s sonic scope, delivering the most refined and immersive iteration of Nothing to date.

San Fran holds a Supervised License. Minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue.

events.name: The 2026 Wellington Raw Comedy Quest - Heats
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Presented by Cover Yours Ltd and Wellington Comedy

The capital's premier comedy competition. Witness the rising stars in stand-up comedy before they hit the big time.

The stepping stone for many New Zealand comedians, the Raw Comedy Quest has brought forth new comedy talent from across the country, with heats in Christchurch, Wellington, Palmerston North and Auckland.

From six heats, the top 24 go to the semifinals, and the top 10 to the Wellington final. 2024 Wellington finalist Chen Wang came runner up in the 2024 National Raw Comedy Quest, and Lydia Sainsbury runner up in 2025, can the capital win it this time?

Each heat is MC'd by a pro comedian, kick your weekend off with a rollercoaster of comedy

Thanks to support from the Wellington City Council, One Foundation, and Cover Yours Ltd

To enter email contact@humorous.co.nz, all you need is 6min of original material, and the guts to give it a go!

events.long_description:
Step into a world of wonder with your family at our enchanting interactive light show walk, where the beautiful grounds of Cobblestones comes alive with dazzling lights, magical soundscapes, and surprises around every corner! Watch your child's face light up as they step on colour-changing pathways, and discover hidden stories told through beams of light.

Perfect for all ages, this unforgettable nighttime adventure combines the outdoors with cutting-edge technology to create a one-of-a-kind experience that sparks imagination, laughter, and memories. Don’t miss your chance to walk through the magic—grab your tickets now! Open for ten nights only, as part of the NZ Festival of Christmas.

Session One: Gates open 5.30pm. Last entry is 6pm
Session Two: Gates open 6.30pm. Last entry is 7pm

2026 Sparkle Pack Presale - on sale 1-7 April, exclusively for Festival Sparkle Pack holders
2026 General Public - on sale from 8 April

Illumination Night Glow is designed to go ahead in typical winter weather conditions. Please dress warmly and come prepared to enjoy the experience, rain or shine.

In the unlikely event that your selected session is cancelled due to extreme weather, your ticket will be transferred to the next available date, so you won’t miss out.

Sorry, no dogs allowed.

events.name: FEVER|DREAM

events.long_description:
FEVER|DREAM
BCA presents FEVER|DREAM, a fresh programme of dance and music featuring four newly created works. This mixed bill takes audiences on a journey from intense, feverish moments to dreamlike states, reflecting on today's fast-paced life and the subconscious ways we process ourselves and our experiences within it.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance.

events.id: FEVER|DREAM - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance-Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: FEVER|DREAM - Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance
events.venue: Hannah Playhouse, 12 Cambridge Terrace, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
BCA presents FEVER|DREAM, a fresh programme of dance and music featuring four newly created works. This mixed bill takes audiences on a journey from intense, feverish moments to dreamlike states, reflecting on today's fast-paced life and the subconscious ways we process ourselves and our experiences within it.

FEVER
FEVER is a high-energy dance work exploring burnout, competitiveness, and the relentless pressure to keep going. Driven by fast-paced, dynamic movement, this piece captures the sensation of being trapped in cycles of productivity and competition — where stopping feels impossible.
PATHS CROSS
In the unpredictability of life, paths cross and intersect, but a singular focus may result in missed opportunities towards an intended destiny.
Through a singular focus, what might we overlook in the periphery? What alternative encounters might have veered us onto a different path and altered our course.
Chance encounters shape our lives, yet unrealised paths might have led to an entirely different existence.
We again introduce a work created by emerging student artists who will explore a ladder of words from FEVER to DREAM. Exploring ways to portray simple concepts from unrelated words through film and performance, graduate and graduating students from the New Zealand School of Dance will bring their individuality to the programme through exploring innovative ways to approach the composition of new movement patterns.
D)rea(m)lity (Dream Reality)
D)rea(m)lity (Dream Reality) is a reflection on our inner worlds, drawing on the interrelationship between a subconscious and conscious state of being. 
The audience is collectively absorbed into a sort of dreamscape, a dance with elusive ideals in the presence of pervasive shadows. Traversing the textures of the imagined and the real, this piece explores tensions between the fears that create voids in our lives, and the illusions that fill them.
Working closely with Jefferson Chen (Goodspace), this is an exchange of bites of movement that Zoe creates with her dancers and Jefferson’s musical response. Pōneke Contemporary Dance Festival will see the premiere of this work, one that will stay in our repertoire for future Festival Programmes, with the aim to present this in Auckland – Zoe’s home base.

Presented as part of the Pōneke Festival of Contemporary Dance.

About the artist:
BalletCollective Aotearoa is a project based dance collective based in Pōneke, Wellington. We are dedicated to exploring new ways to tell the stories of Aotearoa, pushing the boundaries of ballet technique within the contemporary dance space. Collaboration across the arts sector is central to our vision, ensuring that kiwi-trained dancers have the opportunity to develop their artistry at home. Under the BalletCollective Aotearoa banner, we provide a platform for dancers and choreographers to experiment, collaborate, and create new works in contemporary ballet. We aim to bring ballet to new audiences and foster a supportive, innovative environment for artistic growth within the genre.
This year we continue to build a repertoire of homegrown, locally developed contemporary ballet. We aim to present newly created choreography that is both abstract and virtuosic, to highlight the individual strengths of each dancer and their authenticity as contemporary artist’s trained in a ballet technique. We celebrate kiwi ingenuity and collaborate with other kiwi artists to bring you works that reflect the environment we inhabit at this time.
events.description:
Exploring ways to portray simple concepts from unrelated words through film and performance, graduate and graduating students from the New Zealand School of Dance will bring their individuality to the programme through exploring innovative ways to approach the composition of new movement patterns.
Pōneke Contemporary Dance Festival will see the premiere of this work, one that will stay in our repertoire for future Festival Programmes, with the aim to present this in Auckland – Zoe’s home base.
Under the BalletCollective Aotearoa banner, we provide a platform for dancers and choreographers to experiment, collaborate, and create new works in contemporary ballet.
We aim to bring ballet to new audiences and foster a supportive, innovative environment for artistic growth within the genre.
We aim to present newly created choreography that is both abstract and virtuosic, to highlight the individual strengths of each dancer and their authenticity as contemporary artist’s trained in a ballet technique.

events.id: The Improv Lounge-Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Improv Lounge
events.venue: Thistle Hall, 293 Cuba St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
“Delightful energy, alertness and vulnerability . . . fresh and invigorating” – Theatreview

Improvised scenes are inspired by the anecdotes friends share while sitting on a couch. Stories are told from a place that is truthful; the improvisors take the ideas places that are sometimes serious, sometimes whimsical, always playful. WIT's Improv Lounge structure originates with "The Living Room" developed in Chicago nearly forty years ago by Charna Halpern at the iO Theatre.

The Wellington Improvisation Troupe, aka WIT, is a member-based community theatre group and registered charity, teaching and performing in Wellington since 2003. Our theatre is made up on the spot, usually inspired by suggestions from the audience and each other. WIT players share a love of storytelling and work together as a team to inspire the audience, we’re sometimes serious, often hilarious and always totally unpredictable.

Pay-what-you-can tickets from $5, and door sales. Entrance to the Upstairs Hall is in Arthur St, or through the exhibition space entrance in Cuba Street.

events.id: The Tīwhas Present: Māreikura-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Tīwhas Present: Māreikura
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join the Tīwhas for a shared supper after the show, included in your ticket price.

“Me aro koe ki te hā o Hine-ahu-one.”

This Matariki, The Tīwhas present: ‘MĀREIKURA’ – a bold celebration of Wāhine Māori. From the celestial realms to the frontlines of history, we honour the trailblazing women who have left a beauty mark on the face of te ao!

Join Ngāti Tīwha, as the Queens – Dame Jthan, Slay West, Pānia and Tina Coco Couture – ignite the stage with the fire of Mahuika in this soul-stirring night of concert!

Channeling the essence of our mothers, sisters, aunties and nans, we pay tribute to the women who have shaped our lives.

This is our love letter to the unbreakable spirit of wāhine.

Witness the majesty of MĀREIKURA.

Fri 3 Jul – Sat 11 Jul

Price: $75 includes shared supper after the show
Timings: Tues – Sat 7.00pm, Sunday 4pm
Preview Night: Thursday 2 July
Audience Care: Whānau friendly, parental supervision recommended. Some coarse language.
Duration: 90 minutes, no interval.

Cast and Crew:
Dame Jthan – Jthan Morgan
Slay West – Levi Waitere
Pānia – Raureti Ormond
Tina Coco Couture – Te Hāmama Hohua
Kree Matthews
Ngāti Tiwha: Hayden Taylor, Tom Knowles, Kate Anderson, Marshall Rankin, Hami Hawkins, Emma Katene

events.id: Sappho: A Deliciously Sapphic Cabaret-The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Sappho: A Deliciously Sapphic Cabaret
events.venue: The Fringe Bar, 26 Allen Street, Wellington
events.long_description:
Let’s go Lesbians!

This July get ready to U-Haul down to The Fringe Bar for Sappho, A Deliciously Sapphic Cabaret!

There will be talking, laughing, loving, breathing, and absolutely no ultimatums.

Our cast is in a league of their own and will have you yearning and flustered with gay panic. From poetry to burlesque, drag to comedy, our show will leave you weak at the knees and wet in your seats…

Whether you are bisexual, pansexual, demi, ace, or none of our business this stunning show will be sure to please. Bring your friends, your big gay crush, and leave the rest to the history books.

Don’t be a useless sapphic, join the Pink Pony Club and get your tickets on Eventfinda from as little as $20.

Venue, seating & show info

Cash only door sales are available if not sold out prior ($35.00 per ticket)
GA seating is unallocated, make sure to arrive early to nab a good vantage point.


Doors open one hour before showtime.
If you are part of a large group, contact us to save seats together, or you may be split up.
Latecomers may not be admitted, you may be asked to wait before finding your seat if a performance is underway.


The event space is located up 6 upstairs, with narrow walkways between seating. The regular seating are black metal folding chairs, please contact us to discuss alternatives if this isn't an option for you. We will do what we can to accommodate as many bodies and abilities as possible.


Trigger warnings will be given before every show and we have a zero tolerance policy for abusive behaviour.


You can contact us at willysmackntush@gmail.com

events.id: The Veils | Fragile World Tour | Wellington-meow nui wellington,9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: The Veils | Fragile World Tour | Wellington
events.venue: meow nui wellington,9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
The Veils | Fragile World Tour | Wellington
Following the critically acclaimed release of ‘Asphodels’ in 2025, The Veils quickly return with a bold and invigorated new album titled ‘Fragile World’, out June 19 on V2 Records. Today The Veils alongside Banished Music announce a 10-date New Zealand tour including 6 band shows featuring the closing show of Strange Universe: Winter in Auckland, and 4 solo performances.

“It feels right to start our Fragile World tour back home in dear Aotearoa. These are some of our favorite towns to play in on earth and we can’t think of a better way to dust off this moth-bitten black suit and hat and get the show on the road. Come along, be merry, and as always come up and say hi” says Finn Andrews.

Arriving just over a year after their last release, ‘Fragile World’ marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band. Recorded live to tape in New Zealand by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills, Folk Bitch Trio), ‘Fragile World’, captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode.

The album’s title is both a reflection of the present moment - a time in which many institutions appear to be crumbling before our eyes - and a metaphor for the act of creation itself. The process of making music, Andrews notes, is a delicate and fragile undertaking where thousands of small decisions gradually coalesce into a finished whole.

“We went into the studio with a lot of songs, but very little idea of the arrangements or instrumentation. It was truly exciting having no idea what this record would sound like and only a few weeks to figure it out. It’s mostly Tom and I playing everything, with Joseph McCallum coming in at times. It was all very instinctual, quite full-on, and scary at times - but a good kind of scary, not scary like the real world out there.”
‍
With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, ‘Fragile World’ stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

“It feels right to start our Fragile World tour back home in dear Aotearoa.

Come along, be merry, and as always come up and say hi” says Finn Andrews.

events.id: WELCOMER 'CALENDARS' RELEASE TOUR W/ BELLADONNA & KING HIT-Valhalla, Wellington-Valhalla
events.name: WELCOMER 'CALENDARS' RELEASE TOUR W/ BELLADONNA & KING HIT
events.venue: Valhalla, Wellington
events.long_description:
Welcomer are embarking on their first nationwide headline tour to celebrate the release of their debut album Calendars, out June 12 via trusted DIY stalwarts, Papaiti Records.
Calendars follows a string of European shows in 2025 and a self titled EP which was described by Under the Radar as ‘melancholic splendour.. in swirls of harmonious goodness, each packed with presence and perfectly placed’. Single Nothing of You Remaining was described by Rolling Stone AU as “blending folk influences with strings for a warm, layered pop sound”.
https://welcomer.bandcamp.com/album/calendars
Joined in Pōneke by Belladonna and King Hit
Belladonna is the musical project of Bella Cook, a songwriter with a well-honed knack for musical storytelling, capturing the quirk and romance of everyday life. The debut Belladonna album, Arcus Way, was released in 2025 - a stunning collection of sharp and affecting songs with paired back arrangements.
https://belladonnamusicnz.bandcamp.com/album/arcus-way
King Hit is a Pōneke-based four piece melding the abrasiveness of shoe-gaze and punch of hardcore with the melancholy of Midwest-emo. Originally conceived as a three-piece by principal songwriter, guitarist and frontman Stefan Jenkins, King Hit cut their teeth in the Wellington underground scene for the first half of 2025 before adding Caitlin Gabb on guitar and vocals, joining bassist Felix Bird and drummer Sebastian Bell as architects of the band’s formidable wall of noise.
https://kinghit.bandcamp.com/track/sap
Tickets via UTR

events.id: The Bubble Spectacular-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: The Bubble Spectacular
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Get ready for a different sort of pop sensation. Enter the effervescent world of The Bubble Spectacular. Take warm water, liquid soap, glycerin, and the creative talents of Dr Hubble. With those simple ingredients, Dr Hubble conjures up a fun-filled, bubbly world. Prepare to be amazed as the ultimate master of bubbles creates an amazing bubble world. There’ll be little bubbles, big bubbles, volcanic bubbles, tornado bubbles, square bubbles, and with a bit of bubbly-magic, you might even end up inside a bubble. 
It’s the perfect beginning to the school holidays — a bubble party filled with laughter and audience participation. As bubbles fly around the auditorium, get ready to pop, bounce, and boogie in an unbubb-levably fun way. Catch it before it floats away.

events.id: Ruapehu Mountaineer-Plimmerton Railway Station,Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: Ruapehu Mountaineer
events.venue: Plimmerton Railway Station,Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Ruapehu Mountaineer
After a sold-out run in February, the Ruapehu Mountaineer is back. If you’ve been thinking about it, now’s the time to lock in a seat.

This is a full-day steam-hauled rail journey through the Central North Island to Mt Ruapehu. You’ll travel in comfortable heritage carriages with large viewing windows, an open-air viewing platform, and a café carriage onboard. It’s an easy, relaxed way to take in some of the best scenery on the network, from the Kapiti Coast through the Manawatū and across the Mangaweka viaducts.

At Ohakune, you’ll transfer by coach up to Whakapapa Village, where you’ve got a few hours to do your own thing. Grab lunch, take in the alpine views, or add on the Sky Waka gondola for a trip up the mountain.

It’s a long but rewarding day, and a pretty unique way to experience Mt Ruapehu. Ideal for families, groups, or anyone keen on a proper steam day out.

⸻

FARES

Plimmerton, Paraparaumu
Adult $219.00 | Child $179.00

Adult $199.00 | Child $159.00

⸻

RETURN (Southbound)

⸻

Operated by Mainline Steam Heritage Trust

events.id: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mental Health First Aid Aotearoa
NZ Citizens and residents pay $219 with ID.  f you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $499 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register..
Mental Health First Aid is an evidence-based workshop which gives people the skills and confidence to have supportive conversations with their whānau, co-workers and community and help guide them to professional help if needed.
You will learn to recognise common mental health challenges, how to provide initial help to someone experiencing a mental health challenge using a practical, evidence-based action plan, and how to respond in a crisis situation. During the workshop you will have the opportunity to practice new skills in a safe environment.
NOTE: This course is subsidised with funding from the Tertiary Education Commission (TEC) and you must provide photo ID when you attend your first session, if applicable.
Mental health challenges covered in the course include:
Depression
Anxiety
Psychosis
Substance use problems
Mental Health Crisis Covered:
Suicidal thoughts and behaviours
Non- Suicidal self-injury
Panic attack
Traumatic events
Severe effects from Alcohol or other substance use
Aggressive behaviours
People who attend Mental Health First Aid training can complete a short online assessment to be accredited as a Mental Health First Aider. You must attend the full two days to be able to do the online assessment for accreditation. The accreditation is valid for three years.
If you choose not to be accredited, you will receive a certificate of participation if you attend the full course. You will also receive a workbook as part of your course fee.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Land of the Long, Long Drive-Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Land of the Long, Long Drive
events.venue: Circa Theatre, 1 Taranaki St, Wellington
events.long_description:
Wētā, Tuna and Kārearea are mates of Gary the Glowworm, and are travelling to attend his rave in the cave birthday party. The friends face a number of trials on the road, and learn that despite their differences, they can achieve their goals by working together! A tale for kiwi kids, featuring iconic NZ creatures, landscapes and fresh waiata by NZ singer songwriter Benny Tipene.

Land of the Long, Long Drive is the Winner of Playmarket’s 2022 Plays for the Young (3-8 Year-old category), and nominated for the PAYPA production of the Year (2024). A tale about friendship, birthdays and going the extra mile for the ones we love, Horse With No Name and GRIDLEY by Victoria are excited to bring back Land of the Long, Long Drive for Circa’s big 50th birthday celebration!

Written by Catriona Tipene and Ryan Cundy
Directed by Catriona Tipene

Sat 4 – Sat 18 July
Tues – Sat 10am & 11.30am
$20pp or $70 for a family pass (4 x tickets)
CIRCA TWO

events.id: Shalakazap!-Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Shalakazap!
events.venue: Tararua Tramping Club Clubrooms, 4 Moncrieff St, Mt Victoria, Wellington
events.long_description:
How will an apprentice MatheMagician and a Fire Chief from Kindle save the village from the Flabberfamish? With some MatheMagics of course. A Riotous comedy, with a transforming set and audience participation.

Written by the phenomenal Jenny Wake ONZM - a veteran of New Zealand Television and Theatre. In her television career, she wrote, directed and produced ‘Play School’, wrote for ‘Bumble’ and directed ‘What Now’. Jenny has travelled the word championing children's theatre. Currently the National Coordinator of Performing Arts and Young People of Aotearoa (PAYPA) and the International Association of Theatre for Children and Young People (ASSITEJ) New Zealand.

This play has had sell-out tours, and international presentations at Brisbane's Out of the Box festival and Vancouver-based Axis Theatre.

Actors:
Haydn Carter is a Graduate of Whitireia's Stage and Screen. He is one of our favourites at KidzStuff and has been in Badjelly The Witch, Kiwi Courage and Goldilocks. This time he is returning to KidzStuff as our apprentice MatheMagician, Tyro. Amalia Calder is a Kāpiti local and Mum. She has been a performer for 29 years and is the Creative Director of KidzStuff. Amalia has been in numerous KidzStuff shows and first performed Shalakazap! in the year 2000. It was her 1st ever tour, fresh out of drama school, but this time she will be playing Fire Chief Fleet. Adriana Calabrese returns to KidzStuff after her debut performance in Badjelly the Witch in 2025. Adriana is an Italian and Brazilian, Pōneke-based actor and singer. She was most recently seen at Circa Theatre in Sense and Sensibility and Lizzie the Musical as well as The Court Theatre in The Importance of Being Earnest.

Recommended for ages 4 +
Special $7 preview show on Saturday July 4th 2026!

events.id: Alexia Urban Winery Presents: Pies & Pinot-Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Alexia Urban Winery Presents: Pies & Pinot
events.venue: Alexia Urban Winery, 197 West Street, Greytown
events.long_description:
Nothing beats a classic wine and pie combo, which is why Alexia is turning on the ovens and firing up the pie warmer for the return of the world-famous (in Greytown) Pies & Pinot!

It’s a simple concept - hot gourmet pies eaten out of paper bags matched to the different expressions of Pinot Noir made by Alexia – Méthode Traditionelle Rosé (pink fizz), Rosé, White Pinot Noir and of course our classic Pinot Noir!

The pie warmer will be fully stocked with the iconic Clareville Bakery pies, the fresh cream doughnuts will be lined up, and the paper bags will be ready.

Don’t mind the crumbs, it’s wine bar turned pie bar and we can’t wait! Come hungry, thirsty, and wearing stretchy pants for the ultimate warming winter winner!

No bookings required, just turn up. Parking onsite, or a 15 minute walk from Greytown centre.

events.id: Rediscover the Joy of Writing by Hand:Introduction to Italic Writing-Greytown Distilling Co - Judgement Room, Level 2, 110 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown-Event Finder
events.name: Rediscover the Joy of Writing by Hand:Introduction to Italic Writing
events.venue: Greytown Distilling Co - Judgement Room, Level 2, 110 Main Street, Greytown, Greytown
events.long_description:
Do you delight in the magic of putting pen to paper, but wish your handwriting could dance a little more gracefully across the page?

Join us for a gentle introduction to the art of italic writing. Over two hours, Gareth Rapson will work through 10 short lessons with the aim of changing your handwriting into something beautiful, elegant and expressive. This gathering welcomes all ages and skill levels.

Please bring your favourite pens and a lined notebook or lined paper.

Handouts will be provided and we'll have some Esterbrook fountain pens for you to use on the day if you want to try out your new skills with a traditional pen.

About Your Teacher: Gareth Rapson is an ex English teacher with an interest in all aspects of creativity. Often found with a book, pen and a notebook or at the Bridge table!

Book your ticket today via https://villagebookshop.co.nz/products/workshop-introduction-to-italic-writing

Please note, the venue is on level 2 with access via a flight of stairs.

events.id: Paint and Gin Weekend Afternoon - Aoraki-The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu-Event Finder

events.venue: The Bond Store, 21 Birmingham Street, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Join us for a fun adults’ painting class at The Bond Store – a vibrant distillery in the heart of Paraparaumu, known for its award-winning New Zealand craft spirits. No painting experience is needed – your friendly artist will guide you step by step in a relaxed, judgement-free environment.

This event is all about creativity, conversation, and having a great time while unleashing your inner artist.

Your ticket includes a gin tasting tray, giving you the chance to sample some of The Bond Store’s specialty gins while you paint.

Whether you’re coming with friends, planning a date night, or flying solo, this is your chance to unwind, get creative, and experience the joy of painting with Paintvine.

What is Paintvine?
It’s a fun social painting class where our artists will run you through step-by-step instructions on how to create your very own masterpiece in a fun and relaxed bar setting. Perfect for dates, catching up with mates or for meeting new friends!

We provide all the materials you need (high-quality canvas, acrylic paint, brushes). All you need to bring is your creative spirit (or find some at the bottom of a glass). At the end of the night you will leave with your own painting ready to hang up on your wall.

For tickets, future events, FAQs and more info head to the Paintvine website ⇒ paintvine.co.nz

events.name: Tempo Schmempo at Cuba St Tavern: Vol II
events.venue: Cuba St Tavern,172 Cuba Street Te Aro, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Tempo Schmempo at Cuba St Tavern: Vol II
Following a sold-out debut that packed Cuba Street Tavern to the rafters, Tempo Schmempo returns this July with a bigger lineup, longer hours, and another celebration of Aotearoa's thriving underground dance music community.

Born on East London's dancefloors and forged across New Zealand's club and festival circuit, Tempo Schmempo has found another new home in Te Whanganui-a-Tara. This second instalment of the bi-monthly series takes things up a notch, stretching from 4pm through until 1am and featuring six acts across an extended journey of house, disco, Balearic sounds, leftfield grooves and dancefloor oddities.

This time, we're proud to present the delectable prix fixe extravaganza of Disco Drongos, B-Lo and Staciez Mum supported by Tempo Schmempo's founding fathers 22Tango, Everybody, and Vino Paradiso for your extended listening pleasure.

And what's more, we've once again teamed up with Duncan's Brewing Co. to pour the official Tempo Schmempo beer on tap for the night, creating the perfect accompaniment to nine hours of musical exploration and dancefloor mischief.

This one is going to be special. Tell your friends, book those tickets and we'll see you on the dancefloor.

Disco Drongos
B-Lo
Staciez Mum
Everybody
22Tango
Vino Paradiso

Saturday 4 July
4:00pm – 1:00am
Cuba Street Tavern

In 2026, the NZSO National Youth Orchestra brings together Aotearoa New Zealand’s best young musicians to perform Britten’s breathtaking Four Sea Interludes; Raise Hawaiki: Kealaikahiki, commemorating the trans-Pacific voyage of the Hōkūle’a waka from Hawai’i to Tahiti; a new commission by the 2026 NYO Composer-in-Residence Jack Bewley; and Debussy’s La Mer, one of the most influential masterpieces of the 20th century.

events.id: The Veils - Fragile World w/ Jazmine Mary-Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: The Veils - Fragile World w/ Jazmine Mary
events.venue: Meow Nui, 92 Vivian Street, Te Aro, Wellington, 6011, Wellington
events.long_description:
Following the critically acclaimed release of ‘Asphodels’ in 2025, The Veils quickly return with a bold and invigorated new album titled ‘Fragile World’, out June 19 on V2 Records. Today, The Veils alongside Banished Music announce a 10-date New Zealand tour including 6 band shows featuring the closing show of Strange Universe: Winter in Auckland, and 4 solo performances.

“It feels right to start our Fragile World tour back home in dear Aotearoa. These are some of our favourite towns to play in on earth, and we can’t think of a better way to dust off this moth-bitten black suit and hat and get the show on the road. Come along, be merry, and as always, come up and say hi,” says Finn Andrews.

Arriving just over a year after their last release, ‘Fragile World’ marks a striking shift in tone and energy for the band. Recorded live to tape in New Zealand by Paddy Hill, with production by Tom Healy (Tiny Ruins, The Chills, Folk Bitch Trio), ‘Fragile World’ captures The Veils in an urgent and instinctive mode.

The album’s title is both a reflection of the present moment - a time in which many institutions appear to be crumbling before our eyes - and a metaphor for the act of creation itself. The process of making music, Andrews notes, is a delicate and fragile undertaking where thousands of small decisions gradually coalesce into a finished whole.

“We went into the studio with a lot of songs, but very little idea of the arrangements or instrumentation. It was truly exciting, having no idea what this record would sound like and only a few weeks to figure it out. It’s mostly Tom and me playing everything, with Joseph McCallum coming in at times. It was all very instinctual, quite full-on, and scary at times - but a good kind of scary, not scary like the real world out there.”
‍
With its immediacy, intensity, and emotional clarity, ‘Fragile World’ stands as one of The Veils’ most compelling releases to date.

Come along, be merry, and as always, come up and say hi,” says Finn Andrews.

events.id: DJ Craze (US)-Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: DJ Craze (US)
events.venue: Meow, 9 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Five-time DMC World DJ Championships champion DJ Craze is a true heavyweight of global turntablism, renowned for pushing the boundaries of drum & bass, hip hop and bass music. From pioneering the presence of drum & bass on the DMC stage to delivering electrifying sets worldwide, Craze’s technical precision and fearless genre-blending have cemented his reputation as one of the most influential DJs of his generation.

Founder of Slow Roast Records alongside Kill the Noise, Craze has played a key role in shaping the US bass music landscape, while touring globally from underground clubs to major festivals. His sets are a masterclass in energy and control - seamlessly weaving rap, dubstep, Miami bass and drum & bass into a high-impact dancefloor experience.

This is more than a DJ set - it’s a chance to witness a battle champion and crowd-destroyer at the top of his game. Don’t miss one of the most technically gifted and influential DJs to ever touch the decks - secure your tickets now.

Support EThixx

events.id: A Change Is Coming: Journey to Homosexual Law Reform presentation-Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: A Change Is Coming: Journey to Homosexual Law Reform presentation
events.venue: Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui, 65 Victoria St, Wellington
events.long_description:
In July 2026, New Zealand marks 40 years since the passing of the Homosexual Law Reform Act 1986, a landmark change that decriminalised sex between consenting adult men and helped reshape the lives of rainbow communities across the country.

Join Walk Tours NZ and Wellington City Libraries for A Change Is Coming: Journey to Homosexual Law Reform, a powerful one-hour presentation tracing the long and difficult road to reform, presented at Te Matapihi ki te Ao Nui Central Library.

Blending live storytelling, historic images, archival audio and personal memories, the presentation begins in 1967 with the first public meeting in New Zealand calling for homosexual law reform, held in what is now City Gallery Wellington. From there, it follows some of the social, political and personal struggles that led to the historic law change of 9 July 1986.
The presentation explores the realities many rainbow people faced before reform, including criminalisation, discrimination, police surveillance, medical stigma, religious condemnation and social exclusion.

At times confronting, but ultimately uplifting, A Change Is Coming is a celebration of resilience, protest, courage and community. It honours the activists, allies and rainbow communities whose determination helped transform New Zealand society, and whose legacy continues to shape our future.

Mid-winter is a magical time of the year in the Wairarapa. The cooler, crisp days make it perfect to sink into our deep sofas and large open fireplaces as you relax with friends and family soaking in the atmosphere of a mid-winters Christmas feast at Wharekauhau Country Estate. The Mid-Winter Christmas Lunch at Wharekauhau Country Estate is a spectacular event that blends festive cheer with the estate’s renowned luxury and hospitality. Held in the cozy winter months, this special dining experience combines the warmth of traditional Christmas flavors with the stunning, wintry backdrop of the Wairarapa countryside. It’s a perfect way to celebrate the season in a unique and memorable setting.

Chef has recreated some of traditional Christmas dishes using the the seasonal produce for the estates garden.

events.id: Natural Textured Rings - Taster class-Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki-Event Finder
events.name: Natural Textured Rings - Taster class
events.venue: Workspace Studios Ōtaki, 2 Arthur Street, Ōtaki, Ōtaki
events.long_description:
Make your own personalised ring - a gift for yourself or someone special! :-)

Learn the basic technique first in copper or brass, and then craft your final piece in silver!

Over this taster class, you’ll be guided through the process of heating and texturing metal, cutting and shaping with traditional jewellery tools. Use feathers, leaves, or fabrics to create a unique texture for a beautiful ring!

Learn to measure your finger, cut metal to length and texture it without rolling mill, hammers and punches. Then use a jewellers torch, sandpaper and polishing, and your beautiful new ring will be ready to wear.

This 3-hour class is ideal for beginners, groups of friends and visitors to the city.

Included in the class fee are copper and brass for practising on, a sterling silver ring strip and all consumables such as sandpaper, solder and chemicals. Extra metals are available for purchase in the studio.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but any person under the age of 16 must be accompanied by adults.
events.description:

events.id: Titanium Earrings-Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Titanium Earrings
events.venue: Workspace Studios Wellington Market Lane, 3 Market Lane, Wellington Cenral, Wellington
events.long_description:
In this super-fast 3-hour class, you will make a pair of earrings fit for a superhero!

Titanium is an amazingly light metal that can transform from a subtle grey to a whole range of super vibrant, or sleek and pale colours, from gold, pink, purple or deep blue, to light blue, palest green and subtle grey.

You’ll begin by practising fundamental metalworking techniques on copper, then experiment with flame-colouring titanium to understand how heat transforms its surface. With these skills, you’ll craft your own earrings, exploring texture and colour.

We’ll use a rolling mill to imprint natural or abstract patterns into the titanium sheet, shaping pieces into clean geometric forms—rectangles, diamonds, triangles, or squares. You can also experiment with layering elements for added depth and variation.

Finish your design with a choice of comfortable hooks or hoops, and leave with a unique, wearable piece.

Titanium is hypoallergenic, and your findings options include surgical stainless steel and gold-plated stainless steel.

You should take this class:
- If you like large earrings that are bold and badass
- If you like earrings to be feather light
- If you like colourful earrings that will really make a statement
- If you like pastel blues and simple shapes
- If you love earrings and want to try something new
- If you’re curious to learn more about using Titanium in your jewellery making - this is an ideal way to dip your toes.

Kids aged 12 (or so) are welcome to attend the class, but an enrolled parent or caregiver must accompany anyone under the age of 16

events.id: Wairarapa Cinémathèque presents THE GODFATHER (1972)-Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton-Event Finder
events.name: Wairarapa Cinémathèque presents THE GODFATHER (1972)
events.venue: Regent 3 Cinemas, 230 Queen Street, Masterton
events.long_description:
The Godfather (1972)
Dir. Francis Ford Coppola | USA | 2h 57m | Crime, Drama

Francis Ford Coppola’s masterwork traces the rise and transformation of the Corleone family, balancing intimate family drama with the brutal realities of organised crime. Featuring iconic performances by Marlon Brando and Al Pacino, The Godfather remains one of the most influential films in cinema history. This film screening is also a homage to the amazing American actress, Diane Keaton.

This screening is presented as part of the programme for the Wairarapa Cinémathèque 2026.

Wairarapa Cinémathèque is dedicated to celebrating film as part of our artistic and cultural heritage. We value the communal experience of cinema, bringing people together through curated screenings, discussions, and the sharing of films of significance. Your membership ensures access to this shared experience, while providing benefits such as exclusive screenings and partner discounts.

events.long_description:
Mrs Llama the Very Kiwi Farmer
Celebrate 10 years of Toybox Theatre, and 100 years of Wellington Repertory Theatre, with our new children's show for the July school holidays!

On her lively New Zealand farm, Mrs Llama is trying to keep everything under control… but between some very pesky rabbits and a cast of unforgettable animal characters, things quickly spiral into hilarious chaos. With the help of the audience, she brings a collection of beloved stories to life, each with a playful Kiwi twist.

Perfect for young audiences and the young at heart, Mrs Llama the Very Kiwi Farmer is a feel-good theatrical adventure full of surprises, silliness, and just a little bit of farmyard mayhem.

Brought to you by Toybox Theatre, supported by Wellington Repertory.

events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: TEG MJR & TEG Live
Get ready as South African sensation Jeremy Loops returns to bring the vibes to Wellington’s Meow Nui this July!
After a massive 2025, including a standout set on the Acoustic Stage at Glastonbury, the world’s favourite high-energy-folk powerhouse is back for a strictly limited run of headline shows.
Known for his legendary live performances that blend acoustic folk, alt pop and urban grit with loop-pedal wizardry, Jeremy Loops delivers a show that is part intimate storytelling, and part stadium-sized party.
From massive breakout anthems like Down South and Waves to the soaring new melodies of his latest record Feathers And Stone, expect an infectious atmosphere and world-class musicianship. Every room becomes a massive communal celebration. Every night is a pure explosion of energy.
Catch his exclusive Wellington performance. One night only, at Meow Nui, supported by Tom River.

events.id: Matariki Ahi Kā-Odlin’s Plaza, Wellington Waterfront-Wellington NZ
events.name: Matariki Ahi Kā
events.venue: Odlin’s Plaza, Wellington Waterfront
events.long_description:
Gather with friends and whānau to celebrate the Māori New Year on the waterfront. Matariki Ahi Kā, presented by Wellington City Council, honours culture, history, and family. Experience a free, immersive journey along the waterfront. It features fire, stunning projections, live performances, and a ceremony for loved ones who have passed away. There are also kai courts where you can stop off for a bite and listen to live music. Starting at 6pm and running for three hours. You can soak up the atmosphere from 9 to 12 July. 
The rising of Puanga and the Matariki star cluster (Pleiades) mark the Māori New Year. It is a special time to get together and restore faith and hope for the future. Māori refer to celebrating whanaungatanga or kinship. Be with others, share stories and kai, and plan to work towards a sustainable future.

events.id: National Celebration - 40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform-New Zealand Parliament,The Legislative Council Chamber, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: National Celebration - 40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform
events.venue: New Zealand Parliament,The Legislative Council Chamber, Pipitea, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
National Celebration - 40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform
40 Years of Homosexual Law Reform – Community Celebration at Parliament

Join us in the historic Banquet Hall at Parliament as we come together to celebrate 40 years since the passing of the Homosexual Law Reform Act — a landmark moment that helped transform Aotearoa New Zealand into a more inclusive and just society.

This special evening will honour the courage, determination, and advocacy of those who fought for equality, while celebrating the vibrant and diverse rainbow communities who continue that legacy today.

The programme will feature speeches from past and present activists, sharing reflections on the movement, the challenges faced, and the progress achieved over four decades. Guests will also enjoy performances from talented members of the community, bringing music, storytelling, and creative expression to the celebration.

More than a commemoration, this event is also an opportunity to connect across generations of the rainbow community and allies. Join activists, community leaders, supporters, and friends for an evening of reflection, celebration, and whakawhanaungatanga in one of the country’s most significant civic spaces.

Together, we honour the past, celebrate the present, and look toward a future where equality and inclusion continue to grow.

events.id: Krafty Kuts (UK)-Meow,17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Krafty Kuts (UK)
events.venue: Meow,17 Edward St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Krafty Kuts (UK)
KRAFTY KUTS 'Turbulence' ALBUM TOUR | Matariki Weekend

Get a sneak peek of Krafty's new album 'Turbulence' releasing 10 July on Against The Grain. Drawing from UKG, breaks, bassline and jazz, it's his most significant record in over a decade.


A pioneer in the most literal sense. Krafty's Slam The Breaks series and his Mixmag Instant Party covermount didn't just define an era, they set the blueprint for the bass movement that soundtracked the 21st century. Two Breakspoll award-winning albums, a FabricLive 34 mix, and a back catalogue spanning Ministry of Sound, OWSLA, Fabric, Skint and Finger Lickin' tell you everything about where he sits in the canon.


On the road his CV reads like a who's who: Big Day Out alongside Rage Against The Machine and Bjork, Summadayze straight after Groove Armada, Glastonbury, Bestival, sell-out nights at Fabric, Brixton Electric and Matter. He's shared stages with Grand Master Flash, Z Trip, Thievery Corporation and Kurtis Blow and played everything from the Chinese Grand Prix to Spotify's launch party.


Local support from BRYCE | WADE | MASON | STU

Come join us for an original music debut of the project Rivering. Lily Rose Atu recently finished studying Jazz at Te Kōkī, NZ School of Music, and is excited to share her original works at the Rogue & Vagabond on Thursday the 9th of July. These tunes are a collection of reflections from the past few years, inspired by her love for jazz and neo-soul. All proceeds will go into recording this album later on this year.

The music will be brought to life by the incredible: Jono Ben Zur on bass, Paulina Hernandez on piano, Ranu Wijesuriya on drums, Frank Paton on trumpet, Elisa Bird on baritone, Victoria Ferry on vocals, and Lily Rose Atu on saxophone and vocals.

Doors: 8.30pm
Music from: 9pm

* Wheelchair accessible venue | Accessible bathroom available

events.id: Peter Hook and The Light-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Peter Hook and The Light
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Bassist Peter Hook has intimate knowledge of the music of bands Joy Division and New Order. He’s one of the founding members of both British new wave bands. In July 2026, he is bringing his band The Light to The Opera House in Wellington. Together they will perform New Order’s album ‘Get Ready’ in full to celebrate its 25th anniversary. To make the concert even better, they will also perform a selection of greatest hits from Joy Division’s and New Order’s back catalogue. Get ready to enjoy songs like Disorder, Shadowplay, Transmission, Love Will Tear Us Apart, Blue Monday, and True Faith. 
The dark and ethereal sound of Joy Division and New Order ushered in the post-punk era and continue to be influential in the rock music scene. Peter Hook and The Light are careful to meticulously recreate their music in a live setting. Audiences are in for a show where treasured songs are played with the passion and energy they deserve.

events.id: Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai-5710,Kahutara, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai
events.venue: 5710,Kahutara, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Matariki Festival Paetūmōkai
Matariki herenga waka – for all of us.

Kia ora Paetūmōkai whānau, Wairarapa Moana communities. and manuhiri, it's time for us to start planning our Matariki celebrations again.

Soon we will put the call out; for those of you who are storytellers, pumpkin choppers, kawakawa gatherers, kaumatua and kuia chauffeurs, chair putter-outers, table decorators, steam pudding makers, sweepers, child wranglers, photographers, leftover hangi dispersers, fairy light arrangers; our small town makes wonderful things happen when we gather together. If you have helped before, thank you, and if you can help again- thank you- but if you need to rest, we understand. If you haven't helped before- nau mai, haere mai, welcome- and in advance, thank you!

This year’s theme of "Matariki herenga waka" is all about inclusion, and encouraging ALL people to celebrate Matariki together. The theme has strong connections to multi-culturalism and the sharing of cultural practices; we can ALL connect to the core values of Matariki and embrace the celebration of the holiday.

The Māori New Year is a time of reflection, celebration, and preparation. It is a time to remember loved ones, come together with whānau to give thanks to what we have and look forward to the promise of a new year.

In the meantime; here's a new Matariki waiata to get your heart singing. Don't worry, there'll still be the Matariki Macarena!

Puanga Festival
Join us to celebrate Puanga in Palmerston North.

This event returns to the Pit Lanes for 2026, as we come together to bring in the Māori New Year.

We're proud to partner once again with Ngāti Hineaute to bring everyone together for our annual Puanga Festival.

Enjoy a range of kai options, live entertainment from local groups - including kapa haka, and plenty of activities for tamariki. You’ll also have the chance to learn more about why Puanga is celebrated in the region (instead of Matariki) and what it means to local iwi.

More information on what to expect will be shared soon.

events.id: Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park-Wainuiomata Recreation Area,Lower Hutt, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park
events.venue: Wainuiomata Recreation Area,Lower Hutt, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Shooting the Stars- Wainuiomata Regional Park
Calling all aspiring astro-photographers!

Join us to get close-up and personal with our galaxy and learn the basics of dark sky shooting from a professional.

Are you a photographer interested in learning the tips and tricks of dark sky photography? We are thrilled to be offering this exclusive astrophotography workshop and stargazing session guided by Glen Butler (Night & Light Photography) and Wellington Astronomical Society as part of Greater Wellington’s Dark Sky Series.

This event is strictly limited to 30 people, register now via this link:


Please only register if you have the equipment requirements outlined below. What to bring (minimum requirements):
• Camera – with the ability to change manual settings of aperture, shutter speed and ISO
• A wide-angle lens or zoom (e.g. 14mm, 24mm, 35mm)
• A stable tripod
• A remote shutter release or the ability to put your camera onto (2-second) self-timer mode
• Empty memory card(s)
• Spare/fully charged camera batteries
• Head torch (ideally with red light setting)
• Warm clothing and sturdy footwear

During this introductory session to astrophotography, you will learn the fundamentals and some creative ideas to begin taking captivating images in a unique dark sky environment. Wellington Astronomical Society will also have their telescopes on hand for you to have a closer look at our sparkling Southern Skies.

Why Wainuiomata? Wainuiomata Regional Park is close to achieving dark sky place accreditation and offers some of the region's best stargazing just 30 minutes from Wellington's CBD. The location of the park provides a sanctuary from city light pollution, allowing budding astro-photographers a stunning night sky to shoot.

This event is not suitable for children under 18.

events.id: All Blacks vs Italy-Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: All Blacks vs Italy
events.venue: Hnry Stadium, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Make it your mission to support the All Blacks in 2026 when they face Italy at Hnry Stadium. This is an exciting match as it’s Italy’s first test in Wellington. It’s also only the fourth time they’ve met in New Zealand since the 1987 Rugby World Cup. With a 5:10pm kick-off, the match is designed to be family-friendly. Bring the whole whanau for an action-packed afternoon at the stadium. 
The All Blacks’ fixture against Italy is part of the inaugural Nations Championship. This is a new international rugby tournament featuring 12 of the world’s top nations across July and November. Each match has competition points on the line leading to the final, so the Wellington game is important. Wellington will be awash with rugby fever, so head out, support the All Blacks and say ‘ciao’ to all the Italian fans in the city.

events.id: Kiri Te Kanawa Song Quest 2026-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Kiri Te Kanawa Song Quest 2026
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Widely regarded as the launchpad for New Zealand’s leading operatic talent, this competition has championed emerging singers since 1956. In the Kiri Te Kanawa Song Quest 2026, elite singers vie for a share of $95,500 in prizes. Watch as six finalists perform with the New Zealand Symphony Orchestra conducted by Brad Cohen, at the Grand Final Gala. International judge Frederica von Stade will announce the winner live at the end of the night. 
For the first time in its 70-year history, the competition bears the name of its most famous winner, Dame Kiri Te Kanawa. The Gisborne-born singer won the competition in 1965 and went on to an illustrious operatic career across the globe. 
The 2026 judge, Frederica von Stade, is one of the most beloved mezzo-sopranos of her generation, celebrated for her expressive artistry and enduring contribution to classical music. Affectionately known as “Flicka,” she made her Metropolitan Opera debut in 1970 and went on to perform more than 300 times with the company. In concert, she has collaborated with eminent conductors and orchestras across the globe.

events.id: Luude-Shed 6, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Luude
events.venue: Shed 6, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Christian Benson is known professionally as Luude. The Australian drum and bass producer is best known for his 2021 cover of the 1980s iconic anthem Down Under. The D&B rework gave it a whole new dynamic and sold millions of copies. Five years and many successful mixes later, Luude is riding a wave of success. He’s toured relentlessly across some of the biggest stages in dance music and now comes to Wellington’s Shed 6. Along with Down Under, you’ll hear big hits like Big City Life, TMO, and Oh My. 
Luude’s recording achievements also include an ARIA Award for Best Dance Release and a nomination for the Michael Gudinski Breakthrough Artist Award. He also won Best International Artist at the 2022 Drum and Bass Awards, three BBC Radio 1 Hottest Records, and platinum, gold, and silver Certifications. He is also half of Australian electronic duo Choomba.

events.id: Postmodern Jukebox — The Future is Vintage-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Postmodern Jukebox — The Future is Vintage
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The world’s foremost purveyors of time-twisting hits, Postmodern Jukebox, return to Wellington for one highly anticipated show at The Opera House. 
Founded in New York, the trailblazing troupe made up of singers, dancers, and instrumentalists brings their brand new ‘The Future is Vintage’ tour to New Zealand shores. Concertgoers can expect to enter a parallel musical universe where modern-day earworms and iconic pop tracks are reimagined. Expect everything from 70s rock classics and 80s Britpop hits to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks. Dress in your vintage best for the full time-travel experience. 
“We’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee. 
Come and be swept away by the romance and swagger of the past while enjoying your favourite tunes from now.

Matariki on Moana is Back Whānau - held on Saturday 11th of July with KORA - HOLLIE SMITH - TAWAZ hitting the stage In Ōtaki , Kapiti only 45 mins drive from Wellington.
The gates open at 10am with Whakatau and kapahaka kicking off at 11am.
We have plenty of food, kawhe , markets, tamoko , art , clothing and music to help celebrate the Māori New year.
Tawaz the 2026 Māori Performer of the year will open a star studded line up , following is the amazing Hollie Smith to bring her Soulful Wahine Toa voice to the tepu.
only for the table to be flipped and smashed by the one and only KORA ... so come celebrate the Māori new year at the biggest concert on the Kāpiti Coast - Buy tickets now You can also preorder your hangi , 2026 matariki on moana merch including Tee , beanie , tote bag or get a mega matariki ticket which includes everything and better bang for your buck.

events.long_description:
NZ Filifest Cultural Group - Fundraising concert
Join us for an unforgettable evening of music, dance, and culture as we come together to support the talented performers of NZ Filifest on their journey to the Philippines. ✈️

This special fundraising concert will showcase the vibrant spirit of Filipino culture through dance.

Funds raised from this event will help our performers travel to the Philippines, where they will have the incredible opportunity to perform alongside a renowned Filipino dance group, strengthen cultural connections, and proudly represent Aotearoa on an international stage.

Doors open: 5.30pm

Show starts: 6.00pm

🎟️ Buy your concert tickets here: https://events.humanitix.com/nz-filifest-cultural-concert

💸 Bring cash to purchase food and drink and raffle tickets.

events.id: Lockie Bennett Trio-The Rogue & Vagabond-Rogue & Vagabond
events.name: Lockie Bennett Trio
events.venue: The Rogue & Vagabond
events.long_description:
Lockie Bennett is a jazz guitarist based in Wellington… but not for much longer! Catch him before he leaves for the UK.

The rhythm section features Auckland-based drummer George Brajkovich and Wellington stalwart bassist Elijah Mulheron. This new and exciting permutation of the trio toured the North Island earlier this year as part of a 12-stop tour across Australia and New Zealand.

The trio will be playing the music of Charlie Parker, Bud Powell, Tadd Dameron and other notable composers of the early 20th century.

$25 General Admission

Doors at 6:30pm
Music at 7pm

Poster by Dawn Wang

events.id: Postmodern Jukebox [WELLINGTON]-Opera House-All Events In
events.name: Postmodern Jukebox [WELLINGTON]
events.venue: Opera House
events.long_description:
Postmodern Jukebox [WELLINGTON]
The world’s foremost purveyors of time-twisting hits, Postmodern Jukebox, have announced their highly-anticipated return to Australia and New Zealand in June and July 2026!

Having previously performed multiple sold-out tours across Oceania, the trailblazing troupe of singers, dancers and instrumentalists will hit our shores with their brand new The Future is Vintage tour.

“In the midst of the AI hype all around us, some folks have gone so far as to proclaim that in the not-so-distant future, all of the music that we listen to will be AI generated – we’ll gladly take the other side of that wager,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee. “We’re humbly presenting our own unique vision of a spectacular future; one that is built upon the timeless musical genres of the past and the authentically human spirit of creativity that inspired them.”

Concertgoers can expect to enter a parallel musical universe where modern-day earworms and iconic pop tracks alike are reimagined. Expect everything from ’70s rock classics and ’80s Britpop hits, to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks. Dress in your vintage best, for the full time-travel experience!

A TEG Dainty presale begins on Thursday November 20 at 11am (local). Sign up for access:

https://www.tegdainty.com/tour/postmodernjukebox2026/

Tickets to general public go on sale on Friday November 21 at 11am (local).
