“In the midst of the AI hype all around us, some folks have gone so far as to proclaim that in the not-so-distant future, all of the music that we listen to will be AI generated – we’ll gladly take the other side of that wager,” says PMJ founder and arranger, Scott Bradlee.

Expect everything from ’70s rock classics and ’80s Britpop hits, to current chart-toppers, movie and video game soundtracks.

events.id: Luude | New Zealand Tour 2026-Shed 6, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: Luude | New Zealand Tour 2026
events.venue: Shed 6, Wellington
events.long_description:
Age Restriction: 16+ | Doors 7:30PM
Australian producer LUUDE is in the middle of an exciting global breakout moment. Arriving on the scene with back to back global hits "Down Under" and "Big City Life" (which he made for a bit of fun) which have sold millions of copies and earned multi-platinum certifications around the world - he followed this with the Bru-C collaboration "TMO" (UK Top 50) and the Moby sampling "Oh MY" (UK Top 100) - This run of singles makes him one of the highest selling artists in Drum and Bass across the last decade. 
In the short time since he's toured relentlessly across some of the biggest stages in dance music launched his new imprint "No Instruction" as a much needed outlet for more underground pursuits and built that brand over the last 6 months as a platform for new talent.
Luude's recording achievements also include an ARIA award for Best Dance Release, nomination for the Michael Gudinski Breakthrough Artist Award, "Best International Artist" at the 2022 Drum and Bass Awards, 3x BBC Radio 1 Hottest 100 Records and 9x Platinum, 2x Gold, 1x Silver Certifications. 


Live Nation VIP Package Terms and Conditions:
- Please note that the contact details provided at the point of purchase, email & mobile, are the details used to communicate all VIP information
- An email will be sent to the original purchaser of the tickets 2-4 business days before the event with timings and additional information.
Please contact Live Nation here if this is not received.
- A text will also be sent with the check in time & location the day before the show. Keep an eye out for a text on the day of the show in case of any changes or additional information.
Note if attending a show in Australia we are only able to send a text to Australian numbers. If attending a New Zealand show, we are only able to send a text to New Zealand numbers. At this time, we are unable to send to international numbers, outside of Australia & New Zealand.
Please contact us here if you have not received VIP Information.
- It is your responsibility as the purchaser to follow up information if it is not received
- VIP purchasers may need to arrive 1-3 hours before doors open to ensure delivery of all benefits
- Original purchasers name must be known in order to check-in and receive your benefits
- If a VIP Gift (e.g. laminate, drink voucher or merchandise item) is included in the VIP package you purchased, unless otherwise notified, VIP gifts will only be available for collection pre-show from the VIP check-in at the venue. VIP Gifts will not be dispatched after the show
- Given the dynamic nature of live events, we may need to make changes to parts of your VIP Package including seating and entry arrangements, start times, merchandise, food and beverage service arrangements and other things. When changes need to be made, where possible, we will endeavour to provide alternatives that overall have an equivalent value as your package
- If applicable, Early Entry refers to first access onto the floor, there is no sectioned off area (unless otherwise stated)
- If applicable, collectible laminate is commemorative only and does not gain or authorise access into the venue or backstage areas
- Choose carefully as we will not provide refunds for a change of mind or because you can’t make the event. Refunds and exchanges will only be made subject to ticketing terms and conditions, and applicable law, including the Australian or New Zealand Consumer Law
- For any questions regarding your concert ticket, please reach out to the point of purchase
- For any enquiries about your VIP Experience check out our FAQ’s or submit a request here

events.id: School Holiday Steam-Porirua Railway Station,1 Station Rd, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua-All Events In
events.name: School Holiday Steam
events.venue: Porirua Railway Station,1 Station Rd, Porirua City Centre, Porirua 5022, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
School Holiday Steam
Sunday 12th July 2026 - Break the School Holiday Boredom!
Join Steam Incorporated this School Holidays, as we invite you onboard our heritage train for a brilliant steam train ride for the whole family!

Trains are operating all Sunday between Paekakariki and Porirua, a beautiful scenic run along the Paekakariki Escarpment on the Kapiti Coast. In our run between Pukerua Bay and Paekakariki, we pass through 6 short tunnels! A section of railway line with origins dating back to 1886. A scenic train journey for the whole family, steam along the coast onboard our heritage train with our friendly team!

These trains are topped & tailed by one of our coal-fired steam locomotives, and our Da class diesel electric.

🎟️ Fares:
👨‍👩‍👧‍👦 Family - $99 - 2 Adults & 2 Children
👨 General Admission - $29 - Aged 3+
👶 Infant - Free - No allocated seat

📅 Departing Paekakariki Station at 8:45am, 10:15am, 11:45am, 1:45pm, & 3:15pm!

📅 Departing Porirua Station at 9:35am, 11:05am, 12:35pm, & 2:35pm

Note - The 12:35 pm departure will be stopped in Paekakariki for approximately 30mins.

Beat the School Holiday blues — our steam train awaits!

events.long_description:
The year 2026 will see the American exhibition basketball team, the Harlem Globetrotters, celebrate their 100-year anniversary. Wellington gets to celebrate with them when they visit in July. The team will take over TSB Arena and again revel in 100 years of thrills, laughs, and sheer basketball joy. If you aren’t already a fan, then sit back and watch their combined athleticism, sense of theatre, and comedy play out on the court. The gravity-defying dunks and dexterous tricks will have you roaring for more. 
The team has travelled the world visiting more than 120 countries and territories. Their 26,000 exhibition games continue to win new fans wherever they go. From the epic pre-game magic to unforgettable post-game interactions — come and experience their most legendary tour yet.

events.id: Shalakazap-Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Shalakazap
events.venue: Te Raukura ki Kapiti, Coastlands Theatre, 34a Raumati Road, Raumati, New Zealand 5032,34 Raumati Rd, Raumati Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Shalakazap
Tyro, apprentice MatheMagician, can't get his spells right. So, when Fire Chief Fleet arrives with a monster mathemagical problem, they need to ask the audience for help. A Riotous comedy, a transforming set and audience participation.

KidzStuff Theatre for Children brings the magic of live performance to your tamariki these July school holidays, a Wellington's family-friendly favourite since 1997. These 45-minute shows are packed with local Kiwi heart, inviting kids to enjoy a high-energy, theatrical escape. Make some memories and join the fun!

Written by the phenomenal Jenny Wake ONZM - a veteran of New Zealand Television and Theatre. In her television career, she wrote, directed and produced ‘Play School’, wrote for ‘Bumble’ and directed ‘What Now’. Jenny has travelled the word championing children's theatre. Currently the National Coordinator of Performing Arts and Young People of Aotearoa (PAYPA) and the International Association of Theatre for Children and Young People (ASSITEJ) New Zealand.

This play has had sell-out tours, and international presentations at Brisbane's Out of the Box festival and Vancouver-based Axis Theatre.

Ideal for children aged 2–10.

Under twos free on parent's lap.

--

For accessibility seating information and wheelchair space bookings please email or call 04)9740535 during office hours.

Door sales are only available if the event doesn't sell out prior.

events.id: PopRox Presents: An Improvised Nativity at The New Zealand Festival of Christmas-The Offering Greytown,65 Main St, Parkvale, New Zealand, Clareville-All Events In
events.name: PopRox Presents: An Improvised Nativity at The New Zealand Festival of Christmas
events.venue: The Offering Greytown,65 Main St, Parkvale, New Zealand, Clareville
events.long_description:
PopRox Presents: An Improvised Nativity at The New Zealand Festival of Christmas
PopRox is bringing their beloved improvised Christmas comedy show to the New Zealand festival of Christmas! PopRox is a professional improv theatre company from Wellington, specialising in slick, hilarious (and a little bit naughty) comedy shows, and for one night only their fully improvised retelling of the greatest story of them all is lighting up the stage at The Offering in Greytown.

A heavily pregnant Mary and a doting Joseph embark on a road-trip where they'll escape an evil king, come across three wise guys and just try to deliver this holy baby. Think "Life of Brian" with a heavy dose of kiwi humour! It's a night of laughs, heartfelt moments and great food with a dinner and a show at the gorgeous The Offering cafe. Enjoy an adults-only night out filled with laughter where these expert performers craft a story with a little help from you! Will Mary give birth in the back seat of Mazda Demio? Will the evil King Herod outlaw casual Friday? Will the three wise guys give the audience any helpful advice at all? There's only one way to find out. Book your tickets now and be sure to arrive early to enjoy drinks and a delicious dinner at The Offering.

“PopRox delivers on so many levels in its improvisation and comedic skill, with an ensemble of gifted and affecting actors... a performance that dazzles in its brilliance." Mitchell Manuel. Theatreview.

“All of the mahi these performers put in results in a show that is hilarious, engaging and leaves you wanting so much more.”
“What PopRox is doing here is nailing the tone and absurdity that the Nativity surely must have been filled with” Elliott Lam. Wellyott.

“it’s very clever...a rollicking good laugh to ease the pre-Christmas stress.” Sarah Catterall. The Post.

Show Running Information:

6:00pm- The Offering opens for dinner service
7:00pm- Main meal last orders taken
7:30pm- Show Begins
8:30pm (Approx.)- Show Finishes

PopRox is an ensemble of Wellington's top improv comedians, fresh off a two year residency at Circa Theatre and hit original shows at Wellington's "The Hannah" Theatre. Entering their 5th year as a company, PopRox's signature blend of compelling storytelling, hilarious one-liners, dazzling improvised music and slick production is sure to bring the warmth to Greytown this winter. Featuring decades of performing experience with award-winning comedians PopRox is an up and coming collective you don't want to miss.

It's a night of laughs, heartfelt moments and great food with a dinner and a show at the gorgeous The Offering cafe.
Will the three wise guys give the audience any helpful advice at all?

Live Music - Shaun Preston
Get ready for an unforgettable night of world-class music and entertainment as New Zealand’s favorite piano man, Shaun Preston, takes the stage live at The Jetty! Known for his incredible showmanship and infectious energy, Shaun effortlessly blends stunning vocal talent with masterclass piano skills. His dynamic repertoire ranges from soulful, acoustic dinner sets to high-octane, crowd-pleasing dance floor hits—and don’t be surprised if he finishes the night performing right on top of his signature white grand piano! Having performed globally from Los Angeles to London, and for elite audiences including royalty, Shaun brings a world-traveled performance directly to our stunning waterfront. Pair his electrifying, sing-along style with the beautiful coastal views and unmatched atmosphere at The Jetty for the ultimate night out. Spaces are strictly limited for this exclusive evening. This will be a fabulous night of music. We will not be offering bookings or table service for this event and a reduced food menu will be on offer. This is a ticketed event and tickets can be purchased via Eventfinder

events.id: KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced-Valhalla,154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced
events.venue: Valhalla,154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced

KING PARROT : Wellington NZ @ Valhalla w/- Slave Cadaver + Postnatal Abortion + Maced
FRIDAY JULY 17!
Tickets on sale now! https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/tour/33512/King-Parrot---Wellington.utr

King Parrot are pleased to announce their return to New Zealand to launch the blistering new album A Young Persons Guide to King Parrot. Described as a hostile and abrasive slab of sensory destroying anthems, the record features ten thoroughly crafted blasts through the psyche of this crazed Aussie quintet. Recorded at Melbourne’s Dangertone studios with engineer Luke Walton, it’s the first full-length album since 2017’s critically adored Ugly Produce offering and their first new music since 2020’s Holed Up In The Lair EP. Pulling the new songs together was a meticulously creative endeavour and the final product is the most complete, aggressive, and urgent the band has ever sounded.

The band have been in full throttle touring mode since the release in early 2025 and are currently out on a massive 39 date jaunt through the USA alongside Gwar and Soulfly. Much of the last year was spent touring the globe in support of Pantera all over Europe, the UK and the USA where King Parrot amassed performing exactly 100 shows for the year. New Zealand fans can expect a band that is firing on all cylinders and awaiting their first shows in NZ in many years, comments vocalist Matt Young "It's been a while between drinks but we were most certain that we must return to NZ this year and give you guys a dose of King Parrot! We cant wait to get back and see our kiwi mates and bring our brand of off the kilter grind, thrash and punk rock chaos, get your tickets now and well see you real soon!"

events.id: billy woods (Armand Hammer)-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: billy woods (Armand Hammer)
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
For billy woods, literate rhymes and inventive flows are littered through all his works. So much so, it’s hard to pinpoint his exact genre. But for the New York-based rapper, his music career began in the early 2000s, however, he was largely unknown until 2012 after the release of his solo album History Will Absolve Me. Since then, woods has gone onto release music under Armand Hammer – a collaborative project with ELUCID – and an array of solo albums like Today, I Wrote Nothing (2015), Maps (2023) and more.

His latest solo release – the 2025 album GOLLIWOG – has been deemed his most experimental yet. So much so, Pitchforkdescribed it as “a twisty horrorcore masterpiece where history and nightmares are indistinguishable” and a “perfect showcase for his craft”. It mixes horror, humour, surrealism and Afropessimism into the one work: think African zombies, time traveling trap cars, malevolent ragdolls and a dying Frantz Fanon

For one night only, Molewood Orchard transforms into a glowing wonderland during The New Zealand Festival of Christmas.

Step through the hidden door and follow illuminated pathways into the heart of the orchard, where hot air balloons are inflated and will glow, tethered to the ground and set to music.

Grab a bite to eat from one of the food trucks, toast marshmallows over the open fire, and gather around the bonfire as you soak up the magic of this unforgettable evening. Proudly supporting the Greytown Fire Brigade.

Postponement date is Friday 24 July.
If this event needs to be postponed, your tickets will automatically be transferred to the postponement date.

You will be notified on the day of any changes due to weather.

The Festive Night Glow is designed to go ahead in typical winter weather conditions. Please dress warmly and come prepared to enjoy the experience, rain or shine.

Sorry, no dogs allowed.

Tickets:
Adult : $20.00 each
Child: $12.50 each
Family (2 adults, up to 2 kids): $50.00 each
Family (2 adults, up to 3 kids): $55.00 each
Family (2 adults, up to 4 kids): $60.00 each

✨❄️ Brewtown Winter Craft Market ❄️✨

Step into a world of winter magic at the Brewtown Winter Craft Market! 🌨️🛍️ Join us on Sunday, 19 July 2026 from 9am – 2pm for a cozy, joy filled day celebrating creativity, community, and all things handmade.

Join us for a behind-the-scenes tour of Wellington's iconic St James Theatre and The Opera House. Discover hidden stories, architectural splendour, and rich theatrical history as you step behind the velvet curtain and explore two of the city's most beloved performance spaces. events.id: Mel die Storieverteller - LITTLE THEATRE, WELLINGTON - Internet Sensasie-Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Mel die Storieverteller - LITTLE THEATRE, WELLINGTON - Internet Sensasie
events.venue: Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Mel die Storieverteller - LITTLE THEATRE, WELLINGTON - Internet Sensasie
Wellington! Mel is opgewonde om julle in te sluit in haar toer - dit gaan n onvergeetlike middag wees, moet dit nie misloop nie!

In 2023 het nuuskierige gehore opgedaag om te sien hoe internetsensasie, Mel die Storieverteller in lewende lywe op ‘n verhoog lyk. Nou stroom almal na haar vertonings want Mel is toe skreeusnaaks in die regte lewe ook (vir mans en vroue). In haar derde komiese een-vrou vertoning, Internet Sensasie, gaan gehore uitvind dat Mel nou kan sing ook.

Mel deel uit eerstehandse ervaring stories vanuit die elektroniese oerwoud vol aandagsoekers en liggelowiges: Oom Riekert se groot fout, ‘n beplande vuisgeveg in ‘n taai plek met die naam KuduKraal, hoe ‘Mel die Storieverteller’ ontstaan het en die siekte op haar voorkop.

Die stories is vars, onverwags en bleddie belaglik, maar blykbaar alles waar, wel, so sê Facebook, en ons almal weet wat op die internet staan is ‘n feit, maar kan betwyfel word, tensy jy dit wil glo!

Vertoning duur: 80 minute (Geen pouse)
Styl: Komiese Storievertelstyl
Ouderdomsbeperking: Geen onder 14’s
Venue: Little Theatre, Lower Hut (at the Dowse Art Museum)
Adres: 2 Queens Drive, Hutt Central, Lower Hutt 5010, New Zealand
Kaartjieprys: NZ$40
Vertoning begin: 16h00
Kaartjies: www.meldiestorieverteller.co.za

events.long_description:
Negociants Fine Wine Tour 2026 - Wellington
A tasting of premium wines from Australia and New Zealand presented by Negociants New Zealand

About this Event

Join us for the 2026 Negociants Fine Wine Tour — an unforgettable evening celebrating a selection of wines from New Zealand and Australia.
Established in 1985, Negociants New Zealand has built a reputation for representing exceptional wineries from around the world. This exclusive event brings together an outstanding line-up of award-winning producers, giving you the rare opportunity to taste premium wines and meet some of the passionate personalities behind them.
Discover and enjoy wines from leading New Zealand wineries including Auntsfield, Black Barn, Cable Bay, FROMM, Greywacke, Misha’s Vineyard, Mt Beautiful, Nautilus Estate, Opawa, Palliser Estate, Pyramid Valley, Saint Clair Family Estate, Smith & Sheth, Two Paddocks and URLAR.
Experience iconic Australian producers including Brokenwood, Chaffey Bros, d’Arenberg, Jim Barry, Langmeil, Vasse Felix and Yalumba.
Throughout the evening, guests will enjoy:
• Unlimited tastings from all featured wineries
• Light refreshments
• Complimentary Antipodes Water
• A detailed tasting booklet to guide your experience
• A Riedel Ouverture Magnum wine glass presented in a take-home gift box
Whether you are a seasoned wine enthusiast or simply love discovering exceptional wines, this is a rare opportunity to explore an extraordinary portfolio all in one place.
Tickets are limited — secure yours now and experience the 2026 Negociants Fine Wine Tour.

events.id: Songs from the Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand-Corner Molesworth and Aitken Streets, Thorndon, New Zealand 6011,Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Songs from the Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand
events.venue: Corner Molesworth and Aitken Streets, Thorndon, New Zealand 6011,Cellar-Cafe, Molesworth St, Thorndon, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Songs from the Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand
Gareth Shute dives into his book Songs From the Shaky Isles to deliver a whirlwind overview of the past 100 years of popular music in Aotearoa.

Join us at the Library or online:

New Zealand Music: From pubs to playlists
From the era of ‘six-o'clock swill’ venue restrictions through to the grassroots 80s battle for radio airtime, then on to the seismic shift of the streaming age, Gareth Shute reveals how our music has always been intertwined with wider culture.

Gareth’s talk will be packed with fun, behind-the-scenes anecdotes about the wild characters who helped define the New Zealand music scene and will also be accompanied by some fascinating historic images from the Alexander Turnbull Library collection.

About the speaker
Gareth Shute writes regularly for the New Zealand music history website Audioculture | Iwi Waiata and is also the author of six books, including Hip Hop Music in Aotearoa, which won a national book award. He is also a musician who helps run Lil’ Chief Records and has toured overseas with two of their bands — The Brunettes, and The Ruby Suns. His current band is Thee Golden Geese.

Image: Gareth Shute. Book cover of Songs From The Shaky Isles: A Short History of Popular Music in New Zealand (Bateman Books, 2025).

events.id: A Long Exhale: Winter Somatic Breathwork-Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: A Long Exhale: Winter Somatic Breathwork
events.venue: Bhakti Lounge Wellington,175 Vivian Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
A Long Exhale: Winter Somatic Breathwork
Release built-up tension with The Long Exhale: 60-min somatic breathwork to calm your nervous system and find deeper rest.

About this Event


A Long Exhale: Winter Somatic Breathwork

The Long Exhale is a 60-minute somatic breathwork session designed for anyone feeling mentally full, emotionally stretched, or physically tense after months of constant movement and pressure.
You've been inhaling since January. It's time to breathe out.
This winter-focused session offers space for your nervous system to slow down, soften, and release, helping you reconnect with yourself in a gentle, grounded way.
This session may support you if you’re feeling:
Wired but exhausted
Emotionally or physically tense
Stuck in constant “go mode”
In need of deeper rest and nervous system support
Curious about breathwork in a safe, beginner-friendly space

What to expect
Guided breathwork and grounding practices
Gentle, body-led somatic breathing
Optional movement and nervous system release exercises
Rest, reflection, and integration time
A trauma-aware, consent-led environment with low-intensity options available

Benefits you may notice
Greater calm and clarity
Reduced tension in the body
Easier breathing and deeper rest
Emotional release and regulation
A stronger sense of inner steadiness

What to bring
Warm layers and socks
Water bottle
Optional blanket, eye mask, journal, and pen
Mats are provided for. You can bring your own for extra comfort

Pre-session guidance
Eat lightly 2 hours beforehand
Hydrate well throughout the day
Limit caffeine beforehand and avoid alcohol/substances for 24 hours
Arrive 10 minutes early

Important health information
Somatic breathwork may not be suitable for pregnancy, epilepsy, serious cardiovascular conditions, recent surgery/concussion, or severe psychiatric conditions. Please consult your healthcare provider if unsure.

Participant words
“After the session, I felt super light — like the weight of the world was no longer sitting on my shoulders. I felt a deep sense of peace and protection. An unforgettable healing experience.”
— Kaelah, New Zealand




Event followed by a plant-based dinner and dessert, all included in your ticket!



Meet Your Host: Lynn

Lynn Tiang is an Integrative Somatic Coach, breathwork facilitator, and Reiki practitioner based in Aotearoa, New Zealand. Her work combines somatic breathwork, Reiki, and life coaching to support nervous system regulation, emotional wellbeing, and grounded personal transformation in a practical, trauma-aware way.



Agenda


🕑: 05:45 PM - 06:00 PM
Arrive to Bhakti Lounge

🕑: 06:00 PM - 07:00 PM
A Long Exhale: Winter Somatic Breathwork
Host: Hosted by Lynn

🕑: 07:00 PM - 08:15 PM
Plant-based dinner and dessert!

events.id: Mortal Sin - NZ Tour - Wellington-154 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Mortal Sin - NZ Tour - Wellington
events.venue: 154 Vivian Street, New Zealand 6011,154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mortal Sin - NZ Tour - Wellington
FOR THE FIRST TIME IN NEW ZEALAND — AUSTRALIAN THRASH LEGENDS MORTAL SIN

Having toured alongside thrash heavyweights Metallica and Testament in their early years, Mortal Sin forged their legacy in the global metal scene from the very beginning. Formed in 1985, they carved their name into the foundations of thrash with a ferocious, high-energy sound that stood shoulder to shoulder with the genre’s elite.

After years of silence, the band has reformed — returning to the stage with the same intensity and aggression that made them icons of the era.

This marks their first ever shows on New Zealand soil, and the timing couldn’t be more fitting — celebrating 40 years of their debut album Mayhemic Destruction, a landmark release that helped define Australian metal and continues to influence generations.

Joining them on all dates are New Zealand’s own Bulletbelt, delivering their uncompromising, relentless live assault.

Three nights. No filler. Pure thrash.

23 July — Whammy, Auckland
24 July — Valhalla, Wellington
25 July — Churchills, Christchurch

events.id: Girls Rock the Boys - Petone-38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012-All Events In
events.name: Girls Rock the Boys - Petone
events.venue: 38 Fitzherbert St, Petone, Lower Hutt 5012
events.long_description:
Girls Rock the Boys - Petone
Girls Rock the Boys began as a spark - a half‑joke, half‑challenge tossed around in a rehearsal room: What would happen if women took on the big, swaggering, stadium‑sized rock anthems that were never written with them in mind? Not only as a tribute, but as a full‑throttle, joyous celebration of the songs that shaped generations.

The idea stuck. Because everyone knows those songs - the ones you belt in the car, the ones that must be played too loud, the ones you recognise from the very first riff. Whitesnake. Boston. Foreigner. Deep Purple. Journey. Rainbow. Led Zeppelin. Uriah Heep. The giants.

But what no one expects is how they feel when powerhouse women take the mic.

Girls Rock the Boys brings together a lineup of women with range, grit, soul, and the kind of stage presence that pulls you straight into the mosh pit. Behind them is a kickass live band built for exactly this music. Together, they tear into the classics - honouring the songs, laughing a little at the very male lyrics now being belted by women.

These anthems were always big enough and wild enough to belong to anyone brave enough to claim them. The result is a show that’s nostalgic and fresh at the same time - and loud in all the right ways.

Girls Rock the Boys is a celebration of musicianship, of women in rock and of the timeless songs that still shake the walls decades later. It’s the classics you love, reimagined.

When women take the mic, the classics hit different. Experience it live with Ashley Hale-Durham, Georgie Sullivan, Jude Madill, and Julie Lamb leading the charge, backed by the stellar band: Andrew Richardson (drums), Matt Fitzpatrick (bass), Aslan Rowlands (guitar), and Duncan Haynes (keys).

🎟️ Early bird tickets $20. Door sales $30.

It’s the classics you love, reimagined.
When women take the mic, the classics hit different.

events.id: Hermitude | Wellington-San Fran,171 Cuba Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Hermitude | Wellington
events.venue: San Fran,171 Cuba Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Hermitude | Wellington
Award-winning Australian electronic duo Hermitude bring their genre-bending live show to Aotearoa this July in support of their acclaimed new album EIGHT.

Australian electronic duo Hermitude have spent more than a decade shaping the future of electronic and hip hop music, earning acclaim for their boundary-pushing sound, global collaborations and explosive live performances. Consisting of Gusto and Dubs from the Blue Mountains west of Sydney, the pair have continually evolved their craft, blending hip hop roots with expansive electronic production that has resonated across headphones, dancefloors and festival stages worldwide. Their new record is inspired by all things uptempo, with a mix of techno, drum & bass and UK garage defining their new sound.

Award-winning multi-instrumentalists, Hermitude broke through internationally with their 2012 album HyperParadise, which won the Australian Music Prize, while the Flume remix of the title track amassed more than 200 million streams. Their follow-up album Dark Night Sweet Light debuted at #1 on the ARIA Charts and went Gold in New Zealand, with single The Buzz becoming a global hit and earning platinum success. Across releases including Pollyanarchy and latest album EIGHT, the duo have continued to push into new sonic territory while maintaining the experimental edge that has defined their career.

The collaborative spirit of Hermitude extends throughout their catalogue, having worked with artists including Kimbra, Flume, Bibi Bourelly and BJ The Chicago Kid, while also remixing tracks for acts such as ODESZA and Alison Wonderland.

With a globally celebrated catalogue and forward-thinking club sound, Hermitude’s return promises an electrifying live experience this July.

events.id: Vera Ellen - Heave Knows What Time-Meow,9 Edward Street, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Vera Ellen - Heave Knows What Time
events.venue: Meow,9 Edward Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Vera Ellen - Heave Knows What Time
Aotearoa Music Award-winning and Taite Award-winning artist Vera Ellen releases her new album Heaven Knows What Time. on May 1 via Flying Nun Records. Alongside the album release, Vera Ellen returns to Aotearoa in July for a six-date national tour in celebration of Heaven Knows What Time.

The title Heaven Knows What Time. arrived while Vera was lying on the top bunk of a house truck in Buenos Aires, nearly two years after writing the record and almost a year after completing the mix. After multiple working titles, it surfaced with clarity and immediacy - less a decision than a moment of recognition. It reflects the album’s central tension: frustration and surrender to forces outside of control. As Vera describes it, creativity cannot be demanded - it can be nurtured, but ultimately arrives in its own time, “like a baby”.

The album was written during a two-week songwriting residency in Greytown, awarded through the NZ Pacific Studio Artist Residency Programme. The residency provided space and solitude away from touring and performance, culminating in a performance at Studio 73 for fellow residents and members of the local community.
‍

In her residency application, Vera wrote: “After the heaviness of Ideal Home Noise, I feel it is pressing that I now create an uplifting record that has a guiding message of hope, but from a matured perspective.” While that intention is present, Heaven Knows What Time. resists neat resolution, moving through themes of love, joy, self-assurance, humour, moodiness, and heartbreak - positioning Vera as an observer of both inner and outer worlds.

Alongside extensive touring in Aotearoa — including the 2025 Laneway Festival — Vera has performed internationally with her band, including a 14-date tour of China, a sold-out run in Australia supporting Crowded House, appearances at New Colossus Festival and SXSW (Austin and Sydney), and a full UK/EU support tour with Yumi Zouma. She is set to support Aldous Harding on her full UK/EU tour and perform at The Great Escape in Brighton. The Guardian described her performance as: “Arising New Zealand empress Vera Ellen and band were a revelation, a shimmering reminder that pop’n’roll can still nudge one’s soul.”

Presented by: The Aotearoa Jazz Orchestra
The Aotearoa Jazz Orchestra presents: Fiesta! Fiesta! 🔥
This winter, the heat arrives early.
Join the Aotearoa Jazz Orchestra for a spectacular night of rhythm, colour, and irresistible Latin groove as the country’s national jazz orchestra joins forces with an all-star lineup of Aotearoa’s finest musicians from the Latin music community.
From the pulse of Afro-Cuban rhythms to the fire of salsa and the lush sounds of Latin jazz, Fiesta! Fiesta! is a celebration of the rich musical traditions of Latin America — brought vividly to life by the extraordinary talent living here in Aotearoa.
Expect soaring brass, driving percussion, and music that will lift you out of the depths of winter and onto your feet.
Warm the heart. Move the body. Celebrate the music.

events.name: Live Music - Cammy
events.venue: Plimmerton Boating Club,66 Moana Road, Plimmerton, New Zealand, Porirua
events.long_description:
Live Music - Cammy
Cammy is a dynamic pop and R&B performer known for his soulful vocals, smooth guitar work, and magnetic stage presence. Based in Kāpiti, he’s become a favourite across bars, restaurants, and weddings nationwide, delivering cover sets that blend the best pop hits and crowd favourites from every era.
With rich vocal tones, a lush falsetto, and an impressive range, Cammy brings a fresh energy to each performance—whether it’s a chilled afternoon session or a packed night of singalongs. His sets are built around connection, rhythm, and feel-good moments, ensuring every audience leaves with a smile and a song in their head.
Alongside his extensive cover repertoire, Cammy also performs original material, showcasing his artistry and versatility beyond the stage.

events.id: Mastering Paella Workshop-Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand,Level 2, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Mastering Paella Workshop
events.venue: Le Cordon Bleu New Zealand,Level 2, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Mastering Paella Workshop
Learn to authentically create Paella by Spanish Embassy Special guest chef, Edrick Corban-Banks, from Casa Paella.

About this Event

In collaboration with the Embassy of Spain in New Zealand and Casa Paella, we are proud to offer a one-day workshop taught by the award-winning chef, Edrick Corban-Banks.
Chef Edrick is the owner of Casa Paella, Casa Paella holds the official ICEX “Restaurants from Spain” quality seal, recognising its place within Spain’s international network of restaurants committed to authenticity, quality, and the promotion of Spanish gastronomy. This is rare opportunity to learn the culture and traditional Valencian techniques of Paella from a master artisan.



You will spend the day in Teams of 2 as Chef Edrick guides you through the creation of the award winning Arroz con Salmón.
Date: 25th July
Time: 9:30am – 3pm (light lunch is included with a glass of Albarino white wine)
Location: Level 2, 52 Cuba Street, Te Aro, Wellington
Allergies: Ingredients in this class contain the following allergens: Fish, Shellfish/Crustaceans, Molluscs, and Sulphates. This dish will be Gluten Free.
You Will Create:
Arroz con Salmón (Salmon Paella featuring Ōra King Salmon)
First developed in Tasmania using Petuna ocean-grown salmon and awarded the
Tetsuya Wakuda “Best Plate” prize at Taste of Tasmania, this paella is designed as a
technique-led masterclass in stock construction, rice absorption, and heat
management. A concentrated seafood fumet—prepared from shellfish and fish
frames—builds depth and structure, allowing the character of premium local salmon
to remain the centre of the dish.
Course Details: During the workshop, you will learn the following skills:
Prepare a fresh seafood stock using fish and shellfish frames
Build flavour through sofrito, saffron, paprika, and olive oil
Cook Valencian paella rice correctly so each grain remains separate
and full of flavour
Manage heat, evaporation, timing, and liquid levels
Understand the difference between cooking “rice with seafood”
and creating a true Mediterranean rice dish
Develop the prized socarrat — the golden crust at the base of the paella
Finish and serve your paella in the traditional shared style
Course Includes: All ingredients and equipment supplied, at the end of the class, participants will sit down together and enjoy the paella they have created with a glass of Albarino white wine.

What is a Le Cordon Bleu Workshop? A Le Cordon Bleu Workshop is a hands-on culinary training session delivered in our professional teaching kitchens. Be personally guided by world class chefs and taught key techniques step-by-step, with chef feedback throughout. This workshop includes a light lunch, and is designed to give you skills you can repeat confidently at home or in a professional setting.
Contact Details: +64 4 472 9800 |

events.id: Country Day Party: Wellington 🤠-Eva Pub - Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Country Day Party: Wellington 🤠
events.venue: Eva Pub - Wellington
events.long_description:
Country Day Party: Wellington 🤠
Get your cowboys hats ready!
Join us for an unforgettable day filled with the chart-topping tracks of Morgan Wallen, Dolly Parton, and many more, combining the best of the old school and modern country hits. Whether you’re a die-hard country fan or just looking for good vibes, this is the ultimate party. 🤠🌵💃What to Expect:
🎶 Nonstop Modern Country Hits: Sing along to all your favorite songs by artists like Luke Combs, and Shania Twain.
👢Boot Scootin’ Fun: Hit the dance floor and show off your moves with fellow country music lovers.
📷 Instagram-Worthy Moments: Capture the day with scenic backdrops and photo ops.
🌟 Good Times and Great Tunes: Soak in the feel-good energy with a mix of country charm and party spirit.

Perfect for Stag/Hen/Birthday parties or if you just fancy a day out with all your mates! 🪩

Expect to hear music from:Johnny Cash ⭐️ Dolly Parton ⭐️ Luke Combs ⭐️ Morgan Wallen ⭐️ Garth Brooks ⭐️ Willie Nelson ⭐️ Chris Stapleton ⭐️ Hank Williams ⭐️ Patsy Cline ⭐️ John Denver ⭐️ Shania Twain ⭐️ Shaboozey ⭐️ Kenny Rogers ⭐️ Tim McGraw ⭐️ Faith Hill ⭐️ Keith Urban ⭐️ Carrie Underwood ⭐️ Blake Shelton ⭐️ Lainey Wilson ⭐️ Luke Bryan ⭐️ Billy Ray Cyrus⭐️ Megan Moroney ⭐️ Kacey Musgraves ⭐️ Eric Church ⭐️ Zach Bryan

FAQ’S
🗣️ Why do the tickets come in different price tiers?
Whilst there is no physical difference. There is only a limited amount of tickets we can sell. Therefore we operate a festival style ticketing model. The earlier you buy your ticket the cheaper it will be.
🗣️ Do you do group discount? Stag/Hen etc
Yes, we offer a discounted ticket price for groups of 10 or more. These tickets can be found listed as the “Big Group Package!” above, orders for these tickets are restricted to a minimum of 10.
🗣️ Will there be more tickets available if the event is sold out?
Once this event is sold out, there will not be any additional tickets released. In addiction to this we will be unable to sell them at the door due to maximum capacity and health and safety regulations.
🗣️ Is the venue accessible?
Our events take place at a variety of locations across the UK and Ireland. As each varies please ensure to check the specific venue's website for accessibility information.
🗣️ Can I resell my tickets?
Yes, if you are unable to attend an event you are welcome to sell your tickets on. We do not use names on tickets as a condition for entry.
🗣️ Are your events 18+?
All of our events are strictly 18+. A valid photographic ID may be requested on entry.
🗣️ I have more questions?
If you have additional questions and wish to contact us directly, don’t hesitate to drop us a DM on either Facebook (Daytime Disco) or Instagram (@daytimediscoevents)
Refund Policy:
In-line with normal consumer standards for ticket purchases, tickets for Daytime Disco events are non-refundable, on the condition that we not moved the event or canceled it. If you are no longer be able to attend the event, you are of course welcome to gift or sell your tickets onwards as we do not use names on the tickets as a condition of entry.

Disclaimer:
The event listed here is purely for informational purposes. All the details such as venue, date/time, artist information, etc., are subject to change.
This event is not associated with or sponsored by AllEvents, but it may be of interest to you. You can explore more such events with us at AllEvents.

Jordan Luck Band • San Fran, Wellington • NZ Winter Tour

Packed with all the classic Exponents hits you know & love…. from ‘Who Loves Who The Most?’ to ‘Victoria’…. ‘Why Does Love Do This To Me?’ to ‘I'll Say Goodbye’….

events.id: Kevin Fraser Live in Wellington NZ • Decadance 2026-Harbourside Function Venue,4 Taranaki Street Wharf Cnr Cable & Taranaki St, Wellington, New Zealand-All Events In
events.name: Kevin Fraser Live in Wellington NZ • Decadance 2026
events.venue: Harbourside Function Venue,4 Taranaki Street Wharf Cnr Cable & Taranaki St, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Kevin Fraser Live in Wellington NZ • Decadance 2026
Welcome to DECADANCE — a comedy celebration marking 10 years of Kevin Fraser on stage.

Born in 1990, Kevin’s story as an entertainer took an unlikely turn in 2016, when he began filming outrageous accents and characters from a humble container shed in Tasmania, Australia. Those viral videos sparked a journey that moved from small Aussie shows to stages across Africa, Europe, the Middle East, and beyond.

Each year added new chapters: Australia in 2016, more countries joining in 2017, a global explosion by 2018, and a steady climb through 2019, 2020, 2021… all the way to 2025 — with every tour, every city, and every character shaping the phenomenon Kevin is today.

Now, in 2026, Kevin rewinds the clock to bring fans the ultimate throwback party: a decade of chaos, culture, and comedy reimagined for the stage. Expect the nineties nostalgia that shaped him, the container-shed characters that started it all, and the world-travelled stories that turned everyday madness into global punchlines.

This isn’t just a comedy show — it’s a time capsule cracked open, a decade of laughs uncorked, and an invitation to dance through Kevin’s first ten years in comedy.

Event Details:

Date: Sat 25th July 2026

Location: Harbourside Centre (Cable Room)

4 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand

events.id: Communication English - Level 3-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Communication English - Level 3
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Communication English - Level 3
By the end of the course, you will: 
• Have increased your confidence in speaking English
• Be able to understand native speakers more
• Have improved your vocabulary and grammar
• Understand and use informal spoken English
• Understand and describe more about local systems and customs
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents but to enrol you must contact the CEC office (04 385 8919) for an interview. There are limited free places available and you may need to go on a waiting list. You'll also need to come to our office and take a placement test to make sure that the course is the right level for you.
There may be some places on the course for people who are not NZ residents or citizens but you must pay the course fee of $1049. 
This course is subsidised by the Tertiary Education Commission and you must provide evidence of your NZ residency or citizenship (if applicable).
Please note that there is no student parking at the High School during the day. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Beginners
This is an introduction to the wonderful, organic material that is clay. You'll receive a comprehensive overview to working with clay which will cover all steps from clay preparation and re-cycling through to glazing the finished work. You'll learn to throw basic forms with group demonstration and one-to-one instruction.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
•  Wedge and re-cycle clay 
•  Demonstrate the basics of working on a pottery wheel 
•  Centre clay on the wheel 
•  Open a centred piece of clay on wheel 
•  Pull clay up to form basic beaker/cylinder shape 
•  Demonstrate the correct use of prepared glazes 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Handbuilding Basics
Learn how to prepare and alter clay:  pinch, coil, slab, extruder and roller use.  Understand shrinkage and clay care, joining techniques, drape and press moulding, mark making, slip making and application - as well as tetrahedron, cone and cylinder production
The techniques learnt during the making will provide an excellent springboard to creating a wide array of ceramic objects in the future.
The emphasis will be on learning techniques and honing those skills and by the end of the 8 sessions you will have completed three works which will be fired and glazed.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
All materials included in course fee. For more information about Wellington Potters, click on this link. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Pottery - Throwing: Next Step-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Pottery - Throwing: Next Step
A follow-on course for those who have some experience of throwing on the wheel and would like some guidance to take their skills to the next stage.
You’ll learn how to “turn” your pots with more confidence and be able to make a variety of small to medium-sized projects. Basic glazing of your pots will also be covered.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Turn a pot
• Make a lidded container
• Make a rounded vase
• Form a reasonable sized bowl
• Do basic glazing
All materials included in course fee. Wear old clothes and footwear suitable for a workshop environment and bring an old towel.
Pottery courses are held at Wellington Potters' Association, 130 Grant Road, Thorndon.  All our courses are scheduled up to a year in advance which means the at times we may need to make changes to the original schedule.  Please make sure you check your emails regularly in case of any changes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Assertiveness - Stand Up for Yourself
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $79 with ID, all others pay $149).
An opportunity to learn basic practical skills to assist you in learning to communicate in an assertive healthy manner. Come prepared to participate in class, as well as be working on assigned activities between classes. We'll work both individually and as a group using this environment to help us grow as we learn.
  By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
Use a strategy to identify your own feelings, and to express what you would like
Have an increased understanding of your own and other peoples’ anger, dealing with that in a more assertive manner
Use strategies to allow you not to be manipulated by other people
Speak in a way that commands respect and to begin to learn how to say “no”
Begin interacting more effectively in your personal relationships and the workplace
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Drawing and Painting for Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Drawing and Painting for Beginners
Explore a range of painting and drawing techniques from ink and watercolor to impasto and realism using acrylic paint. Each week we will explore a different medium and subject to expand your knowledge, and confidence in your own artistic ability.
Bring along your own sketchbook (A4 or larger) All other course materials will be provided.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Use  a variety of painting and brush techniques in your own work
Understand how light moves around an object
Blend and shade using wet and dry media
Mix and apply colour to your painting and drawings
Further develop your own painting and drawing style
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: French - Introductory 3
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
French - Introductory 3
For those who have done Introductory 2 or equivalent, this level assumes confidence in using basic spoken language (e.g. greetings, directions, use of numbers, shopping, ordering food and drink and talking about the weather).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Talk about your home: rooms, furniture, housework
• Describe people/family members
• Talk about your daily routine
• Organise an outing: invite / accept / refuse an invitation (giving reasons) 
• Talk about past events using the perfect tense (le passé composé) of verbs conjugated with “avoir” and “être”
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.id: Sewing - All Levels-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Sewing - All Levels
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sewing - All Levels
All levels welcome! Beginning sewers will learn basic skills and techniques and those with some experience will improve their skills and learn tips and tricks.
Beginners - If you're a beginner, you'll start by making a lined bag, which teaches all the basic skills involved. After that you'll move on to a project of your choice, in consultation with the tutor.  Please bring: 1 metre of firm cotton fabric for your bag outer and 1 metre of a lighter cotton fabric for the lining. You'll also need to bring scissors, a Bernina bobbin (this can be purchased from the CEC office on the day of the course for $7), pins, thread to match your outer fabric, an 18 cm zip and tape measure to the first session.
 If you're a returning student, or have already mastered the basic skills, you'll work on your own project so please bring along whatever you're currently working on (or something new) and your sewing kit. 
Please note PDF patterns are not suitable for this course unless you are very experienced in using them;  we strongly recommend patterns from the following companies:  Simplicity, Butterick, New Look or Quicksew.
By the end of the course, you'll have been introduced to basic skills in dressmaking, if beginners, or further skills for the more experienced.
You'll be able to:
• Use all sewing machine equipment and demonstrate basic sewing techniques (basic seams and finishes)
• Begin to read a commercial pattern and select appropriate materials for task
• Cut out and construct a garment using techniques learned
• (In addition), use a variety of advanced sewing techniques e.g. rolled hems, various zip insertions (invisible and standard), make buttonholes or line garments. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Writing a Novel-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Writing a Novel
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Writing a Novel
An introductory course for those keen to start writing a novel. 
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Generate ideas for writing your novel
• Create convincing characters
• Write compelling dialogue
• Develop a strong structure for your story
• Follow the process towards completing your first draft 
Bring pen and paper.
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers' work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Vietnamese Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Vietnamese Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Vietnamese Street Food
Join our experienced Vietnamese cook and find out how to create a range of traditional dishes from different parts of Vietnam that you can eat for breakfast, lunch and dinner or at any time of day. Recipes include delicious wraps, pancakes, beef noodle soup, fried rolls with rice noodles and much more.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Define what characterises Vietnamese cooking and key ingredients
• Recognise and make popular street food from North to South of Vietnam
• Identify retail outlets which supply Vietnamese ingredients
• More confidently follow recipes provided and prepare those recipes with supervision
• Prepare dishes for home eating or entertaining
All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dish cloth, tea towels and containers to take food home. 
Note:  
Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Understanding and Supporting Your Neurodivergent Child: A Practical Parent Course-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Understanding and Supporting Your Neurodivergent Child: A Practical Parent Course
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course is free for NZ citizens and residents.  Please enrol online and bring photo ID when attending the workshop.
If you are not a NZ citizen or resident, a fee of $120 applies and you must phone the office on 04 385 8919 to register.
This course is designed for parents and caregivers of neurodivergent tamariki who want practical, evidence-informed strategies to better support their child’s wellbeing, behaviour, and participation at home and school.
Across three sessions, we will explore the foundations of neurodiversity, including different neurotypes, strengths-based perspectives, and neuroaffirming language. You will gain insight into sensory processing, regulation, and the critical role of connection and co-regulation in supporting your child.
The course also provides guidance on navigating Aotearoa New Zealand’s health, education, and disability systems, including how to access supports and advocate effectively for your child. We will explore common challenges such as school refusal, burnout, and meltdowns, with practical strategies for prevention, in-the-moment support, and recovery.
There will be opportunities for discussion, reflection, and shared learning, as well as a Q&A-style panel session incorporating both professional and lived experience perspectives.
This is a group-based learning environment (not individual therapy), with an emphasis on practical tools, connection, and empowerment.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Night Photography - In the Dark-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Night Photography - In the Dark
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Night Photography - In the Dark
This course gives you the opportunity to explore and sharpen both your technical and creative skills, so you have the ability and vision to capture the enchanting world that resides in the darkness.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Identify the equipment needed to take photographs at night
• Learn fundamentals of photography composition and editing of night images
• Identify how to use technical photographic tools in a night time context
• Explore techniques that allow you to develop a more creative approach to picture making including: light painting, intentional camera movement and light trails
• Recognise the importance of light and colour in night photography and develop your ability to use it to make dynamic images
Course includes a couple of evening field trips (tutor will advise of suitable dates once course is under way).
You must bring a tripod and, if you have one, a neutral density filter and cable release remote. You need a fully manual DSLR or mirrorless camera and an intermediate understanding of camera functions. Not suitable for point and shoot cameras.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Storytelling On Stage-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Storytelling On Stage
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Storytelling On Stage
Do you love storytelling shows like The Moth? Come join the growing art of live non-fiction storytelling. Learn how to find ideas from your life, craft a compelling narrative, and deliver your personal story in a confident and entertaining way.
 Website description:
Live storytelling is an artform unto itself, requiring different skills and techniques from standup comedy or prose writing. In this four-part workshop, you’ll learn:
- Creative writing exercises and daily practices to to find meaningful stories from your life
- Tools for crafting a compelling personal narrative
- Techniques to build suspense, drama, and humour into your stories
- Stagecraft and performance skills to heighten and polish your delivery
By the end of the class you'll have
- One or more polished 5-10 minute stories to showcase on stage
- Dozens of ideas to expand upon for future stories
- Writing practices to improve your life as a creative
- Less stage fright and more confidence as a speaker
 NOTE: You will be telling true stories from your life in front of the class, building to an (optional) LIVE graduation storytelling showcase at a local venue!
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.description:
Storytelling On Stage Do you love storytelling shows like The Moth?
Come join the growing art of live non-fiction storytelling.
Website description: Live storytelling is an artform unto itself, requiring different skills and techniques from standup comedy or prose writing.
In this four-part workshop, you’ll learn: - Creative writing exercises and daily practices to to find meaningful stories from your life - Tools for crafting a compelling personal narrative - Techniques to build suspense, drama, and humour into your stories - Stagecraft and performance skills to heighten and polish your delivery By the end of the class you'll have - One or more polished 5-10 minute stories to showcase on stage - Dozens of ideas to expand upon for future stories - Writing practices to improve your life as a creative - Less stage fright and more confidence as a speaker NOTE: You will be telling true stories from your life in front of the class, building to an (optional) LIVE graduation storytelling showcase at a local venue!
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel.

events.id: Distilled Comedy-The Bond Store,3C Magrath Ave, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu-All Events In
events.name: Distilled Comedy
events.venue: The Bond Store,3C Magrath Ave, Paraparaumu Beach, Paraparaumu 5032, New Zealand, Paraparaumu
events.long_description:
Distilled Comedy
The monthly merriment kicks off on July 29th — and we bring light to the depth of winter.

Welcome to the comedy open mic that politely asks structure to wait outside while we set fire to it.

This is the Home of The Jar — our slightly unhinged, deeply democratic system where the audience randomly selects who goes up next. No lineups. No pecking order. No sacred cows. Just brave humans, questionable decisions, and whatever name comes out of the jar. It’s equal parts comedy show, social experiment, and mild psychological thriller.

You might see a first-timer stepping up for their debut. You might see a seasoned comic testing fresh material. You might see someone absolutely smash it. You might see someone realise, in real time, that jokes are harder than they look. That’s the magic.

Come in from the cooler autumn air, grab some food, sip a cocktail, and settle in. The room fills fast, the energy builds quicker, and being there from the start means you’re fully invested when the jar starts causing trouble.

If you love comedy in its rawest, most unpredictable form — this is your night.
If you’ve ever thought, “I could probably do that” — the jar would love to meet you.

Cheap laughs. Big vibes. Absolute chaos.

events.id: Creative Writing - A Tasting Plate-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Writing - A Tasting Plate
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Writing - A Tasting Plate
How often have you said, ‘I wish I could write’? There are many different kinds of writing. Starting is easy so why not give it a go by joining this creative writing ‘tasting plate’.
Each week we’ll focus on a different area of fiction or creative non-fiction. By the end of the programme, you’ll have tried your hand at writing a short story, a narrative poem, a travel memoir, a personal essay drawing from your life, an interview article, a story for children, a short film script and a piece of historical fiction.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Create works of fiction 
• Develop creative non-fiction text 
• Explore the natural inner spirit of writing 
• Discover your unique writer’s voice 
• Reflect on your own experiences in writing 
• Investigate different writing strategies
• Discover where your personal writing passions sit and what works for you.
Bring pen and paper or a laptop. 
This course is designed to help you improve your skills, to develop your unique writer's voice and to encourage you to take risks on the page. It is not a course where you'll be asked to read your work out loud, critique other writers work, or hand in your writing to the tutor. This is a safe place where you will be supported to do what writers do best - write.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Exploring our Shared Humanity-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Exploring our Shared Humanity
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Join experienced psychotherapist Rohit Grover for a six-week course that invites you to look beyond the surface of everyday life. In these two-hour sessions, we will explore the core tenets of existential therapy, moving away from the conventional focus on self and hedonism. Instead, we'll grapple with life's big questions:
What does it truly mean to live a value-driven life?
Do our personal values matter, or is the search for meaning just an illusion?
What is the "being" part of "human being" all about?
Through guided discussions, reflective exercises, and insights from classic existential thinkers, Rohit will help you navigate this profound territory. This isn't about finding easy answers. It's about developing the courage to ask the right questions, to confront the realities of freedom, responsibility, and meaning, and to discover a more authentic way of living.
This course is for anyone ready to step off the treadmill and begin a deeper exploration of what it means to be alive. Come and find out what it means to truly exist
Upon completion of this course, participants will be able to:
Differentiate between a life driven by external pressures or fleeting pleasures and one rooted in personal values and authentic meaning.
Identify and critically examine the core existential givens of human existence, such as freedom, responsibility, isolation, and the search for meaning.
Articulate a personal understanding of what it means to "be" a human being, moving beyond a purely functional or biological definition.
Arrive at sensing rather than thinking. Sensing this existence, this dance of life. Widen your view of existence.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Mindfulness Meditation-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Mindfulness Meditation
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Mindfulness Meditation
Meditation can help us with life’s challenges and is a wise way to work with strong emotions and stress, as well as enlivening the ordinary times. It’s simple and doesn’t need any special resources. Often learning to meditate is presented as something esoteric and mysterious but really it should be a straightforward thing based on our everyday experience.
Come along to this course and learn how to put a meditation practice in place in your life. You’ll be introduced to some different meditation and mindfulness techniques and get an insight into the modern psychology and the ancient wisdom that supports a meditation practice.
The four sessions will provide time to practise different styles of meditation, lots of hints about a fruitful approach to take to set up a regular practice in your life and opportunities to discuss progress with others in the group.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Describe some ways in which modern psychology and ancient wisdom support meditation
Understand what you need to do to make time for meditation in your life
Describe practical tips to make meditation fruitful
Practise different kinds of guided and silent meditation
Find resources (books, on-line information, other classes and retreats) to support your meditation
Bring a notepad and pen. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Qi Gong-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Qi Gong
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Qi Gong
Qigong has both psychological and physical components and involves the regulation of the mind, breath, and body’s movement and posture.
In most forms of qigong:
Breath is slow, long, and deep. Breath patterns may switch from abdominal breathing to breathing combined with speech sounds.
Movements are typically gentle and smooth, aimed for relaxation.
Mind regulation includes focusing one’s attention and visualization.
Dynamic (active) qigong techniques primarily focus on body movements, especially movements of the whole body or arms and legs. Meditative (passive) qigong techniques can be practiced in any posture that can be maintained over time and involve breath and mind exercises, with almost no body movement.
Wear loose, comfortable clothing and bring a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Crochet for Calmness-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Crochet for Calmness
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Crochet for Calmness
The repetitive movements of crochet help the brain make more serotonin, which can decrease tension and feelings of depression. We’ll move through all the steps from the starting slipknot to reading patterns and diagrams and making your first crochet projects.
You’ll learn to make a granny square, change colours and how to increase and decrease. You’ll also find out more about the notations and abbreviations of crochet stitches so you can make those awesome crochet projects you've been waiting to make!
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Discuss the benefits of crochet for relaxation and alleviation of mood
• Read a basic crochet pattern and recognise crochet terminology
• Choose yarns and hooks for projects
• Use a range of stitches with confidence
• Complete at least one project of your choice (granny square, keychain, garden decoration)
Suitable for all beginners, both left and right-handers and those who may have struggled to learn to crochet. Initial materials provided but please bring along any yarn or crochet hooks you would like to work with.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

By the end of the course, you'll be able to: 
• Greet people and introduce yourself (and others) at a basic level
• Use expressions for basic everyday communication
• Ask and answer questions about people’s names, nationalities, family, work situation, likes and dislikes (both formal and informal)

• Become more familiar with the cultural aspects related to these topics

events.id: How to be a DJ - An Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: How to be a DJ - An Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
How to be a DJ - An Introduction
Always wanted to try being a DJ but not sure where to begin? Experienced and professional DJ Shan (Shannon Chng-Rawiri) will teach you all about the technical aspects, equipment and terminology, as well as the industry in general, and enable you to put together a confident short performance.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Gain an understanding of the work content and career opportunities in this industry
Learn the difference between what a DJ is required to deliver in the following situations: Mobile, Club, Radio, Turntablist and Hobby
Participate in various practical activities: Slip Cueing and Baby Scratch, basic beat matching and song structures
Gain a good understanding of the equipment used and terminology including: - Tables: pitch control - Mixer: crossfader, input faders, EQ, gain, cue system - Needles: back tracking - Slip Mats
Learn about Counting Bars and Matching Tempos - The difference between a Bar, Phrases and BPM - Techniques to Count, Hand position and movement etc
Confidently present a 5 minute performance in class
The course will be held at DJ Shan’s studio at Shop 21, 73 Cuba Street, Wellington.  Bring headphones, a USB with your favourite music and a laptop, if you have one. Limited to eight participants.
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Photography - Beginners
If you want to get the best results from your camera, you need to learn the fundamental skills and theory of good photography. 
Bring your fully manual-capable (D)SLR or mirrorless camera and owner's manual (if you have one) to the first session.
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:
• Operate your camera in manual mode with more confidence 
• Use your camera controls such as shutter speed and aperture with confidence 
• Determine a correct exposure 
• Present and discuss images in a group environment 
• Demonstrate technical concepts through their practical application
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
NOTE:  Compact cameras are not suitable for this course. Includes a Saturday field trip at a date and time to be advised - check individual course details. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel.
