events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
This course helps small business owners and entrepreneurs unlock the full potential of social media. Each week we’ll cover practical steps to review your current channels, set up strong profiles, and create a tailored content plan that works for your business. You’ll learn how to choose the right platforms, use hashtags effectively, create engaging posts, select visuals that connect with your audience, and understand the role of AI tools. By the end, you’ll walk away with a clear and actionable plan to grow your social media channels and attract the right customers.
Whether you’re starting out or already running a business, a strong social media presence is no longer optional. The challenge is knowing where to focus your efforts when every platform seems to demand something different.
This course is designed to cut through the noise and give you a roadmap that fits your business. Together, we’ll:
Review the main platforms and choose the right ones for your goals
Tailor your profiles so they look professional and trustworthy
Build a realistic content plan you can actually stick to
Learn how to use hashtags and trends to boost reach
Explore how AI can help (and where it can’t)
Understand what visuals work best and how to avoid common pitfalls
Discuss tips for creating engagement and growing a loyal following
The focus is practical and hands-on. Each week you’ll apply the ideas directly to your own business, so by the end of the course you’ll have a solid social media strategy ready to put into action.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.id: Dita Von Teese-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Dita Von Teese
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
With her signature mystery and old-Hollywood glamour, Dita Von Teese is a global icon in the world of burlesque. She’s bringing an exciting new show to The Opera House in August. ‘Nocturnelle’ is a sensual spectacle inspired by the golden age of magic. It blends sophisticated striptease with the art of illusion for an unforgettable evening. Dita is accompanied by an ensemble of hand-picked neo-burlesque artists, so expect to be dazzled. 
Dita Von Teese is considered to have set the benchmark for American burlesque. The exotic and provocative art form has advanced under her unrivalled artistry and flair. The performer, creative director, and choreographer has sold out some of the world’s grandest venues, from Shanghai to New York. She is also a designer, collaborator, and entrepreneur. She’s forayed into fashion, beauty, music, and publishing.

Join us for the BNI Wellington/Wairarapa Big Breakfast!
One of the region’s most energising business events, bringing together a powerful network of professionals, business owners, and decision-makers.
This isn’t just another networking event. It’s a high energy, results focused morning designed to create real connections, spark new opportunities, and showcase the strength of the BNI community.
Whether you’re looking to grow your business, expand your network, or simply experience the power of BNI in action, this is the room to be in.
Come ready to connect, be inspired, and leave with opportunities you didn’t walk in with.
Doors open at 6:30am for a 7:00am kick-off.
Start the morning with a high energy welcome and introduction to BNI, setting the tone for a focused and engaging event. Connect with a wide range of business professionals through structured networking designed to maximise meaningful conversations.
Hear from dynamic speakers sharing practical insights and real business experiences, followed by member success stories that bring the power of referrals and relationships to life.
Celebrate standout achievements with awards recognising growth, contribution, and leadership across the network.
We’ll wrap up with final connections, giving you the opportunity to strengthen relationships and turn conversations into real opportunities.
Position your business in the room that matters, limited sponsorship opportunities available. Packages below or contact please contact Steve for more information

events.venue: Wellington Opera House,114 Manners St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Dita Von Teese: Nocturnelle 2026 [WELLINGTON]
The ever-reigning Queen of Burlesque, Dita Von Teese, returns to Australia and New Zealand with her most opulent show yet, the all-new Nocturnelle.

With Nocturnelle, Dita unveils a bewitching new vision - an exquisite blend of old-Hollywood allure, theatrical magic, and the high-art “Stripscapes” Dita is known for. Inspired by the magicians of the 19th century, this production conjures a world of wonder where sensuality meets magic.

Following her sold-out GLAMONATRIX tour in 2019, Dita is back to dazzle once more. Joined by a hand-picked cast of celebrated neo-burlesque artists, this is your chance to witness an icon in action. Prepare to be mesmerised by the glamour, the mystery, and the magic of Dita Von Teese.

Don't miss out - secure your tickets for an evening filled with glamour and true showmanship.

events.id: Orchestra Wellington — Kiri and Lou In Concert-Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Orchestra Wellington — Kiri and Lou In Concert
events.venue: Michael Fowler Centre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Award-winning animated children’s series, Kiri and Lou, follows the adventures of Kiri, a feisty little dinosaur, and her best friend Lou. The New Zealand made show explores the world of feelings through laughter, song and play. Now, the songs from the show come to life in delicious orchestral colour thanks to the players of Orchestra Wellington.
Presented by Liv Tennet, the voice of Kiri, this concert brings the music of one of Aotearoa’s favourite TV shows to life. Beautiful visuals from some of the episodes will be projected during the concert. This is a family-friendly event and is recommended for children aged 3 and above.
This handcrafted stop-motion animation show is now in its third season. Kiri and Lou shows in many countries around the world including Sweden, Czech Republic, Canada, Mexico, United States, and Latvia.

events.id: Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: More Tastes of Singapore Street Food
Come and try some delicious new recipes - you don’t have to have done Mei-Fen's first course to join in!
Singaporean cooking is characterised by being mildly spiced, but you can always adjust to your preference.
By the end of the workshop, you’ll be able to:
• Follow recipes using correct procedures
• Create a selection of tasty Singapore Hawker dishes, which may includes recipes such as Chicken Rendang and Nasi Lemak.
• Feel more confident to cook Singapore Hawker Food at home
Some ingredients supplied but a list of additional ingredients needed (approximate cost $35) will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed. NOTE: Nuts and gluten will be used in some recipes - this course is not suitable for vegetarians.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Getting the Most from AI: Practical AI for Everyday Use-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Getting the Most from AI: Practical AI for Everyday Use
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Getting the Most from AI: Practical Skills for Everyday Use
(NZ Citizens and residents pay $29 with ID, all others pays $120).
AI tools are everywhere, but knowing which one to use — and how to use it well — makes all the difference. In this hands-on workshop, Jon Bell will guide you through practical techniques to make AI a genuinely useful partner in your work and life. Whether you've tried it a few times or use it regularly, you'll leave with real skills you can apply straight away, with no technical background needed.
We'll start with a quick tour of the main tools — ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Copilot — so you understand what makes each one different. (They're less different than you might think!) Then we'll spend most of the day going deep on Claude, which Jon considers the easiest to use, while also being the most powerful.
We'll cover real examples, answer your questions as they come up, and spend plenty of time experimenting together.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
- Write effective prompts that get clearer, more useful responses
- Use AI for research, brainstorming, writing, and creative work
- Understand the differences between the major tools and when to use each
- Recognise the tools' limitations (and how to work around them)
- Apply practical strategies for using AI in your personal or professional projects
- Confidently troubleshoot when AI gives unexpected or unhelpful answers
Please bring: A phone or laptop with ChatGPT and Claude installed and signed in to both. Enough battery to last the day.
A condition of enrolment is that you must provide photo ID when you attend.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.long_description:
Patterns from Favourite Garments
Love a piece of clothing but can't find a pattern?  Bring along clothes you want to take patterns off and learn some useful tips and sizing rules. You'll make a pattern for at least one garment and get help on how to get going with others.
A list of what else to bring will be sent out when course numbers are confirmed. Tea and coffee provided but bring your lunch.
By the end of the workshop, you'll be able to:
• Use techniques to reproduce a paper copy of an existing garment
• Demonstrate how to include seam allowances
• Demonstrate how to accommodate facings and hems in construction
• Reproduce a pattern of a garment of your choice 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. events.id: Sound Healing Experience at Tusk Yoga with Claudio-TUSK Yoga,Knight Frank House, 23 Waring Taylor St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington-All Events In
events.name: Sound Healing Experience at Tusk Yoga with Claudio
events.venue: TUSK Yoga,Knight Frank House, 23 Waring Taylor St, Wellington Central, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Sound Healing Experience at Tusk Yoga with Claudio
We’re so excited to bring this deeply restorative sound journey to Tusk Yoga for the very first time — an immersive evening of vibration, stillness, and heart-centred connection with multi-instrumentalist Claudio Escutia.

Let yourself be held by the layered tones of Gongs, Crystal and Tibetan singing bowls, Harmonium Native American flutes, and other traditional instruments, all delivered through high-quality audio so the sound is heard *and* felt throughout your entire body.

In this state of deep rest, your nervous system softens, the thinking mind quiets, and you naturally arrive at the heart-mind. Here, silence rises gently through the vehicle of sound itself. 🌿

No yoga or meditation experience is needed — absolutely everyone is welcome.

**What to bring**
Bring your own yoga mat if you like, though studio mats are available. We’ll provide bolsters, blankets, and eye pillows so you can settle in completely.

🎟️ **Booking is essential** — spaces are limited and these events tend to fill quickly. Don’t miss this special first gathering at Tusk Yoga.

Contribution:
Early bird: First 10 bookings $40
General admission : $45


Learn more buy tickets and listen to Claudio’s music here:

https://www.claudioescutia.com/events/sound-healing-experience-at-tusk-yoga-with-claudio

---

**About Claudio**
Born into a family of musicians in Mexico City and now based in Wellington, Claudio began playing music as a child and later studied classical guitar. His path led him through jazz, world music, and spiritual sound traditions in Montreal, and extensive travels across Asia and Europe deepened his connection with Indian music and yogic philosophy.

He trained in Tibetan singing bowls in Kathmandu and Varanasi, and his work now embraces instruments including sitar, esraj, bansuri, harmonium, flutes, gongs, and voice. Every session is a weave of technical skill, grounded presence, and deep reverence for the healing power of sound.

🎧 Listen & connect:
• SoundCloud: soundcloud.com/claudioescutiasounds
• Instagram: instagram.com/claudio_yoga_sound_healing
• Facebook: facebook.com/soundhealingyogamusicclaudio

Be part of the very first Sacred Harmonic Sound evening at Tusk Yoga. Reserve your space now — we can’t wait to share this with you. 💫

events.id: Hiatus Kaiyote tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Hiatus Kaiyote tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Live Nation
Genre-bending future soul visionaries Hiatus Kaiyote return to celebrate their 15th anniversary with an electrifying night of funk, jazz, hip-hop, rock and progressive R&B as only they know how.
Led by singer-guitarist Naomi “Nai Palm” Saalfield, the Melbourne funkateers –featuring bassist Paul Bender, keyboardist Simon Mavin and drummer Perrin Moss –exploded internationally with their 2012 debut Tawk Tomahawk, which drew praise from the likes of Prince, Erykah Badu and Questlove. They landed their first of three Grammy nominations for their Q-Tip collaboration Nakamarra, received their second for their polyrhythmic record Choose Your Weapon (2015), and had their music sampled by no less than Kendrick Lamar, Beyoncé and Drake (with whom Nai Palm also collaborated on 2018’s monster hit Scorpion).
The band’s kaleidoscopic 2021 album Mood Valiant sent them into the national top five and earned them another Grammy nod, while their latest, 2024’s Love Heart Cheat Code, landed them an ARIA nomination for Best Group. Wizards of the shapeshifting live show, Hiatus Kaiyote have built a formidable reputation for their spectacular stage performances – a high-wire mix of musical mastery and thrilling, edge-of-your-seat chaos.

events.id: Burn The Floor — Luminous-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Burn The Floor — Luminous
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Ballroom dance has never been more thrilling. For over 20 years, Burn the Floor has been revolutionising the fast and furious steps of ballroom style. Groundbreaking moves and rebellious energy are brought to the stage every night to create an electrifying experience. For its latest show. Luminous, twenty-six dancers, musicians, and vocalists will take to the stage. You’ll get to witness romantic waltzes, a futuristic foxtrot, and the passion of the paso doble and tango. You’ll also marvel at the footwork of the cha cha, samba, jive, and a smokingly irresistible Rumba. With lights, costumes, and eclectic live music, you’ll want to leap out of your seat to join in.
Director Jason Gilkison sets an uncompromising standard and comments, “From the very beginning, we set out to shatter every expectation of what ballroom dance can be. Burn the Floor has always been about pushing boundaries — and that hunger to evolve is what has kept us alive and thriving.”
Burn the Floor has grown into a global phenomenon, selling more than four million tickets across theatres in over 30 countries. The show is also seen by more than one million people each year through its four permanently installed productions with Norwegian Cruise Line.

events.id: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Chris Parker — Take a Good Hard Look at Me
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
When comedian Chris Parker was young, he liked attention. He’s always endeavoured to make people look at him. In his latest show, he continues the theme of a lifetime. Take a Good Hard Look at Me displays the Kiwi comic’s sharp wit and clever timing. Chris says of his hour-long show — “There’s no secrets about to be dished, no valuable lesson to take away. I’m just a 35-year-old gay man, who was told there was something special about him as a kid and has been grappling with that ever since.” 
Chris is an actor, comedian, writer, and podcaster. He gained fame on TV’s Have You Been Paying Attention and Thank God You’re Here. He has written and performed extensively with Tom Sainsbury on live shows such as The Opening Night Before Christmas, Camping, Giggly Gerties, and Hauraki Horror. He also created and performed several sketch comedy shows with fellow comedian Hayley Sproull, including Tighty Whiteys, The Outsiders’ Guide, and Milky Bits.

events.venue: Hnry Stadium,105 Waterloo Quay, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Wellington Home & Garden Show 2026
The iconic Wellington Home & Garden Show is back at Hnry Stadium. Join us for a tonne of inspiration for the home, garden & lifestyle!

About this Event

The entry ticket is non-refundable, but it is transferable so you if you can't attend for whatever reason, you can give the ticket to a friend.
It’s all about ideas at the Wellington Home & Garden Show 2026. A wonderful range of exhibitors, 'Ask an Expert' sessions, latest trends, innovations and exclusive show specials it’s the place to be for inspiration for indoors and out.
For more information visit homeandgardenshow.co.nz or 'Like us' on Facebook.
We are working very hard to keep our events safe and we ask that you please stay home if you are unwell.
Show opening hours: Friday & Saturday 10-5pm, Sunday 10-4pm
FAQs
1. What are my transport/parking options for getting to and from the event?
Parking is available at the venue, at a cost of $10. Please note parking is card payment only (entry to the Show is card or cash).
2. What is the best time to attend?
Peak visitor times are normally between 11am - 2pm so the crowds are less outside of these times.
3. What days is the ticket valid for?
General Admission entry tickets is for one entry only and is valid for any one of the 3 event days.
Gold Card Special 1/2 price tickets valid for Friday only.
4. Do I have to bring my printed ticket to the event?
A printed ticket or showing the barcode on your phone to the entry staff is required.

events.id: Yoga Nidra-Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington-Event Finder
events.name: Yoga Nidra
events.venue: Awhi Yoga and Wellbeing Centre, 15 Johnston St., Wellington
events.long_description:
Unwind from the stresses of the week and start your weekend in a state of relaxation. Yoga Nidra, often referred to as "yogic sleep," is a deeply restorative practice that promotes physical relaxation, mental clarity, and emotional balance. It's the perfect way to rejuvenate your mind, body, and soul: your hinengaro, tinana and wairua.

Yoga nidra is a powerful technique that directly works to heal your mind by removing samskaras - untying knots and mental impressions. This practice of learning to relax consciously is well regarded as an antidote for stress, anxiety, poor sleep amongst other health benefits.

Everyone is welcome to experience the profound benefits of Yoga Nidra, and no prior experience is required. For some people, however, Yoga Nidra’s prolonged stillness or internal focus can bring up strong emotions, memories, and/or sensations. You are encouraged to listen and respond in a way that feels safe for you — adjusting your position, keeping your eyes open, grounding yourself, or stepping out of the room at any time.

Yoga Nidra may not be suitable for everyone. If you are experiencing acute mental health challenges, unprocessed trauma, or conditions that make stillness or internal focus difficult, we recommend seeking guidance from a qualified healthcare provider or speaking with the teacher before participating.

events.id: Mel Parsons — Castle Hill Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Mel Parsons — Castle Hill Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Expect soaring harmonies, a dynamic live show, and feel-good vibes when Mel Parsons performs live in Wellington this September. With a style that ranges from indie folk to alt-country, the Kiwi artist has established a reputation for moving songwriting. 
The tour repertoire is based around songs from her latest album, Castle Hill. Named after the remote mountain town in the Southern Alps where it was recorded, Castle Hill was recorded live with Mel’s longtime band members, Aaron Stewart on bass and Jed Parsons on drums. It also includes Ben King (guitarist from Goldenhorse and Grand Rapids). They will support her on stage too. Singles from the record that you’ll hear include alt radio chart toppers, Post High Slide, Brick By Brick, Be Here Now, and Grain by Grain. 
Since her first album in 2009, Mel has established herself in New Zealand’s musical landscape. Awards include Best Folk Artist (2020 New Zealand Music Awards) and Best Country Music Song (2016 New Zealand Music Awards).

events.id: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop (held in Seaview, Lower Hutt)
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Bob Ross Landscape Painting Workshop
If you don’t already know who Bob Ross is then Google him, and you will be amazed at how he creates fantastic paintings using simple tools.  You can too! 
Please wear old clothes and bring your lunch.  You'll go home with an oil painting completed 100% by yourself.
All materials are included in course fee –  a 16x20 inch professional canvas, Bob Ross oil paints, brushes, and all equipment, as seen on NZ TV and YouTube.
This is a unique technique of wet-into-wet oil painting, which is ideal for those who have never painted before, as well as more experienced artists.
Please make sure you have a method to transport your wet oil painting home at the end of day.  Each workshop focuses on doing a specific set landscape painting - the whole class group does the same painting.
(Please note there is no wheelchair access to this venue - held at NZ Art School studio at 61 Seaview Road, Lower Hutt).
Painting for 17 May
Painting for 30 August
Painting for 15 November
Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.name: Cake Decorating Workshop
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cale Decorating Workshop
Join cake expert, Lina, for a hands-on cake building and decorating workshop where creativity meets craftsmanship!
With over 20 years of experience in the culinary and hospitality industries - from managing restaurants across Europe and Asia to running her own cake design business - Lina brings not only technical expertise, but also a genuine passion for sharing the joy of decorating.
Known for her friendly, engaging style and eye for detail, Lina will guide you through each step of the process - from building cakes and decorating including palette knife and  gum-paste techniques, to using buttercream and creating a centrepiece.
Whether you're a complete beginner or simply want to sharpen your decorating skills, you'll leave with new techniques, confidence, and of course, your own edible masterpiece.
Let’s make something beautiful - and delicious - together!
You will leave with a tall 6” decorated cake in its own cake box to take home and enjoy. All ingredients included in course fee - just bring an apron and a couple of teatowels. 
Not suitable for those who are gluten-free. 
Note: Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Lentils – Types, Recipes and How to Cook Them
The lentil is perhaps the world’s most versatile and indestructible food. It has significant health benefits from lowering cholesterol to supporting weight loss – and lentils are delicious when cooked well. Enjoy this half-day course that will show you how to cook lentils properly and make the most of this nutritious staple ingredient. Discover spices to complement and enhance lentils and create some mouth-watering dishes.
All ingredients included in course fee.  Please bring your apron, a couple of tea-towels, a sharp knife and leak-proof containers to take your food home in.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Recognise some different types of lentils.
Cook some delicious lentil dishes.
Understand some spices that will work well with lentils.
Follow simple recipes.
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Creative Writing - From Idea to Page-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Creative Writing - From Idea to Page
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Creative Writing - From Idea to Page
“Just write!”
That’s the advice given to every newbie writer… but what happens when you
struggle to get words on the page?
In this one-day course, we’ll brainstorm ideas, complete writing exercises and
discuss strategies for keeping up writing momentum.
Please bring pen and paper.  Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring lunch and any snacks you may need.  
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
- Identify different brainstorming methods
- Complete writing exercises to develop ideas
- Describe your own writing process, and identify what works for you
- Apply strategies to keep up writing momentum
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".
events.description:
Creative Writing - From Idea to Page “Just write!” That’s the advice given to every newbie writer… but what happens when you struggle to get words on the page?
Please bring pen and paper.
Tea and coffee available for your breaks, please bring lunch and any snacks you may need.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to: - Identify different brainstorming methods - Complete writing exercises to develop ideas - Describe your own writing process, and identify what works for you - Apply strategies to keep up writing momentum Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel.
We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly.

events.id: Zine Making - Introduction-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Zine Making - Introduction
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Zine Making - Introduction
In this workshop you will be guided in making a classic cut and fold 8-page zine from a standard A4 sheet of paper - this is a clever design you can easily photocopy and reproduce later! You will be introduced to a range of techniques and approaches such as cut and paste photos and text, collage and drawing on paper.
If you’re keen to make more zines and share them with the world, there's a broad and growing community of Zine-makers and traders, as well as the well-attended annual Wellington Zinefest!
Paper, newspapers, magazines and basic drawing materials will be provided. We recommend you bring along a sketch book and any favourite drawing materials, newspapers, magazines or other interesting paper material to cut up and collage with in class.
Tea and coffee provided, please bring your lunch.
 By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Cut and fold a classic 8-page zine format from a single sheet of paper
Brainstorm content ideas for your personalised zine publications
Arrange & compose text and images to fit a classic 8-page zine layout
Combine cut & paste magazine content with hand drawn/ written content
Create a draft zine of your personal interests
  Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Suzanne Vega-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Suzanne Vega
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Widely regarded as one of the foremost songwriters of her generation, Suzanne Vega emerged as a leading figure of the folk revival of the early 1980s. Four decades later, she is still writing and singing great songs. In her Wellington show, Suzanne will perform a career-spanning set. See her on stage at St James Theatre performing favourites like Tom’s Diner, Luka, Marlene On The Wall, and plenty more. There’ll also be new songs from her 2025 album, ‘Flying with Angels’. Joining her on stage will be longtime guitarist, Gerry Leonard, and cellist Stephanie Winters. 
Since the release of her self-titled, critically acclaimed 1985 debut album, Suzanne Vega has played to sold-out concerts across the world. She is a masterful storyteller who, according to one The New York Times critique, “observes the world with a clinically poetic eye.” Her song lyrics often focus on city life, ordinary people, and real-world subjects. They are notably succinct, understated, and immediately recognisable.

events.long_description:
SUZANNE VEGA | Sun 6 Sept 2026 | St. James Theatre, Wellington
𝐒𝐮𝐳𝐚𝐧𝐧𝐞 𝐕𝐞𝐠𝐚 will perform a career-spanning show including favorites like “𝑇𝑜𝑚’𝑠 𝐷𝑖𝑛𝑒𝑟”, “𝐿𝑢𝑘𝑎”, “𝑀𝑎𝑟𝑙𝑒𝑛𝑒 𝑂𝑛 𝑇ℎ𝑒 𝑊𝑎𝑙𝑙” and many more, plus songs from her latest album, 𝐹𝑙𝑦𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝐴𝑛𝑔𝑒𝑙𝑠, which was released in May 2025 to critical acclaim. She will be joined on stage by her longtime guitarist, Gerry Leonard (of David Bowie band), and cellist Stephanie Winters.


𝐅𝐋𝐘𝐈𝐍𝐆 𝐖𝐈𝐓𝐇 𝐀𝐍𝐆𝐄𝐋𝐒 𝐑𝐄𝐕𝐈𝐄𝐖𝐒

“𝐹𝑜𝑢𝑟 𝑑𝑒𝑐𝑎𝑑𝑒𝑠 𝑎𝑓𝑡𝑒𝑟 ℎ𝑒𝑟 𝑛𝑒𝑤-𝑤𝑎𝑖𝑓 𝑑𝑒𝑏𝑢𝑡, 𝐒𝐮𝐳𝐚𝐧𝐧𝐞 𝐕𝐞𝐠𝐚 𝑟𝑒𝑡𝑎𝑖𝑛𝑠 ℎ𝑒𝑟 𝑘𝑛𝑎𝑐𝑘 𝑓𝑜𝑟 𝑙𝑢𝑐𝑖𝑑 𝑟𝑒𝑓𝑙𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑐𝑟𝑖𝑠𝑝 𝑚𝑢𝑠𝑖𝑐 𝑡𝑜 𝑚𝑎𝑡𝑐ℎ… 𝐻𝑒𝑟 𝑣𝑜𝑖𝑐𝑒 𝑟𝑒𝑚𝑎𝑖𝑛𝑠 𝑏𝑜𝑡ℎ 𝑘𝑛𝑜𝑤𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑜𝑏𝑠𝑒𝑟𝑣𝑎𝑛𝑡.” – 𝐑𝐨𝐥𝐥𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐒𝐭𝐨𝐧𝐞 | 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐁𝐞𝐬𝐭 𝐀𝐥𝐛𝐮𝐦𝐬 𝐨𝐟 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓 𝐒𝐨 𝐅𝐚𝐫

"𝑖𝑛𝑓𝑢𝑠𝑒𝑑 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝑎 𝑣𝑖𝑏𝑟𝑎𝑛𝑐𝑦 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑝𝑜𝑙𝑖𝑡𝑖𝑐𝑎𝑙 𝑢𝑟𝑔𝑒𝑛𝑐𝑦 𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑖𝑠 𝑣𝑒𝑟𝑦 ‘𝑛𝑜𝑤’”
- 𝐌𝐎𝐉𝐎

“𝑓𝑖𝑛𝑑𝑠 ℎ𝑢𝑚𝑖𝑙𝑖𝑡𝑦 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑔𝑟𝑎𝑐𝑒 𝑎𝑚𝑖𝑑 𝑡ℎ𝑒 ‘𝑝𝑒𝑟𝑚𝑎𝑛𝑒𝑛𝑡 𝑒𝑚𝑒𝑟𝑔𝑒𝑛𝑐𝑦’ 𝑜𝑓 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑝𝑟𝑒𝑠𝑒𝑛𝑡”
- 𝐔𝐍𝐂𝐔𝐓 𝐌𝐚𝐠𝐚𝐳𝐢𝐧𝐞


𝐋𝐈𝐕𝐄 𝐑𝐄𝐕𝐈𝐄𝐖𝐒 𝟐𝟎𝟐𝟓

"𝑉𝑒𝑔𝑎’𝑠 𝑝𝑒𝑟𝑓𝑜𝑟𝑚𝑎𝑛𝑐𝑒 𝑤𝑎𝑠 𝑛𝑜𝑡ℎ𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑠ℎ𝑜𝑟𝑡 𝑜𝑓 𝑚𝑒𝑠𝑚𝑒𝑟𝑖𝑧𝑖𝑛𝑔, 𝑤𝑒𝑎𝑣𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑡𝑎𝑙𝑒𝑠 𝑡ℎ𝑟𝑜𝑢𝑔ℎ ℎ𝑒𝑟 𝑠𝑜𝑢𝑙𝑓𝑢𝑙 𝑠𝑜𝑛𝑔𝑠 𝑖𝑛 𝑎 𝑐𝑜𝑧𝑦 𝑠𝑒𝑡𝑡𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑡ℎ𝑎𝑡 𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑜𝑤𝑒𝑑 𝑓𝑜𝑟 𝑎 𝑑𝑒𝑒𝑝 𝑐𝑜𝑛𝑛𝑒𝑐𝑡𝑖𝑜𝑛 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝑡ℎ𝑒 𝑎𝑢𝑑𝑖𝑒𝑛𝑐𝑒."
- 𝐅𝐢𝐧𝐝𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝐂𝐨𝐧𝐧𝐞𝐜𝐭𝐢𝐜𝐮𝐭

"𝑆𝑢𝑧𝑎𝑛𝑛𝑒 𝑉𝑒𝑔𝑎 𝑤𝑜𝑣𝑒 𝑎 𝑠𝑝𝑒𝑙𝑙 𝑜𝑓 𝑙𝑖𝑔ℎ𝑡 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑠ℎ𝑎𝑑𝑜𝑤, 𝑏𝑙𝑒𝑛𝑑𝑖𝑛𝑔 𝑐𝑙𝑎𝑠𝑠𝑖𝑐𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝑛𝑒𝑤 𝑚𝑎𝑡𝑒𝑟𝑖𝑎𝑙 𝑤𝑖𝑡ℎ 𝑖𝑛𝑡𝑖𝑚𝑎𝑡𝑒 𝑠𝑡𝑜𝑟𝑖𝑒𝑠 𝑓𝑜𝑟 𝑎 𝑚𝑒𝑚𝑜𝑟𝑎𝑏𝑙𝑒 𝑛𝑖𝑔ℎ𝑡.”
- 𝐏𝐚𝐭𝐜𝐡

𝐀𝐁𝐎𝐔𝐓 𝐒𝐔𝐙𝐀𝐍𝐍𝐄 𝐕𝐄𝐆𝐀

Widely regarded as one of the foremost songwriters of her generation, Suzanne Vega emerged as a leading figure of the folk revival of the early 1980s. Accompanying herself on acoustic guitar, Vega sang what has been called contemporary folk or neo-folk songs of her own creation in Greenwich Village clubs. Since the release of her self-titled, critically acclaimed 1985 debut album, she has given sold-out concerts in many of the world’s best-known venues. Known for performances that convey deep emotion, Vega’s distinctive, “clear, unwavering voice” (Rolling Stone) has been described as “a cool, dry sandpaper-brushed near-whisper” by The Washington Post, with NPR Music noting that she “has been making vital, inventive music” throughout the course of her decades-long career. Bearing the stamp of a masterful storyteller who “observes the world with a clinically poetic eye” (The New York Times), Vega’s songs have tended to focus on city life, ordinary people and real-world subjects. Notably succinct and understated, her work is immediately recognizable—as utterly distinct and thoughtful as it was when her voice was first heard on the radio over 40 years ago.

events.id: Cooking: Gourmet Sausage Making-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Cooking: Gourmet Sausage Making
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: Gourmet Sausage Making
Come along and learn how to fill sausage casings with a tasty mincemeat and get tips and tricks on how to make your own customized sausage mixes at home with the use of various herbs and spices. Everyone will get some sausages to take home and enjoy.
All ingredients included in course fee but please bring your apron, a dish cloth and a couple of tea towels to the session. 
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Recognise how to handle a knife safely
-  Understand food safety and hygiene around sausage-making and avoiding cross contamination
-  Recognise the difference between two kinds of sausage casings and why you use each of them
-  Have some ideas on how to make and cook a good quality sausage properly
-  Be able to safely store sausages products
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Verb Wellington Literary Festival--Wellington NZ
events.name: Verb Wellington Literary Festival
events.long_description:
Welcome in spring with a literary bonanza at Wellington’s iconic readers and writers’ festival. Verb has supported and nurtured Wellington’s arts community since its founding, and its annual festival has become a highlight of the literary calendar. Over three days, writers from across Aotearoa and several from around the world will share their ideas and stories with curious readers. Verb 2026 will be jam-packed with writers’ talks, workshops and panel discussions. 
Taking place from 11 to 13 September, Verb is a celebration of the written word and the community that surrounds it. See their website for a full list of the festival and special events.

events.id: Happy Mondays tickets-Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand-Ticketek
events.name: Happy Mondays tickets
events.venue: Meow Nui, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Presented by: Destroy All Lines
Pioneers of the iconic Madchester sound, the Happy Mondays are heading back to New Zealand to celebrate the 35th anniversary of their seminal album, Pills 'n' Thrills and Bellyaches.
Fusing funk, rock, psychedelia, and house with the pulsating energy of the UK’s burgeoning rave scene, the Happy Mondays became the face of a cultural revolution. Their 1990 release, Pills 'n' Thrills and Bellyaches, catapulted them into the mainstream, spending over six months on the UK Albums Chart and earning its place as one of the defining records of the decade.
Fronted by the inimitable Shaun Ryder, with Bez ( freaky dancing and percussion), Mark Day (guitar), Gary Whelan (drums), Mikey Shine (bass), Dan Broad (musical director/guitar/keys), and Firouzeh Berry (backing vocals), the band will perform highlights from the classic album; including anthems like Step On, Kinky Afro, and Loose Fit, alongside fan favourites such as 24 Hour Party People, Hallelujah, Judge Fudge, and W.F.L.
Their enduring influence on British music was formally recognised in 2016 when they received the prestigious Ivor Novello Inspiration Award, a testament to their legacy as one of the UK’s most beloved and groundbreaking bands.
‘Always a great buzz when we play Australia and New Zealand . Looking forward to bringing the Happy Mondays Pills ‘n’ Thrills party there in September’’ - Shaun Ryder
This isn’t just a tour, it’s a full-throttle revival of Madchester chaos, and the year's wildest party is coming.

events.long_description:
Happy Mondays // Wellington // Celebrating 35 years of Pills 'n' Thrills & Bellyaches
𝑫𝒆𝒔𝒕𝒓𝒐𝒚 𝑨𝒍𝒍 𝑳𝒊𝒏𝒆𝒔 𝒂𝒏𝒅 𝑺𝑩𝑴 𝑷𝒓𝒆𝒔𝒆𝒏𝒕𝒔 𝒑𝒓𝒆𝒔𝒆𝒏𝒕
𝐇𝐀𝐏𝐏𝐘 𝐌𝐎𝐍𝐃𝐀𝐘𝐒
𝐂𝐞𝐥𝐞𝐛𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐧𝐠 𝟑𝟓 𝐘𝐞𝐚𝐫𝐬 𝐨𝐟 "𝐏𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐬 '𝐧' 𝐓𝐡𝐫𝐢𝐥𝐥𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐁𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐲𝐚𝐜𝐡𝐞𝐬

Sᴀᴛᴜʀᴅᴀʏ, 12ᴛʜ Sᴇᴘᴛᴇᴍʙᴇʀ 2026
Mᴇᴏᴡ Nᴜɪ, Wᴇʟʟɪɴɢᴛᴏɴ

⏰ 𝐊𝐞𝐲 𝐝𝐚𝐭𝐞𝐬 𝐚𝐧𝐝 𝐓𝐢𝐜𝐤𝐞𝐭𝐬:
DAL Presale: Wed 28 Jan @ 9AM 𝑳𝑶𝑪𝑨𝑳
𝐒𝐢𝐠𝐧 𝐮𝐩: 𝐃𝐀𝐋 𝐏𝐫𝐞𝐬𝐚𝐥𝐞 ➟ https://daltours.cc/HappyMondays
General On Sale: Fri 30 Jan @ 9AM 𝑳𝑶𝑪𝑨𝑳

Pioneers of the iconic Madchester sound, the 𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐲 𝐌𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐲𝐬 are heading back to Australia and New Zealand to celebrate the 35th anniversary of their seminal album, 𝑃𝑖𝑙𝑙𝑠 '𝑛' 𝑇ℎ𝑟𝑖𝑙𝑙𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐵𝑒𝑙𝑙𝑦𝑎𝑐ℎ𝑒𝑠.

Fusing funk, rock, psychedelia, and house with the pulsating energy of the UK’s burgeoning rave scene, the 𝐇𝐚𝐩𝐩𝐲 𝐌𝐨𝐧𝐝𝐚𝐲𝐬 became the face of a cultural revolution. Their 1990 release, 𝑃𝑖𝑙𝑙𝑠 '𝑛' 𝑇ℎ𝑟𝑖𝑙𝑙𝑠 𝑎𝑛𝑑 𝐵𝑒𝑙𝑙𝑦𝑎𝑐ℎ𝑒𝑠, catapulted them into the mainstream, spending over six months on the UK Albums Chart and earning its place as one of the defining records of the decade.

Fronted by the inimitable 𝐒𝐡𝐚𝐮𝐧 𝐑𝐲𝐝𝐞𝐫, with 𝐁𝐞𝐳 ( freaky dancing and percussion), 𝐌𝐚𝐫𝐤 𝐃𝐚𝐲 (guitar), 𝐆𝐚𝐫𝐲 𝐖𝐡𝐞𝐥𝐚𝐧 (drums), 𝐌𝐢𝐤𝐞𝐲 𝐒𝐡𝐢𝐧𝐞 (bass) 𝐃𝐚𝐧 𝐁𝐫𝐨𝐚𝐝 (musical director/guitar/keys), and 𝐅𝐢𝐫𝐨𝐮𝐳𝐞𝐡 𝐁𝐞𝐫𝐫𝐲 (backing vocals), the band will perform highlights from the classic album; including anthems like 𝑆𝑡𝑒𝑝 𝑂𝑛, 𝐾𝑖𝑛𝑘𝑦 𝐴𝑓𝑟𝑜, and 𝐿𝑜𝑜𝑠𝑒 𝐹𝑖𝑡, alongside fan favourites such as 24 𝐻𝑜𝑢𝑟 𝑃𝑎𝑟𝑡𝑦 𝑃𝑒𝑜𝑝𝑙𝑒, 𝐻𝑎𝑙𝑙𝑒𝑙𝑢𝑗𝑎ℎ, 𝐽𝑢𝑑𝑔𝑒 𝐹𝑢𝑑𝑔𝑒, and 𝑊.𝐹.𝐿.

Their enduring influence on British music was formally recognised in 2016 when they received the prestigious 𝐈𝐯𝐨𝐫 𝐍𝐨𝐯𝐞𝐥𝐥𝐨 𝐈𝐧𝐬𝐩𝐢𝐫𝐚𝐭𝐢𝐨𝐧 𝐀𝐰𝐚𝐫𝐝, a testament to their legacy as one of the UK’s most beloved and groundbreaking bands.

‘Always a great buzz when we play Australia and New Zealand . Looking forward to bringing the Happy Mondays Pills ‘n’ Thrills party there in September’’ - 𝐒𝐡𝐚𝐮𝐧 𝐑𝐲𝐝𝐞𝐫

This isn’t just a tour, it’s a full-throttle revival of Madchester chaos, and the year's wildest party is coming.

‘’Funky dancing, maraca shaking, and incredible vocal performances Happy Mondays showed why they were so big in the 90s and have been able to keep that special place in the hearts of the fans for so long.’’ – 𝐌𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐜 𝐍𝐞𝐰𝐬 𝐌𝐨𝐧𝐭𝐡𝐥𝐲

‘’With Bez and Ryder’s between song banter raising the amusement stakes, the promised Hallelujah was given an airing and its iconic sounds had the crowd bouncing and swaying, it was clear that the Monday’s have lost none of their magic. They are still funky, downright vulgar but so endearing.’’ – 𝐔𝐊 𝐌𝐮𝐬𝐢𝐜 𝐑𝐞𝐯𝐢𝐞𝐰𝐬

‘’The show was packed with anthems. Friday night's headline set from the Happy Mondays provided a wonderful dose of nostalgia from one of Manchester's most iconic outfits.’’ – 𝐓𝐡𝐞 𝐍𝐨𝐫𝐭𝐡𝐞𝐫𝐧 𝐄𝐜𝐡𝐨

https://www.undertheradar.co.nz/.../33281/Strung-Out-USA.utr

Strung Out tore through NZ in 2023, and you lapped it up. You loved them, and they loved
you—so much so that they’re returning this September for a full-blown New Zealand tour, hitting
four locations across both the North and South Island.

And this time, they’re bringing something special.

To celebrate the 30th Anniversary of SUBURBANTEENAGEWASTELANDBLUES, Strung Out
will be playing the album in its entirety.

One of punk’s holy grails, it’s fast, ferocious, and
precise—a record that helped define the genre and influenced generations of bands that
followed.

Released in 1996, SUBURBANTEENAGEWASTELANDBLUES pushed the boundaries of the
genre—faster, sharper, and more intricate than anything that came before. Three decades on, it
still hits hard, and hearing it played live, front to back, is an experience no self-respecting punk
fan can miss.

Hailing from Simi Valley, California, Strung Out are long-standing punk rock royalty and one of
Fat Wreck Chords’ defining “big four.” With decades of touring under their belts, they have a
proven track record of igniting crowds and delivering high-octane, full-throttle punk shows.

Expect a night of relentless energy: razor-sharp riffs, pounding drums, and shout-along
choruses delivered with force.

This is controlled chaos at its best: sweat-soaked rooms, ringing
ears, and intensity that pulls you right in.

Last time Strung Out were here, shows sold out. This run will be no different.

Don’t sleep on it, or you’ll miss it.

Friday September 11th, 2026 — Last Place — Hamilton
Saturday September 12th, 2026 — Whammy Bar — Auckland
Sunday September 13th, 2026 — Valhalla — Wellington
Monday September 14th, 2026 — Loons — Christchurch

events.id: ASH ‘1977’ 30th Anniversary Tour-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: ASH ‘1977’ 30th Anniversary Tour
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
"Ash rule. They are geniuses. Gods maybe."- NME
Ireland’s favourite power pop trio, Ash, finally return to Australia and New Zealand to Celebrate the 30th Anniversary of their iconic debut album, ‘1977’, by performing the album in full. Released in 1996, the album’s title references both the decade the band members were born in and the year Star Wars first hit cinemas.
‘1977’ has sold more than 2 million copies worldwide and was written, recorded and released when most of Ash were still only 19. A cinematic punk-pop soundtrack to binge drinking, chasing girls, youth and frustrated adolescent idealism, the album fused power pop, glam rock, post-grunge and Britpop into a loud, melodic and exuberant sound.
It struck a chord with the hearts, minds and hormones of a generation of like-minded teens, delivering a run of singles including “Girl From Mars”, “Kung Fu”, “Angel Interceptor”, “Goldfinger” and “Oh Yeah” — all of which reached the UK Top 20 and helped cement Ash’s reputation.
The 30th Anniversary Celebration of ‘1977’ will give Ash fans the chance to relive the album that featured “hit after honey-topped hit” and Britpop ruled the airwaves. Oh yeah!

San Fran holds a Supervised License, meaning minors are welcome to attend events at San Fran so long as they are accompanied by a legal guardian at all times whilst at the venue. Minors are of course not allowed to purchase alcohol under any circumstance.

events.name: Shred Fest 2026 - Wellington
events.venue: Valhalla,154 Vivian St, Te Aro, Wellington 6011, New Zealand, Wellington
events.long_description:
Shred Fest 2026 - Wellington
SHRED FEST 2026 is coming! The renowned travelling metal festival will storm across New Zealand, delivering an unmatched celebration of technical excellence, intensity, and musical precision.

This year’s New Zealand edition features two of the most acclaimed names in modern extreme metal Obscura (GER) & Fvkvshima (NZ)

OBSCURA (GER) – Progressive death metal pioneers and masters of technical virtuosity. Following two successful Australian tours along with a return to New Zealand, OBSCURA is back to present their latest album A Sonication as part of their ongoing world tour.

Joining the international lineup are one of New Zealands most formidable local acts:

FVKVSHIMA - A progressive, driving, mechanical powerhouse surfacing from the shores of Wellington, NZ. Gargantuan, lurching drop-tuned phrases, layered with soaring apocalyptic textures. Resurrecting members from Shakahn, Backyard Burial, Planet of the Dead, and Diecast, FVKVSHIMA has collective decades of songwriting experience across a variety of genres, now condensed into its menacing live set.

Additional guests will appear in every city on this tour

Line Up
OBSCURA (GER)
FVKVSHIMA (NZ)
+ Special Guests

Dates
15/09 NZ Auckland – Whammy
16/09 NZ Wellington – Valhalla
17/09 NZ Christchurch – Churchills Tavern

SHRED FEST 2026 promises a diverse and uncompromising showcase of extreme metal — from technical precision to devastating heaviness. Expect the ultimate convergence of musicianship, passion, and power.

Tickets on sale via www.yourmatebookings.com & https://www.shredfest.net/

Obscura shared their thoughts on the upcoming tour:
"We're thrilled to bring Shred Fest to Australia in 2026 alongside the crushing talents of Fallujah, Anoxia, and Ashen. It’s been a long time coming, and we're ready to deliver a relentless set for our fans down under. No VIP packages — every band will be at the venue to meet you, talk music, and celebrate the most extreme sounds around. Come early, stay late, and support every act. We can’t wait to see you all there."

Official SHRED FEST channels;

Website: https://www.shredfest.net/
Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/profile.php?id=61569365417641 / https://www.facebook.com/shredfestausnz / https://www.facebook.com/yourmatebookings
Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/shredfest_moreisnotenough / https://www.instagram.com/shredfestausnz/
Tiktok: @shredfest_world
YouTube: youtube.com/@SHREDFEST_MOREISNOTENOUGH

All Interviews and Press Enquiries; please get in touch with Anthony

events.venue: TSB Arena, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The World of WearableArt (WOW) Show is an internationally acclaimed annual event. Held every spring, WOW is New Zealand’s largest arena spectacle. This not-to-be-missed event features an electrifying combination of live music, cirque, dance and dazzling wearable art.
This year, the inspiration for WOW is GLO! It will see imagination run riot across six sections of pure, unfiltered, high-voltage entertainment. Expect live music, aerialists, dance, and of course, dazzling wearable art. Those six sections correspond with the competition categories at the heart of WOW. As the world’s premier wearable art competition, every garment on stage is a finalist. They are selected for imaginative storytelling and execution excellence. The competition offers a global platform for artists and designers and the chance to win over NZD$200,000 in prizes.

SOTA RETURNS TO NEW ZEALAND FOR STATE OF THE ART 3.0 AFTER HIS SOLD OUT RUN IN 2024

_______________


SOTA (UK) “STATE OF THE ART 3.0”

w SPECIAL GUEST SKANTIA (UK)

SOTA has rapidly risen to the top of the global drum and bass scene, becoming one of the most in-demand artists of his generation. Hailing from Horsham, UK, he broke through after winning Best Newcomer at the Drum&BassArena Awards in 2023 and has since built one of the most explosive live shows in electronic music. His headline production concept State Of The Art is widely regarded as the most coveted show in drum and bass, selling out tours across the UK, Australia and New Zealand. Momentum continues to surge with SOTA selling out London’s Roundhouse in 10 minutes, moving 1,000 tickets in Los Angeles and being named a BBC Radio 1 Future Star. events.name: Dare to Lead™ | Wellington | 23-25 September 2026
events.venue: Museum of New Zealand Te Papa Tongarewa,55 Cable Street, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
Dare to Lead™ | Wellington | 23-25 September 2026
Dare to Lead is the ultimate starting point for brave leaders, courageous cultures, and organisational change.

About this Event


The ultimate starting point for brave leaders, courageous cultures and organisational change.


"Highly recommend."
"Inspiring."
"An amazing experience."
"One 'aha moment' after the other."

Founded on the research of Dr. Brené Brown, Dare to Lead™ is an empirically based courage-building programme, delivered by Boma New Zealand CEO and Certified Dare to Lead™ Facilitator Kaila Colbin.
Brené has spent two decades studying shame and more than seven years specifically looking at leadership. During her research, she interviewed leaders from all over the world — from Fortune 100 companies, non-profits, public sector entities, and more — and asked one question: what is the future of leadership?
Remarkably, their answers pointed to one thing: we need braver leaders.


We need more courage.

The most significant finding from Brené’s latest research, and the foundation of Dare to Lead™, is that courage is a collection of four skill sets that are teachable, measurable, and observable.


After completing the three-day Dare to Lead™ programme you'll understand how to:
Effectively navigate vulnerability and unlock change
Turn values from fluffy language into tangible, practical behaviours
Build a common culture of trust in your team
Cultivate the skills to rise from setbacks and be more resilient


This curriculum is essential for teams who want to:
Be better equipped to deal with difficult conversations
Reduce unhealthy and unproductive behaviour
Be more creative and innovative
Build resilience and improve alignment
Foster a culture of accountability and trust


This experience is for you if:
You're in a position of leadership within your organisation
You see the potential in people and processes, and are willing to take responsibility for achieving that potential
You’re passionate about accelerating performance in yourself and others
You have to navigate organisational change as part of your role



Need help with the cost?
If you are an Educator or at a Not-for-Profit, you can automatically access a special price of $1,995 + GST when you register.
We also have partial scholarships available to attend the programme. Apply here and we’ll be in touch.
Boma is a Regional Business Network Provider. Your organisation may be eligible for funding support to attend this programme. Find out more at regionalbusinesspartners.co.nz


Our Health and Safety Plan
Boma is committed to delivering safe events. We follow best practices suggested by the New Zealand health authorities and work closely with our suppliers to prioritise health and wellbeing.
Our comprehensive health and safety plans ensure you can book with certainty and attend with confidence.



Our refund policy
For details on event refunds and transfers, please see our refund policy.


Contact us
Please email with any questions.

events.long_description:
BHNZ Convention 2026 - Musika o Mātairangi
The 2026 Barbershop Harmony New Zealand Convention will take place in Wellington from September 25–27, 2026, hosted at the modern Tākina Convention Centre. This three-day event will bring together barbershop singers, quartets, choruses, and enthusiasts from across the region for a celebration of harmony, performance, and community.

Attendees can look forward to a full schedule featuring competitions, workshops, coaching sessions, mass sing-alongs, and showcase concerts highlighting both emerging talent and seasoned performers. Tākina’s world-class facilities provide an ideal setting for vibrant performances and social gatherings, all within walking distance of Wellington’s cultural and culinary attractions.

The convention aims to inspire musical excellence, foster connection within the barbershop community, and offer an unforgettable experience for performers and audiences alike.

events.name: Producers Night Market
events.venue: State Highway 1, opposite New World, New Zealand 5512,155 Old SH 1, Otaki 5512, New Zealand, Otaki
events.long_description:
Producers Night Market
✨ Producers Night Market – Kāpiti & Horowhenua ✨

Following the fantastic feedback from our February Producers Market and growing demand for a night-time experience, we’re excited to be partnering once again with the Ōtaki and Te Horo Business Association to bring you the Producers Night Market!

This relaxed, family-friendly evening market will showcase the very best of our region, fresh local produce, plants, flowers, and healthcare products, with a strong focus on supporting local growers, makers, and small businesses across Kāpiti and Horowhenua.

🌿 What to expect
- Beautiful twilight setting under the trees with festive lighting
- Live local musicians
- Seating areas to relax and enjoy the atmosphere
- A curated selection of food trucks in the car park
- A vibrant community vibe celebrating local talent and produce

👉 Find out more / register: www.otakiwomensclub.org/producers-market

💡 Please note: Stallholders will need to provide their own lighting for their stall spaces. At this time of year there will still be good natural light early in the evening.

💰 Stall fees
$20 for a 3m x 3m site
$40 for a 3m x 6m site

📅 Dates
Thursday 1 October 2026 | 4:30pm – 8:30pm
Thursday 28 January 2027 | 4:30pm – 8:30pm
Thursday 1 April 2027 | 4:30pm – 8:30pm
(If postponement is required, the event will move to the following Thursday.)

📍 Proudly supported by the Ōtaki Women’s Community Club and the Ōtaki & Te Horo Business Association.


Producers can sell:
- Fresh fruit and vegetables, eggs, olives, seasonal produce.
- Crackers, breads, cakes, biscuits and pastries.
- Coffee, tea, small batch jams, chutneys and honey.
- Wine, cider, beer, spirits, Liqueurs.
- Juice or other non alcoholic beverages.
- Olive oil, sauces, chocolates, sweets.
- Ice Cream, cream, milk, cheese and dairy products.
- Herbal tonics and food-based wellness blends.
- Healthcare products locally made, natural body products.
- Flowers, trees, plants and seeds.

Producers Night Market ✨ Producers Night Market – Kāpiti & Horowhenua ✨ Following the fantastic feedback from our February Producers Market and growing demand for a night-time experience, we’re excited to be partnering once again with the Ōtaki and Te Horo Business Association to bring you the Producers Night Market!
This relaxed, family-friendly evening market will showcase the very best of our region, fresh local produce, plants, flowers, and healthcare products, with a strong focus on supporting local growers, makers, and small businesses across Kāpiti and Horowhenua.
🌿 What to expect - Beautiful twilight setting under the trees with festive lighting - Live local musicians - Seating areas to relax and enjoy the atmosphere - A curated selection of food trucks in the car park - A vibrant community vibe celebrating local talent and produce 👉 Find out more / register: www.otakiwomensclub.org/producers-market 💡 Please note: Stallholders will need to provide their own lighting for their stall spaces.
- Crackers, breads, cakes, biscuits and pastries.
- Healthcare products locally made, natural body products.

events.long_description:
Get Ya Geek On! Mini-Con '26
Where: Te Rauparaha Arena, Porirua, Wellington
When: Saturday 3rd Oct 2026
11am* - 5pm
Cost: $5. Children 12 and under are FREE.
ALL AGES

Unleash your inner geek at the Get Ya Geek On Mini-Con! Join us on October 3, 2026, starting at 11:00 AM at the vibrant Te Rauparaha Arena and Aquatic Centre in Porirua City Centre, New Zealand. Get ready for an immersive experience filled with all things geeky!

At this market-style event hosted by Get Ya Geek On, we welcome adults, teenagers, kids, and geeks of all ages. Dive into a world of fantasy and fun with our diverse array of activities and merchandise designed to cater to every geek's interest.

Explore the Cosplay Showcase, engage in exhilarating Laser Tag battles, unleash your strategic skills in Trading Card Gaming, and relive your favorite arcade memories with free-to-play machines. Don't miss out on the animation and film room or the delectable offerings at our food court!

For the VIPs seeking a quieter start, we are thrilled to offer a Low Sensory VIP hour starting at 10:00 AM. Find out more on our Facebook event page. https://www.facebook.com/events/3049565388542611

The Te Rauparaha Arena and Aquatic Centre is not just a venue; it's an experience in itself. Conveniently located with ample free parking, onsite cafe, and even a swimming pool for added relaxation, your visit promises to be as enjoyable as the event itself.

In this geek paradise, the main corridor beckons with tables for Trading Card Gaming and a selection of arcade machines. The main arena hosts vendor stalls and the exciting main stage, while the smaller arena is dedicated to Laser Tag and the bustling Food Court.

Join us at Get Ya Geek On Mini-Con for a day of entertainment and celebration of all things geeky

events.id: August Burns Red 'Season Of Surrender' NZ Tour 2026-San Fran, Wellington-Ticket Master
events.name: August Burns Red 'Season Of Surrender' NZ Tour 2026
events.venue: San Fran, Wellington
events.long_description:
New Zealand, prepare to surrender to the might of AUGUST BURNS RED this October, with the GRAMMY Award-nominated stalwarts set to return to our shores joined by special guests BLOOM,
“We are so excited to headline and return back to New Zealand after a decade! We are bringing along our new friends in Bloom to make this a can’t miss show. This tour will also be the first place in the world where we do a tour celebrating the release of our brand new record Season of Surrender. We hope to see you all soon!” - August Burns Red
Crafting their signature brand of metalcore for over two decades, AUGUST BURNS RED have ferociously established themselves as one of the foremost acts in the genre. Hailing from Lancaster, Pennsylvania, the band's milestones and legacy spans everything from multiple top 10 placements on the Billboard Top 200 and tours with everyone from Bullet For My Valentine through to A Day To Remember and countless more. Also with two GRAMMY Award nominations in their wake, nominated for Best Metal Performance in 2016 for Identity and again in 2018 for Invisible Enemy, AUGUST BURNS RED have never rested on their laurels or stopped pushing the limits of their sound; a fact that has loudly carried into 2026 with the upcoming release of a their tenth studio album Season Of Surrender due out on June 5 via Fearless Records. 
On a live front, AUGUST BURNS RED's reputation is as formidable as their creative output, renowned for their visceral performances across the globe, most recently stupefying Australian crowds in 2023 supporting Polaris nationally on the Fatalism Tour. Praised by Scenestr for delivering "a raging masterclass in pure American metalcore with a 20-year, career-spanning set list that resonated spectacularly" of the Adelaide show supporting Polaris, X-Press Magazine also declared of the band's 2023 Perth performance on the same run that ABR demonstrated "why they have cemented themselves as one of the most respected names in the scene." 
One of Australia's most prolific rising heavy acts, BLOOM wield an open-veined take on melodic hardcore that has seen them tour the globe, release two full-length albums and cultivate an army of dedicated fans along the way. Emerging in 2017, BLOOM released their 2024 debut full-length, Maybe In Another Life, before swiftly turning their gaze to their 2025 sophomore album, The Light We Chase. Wowing with their multi-faceted and matured collection of songs on album #2, also expanding their creative reach further than ever before, BLOOM kicked off 2026 with their biggest headline performances to date, touring Australia nationally to celebrate The Light We Chase. Whether sharing stages with the likes of Silverstein and Thornhill or enamouring as headliners, you can definitely count on one thing when it comes to a BLOOM live show: it is seriously not to be missed.

events.id: Fletch, Vaughan & Hayley: The Genuine Friends Tour-The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Fletch, Vaughan & Hayley: The Genuine Friends Tour
events.venue: The Opera House, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
After years of filling Aotearoa’s mornings with laughter, three iconic radio hosts are stepping onto the stage. Carl Fletcher, Vaughan Smith and Hayley Sproull are otherwise known as Fletch, Vaughan, and Hayley. Their brand-new interactive stage show, The Genuine Friends Tour, is coming to The Opera House. It’ll be packed with fan-favourite segments from their ZM radio show, plus a few cheeky surprises. Expect games like Fact of the Day and Bet I Can Guess Your Mum’s Name. 
Join cutie Fletch (who insists audiobooks do count as reading), part-time farmer and full-time nerd Vaughan, and your relatable, smut-loving horn bag Hayley. Together they’ll charm Wellington audiences with hilarious energy, unfiltered storytelling, and quick-fire chemistry.

events.id: Cornelia Parker-City Gallery Wellington-Wellington NZ
events.name: Cornelia Parker
events.venue: City Gallery Wellington
events.long_description:
After three years of closure, the newly renovated and strengthened City Gallery Wellington Te Whare Toi will host the art of Cornelia Parker. The British visual artist is best known for her innovative and contemporary sculpture and installation art. Her large-scale, immersive installations explore themes of destruction and reconstruction, transformation and metamorphosis. Objects are exploded, crushed, and suspended in a world upended and remade anew.
Curated by independent UK-based curator Andrea Schlieker and City Gallery Wellington’s Senior Curator Aaron Lister, City Gallery Wellington’s exhibition will encompass Parker’s long career as an artist. This includes an immersive sound-and-light installation, ‘Stolen Thunder’. The work gathers the sounds of approaching storms from around the globe and across time. The exhibition will also showcase her paintings, photos, films, and sculptures.
“I have always said this is the one exhibition I want to do before I die,” said Charlotte Davy, Ringatohu Toi Director, Art and City Gallery Wellington. “I have been following Cornelia Parker’s work for the last twenty years. Bringing her mischievous, dark, and literally explosive work to Wellington is incredible.” 
Parker’s work often fills entire rooms and invites the viewer to move through and around the art. Given its physical scale, the blank canvas of the reopened gallery offers the perfect opportunity to host the exhibition. The exhibition will kick off in October 2026 and run through March 2027. 
Charlotte notes the exhibition’s themes are also of particular relevance to Wellington. “I hope that the exhibition and our return to Te Ngākau Civic Square will reflect our city and also help remake it. We are looking forward to opening with a bang.”
Hero image: © Cornelia Parker. Courtesy of the artist and Frith Street Gallery, London

Recommended 15+| Doors 5:30PM - Start Time 6:00PM
When Ray O’Leary ( Taskmaster NZ, Have You Been Paying Attention?, Best Show nominee Melbourne Comedy Festival) was born, winter changed to spring, a new star appeared in the heavens, and a double rainbow spontaneously appeared. With this auspicious start, it is no wonder he became leader of North Korea in 1994 until his untimely death in 2011. Despite this minor setback, he found time to bring you a whole new show.
“A very strong comic indeed” 
**** Chortle
“A jokesmith of the highest order”
**** The Skinny
“He twitches and moves nervously” 
***** Starburst Magazine

events.long_description:
After a two-year hiatus, a global children’s entertainment phenomenon is heading back to Wellington. The Wiggles — Live in Concert will bring unmistakable energy, music and fun to the TSB Arena.   
Designed as an interactive, joy-filled experience for families, the tour will see Anthony and Lucia (Blue Wiggles), Simon and Caterina (Red Wiggles), Tsehay and Evie (Yellow Wiggles) and Lachy and John (Purple Wiggles) light up the stage. Joining them are beloved Wiggly favourites Dorothy the Dinosaur, Wags the Dog, Henry the Octopus, Captain Feathersword and viral sensation, the Tree of Wisdom. 
The show is packed with playful pop culture parodies that parents will no doubt laugh along to. Kids can sing well-known songs like Portaloo, Do Do Do Doo, and Hootin’ Tootin’ Jump Suitin and learn the newest release Sparkle! This vibrant, must-watch experience highlights exactly why The Wiggles have captivated generations of families for over 35 years.

events.name: Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Cooking: More Tastes of Vietnamese Street Food
Come along and learn how to create wonderful Vietnamese dishes bursting with fresh flavours. 
Depending on what's seasonally available, dishes taught may include some of the following: green papaya prawn salad (Nộm tôm đu đủ xanh), lemongrass chicken stir fry with steamed rice (Cơm Gà rang sả) , grilled pork noodle and fried rolls (bún nem thịt nướng) and beef noodle soup (Phở bò). 
All ingredients provided but bring an apron, dish cloth, tea towels and containers to take food home.
Note:  Our cooking courses are designed to provide a collaborative and immersive learning experience where learners often work in pairs and/or groups. While we wish to create an inclusive environment for all participants, it is important to note that our courses are unable to accommodate specific food allergies or deviate from the preplanned dishes and menus.

events.id: Scottish Country Dancing-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Scottish Country Dancing
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Scottish Country Dancing
Why do Scottish Country Dancing? Great music and amazing ‘brain gym’ for body and mind! Scottish Country Dancing is a very social form of dancing, danced in "sets" usually with four "couples" - unlike Highland Dancing which is usually solo dancing. Have fun and meet new people, while getting fit!
It’s suitable for all levels of fitness, and is a great way of meeting and socialising with new people. Scottish country dancing is a common pastime for thousands of dancers in many countries, whether you’re in Japan, Germany, the US or Scotland itself.
Wear soft flat shoes and loose clothing and bring a bottle of water. No partner is needed – we change partners for every dance!
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Name and perform to beginner level the five major steps of Scottish Country Dancing
• Name and perform ten simple formations
• Participate in five simple Scottish Country dances, in strathspey (slow) and reel/jig (fast) tempo
• Clap rhythm to reel, jig and strathspey tempo
• Have the confidence and skills to join a club to progress your social dancing 
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk]. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

Bring along your project ideas and learn the techniques of glass cutting and making stained glass panels. Suitable for both beginners and those with previous knowledge.
NOTE: If this is your first Leadlighting course, you MUST attend the first session when key instruction is given. If you do not attend the compulsory first session, you will forfeit your course fees and will not be able to transfer to a future session of this course

events.id: Make Your Own Roman Blinds-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Make Your Own Roman Blinds
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Make Your Own Roman Blinds
Roman blinds are a perennial favourite because they use a lot less fabric than the usual pencil pleated or gathered curtains - as well as making your rooms look streamlined and uncluttered.
During the four weeks you will learn how to accurately measure your window to enable you to create very own customized roman blind. 
Some hardware included in course fee but a list of all other materials (fabric, etc) will be sent out to you when the course is confirmed.
Please choose a narrow window for your first project - max 150cms wide.
By the end of the course you'll be able to
Accurately measure your window 
Calculate how much fabric to purchase
Measure and cut out your curtain
Use appropriate machine skills to sew your curtain fabric
Use finishing skills to apply rings and cords to your curtain
Identify appropriate method to hang Roman Blind
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: Songwriting: How to Write Your Own Songs-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Songwriting: How to Write Your Own Songs
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Songwriting: How to Write Your Own Songs
The course is ideal for anyone who wants to write better songs - whether you’ve got a few songs under your belt or you’re a complete beginner. Take a song idea through to a finished song you can be proud of. Split into easy-to-follow steps, this course walks you through everything from finding titles to writing chord progressions and ensuring your lyrics and melodies fit together. You’ll share your creations with fellow songwriters and get personalised feedback. You’ll see exactly how to turn theory into practice and write your best songs yet.
This course includes:
-  Practical tips and techniques for songwriting
-  Ideas for making your songs stronger, more expressive and more appealing to your audience
-  How to overcome writer’s block, be an inspired and prolific songwriter who writes authentic songs
-  An opportunity to share your songs with fellow students, bounce off ideas and learn from one another
-  Learn how to structure a song effectively
-  The pillars of songwriting and how to use them
-  How to write better lyrics
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
-  Discover the exact steps to successfully write your own original songs; expanding a song from a verse and chorus, to a complete song while keeping your listeners engaged using transitions and dynamic contrasts between sections
-  Come up with ideas, write lyrics, melodies, chord progressions and instrumental grooves that have an emotional impact on you and your audience
-  Identify the structure of a great song, including how to combine melody, chords, lyrics and rhythm that create magic for your listeners
-  Possess the confidence and skills to find inspiration and get past common creative hurdles and blocks so you can develop an initial spark of creativity into a complete song
-  Stay inspired and find your authentic voice as a songwriter. r
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Latin American Dance
Join Alex White from "Viva Latina" dance school and learn some Spanish language and culture through dance and, to keep it as authentic as possible, many of the dance sequence names will be in Spanish as well as English. We’ll be focusing on Salsa and Tango, but will also look at the basics of other common Latin dance styles: Merengue, Bachata, Kizomba and Zouk.
Couples are welcome but partners are not essential (please note however that we cannot guarantee equal numbers of men and women).
By the end of the course, you'll be able to:  
• Demonstrate basic steps
• Confidently lead or follow basic turn patterns and key fundamentals in Salsa and Tango
• Identify and practise the basic movement characteristics of Zouk, Bachata, Kizomba and Merengue  
• Recognise different styles and tempos of Latin dance music
Wear comfortable clothing and shoes suitable for dancing and a bottle of water.
[This is a physical activity that involves an element of risk.  While all possible measures are taken to prevent accidents or injury, please note that participation is at your own risk].

events.id: Macy Gray Live-St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand-Wellington NZ
events.name: Macy Gray Live
events.venue: St James Theatre, Wellington, New Zealand
events.long_description:
The rasp in her singing voice is as immediately recognisable as her talent for entertaining. Nobody puts on a show like R&B/soul singer Macy Gray. The Ohio-born artist came to fame in 1999 with her triple-platinum debut album ‘On How Life Is’. Her concert at Wellington’s St James Theatre will deliver a jaw-dropping celebration of chart-topping hits I Try, Still, Do Something, Sexual Revolution, and Sweet Baby. You can also expect her new single I’m Too Sexy from her upcoming album, The Trouble With Truth. 
Presented in association with the Wellington Jazz Festival, this is a rare chance to see one of the great R&B and soul artists of a generation. Macy Gray has sold over 25 million albums worldwide and won a GRAMMY Award along with two BRIT Awards. She has collaborated with everyone from Ariana Grande to Maino and Galactic. Her music consistently resonates throughout pop culture, transcending many genres and building a devout and diverse fan base.

events.id: Calligratherapy - Beginners-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: Calligratherapy - Beginners
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
Calligratherapy - Beginners
Many of us live sedentary lifestyles, sitting for up to eight hours or more a day hunched over our keyboard - all the while rarely taking a proper breath! Even if we wanted to take a deep enriching breath into the lower lobes of our lungs, we are limited due to our posture. Often due to the stresses and strains of a busy work and life balance, our unconscious brain can develop a pattern of holding, irregular and shadow breaths.
Does this sound like your experience? 
Not being able to breathe properly is the key cause of many chronic respiratory diseases (Nestor, 2020)
Calligratherapy combines science journalist James Nestor's theory on proper breathing with the philosophy of ancient Chinese calligraphy to promote a state of optimal wellbeing.
Calligratherapy has been developed specifically for those who have never practiced calligraphy before and who are interested in deriving the benefits without having to learn the language or all the characters. It integrates the simple and intuitive act of inhaling and exhaling into calligraphy practice with Eastern philosophy behind it.
A range of different breathing exercises are embedded into each 90 minute session focusing on different calligraphy scripts.  Over the five weeks, you will develop and practice the awareness of healthy breathing, while learning new techniques of Chinese soft brush calligraphy covering five different scripts. 
All materials included in course fee.
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
Start a new creative practice in Chinese soft brush calligraphy
Develop your own mindfulness strategy
Better manage everyday stress levels and access a calm, empowered and positive state of mind
Find ways to explore eastern philosophy and develop deeper connections with your spiritual self. 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".

events.id: French - Introductory 2-Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington-Wellington High School
events.name: French - Introductory 2
events.venue: Wellington High School, 249 Taranaki Street, Te Aro, Wellington
events.long_description:
French - Introductory 2
For those who have done Introductory 1 (16 hours) or have equivalent knowledge (e.g. who may have picked up some basic survival language through travel).
By the end of the course, you’ll be able to:
• Use basic French in a variety of everyday situations with some confidence (e.g. holidays, travel, at home)
• Use the present, the imperative and the near future tenses of regular and some common irregular verbs, in both the affirmative and negative forms
• Use prepositions with names of countries, cities and places
• Name and locate places in town, including shops
• Ask and give directions and ask and understand the time 
Note:  Courses need a minimum number to go ahead so if we don't have enough enrolments a few days before the start date, we have to cancel. We'll let you know by email so please check yours regularly. If you need to cancel your registration, here are our terms and conditions: "What You Need to Know".
